VORSITZ: KATARINA BARLEY
Vizepr脙陇sidentin

脗聽

2. Recent peace agreement in the Middle East and the role of the EU (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Als n脙陇chster Punkt der Tagesordnung folgt die Aussprache 脙录ber die Erkl脙陇rung der Kommission zu dem j脙录ngsten Friedensabkommen im Nahen Osten und der Rolle der EU (2025/2930(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dubravka 脜聽uica, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, Excellencies, ladies and gentlemen, first of all, thank you for providing the opportunity to follow up on the debate which we had on 7脗聽October, and to address the major developments which took place in the meantime.

As you know, the situation is evolving rapidly, yet it remains deeply fragile 芒聙聯 in particular, with regards to the ongoing dynamics in Gaza and its broader implications for the Middle East.

In that context, what can be the role of the EU? I am aware that some of you, dear Members, are travelling to Israel and Palestine next week, and I very much welcome your engagement.

The Gaza conflict remains at the heart of regional politics. Despite the recent ceasefire and the hostage release deal between Israel and Hamas, the humanitarian situation in Gaza remains catastrophic.

The first phase of the agreement has shown promising signs, with the release of hostages and political prisoners. This is a major achievement and an immense relief for their families. However, we must see tangible progress on three critical fronts. First, the full release of all deceased hostages also; the withdrawal of the Israeli army along the agreed lines; and the substantial and unimpeded flow of humanitarian aid to Gaza.

The European Union is uniquely positioned to play a pivotal role in this delicate situation. We stand ready to contribute to the comprehensive 20-point plan aimed at stabilising the region, whilst also continuing to engage both with the Palestinian Authority and the Israeli Government.

First, the European Union can play a crucial role in supporting Gaza through immediate and large-scale humanitarian aid, working through key partners. This involves increasing aid deliveries via the Humanitarian Air Bridge and exploring all access routes, including the Cyprus corridor.

Additionally, we can scale up medical evacuations of Palestinian patients to Europe through the EU Civil Protection Mechanism. We can also consider deploying European civil protection resources to support critical operations, such as search and rescue, rubble removal and decontamination.

In the humanitarian context, the European Union will remain a steadfast supporter of the United Nations and its agencies. In fact, the United Nations' presence is indispensable to achieving immediate humanitarian relief. But we also need the United Nations to transition from emergency assistance to comprehensive recovery and overall stabilisation, in view of supporting and empowering Palestinian institutions.

Secondly, the European Union has the tools to support security arrangements. The mission EUBAM Rafah is ready to redeploy and ensure a third-party presence at the Rafah Crossing Point. EUPOL COPPS could consider extending police training activities further to Gaza. We are also assessing the ways to finance and provide expertise for disarmament, demobilisation and reintegration. However, this needs to be rooted in a clear political process.

Thirdly, we have a keen interest to contribute to governance arrangements. We explore membership in the Board of Peace oversight body, while we can also provide technical support to the technocratic Palestinian committee.

Governance also comprises the reform of the Palestinian Authority that we would like to jointly monitor via the newly established Palestine Donor Group, to which I'll come back later. On governance issues, the European Union has also been consistently clear on the need for the role of the Palestinian Authority in the future of Gaza.

Fourth, on reconstruction, President von der Leyen announced, in her State of the Union speech here, a dedicated support package for Gaza's recovery and reconstruction. The Commission is currently evaluating options, given the rapidly evolving situation on the ground.

Dear colleagues, let me assure you that the European Union remains steadfast in its support for the two-state solution. Providing a clear political horizon for the Palestinians is not just a matter of immediate necessity, but is crucial for the successful implementation of the peace plan.

In this respect, the European Union will take forward the work of the Global Alliance based on its renewed mandate at UNGA in a follow-up to the New York Declaration. On our side, we will mobilise all relevant Commission tools at our disposal and we will start with the first meeting of the Palestine Donor Group soon, around the date of 20脗聽November, jointly with the Palestinian Authority 芒聙聯 we hope in the margins of the next Foreign Affairs Council in November.

This Palestine Donor Group will serve a twofold objective. First, to establish a platform for the Palestine Authority to present the implementation of its reforms agenda and attract more donors and regional partners. And second, to coordinate efforts for Gaza's recovery and reconstruction, once conditions allow.

In conclusion, the Middle East stands at a critical juncture, as all of us know. While the recent developments in Gaza offer glimmers of hope, sustained and concerted efforts are required to ensure lasting peace.

Thank you so much for listening and I am looking forward to your exchange.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽David McAllister, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Madam Commissioner, for one week there is renewed hope for a genuine, lasting peace in the Middle East. But hope alone will not sustain peace. There are many actors determined to sabotage this process; first and foremost, the terror organisation Hamas itself. Indeed, Hamas attacks on Palestinian civilians and its refusal to disarm make the ceasefire increasingly fragile.

What the region needs now is our engagement, and what the European Union must offer is a united Middle East strategy. We ought to focus our energy where Europe can make a real difference. Let us work with regional partners to reinforce and deliver humanitarian aid as quickly as possible. Let us support key regional actors 芒聙聯 the United States, the Gulf states and Israel's Arab neighbours 芒聙聯 in securing the ceasefire. The willingness of several Arab countries to commit themselves politically, financially and diplomatically is, in itself, a breakthrough. Let us counter those who thrive on instability, for example Iran, and ensure that opponents to peace cannot derail it. Let us keep the prospect of a negotiated two-state solution alive, however distant it may seem today. Above all, let us resist the temptation to divide ourselves further. We must focus on the common denominator that unites us 芒聙聯 our unwavering commitment to peace.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jordan Bardella, au nom du groupe PfE. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, dans les pierres br脙禄lantes de J脙漏rusalem se trouvent les racines des trois grands monoth脙漏ismes. Sur cette terre o脙鹿 le ciel fr脙麓le la guerre depuis des si脙篓cles, la paix subsiste comme une esp脙漏rance fragile et pr脙漏cieuse. L'accord r脙漏cemment conclu entre Isra脙漏liens et Palestiniens sous l'脙漏gide du pr脙漏sident脗聽Trump n'est pas un miracle, mais un courage politique devenu r脙漏alit脙漏. Il marque un pas, peut-脙陋tre h脙漏sitant, mais essentiel, vers cette paix que tant d'hommes et de femmes osent 脙聽 peine esp脙漏rer.

La lib脙漏ration des derniers otages, ces vies arrach脙漏es 脙聽 l'enfer depuis le脗聽7脗聽octobre脗聽2023, est un soulagement profond pour toutes les familles 脙漏prouv脙漏es dans ce conflit. Mais qu'on ne s'y trompe pas, la paix ne se d脙漏cr脙篓te pas. Elle se b脙垄tit chaque minute, chaque seconde. Ce processus fragile, autant que le d脙漏sarmement total du Hamas, exige un appui international constant, bien au-del脙聽 des postures diplomatiques sans lendemain. Je veux ici saluer le courage du Pr脙漏sident am脙漏ricain. Il a accompli ce que beaucoup jugeaient impossible dans cet Orient compliqu脙漏. Il a rouvert le champ du possible.

Nous, Fran脙搂ais, h脙漏ritiers d'une nation s脙漏culaire qui fut 脙聽 la fois phare et 脙漏quilibre du monde, nous ne pouvons accepter d'脙陋tre rel脙漏gu脙漏s aux marges de l'Histoire, ni de voir notre voix confondue dans celle d'une Union europ脙漏enne que certains, ici m脙陋me, voudraient 脙漏riger en super-脙聣tat, capable de parler au nom des nations. La France doit retrouver la force d'une voix singuli脙篓re, libre et pleinement autonome.

Par ses volte-faces et son narcissisme diplomatique, le pr脙漏sident fran脙搂ais a rendu notre nation illisible. Le pr脙漏sident脗聽Macron aura r脙漏ussi le triste exploit de froisser Isra脙芦l sans convaincre le monde arabe, d'avoir voulu incarner tout et son contraire, et de n'脙陋tre au final plus rien. Le g脙漏n脙漏ral de Gaulle le disait, il n'y a de politique que celle des r脙漏alit脙漏s. C'est dans cette fid脙漏lit脙漏 que nous devons 脜聯uvrer, non pour plaire, mais pour peser, pour redonner 脙聽 la parole fran脙搂aise sa constance, pour retrouver la noblesse d'une diplomatie faite de dialogue, d'脙漏quilibre et de grandeur.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nicola Procaccini, a nome del gruppo ECR. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, nell'ultima plenaria abbiamo salutato con gioia la fine della guerra a Gaza, dunque il rilascio degli ostaggi israeliani e l'ingresso degli aiuti umanitari attraverso i valichi finalmente riaperti.

Non tutti, qui dentro, hanno mostrato lo stesso sollievo. La faccia di molti colleghi era piuttosto quella di chi non ha pi脙鹿 il giocattolo con cui giocare a fare "i rivoluzionari in barca a vela". Alcuni sembrano addirittura sofferenti per questo accordo di pace, perch脙漏 lo considerano un tradimento della causa palestinese ad opera degli stessi palestinesi: una tesi surreale!

Purtroppo, 脙篓 vero che questo accordo 脙篓 pericolosamente precario. E lo 脙篓 perch脙漏 il punto pi脙鹿 difficile del piano 脙篓 il disarmo di Hamas, la rete terroristica che tiene in ostaggio gli uomini e le donne di Gaza.

Adesso la domanda 脙篓: con chi state voi? Una deputata europea della sinistra pubblica post sui social network in cui si celebra Hamas per le fucilazioni dei palestinesi accusati di collaborare con Israele. "One by one" 脙篓 stato scritto: uno per uno devono essere presi e uccisi. Complimenti! E voi sareste i pacifisti?

Noi conservatori pensiamo che si debba andare avanti sul piano di pace avanzato dal Presidente Trump, sottoscritto da israeliani e palestinesi, oltre che dagli Stati arabi ed europei, anche perch脙漏 i suoi effetti di stabilit脙聽 e sicurezza si estenderebbero in tutto il Medio Oriente e, con un po' di ottimismo, anche oltre.

Oggi sembra possibile pensare ad un congelamento della linea di conflitto tra Russia e Ucraina: noi la saluteremmo come una buona notizia. Non credo sarebbe lo stesso per altri colleghi qui dentro. Mi riferisco a quelli che amano definirsi pacifisti ma si innamorano delle guerre.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hilde Vautmans, namens de Renew-Fractie. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, collega芒聙聶s, ja, we moeten het vredesakkoord toejuichen, de vrijlating van de gijzelaars en het staakt-het-vuren verwelkomen. Maar heel voorzichtig, want laat ons eerlijk zijn: het is maar een heel klein stapje, heel broos en het is eigenlijk al geschonden door beide partijen. Mensen blijven sterven door geweld, door hongersnood. Daarom herhaal ik hier onze oproep luid en duidelijk: open alle grensovergangen, laat die hulp toe en schort de EU-Isra脙芦l- handelsovereenkomst op.

Sanctioneer alle verantwoordelijken: Hamasleiders, extremistische kolonisten, Isra脙芦lische ministers, iedereen die aanzet tot misdaden of misdaden pleegt. Stop de wapenexporten die oorlogsmisdaden mogelijk maken, erken de Palestijnse staat met een concrete tijdslijn en duidelijke voorwaarden, en investeer in de heropbouw van Gaza.

Terwijl we allemaal naar Gaza kijken, staat ook de Westelijke Jordaanoever in brand. Tussen 2008 en 2023 werden minstens duizend door Europa gefinancierde projecten vernield, waaronder ook Belgische scholen en opvangplaatsen. Dit werd onlangs onthuld in een heel mooie reportage van de Belgische VRT. En dat allemaal zonder sancties. Er is een Vlaams gezegde: 芒聙聹potje breken, potje betalen芒聙聺. Laten we stoppen met brieven schrijven. De tijd van vriendelijke diplomatie is voorbij. Laten we de druk opvoeren. Ik reken op Europa om het werk van Trump af te maken en te zorgen voor die tweestatenoplossing.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hannah Neumann, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, Donald Trump may get a ceasefire signed, but for sure he has neither the ability nor the patience to build lasting peace. His plan is riddled with flaws: no path for Palestinian self-determination, no timeline for Israel's full withdrawal, no agreement on disarming Hamas, no word on accountability for the crimes of the past two years. Trump wants the applause and counts on everyone else to deal with the messy reality, and on the Gulf countries and Europe to pay the bill. But who says we need to just nod along? It's time for Europe to move from being the passive payer to using our leverage, to finally break the cycle of rebuilding what will only be destroyed again.

The hostages are free. May they, and their families, find the strength to heal. But for civilians in Gaza, little has changed. They still suffer from Israeli attacks, from Hamas violence, from starvation. The Arab states need to keep the pressure on Hamas; Europe needs to keep the focus on the Israeli Government. Netanyahu still turns aid on and off at will. As long as food and medicine do not reach everyone, consequences 芒聙聯 from the suspension of the EU-Israel Association Agreement to sanctions 芒聙聯 need to be back on the table.

While the world watches Gaza, our attention needs to extend to the West Bank. Settler violence grows, in an open attempt to undermine the two-state solution. Palestinians have a right to their own state and to ownership of reconstruction. Europe was too divided to stop the war. Let us at least be united now, to protect and nurture the fragile hope that remains.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lynn Boylan, on behalf of The Left Group. 芒聙聯 A Uachtar脙隆in, 11脗聽days ago, we welcomed the ceasefire, but since then, 153脗聽tons of bombs have dropped on Gaza. 62 is the number of Israeli occupation forces shooting incidents in the West Bank. 67脗聽000 is the very conservative estimate for Palestinians killed, unknown is the number under the rubble. 169脗聽000 is the number of Palestinians injured, 4脗聽000 is the number of children who have lost limbs. 154 is the number of children who have died from starvation. 355 is the number of bullets fired at Hind Rajab by the Israeli occupation forces. 1脗聽722 is the number of health and aid workers murdered. 34 is the number of hospitals attacked. Almost 300 is the number of journalists and media workers murdered. 2 is the number of ICC arrest warrants for members of the Israeli Government. 0 is the number of actions taken by the European Union.

This ceasefire cannot be an excuse for the EU to sit on its hands. Now is the time for the EU to restore its credibility and to end its complicity in this genocide. There can be no peace without justice 芒聙聯 there must be accountability for the lives behind these numbers. Their lives have to mean something. So please, EU, there must be accountability.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hildegard Bentele (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear Commissioner, dear colleagues, what if Hamas refuses to lay down its arms? That's what I asked in the last plenary, referring to the frequently mentioned notion of High Representative Kallas: that there is no military solution. And this is exactly the situation we face today.

Hamas has released the living hostages and Israel has withdrawn to the agreed yellow line, enacted a ceasefire and released hundreds of prisoners. The next step should be the disarmament and withdrawal of Hamas from Gaza. Any guess who's not fulfilling its part? The moment Israel withdrew, Hamas fighters returned, executing civilians, rearming, attacking IDF positions.

All the pressure must now be on Hamas and its main sponsors, T脙录rkiye and Qatar, to uphold the deal. My recommendation: travel the region instead of giving Hamas the illusion that Europe will help rebuild their terror state. There can be no European money for reconstruction as long as Hamas remains in power. Full stop.

There can be no vacuum either. The next diplomatic tasks are clear: establish the peace board and international oversight for Gaza. We need guarantors, verifiers of the ceasefire and credible non-terror-affiliated Palestinians to help form new governance structures.

According to the UN declaration you signed, elections in the PA-controlled territories are due within a year 芒聙聯 time to accelerate the process. Please convey to the High Representative: she is the EU's top diplomat. There are a dozen urgent diplomatic tasks ahead, and in all of them, Israel must play a key role.

So please come up with new messages beyond sanctions or the EU will still be marginalised.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nacho S脙隆nchez Amor (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, hay un riesgo nuevo alrededor del conflicto de Gaza: la relajaci脙鲁n, la ca脙颅da de la atenci脙鲁n de la opini脙鲁n p脙潞blica internacional, lo cual es hasta l脙鲁gico despu脙漏s de tantos meses de tensi脙鲁n; pero es un riesgo que hay que conjurar, y no abandonarse a la idea de que hay un proceso de paz en marcha que 芒聙聰con sobresaltos芒聙聰 ir脙隆 dando resultados.

Para empezar 芒聙聰como se ha dicho aqu脙颅芒聙聰 no es un verdadero proceso de paz sino, de momento, solo un alto el fuego que ni siquiera ha conseguido parar las muertes, que ya se cuentan 芒聙聰otra vez芒聙聰 por decenas; un alto el fuego, por cierto, que compromet脙颅a a Israel a permitir la entrada de ayuda humanitaria y que, en una semana, ha permitido 800 camiones cuando hacen falta 600 cada d脙颅a y, por supuesto, esta ayuda 芒聙聰inevitablemente芒聙聰 no puede llegar al norte devastado de la Franja; pero es que esto no solo va de comida, de alimentos, esto va de servicios esenciales como el agua y la atenci脙鲁n sanitaria, al menos b脙隆sica.

Otro aspecto en el que no hay que relajarse: la rendici脙鲁n de cuentas por los cr脙颅menes cometidos. Como actuemos ahora ser脙隆 la medida para Putin: cuando haya un alto el fuego en Ucrania, 脗驴vamos a abandonar la exigencia de responsabilidades a Putin? Atenci脙鲁n al dilema y a la respuesta, porque un tratamiento diferente pondr脙隆 de nuevo a prueba nuestra credibilidad en el Sur Global. As脙颅 que, cuando se plantee el dilema sobre las responsabilidades en la Franja, no perdamos de vista el caso de Rusia y las responsabilidades personales de Putin.

Este Parlamento aprob脙鲁 en septiembre que se restablezcan en su totalidad el mandato y la financiaci脙鲁n del UNRWA. El plan de Trump, afortunadamente, no excluye a ninguna agencia internacional. En Gaza van a aparecer muchos actores novatos 芒聙聰desde pol脙颅ticos a agentes de la propiedad inmobiliaria芒聙聰 y algunos, seguramente, combinando ambas almas, pero solo el UNRWA tiene 20 000 palestinos sobre el terreno, expertos en la provisi脙鲁n de todo tipo de servicios p脙潞blicos, que podr脙颅an ser el germen de una futura Administraci脙鲁n palestina sin Ham脙隆s.

脗聽脗聽Kinga G脙隆l (PfE). 芒聙聯 Eln脙露k Asszony! K脙漏t 脙漏vvel azut脙隆n, hogy a Ham脙隆sz terrorizmusa embertelen szenved脙漏st hozott izraeliekre, palesztinekre, v脙漏get 脙漏rhet a gy脙露trelem a t脙漏rs脙漏gben. K脙漏t 脙漏v tort脙潞ra ut脙隆n az izraeli t脙潞szok v脙漏gre hazat脙漏rhettek. Trump eln脙露k t脙露rt脙漏nelmi l脙漏p脙漏se azt 脙录zeni: a diplom脙隆cia m脜卤k脙露dik, ha van m脙露g脙露tte er脜聭 脙漏s akarat. A k脙露zel-keleti rendez脙漏s nem puszt脙隆n region脙隆lis 脙录gy, hanem glob脙隆lis geopolitikai fordul脙鲁pont. Az Uni脙鲁 m脙漏gis kimaradt ebb脜聭l a rendez脙漏sb脜聭l a K脙露zel-Keleten. K脙漏t h脙漏ttel ezel脜聭tt a Bizotts脙隆g eln脙露ke az Izrael elleni szankci脙鲁kr脙鲁l besz脙漏lt itt, ebben a teremben, mik脙露zben m脙隆r zajlottak az el脜聭k脙漏sz脙录letek a b脙漏kemeg脙隆llapod脙隆s ir脙隆ny脙隆ba.

Az Uni脙鲁 ezzel strat脙漏giai helyzet脙漏t gyeng脙颅tette, mik脙露zben az Eur脙鲁pai Uni脙鲁nak meg kell k脙录zdenie egy tov脙隆bbi s脙潞lyos probl脙漏m脙隆val, az egyre agressz脙颅vebb antiszemitizmussal. A nyugat-eur脙鲁pai utc脙隆kon zajl脙鲁 antiszemita, Izrael-ellenes incidensek megengedhetetlenek. B脙录szke vagyok r脙隆, hogy ezzel szemben Magyarorsz脙隆g a biztons脙隆g szigete a zsid脙鲁 k脙露z脙露ss脙漏g sz脙隆m脙隆ra. Ezt sz脙隆mos rendezv脙漏ny biztons脙隆gos megszervez脙漏se bizony脙颅tja. A Trump 脙隆ltal el脙漏rt b脙漏ke G脙隆z脙隆ban 脙露szt脙露nz脜聭 kell legyen a tov脙隆bbi b脙漏kekezdem脙漏nyez脙漏sre, hogy a K脙露zel-Kelet ut脙隆n val脙鲁s脙隆gg脙隆 v脙隆ljon Ukrajn脙隆ban is. Egy potenci脙隆lis budapesti Trump芒聙聯Putyin cs脙潞cstal脙隆lkoz脙鲁 lehet erre egy t脙露rt脙漏nelmi lehet脜聭s脙漏g, egy es脙漏ly a b脙漏k脙漏re. Ehhez az Uni脙鲁nak v脙漏gre konstrukt脙颅van kellene viszonyulnia, nehogy megint a kispadon maradjon.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bert-Jan Ruissen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, we hebben ze allemaal kunnen zien: de beelden van thuiskomende gijzelaars, de innige omhelzingen, de intense vreugde. Daar werd het leven gevierd. We zagen echter ook andere beelden, die van geknielde Gazanen die door Hamas standrechtelijk werden ge脙芦xecuteerd. Een uitzinnige menigte eromheen, juichend bij de dood. Huiveringwekkend. En wat een contrast!

Opnieuw werd schrijnend duidelijk hoe kwaadaardig de Hamas-ideologie is en hoe deze nog velen in Gaza in haar greep houdt. Het is daarom zaak prioriteit te geven aan twee belangrijke onderdelen van het vredesplan, namelijk ontwapening van Hamas en deradicalisering van de Palestijnse samenleving.

Nu heeft de Europese Unie bij de totstandkoming van het vredesakkoord geen rol van betekenis gespeeld. Daar moeten wij eerlijk in zijn. Maar nu er een akkoord ligt, kunnen wij wel degelijk helpen bij de realisering ervan. Ik vraag de Commissie daarom drie dingen te doen:脗聽te garanderen dat er geen euro Europees belastinggeld ten goede komt aan Hamas;脗聽te eisen dat alle aanzetten tot haat en geweld uit de Palestijnse schoolboeken worden verwijderd;脗聽per direct het voorgestelde sanctiepakket tegen Isra脙芦l in te trekken.

Zeker nu Isra脙芦l zich aan de afspraken houdt, is er geen enkele aanleiding om daar nog mee door te gaan.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bernard Guetta (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, inutile, se dit-on d'脙漏taler nos divergences, et pourtant si. Il est h脙漏las temps, vraiment temps, de vous dire, M.脗聽Trump, que votre incons脙漏quence est aussi irresponsable sur le Proche-Orient que sur l'Ukraine. Vous ne pouvez pas vous laissez retourner par le bluff d'un Poutine promettant 脙聽 l'Ukraine de la d脙漏truire si elle ne met pas genou 脙聽 terre. Vous ne pouvez pas trahir l'Europe en abandonnant les Ukrainiens, et si vous ne voulez malheureusement plus leur donner un seul dollar, au moins n'allez pas sceller leur destin avec ce lointain successeur d'Ivan le Terrible.

Si vous pr脙漏tendez apporter la paix 脙漏ternelle au Proche-Orient, cessez, oui, cessez, M.脗聽Trump, d'oublier la colonisation de la Cisjordanie et de dire n'importe quoi sur le Hamas, f脙漏licit脙漏 avant-hier d'脙漏liminer les 脗芦bad guys脗禄 脙聽 Gaza et aujourd'hui menac脙漏 d'un envoi de vos troupes.

Vous avez achev脙漏 cette semaine de briser toute confiance en vous et c'est d脙漏cid脙漏ment sans vous qu'il nous faut 脙陋tre et agir.

脗聽脗聽Villy S脙赂vndal (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Siden fredsaftalen tr脙楼dte i kraft, er n脙娄sten et hundrede pal脙娄stinensere blevet dr脙娄bt, og kun halvdelen af de lovede m脙娄ngder af n脙赂dhj脙娄lp f脙楼r lov til at komme ind. De humanit脙娄re forhold er katastrofale, som kommiss脙娄ren sagde.

P脙楼 Vestbredden er bos脙娄ttervolden brutal, antallet af vejsp脙娄rringer er i voldsom v脙娄kst, pal脙娄stinensiske b脙赂nders olivenh脙赂st bliver 脙赂delagt, og folk forvises fra deres eget land.

Men tusind m脙娄nd, kvinder og b脙赂rn sidder stadig i de israelske f脙娄ngsler - mange uden rettergang og rettigheder - de fleste udsat for ydmygelser. En af dem, der burde s脙娄ttes fri er Marwan Barghouti. En mand, der potentielt kan samle pal脙娄stinenserne. Han og Pal脙娄stina b脙赂r f脙楼 en chance for at finde en f脙娄lles vej for frihed og fred. Det b脙赂r EU presse p脙楼 for, ligesom vi skal st脙赂tte afradikaliseringen p脙楼 begge sider og kr脙娄ve, at krigsforbrydere stilles til ansvar for deres krigsforbrydelser.

脗聽脗聽Rima Hassan (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, dans un contexte colonial, la paix d脙漏crit toujours la tranquillit脙漏 du colonisateur, jamais la justice pour le colonis脙漏. Derri脙篓re le masque des faiseurs de paix, il y a aussi le visage des architectes du g脙漏nocide et du colonialisme, pour qui la Palestine est d'abord le terrain d'une expansion politique, id脙漏ologique et 脙漏conomique.

Depuis son entr脙漏e en vigueur, le cessez-le-feu a 脙漏t脙漏 viol脙漏 plus de 80脗聽fois par Isra脙芦l, faisant 97脗聽victimes palestiniennes. Sur la seule journ脙漏e de dimanche, 153脗聽tonnes de bombes ont 脙漏t脙漏 largu脙漏es sur Gaza. La menace de sanctions commerciales annonc脙漏es le mois dernier par l'Union europ脙漏enne serait actuellement en passe d'脙陋tre abandonn脙漏e. L'Union europ脙漏enne ne rate jamais une occasion de blanchir Isra脙芦l de ses crimes, qui perdurent encore aujourd'hui 脙聽 Gaza et en Cisjordanie.

De la d脙漏claration Balfour 脙聽 nos jours, les 脙聣tats occidentaux ont syst脙漏matiquement commis l'erreur de penser l'avenir de la Palestine sans les Palestiniens. D'une main, ils signent les accords de paix, de l'autre, ils livrent les armes 脙聽 l'脙聣tat qui pratique le g脙漏nocide. Depuis la Nakba, les Palestiniens n'ont en r脙漏alit脙漏 jamais connu ce que le monde libre et civilis脙漏 appelle la paix, car la premi脙篓re qu脙陋te du colonis脙漏, c'est sa lib脙漏ration du joug colonial. Rien, absolument rien dans cet accord n'embrasse cette qu脙陋te du peuple palestinien qui est mis sous tutelle internationale.

La paix en Palestine ne peut se traduire que par la fin de l'impunit脙漏 d'Isra脙芦l, la fin de la complicit脙漏 occidentale avec ces crimes de colonisation, d'occupation, d'apartheid et de g脙漏nocide. Autrement dit, pas de paix sans justice. La paix impos脙漏e par l'imp脙漏rialisme est une guerre invisible, une guerre qui n'a pas de nom, mais qui est pr脙漏sente dans chaque geste de domination. Si Edward脗聽Sa脙炉d 脙漏tait vivant, il ajouterait 脙聽 sa propre citation: 脗芦Cet accord est un 脙漏ni脙篓me geste de domination de l'Occident sur l'Orient脗禄.

脗聽脗聽Siegbert Frank Droese (ESN). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, verehrte Kollegen! Der j脙录ngste Friedensschluss im Nahen Osten ist ein historisches Ereignis. Der Frieden wurde nicht in Br脙录ssel vorbereitet, nicht in den Gremien dieser EU, nicht im Europ脙陇ischen Rat 芒聙聯 nein, w脙陇hrend diese EU von Werten palavert und sich mit voller Kraft der Aufr脙录stung gegen Russland widmet, schaffen echte Staatsm脙陇nner Tatsachen. Donald Trump schafft Frieden.

Das Friedensabkommen im Nahen Osten zeigt also: Es braucht entschlossene Diplomatie, und es braucht den Willen zum Frieden. Es zeigt auch: Diese EU ist drau脙聼en 芒聙聯 mal mit Ausnahme von Viktor Orb脙隆n. Umso absurder ist es, dass sich Politiker wie Macron, S脙隆nchez oder Merz in 脙聞gypten feiern lassen wollten, w脙陇hrend sie in ihren Heimatl脙陇ndern die importierte Gewalt der Islamisten hofieren und Demonstrationen gegen j脙录dische Mitb脙录rger zulassen.

Machen wir uns nichts vor: Die Welt ordnet sich neu. Diese EU der von der Leyens und Eurokraten-Nichtsnutze ist Zuschauer. Fakt ist: Am Ende wird die Geschichte jene belohnen, die f脙录r den Frieden, die Koexistenz k脙陇mpfen. Den Rest bestraft bekanntlich das Leben.

脗聽脗聽Katar脙颅na Roth Neve脛聫alov脙隆 (NI). 芒聙聯 Pani predsedn脙颅脛聧ka, kolegovia, kone脛聧ne m脙隆me na tomto 脙潞zem脙颅 mier. Ja som hrd脙隆, 脜戮e Slovensk脙隆 republika uznala Palest脙颅nu ako 脜隆t脙隆t u脜戮 v roku 1993 a 脜戮e sme takisto nev脙隆hali po脛聧as t脙陆chto bojov, ktor脙漏 tam prebiehali posledn脙漏 dva roky, tam posiela脜楼 humanit脙隆rnu pomoc. Dva 脜隆t脙隆ty s脙潞 jedin脙陆m rie脜隆en脙颅m tohto konfliktu a teraz je aj na Eur脙鲁pskej 脙潞nii, aby sa sna脜戮ila, aby to takto dopadlo. Samozrejme, na脜隆ou 脙潞lohou je takisto podpori脜楼 Palest脙颅n脛聧anov, naozaj zabezpe脛聧i脜楼, aby bola humanit脙隆rna pomoc na svojom mieste, aby sa ukon脛聧il hladomor na tomto 脙潞zem脙颅 a aby z toho nevznikol nov脙陆 Afganistan. A to m脙麓脜戮eme robi脜楼 len t脙陆m, 脜戮e budeme na mieste, 脜戮e budeme podporova脜楼 t脙陆ch 脛戮ud脙颅, ktor脙颅 stoja za tou mierovou iniciat脙颅vou. Aj ke脛聫 je to Donald Trump, ktor脙漏ho mo脜戮no nem脙隆me v脜隆etci radi, na druhej strane je to jedin脙陆 脛聧lovek a Spojen脙漏 脜隆t脙隆ty boli jedin脙隆 krajina, ktor脙隆 dok脙隆zala nakoniec tieto dve strany prinies脜楼 za jeden st脙麓l, a naozaj vytvori脜楼 tam moment脙隆lne t脙潞 dohodu, ktor脙潞 ver脙颅me, 脜戮e bude trval脙隆. Pri脜隆iel 脛聧as na re脜隆pekt a 脛戮udsk脙潞 d脙麓stojnos脜楼 pre v脜隆etk脙陆ch a takisto na to, aby sme nezab脙潞dali, 脜戮e t脙颅 脛戮udia, ktor脙颅 zahynuli, nielen t脙颅 civilisti, ale aj t脙颅 novin脙隆ri, aj t脙颅 humanit脙隆rni pracovn脙颅ci, si zasl脙潞脜戮ia spravodlivos脜楼.

脗聽脗聽Michael Gahler (PPE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Vielen Dank, Frau Kommissarin, f脙录r den 脙聹berblick 脙录ber all das, was wir als EU tun k脙露nnen und tun werden. Das passt auch alles in den 20-Punkte-Plan. Von diesen 20 Punkten ist tats脙陇chlich bisher die Freilassung der Geiseln erfolgt, wor脙录ber wir uns mit den Betroffenen, ihren Familien und ganz Israel freuen. Wie vereinbart sind auch pal脙陇stinensische H脙陇ftlinge freigelassen worden, und Israel hat sich auf die vereinbarte Linie zur脙录ckgezogen.

Und jetzt? Neben unseren eigenen M脙露glichkeiten m脙录ssen wir gegen脙录ber den USA deutlich machen, dass es mit gro脙聼en Auftritten in der Knesset und Scharm-El-Scheich nicht getan ist. Gibt es Ideen, wie die Hamas tats脙陇chlich entwaffnet werden kann? Wird eine Resolution des Sicherheitsrates vorbereitet, um die notwendigen internationalen Stabilisierungsstreitkr脙陇fte einzusetzen? Ist der ungehinderte Zugang aller humanit脙陇ren Organisationen, der Vereinten Nationen und des Roten Kreuzes sichergestellt?

Ich erwarte, dass unsere Exekutive zu all diesen Fragen mit den USA in Kontakt bleibt und daf脙录r sorgt, dass es Antworten auf diese Fragen gibt.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Evin Incir (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, so much for a peace deal, so much for a ceasefire. The so-called peace deal, ceasefire plan or name it whatever you want is declared 芒聙聯 but a few moments pass and the story rewinds once again. In the last two days alone, Israeli airstrikes have resumed, and 芒聙聯 once more 芒聙聯 innocent Palestinians are paying the price 芒聙聯 victims of crimes. The Israeli far-right government has proven to not be a partner for anything right now; not for peace, not for ceasefire, nor for justice or anything else. How much more must Israel do before the European Union finally acts for real?

There are no words left. Time and again, Israel commits war crimes and not even humanitarian aid is still let into Gaza. Do not let us fool ourselves! That's the reality right now while we continue to debate whether to suspend a mere fraction of our association agreement. Suspend the entire EU-Israeli Association Agreement, impose a full arms embargo, sanction the entire Israeli Government, recognise Palestine.

The claim that Israel's war is against Hamas is still a lie. In reality, both Israel and Hamas are terrorising innocent Palestinians. Israel claims that innocent Palestinians are collaborating with Hamas, Hamas claims innocent Palestinians are collaborating with Israel. In reality, Israel and Hamas are each other's best friends. Leave innocent Palestinians alone.

Let's talk about the West Bank; Hamas does not rule there, so why are Palestinians in the West Bank being punished simply for existing? Every settler in the West Bank and beyond lives in violation of international law. Sanction them all, not just the most violent ones. They live on stolen land 芒聙聯 land stolen from the Palestinians. Those settlers who hold dual EU citizenship should lose the citizenship. Let's call violent settlers what they are 芒聙聯 terrorists because they terrorise Palestinians every minute, every hour, every day, every week, every month and every year. My friend Abu Hassan was one of their victims. What those settlers tried to do as the Israeli military stood by and watched, is something no human being should ever have to endure.

Do not misunderstand me 芒聙聯 the initial step of releasing hostages on both sides was crucial, but much more must be done to put an end to the bloodshed that is continuing to take place right now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hermann Tertsch (PfE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta. 脗隆Caramba! Algunos parece que est脙漏n enfadados con que haya un acuerdo de paz.

El propio t脙颅tulo de este debate es ya un mal chiste y explica la mala conciencia que existe: el reciente acuerdo de paz en Oriente Pr脙鲁ximo脗聽y el papel de la Uni脙鲁n. El papel de la Uni脙鲁n Europea se resume pronto: ha sido obst脙隆culo, y ha sido rid脙颅culo. 脗驴Papel positivo? Ninguno. Nada. Cero. Absolutamente cero.

El espa脙卤ol Pedro S脙隆nchez ha sido directamente un colaborador del terrorismo de Ham脙隆s. Macron se fue con Starmer y su despreciable decisi脙鲁n de reconocer al inexistente Estado palestino. Y a punto estuvo de hacer fracasar las negociaciones que avanzaban, con esa medida absolutamente irresponsable y absurda. Si hay alto el fuego en este momento en Gaza, y esperanzas de paz, es gracias al enemigo favorito de todos ustedes: el se脙卤or Donald Trump.

Mientras tanto, ustedes han estado jaleando a Ham脙隆s y han permitido que, adem脙隆s, ese Estado palestino se manifieste en todas las ciudades europeas, generando una prepotencia que vamos a pagar muy pronto aqu脙颅. Aqu脙颅 en Europa.

(El orador acepta responder a una pregunta formulada con arreglo al procedimiento de la 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄)

脗聽脗聽Abir Al-Sahlani (Renew). 芒聙聯 Dear colleague, do you claim that our demonstrators 芒聙聯 our citizens who are outside demonstrating for peace, for human rights, for Palestinians 芒聙聯 are on the wrong side of history? Do you, by any chance, mean that the recognition of a Palestinian state is a big mistake? Something that I think is a debt we have to the Palestinians for the last 70 years.

Do you really mean that when we demand justice for the over 60 000 Palestinians killed, 2脗聽000 of them under the age of one year, that this is Hamas propaganda? A call for human rights 芒聙聯 is that Hamas propaganda for you?

脗聽脗聽Marco Squarta (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, in Medio Oriente si 脙篓 aperta una speranza vera, non un sogno, una speranza costruita con coraggio, grazie alla leadership di Donald Trump e alla determinazione anche di Giorgia Meloni, unica donna italiana a quel tavolo storico, che ha scelto la via del realismo, non delle illusioni ideologiche. Ha vinto la linea del coraggio, quella che mette la sicurezza e la pace prima della propaganda.

Adesso, per脙虏, 脙篓 il momento dell'Europa. Non possiamo limitarci ad applaudire da lontano. Dobbiamo esserci, agire, contare, essere protagonisti di questa nuova stagione di pace, presenti sul territorio in un quadro NATO, per garantire la sicurezza, sostenere gli aiuti umanitari e guidare la ricostruzione. Solo cos脙卢 l'Europa torner脙聽 ad essere ci脙虏 che deve: una forza di pace, di libert脙聽 e di speranza nel mondo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Barry Andrews (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner and colleagues, peace is not just the absence of war. A true peace delivers justice, freedom and accountability. A true peace 芒聙聯 as we know in Ireland 芒聙聯 takes decades of patience, of careful nurturing, of reconciliation and investment. It's notable that the decommissioning of arms by terrorists in Ireland took more than a decade after the 1998 peace agreement.

Last month, European leaders finally found their moral voice and began to see that they could influence global affairs. No one was more surprised than me to hear President von der Leyen talk about sanctions, or Chancellor Merz bringing an end to armed supplies in certain circumstances, and President Macron and others recognising the Palestinian State.

So now the European Union must do three things. Firstly, it must lead the humanitarian response, ensuring urgent access for all life-saving supplies to Gaza.

Secondly, it must invest heavily in the political economy of reconciliation, including a major donor conference for reconstruction.

And thirdly, we need to use all diplomatic and political channels to make it clear that it is inevitable and unavoidable that sanctions and a full trade embargo will follow if Israel doesn't follow through with an end to the illegal occupation and a pathway to Palestinian self-determination.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mounir Satouri (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, hier, la haute repr脙漏sentante Kaja脗聽Kallas a sous-entendu que les sanctions pr脙漏vues contre Isra脙芦l pourraient 脙陋tre abandonn脙漏es si le cessez-le-feu tenait et l'aide humanitaire rentrait 脙聽 Gaza. Mais attention, chers coll脙篓gues, il y a erreur sur la marchandise. D'abord, ce n'est pas un cessez-le-feu. Depuis le脗聽10脗聽octobre, 97脗聽morts, 153脗聽tonnes de bombes largu脙漏es en un jour, de l'aveu m脙陋me de Netanyahou, et seulement 300脗聽camions d'aide humanitaire rentr脙漏s 脙聽 la place des 600 pr脙漏vus.

Alors d'accord, vous voulez croire au cessez-le-feu, mais en quoi cela efface-t-il deux ann脙漏es de g脙漏nocide? Avec ce plan, vous n'effacerez pas la complicit脙漏 europ脙漏enne comme Netanyahou a effac脙漏 Gaza de la carte du Moyen-Orient. Comment oublier l'utilisation de la famine? Comment oublier les journalistes, les humanitaires tu脙漏s?

M脙陋me si les armes se taisent et l'aide humanitaire afflue, l'UE doit porter les sanctions, suspendre l'accord d'association, soutenir la CPI, seule condition d'une paix durable, juste, et d'une solution 脙聽 deux 脙聣tats.

脗聽脗聽Marc Botenga (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame la Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, Isra脙芦l a tu脙漏 plus de 70脗聽000脗聽Palestiniens 脙聽 Gaza. Au total, avec les bless脙漏s, cela fait 200脗聽000脗聽personnes touch脙漏es. C'est 10脗聽% de la population de Gaza.

Isra脙芦l occupe maintenant plus de la moiti脙漏 de la bande de Gaza, qui n'est d脙漏j脙聽 pas tr脙篓s longue, elle fait 42脗聽kilom脙篓tres, c'est approximativement Bruxelles-La Louvi脙篓re ou Bruxelles-Anvers. Et que venez-vous dire ici? Il est grand temps de d脙漏sarmer les Palestiniens, pas seulement le Hamas, et de d脙漏radicaliser les Palestiniens, parce que nous devons garantir la s脙漏curit脙漏 d'Isra脙芦l.

Alors qui a plus besoin de s脙漏curit脙漏, l'occupant ou l'occup脙漏? C'est cela la question. Et votre r脙漏ponse, c'est la mauvaise. F脙漏licitations, d'ailleurs, Madame la Commissaire, deux ans de g脙漏nocide, pas une seule mesure de prise. Franchement, un jour, je pense que toute votre Commission rentrera 脙聽 la maison remplie de honte.

Disons-le clairement: quand la Russie d脙漏truit en Ukraine, tout le monde veut que la Russie paie les d脙漏g脙垄ts. Mais quand Isra脙芦l rase la bande de Gaza, personne n'ose demander qu'Isra脙芦l paie. Ah non! Les Isra脙漏liens sont immunis脙漏s pour une raison ou une autre. La r脙漏alit脙漏, c'est que cela fait deux ans que l'Union europ脙漏enne, les pays europ脙漏ens financent et arment un g脙漏nocide. Vous devez avoir honte pour le reste de votre vie.

脗聽脗聽Sebasti脙拢o Bugalho (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, reconhecer a fragilidade do cessar芒聙聭fogo n脙拢o nega a necessidade da paz. E admitir a dificuldade da paz n脙拢o nega o direito de Israel a existir, nem o direito do povo palestiniano a mais do que sobreviver. Este plano n脙拢o 脙漏 uma vit脙鲁ria, mas 脙漏 um avan脙搂o, liderado por quem se consegue fazer ouvir, mas potenciado por quem nunca desistiu de ver.

O respeito pela autodetermina脙搂脙拢o da Palestina e pelo papel das Na脙搂脙碌es Unidas est脙隆 neste plano. N脙拢o estaria sem a Declara脙搂脙拢o de Nova Iorque, aprovada em setembro na Assembleia Geral da ONU, e sem o multilateralismo que se empenhou na confer脙陋ncia franco芒聙聭saudita.

A Europa foi fundada no valor da vida. Em Gaza, nos 脙潞ltimos dois anos, esse valor n脙拢o existiu. Tem de ser poss脙颅vel diz脙陋-lo sobre os ref脙漏ns, aprisionados e assassinados, como sobre os palestinianos, meses a fio, sem mantimentos e medicamentos. Tem de ser poss脙颅vel defender o direito de Israel a existir, sem se ser c脙潞mplice de genoc脙颅dio, como tem de ser poss脙颅vel condenar o que se passou em Gaza, sem se ser simpatizante de terrorismo.

Senhores Deputados, se n脙拢o for poss脙颅vel neste Parlamento, nesta Europa, n脙拢o ser脙隆 poss脙颅vel em mais lado nenhum.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pina Picierno (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, tra pochi giorni ricorreranno trent'anni dall'assassinio di Yitzhak Rabin ed 脙篓 un anniversario che ci impone di ricordare una stagione in cui il coraggio della pace era considerato la pi脙鹿 alta forma di sicurezza, in cui il dialogo era un dovere politico e morale.

E oggi, in un Mediterraneo che 脙篓 attraversato da nuove, preoccupanti, impressionanti fratture, l'Unione europea ha il compito di recuperare quello spirito, trasformandolo in azione concreta. Il patto per il Mediterraneo, appena presentato, grazie anche al lavoro della commissaria 脜聽uica, indica una strada che 脙篓 quella della cooperazione economica, culturale, energetica per costruire stabilit脙聽 e per costruire sviluppo condiviso. In questo quadro io credo che anche il recente piano di pace pu脙虏 diventare uno strumento certamente utile, se letto in chiave multilaterale e coerente con il diritto internazionale.

E noi sappiamo che la risoluzione approvata dal Parlamento a settembre ci affida un mandato chiaro: lavorare per una pace giusta, inclusiva, fondata sul rispetto e sulla dignit脙聽 di tutti i popoli. 脙聢 questo l'impegno che dobbiamo onorare, nel nome di Rabin e della sua eredit脙聽 di pace.

脗聽脗聽Ant脙鲁nio T脙垄nger Corr脙陋a (PfE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, meus caros amigos, parece que a hist脙鲁ria n脙拢o passa por este Parlamento. Parece que a hist脙鲁ria no M脙漏dio Oriente come脙搂ou no dia 7 de outubro de h脙隆 dois anos e que o que se passou para tr脙隆s n脙拢o interessa. Quantas vezes foram acossados os israelitas, quantas vezes Israel n脙拢o foi atacado? E digo-vos que isto n脙拢o 脙漏 uma situa脙搂脙拢o que se v脙隆 resolver de um dia para o outro. Vai ser um assunto que vai ter de ser resolvido a seu tempo, com o seu tempo, e n脙拢o 脙漏 com solu脙搂脙碌es miraculosas de dois Estados ou com um cessar芒聙聭fogo prec脙隆rio, como o que existe neste momento.

Meus caros amigos, estas situa脙搂脙碌es de crise n脙拢o s脙拢o para se resolver, s脙拢o para se conseguir manter uma paz e estabilidade. 脙聣 muito importante que as pessoas tenham consci脙陋ncia disto, e que a Comiss脙拢o e este Parlamento tenham a no脙搂脙拢o de que o conflito n脙拢o acabou, porque os palestinianos s脙鲁 t脙陋m um objetivo, que 脙漏 acabar com o Estado de Israel, do rio at脙漏 ao mar.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Abir Al-Sahlani (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, on my way here this morning, I was looking at my phone and I had to see yet another Palestinian mother hug her kid, calling him to wake up from his death. Yet another mother, yet another parent, had to bury their child.

What role can the EU play for these people? My answer would be to make the short lives of these kids, and their violent deaths, matter. And how can we do that? Yes, we know that peace cannot be achieved only when justice is served. That's why the EU has to, first, stop the Association Agreement with Israel, because we cannot be associated with war criminals.

Two, we have to fully support the ICC and ICJ, because these war criminals have to stand in responsibility and end the impunity on both sides, Hamas and Israel.

We have to stop the settler economy. We cannot have products from violent settlers' stolen land. We have to stop the land theft in the West Bank and the settler violence.

And there is no other solution than the two-state solution, because Israelis owe it to the Palestinians, and the Palestinians well damn deserve their own state now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Catarina Vieira (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, a peace plan that circumvents international law; a ceasefire that has already been violated, killing dozens; humanitarian aid turned on and off, using civilians as leverage; Palestinians having no say on who will lead Gaza in rebuilding what Israel has destroyed; impunity in the face of genocide 芒聙聯 this is the reality in Gaza.

And meanwhile, in the West Bank, the illegal occupation grows, subjecting millions of people to killing, torture and displacement.

Yes, much has changed in the past few weeks: a ceasefire agreement, the return of the hostages, more humanitarian aid coming in. These are all crucial steps. But let us be clear: this is not peace and this is not justice.

Colleagues, our credibility is on the line. What influence can we have with Russia, with any other state, if we don't stand by our values? We must uphold international law, and that means taking action: suspend the association agreement and all trade with illegal settlements, impose sanctions and stand firmly behind the ICC and the ICJ.

This is the moment to secure a path 芒聙聯 a just and a lasting path 芒聙聯 that guarantees safety, freedom and self-determination to Israelis and Palestinians alike.

脗聽脗聽Malika Sorel (NI). 芒聙聯 La vie de nos. 脙聣l脙篓ves se ternit. Celui qui agit en d脙漏miurge palestinien gradue t脙麓t le matin du jour, dit Historia, qui sera abolie. Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, une approche pragmatique fond脙漏e sur la s脙漏curit脙漏 mutuelle centr脙漏e sur la population civile est n脙漏cessaire. Sortir du face 脙聽 face destructeur, suspendre le cycle des repr脙漏sailles commande que des forces d'interposition et des observateurs internationaux soient d脙漏ploy脙漏s sous mandat des Nations unies.

Des sanctions 脙漏conomiques contre Isra脙芦l ne m脙篓neraient 脙聽 rien. Par ailleurs, l'Europe, premier bailleur, ne peut suppl脙漏er 脙聽 une absence arabe, absence qui vouerait 脙聽 l'脙漏chec tout processus de paix. La paix exige un engagement occidental lucide et une coalition arabe qui assume un r脙麓le concret dans la reconstruction et la s脙漏curit脙漏. Chers coll脙篓gues, l'enjeu d脙漏cisif et le lien avec les soci脙漏t脙漏s civiles, les enseignants, les m脙漏decins, les artistes, les entrepreneurs d脙漏tiennent les cl脙漏s de la r脙漏conciliation. C'est 脙聽 eux que l'aide internationale doit 脙陋tre adress脙漏e par des circuits fiables et v脙漏rifiables. La reconstruction doit devenir un instrument d'脙漏mancipation et non un instrument de d脙漏pendance.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Massimiliano Salini (PPE). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, signora Commissaria, onorevoli colleghi, grazie innanzitutto alla Commissaria perch脙漏, come ricordava la collega Picierno, 脙篓 stato presentato un patto per il Mediterraneo che sicuramente costituisce una cornice privilegiata per il lavoro, anche politico, che ci apprestiamo a fare sullo scacchiere eccezionalmente critico del Medio Oriente.

L'accordo di pace 芒聙聯 che 脙篓 una tregua pi脙鹿 che una pace 芒聙聯 ha un grande pregio, a mio modo di vedere: 脙篓 stato sottoscritto nonostante molte fossero le assenze, perch脙漏 bisogna avere coraggio politico per chiudere un accordo nonostante le importanti assenze. Ma questo non ci autorizza a non domandarci per quale motivo gli assenti fossero assenti. Perch脙漏 la compresenza 芒聙聯 da un lato 芒聙聯 di realt脙聽 come Turchia e Qatar e 芒聙聯 dall'altro 芒聙聯 l'assenza, ad esempio, dell'Arabia Saudita introduce una domanda su quale ruolo avranno organizzazioni come i Fratelli musulmani sul futuro di questo accordo.

Noi abbiamo il dovere, quindi, di garantire che sia un accordo di pace e non di sola tregua e non autorizzare, a mio modo di vedere, che accada quello che alcuni chiedono, cio脙篓 che il Consiglio di pace inizi da dove Israele ha eradicato la presenza di Hamas. Sarebbe l'indizio di una sorta di "Donbass palestinese".

Noi vogliamo due popoli e due Stati.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Temido (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, o recente acordo de cessar芒聙聭fogo em Gaza trouxe al脙颅vio e esperan脙搂a, mas n脙拢o 脙漏 um acordo de paz. Trata芒聙聭se de uma tr脙漏gua fr脙隆gil e prec脙隆ria.

Por isso, este ainda n脙拢o 脙漏 o tempo de celebra脙搂脙拢o. 脙聣 o momento em que a diplomacia europeia deve intensificar os seus esfor脙搂os e este Parlamento deve continuar a bater芒聙聭se pelo respeito pela sua resolu脙搂脙拢o de setembro passado: pela entrada livre de ajuda humanit脙隆ria e pelo trabalho independente da UNRWA; pela suspens脙拢o da componente comercial do Acordo de Associa脙搂脙拢o entre a Uni脙拢o Europeia e Israel; pela desmilitariza脙搂脙拢o do Hamas e o seu afastamento de qualquer futuro para Gaza; pela condena脙搂脙拢o absoluta da viol脙陋ncia e expans脙拢o dos colonatos ilegais na Cisjord脙垄nia; pelo apoio a um governo de transi脙搂脙拢o que conduza a uma solu脙搂脙拢o de viabiliza脙搂脙拢o do Estado da Palestina que n脙拢o seja apenas um protetorado.

A paz regional s脙鲁 ser脙隆 sustent脙隆vel se for baseada no respeito pela autodetermina脙搂脙拢o e na consolida脙搂脙拢o de institui脙搂脙碌es democr脙隆ticas. A paz global s脙鲁 ser脙隆 duradoura se os crimes contra o direito internacional forem investigados e punidos.

Este 脙漏 o tempo da expetativa. Cabe芒聙聭nos estar 脙聽 altura.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Anders Vistisen (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, a ceasefire and the release of hostages should be a cause of celebration. Yet when I hear the debate in this Chamber from the liberals, from the socialists, from the Greens, you seem even more infuriated, even more angry than when the war was prosecuted. Why is that? Is it your hatred towards the Trump administration that takes over? Or is it that you have lost what you saw as a golden opportunity to align with Islamists and Hamas apologists in the West that has now come to an end?

Please, be happy. We have peace. And when we have peace, the first and foremost task for any European government should be how to plan the return of Palestinian refugees residing here in Europe. Every man and woman has a duty, when there is peace, to go back and build up their home country and be grateful for the many services they have gotten here in Europe, but willingly return to the country of Palestine that you have helped proclaiming.

So please, I am looking forward to seeing these plans, to see deportation starting, because, of course, we cannot keep refugees here that are no longer in fear of war and persecution.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Evin Incir (S&D), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Colleague, I have a simple question to you: what do you call it when the bombs are falling down on an area? When civilians are being been killed, when civilians are being slaughtered, what do you call it? Do you call it a ceasefire? Do you call it peace? Would you call it what it is: war?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Anders Vistisen (PfE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 What I call it is that when parties in a ceasefire don't live up to the expectations, ceasefires cease and luckily the ceasefire has already now been re-established.

What I call you is a Hamas apologist, a person who doesn't take a terrorist attack seriously, and who always is ready to apologise to the most vicious and evil ideology we have on this planet: the radical Islamism of Hamas.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 If I understood correctly, you called a colleague an apologist.

I would like to remind you 芒聙聯 I'll put it that way 芒聙聯 that we owe each other a respectful demeanour here.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Irena Joveva (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Commissioner, colleagues, for years, many of you sat here watching Gaza burn, debating, condemning, expressing concern 芒聙聯 doing absolutely nothing. You watched the genocide and didn't dare to call its name. You spoke of principles and delivered none.

We failed the Palestinians. We failed the world. We failed humanity.

'Recent peace agreement' is the title of this debate 芒聙聯 a peace for whom? Is there a ceasefire 芒聙聯 or is there, as Francesca Albanese said, 'you cease, I fire'. The mutual hatred between the far-right and Israel, and Hamas feeds them both, while the innocent pay the price.

'The role of the EU' is also in the title 芒聙聯 where is the EU, indeed? Just yesterday the Commission presented the new Pact for the Mediterranean, preaching stability and security while blatantly favouring the aggressor and just symbolically addressing Palestine.

Is that the role we want to play: watching human rights 芒聙聯 humans actually 芒聙聯 being erased and the world burn? All violations of the agreement must mean sanctions, isolation, consequences. So just act. Better late than never, but never again this late.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Benedetta Scuderi (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, "Hanno distrutto quello che avevamo dentro. Hanno demolito la mia casa, tutti i miei ricordi. Mi hanno portato via tutto ci脙虏 che mi aiutava a vivere. Come posso essere quella di prima?" Ghazal, 14 anni.

Immaginate di essere una bambina palestinese e tutto quello che conoscevate 脙篓 stato distrutto. Poi vi dicono che 脙篓 arrivata la pace, che c'脙篓 un piano. Ma il mondo intorno a noi rimane uguale, occupato: i checkpoint restano; l'esercito genocida 脙篓 nelle vostre case; gli aiuti sono controllati da chi li usava per affamarvi; le terre sono confiscate e gli spari e le bombe continuano a fare rumore.

Dall'inizio della tregua almeno otto bambini palestinesi sono stati uccisi da Israele. La pace non pu脙虏 esserci senza la fine dell'occupazione e la giustizia per i crimini di guerra commessi. Finch脙漏 non sar脙聽 riconosciuto lo Stato di Palestina e l'autodeterminazione del suo popolo; finch脙漏 i rapporti economici e politici con Netanyahu proseguono come se nulla fosse, la parola "pace" resta solo una copertura per "oppressione".

Cara UE, finora siamo stati dei grandi assenti, ma abbiamo ancora una chance per lavorare su giustizia e diritto internazionale: attuiamo le sanzioni annunciate, sospendiamo gli accordi commerciali finch脙漏 non ci sar脙聽 una riparazione vera! La convivenza di due popoli senza responsabilit脙聽 non pu脙虏 esistere.

(L'oratrice accetta di rispondere a una domanda "cartellino blu")

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Malika Sorel (NI), question 脗芦carton bleu脗禄. 芒聙聯 Ce conflit est un conflit extr脙陋mement grave qui nous pr脙漏occupe tous. Je voudrais savoir pour quelle raison vous ne dites aucun mot des Palestiniens qui sont aujourd'hui assassin脙漏s de mani脙篓re extr脙陋mement violente par le Hamas.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Benedetta Scuderi (Verts/ALE), risposta a una domanda "cartellino blu". 芒聙聯 C'脙篓 un punto che a Lei manca: il punto 脙篓 che noi abbiamo sempre condannato Hamas, che stiamo richiedendo che Hamas 芒聙聯 giustamente 芒聙聯 sia disarmato ed esca dalla striscia di Gaza, che dobbiamo assolutamente far rispettare le responsabilit脙聽 di Hamas.

Il problema 脙篓 che nessuno sta parlando di far rispettare le responsabilit脙聽 di un governo genocida che sta continuando a occupare, che sta perpetrando una pulizia etnica e nessuno cerca di far rispettare le sue responsabilit脙聽 e di far valere il diritto internazionale anche per quel governo.

Allora no, una pace non ci pu脙虏 essere se c'脙篓 un governo che ha fatto quello che ha fatto e nessuno sta chiedendo che risponda delle sue responsabilit脙聽. Mi dispiace, questa 脙篓 la grande differenza.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Luke Ming Flanagan (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madam President, so what is the role of the EU in bringing about peace in the Middle East? Firstly, we must recognise the EU's role in facilitating a genocide. We must accept that Ursula von der Leyen's unconditional support for Israel emboldened it in committing the most horrific war crimes. We must accept that the EU's refusal to suspend our trade agreement with Israel has helped it bankroll a genocide. We must accept that, in spite of Israel telling us it would do exactly what it has done, Germany continued to supply it with weapons until two months ago.

So now 芒聙聯 to make up for this 芒聙聯 the EU must recognise the Palestinian State and end all trade with Israel until it removes its war machine from Gaza. The EU must also demand that Israel pays for the reconstruction of Gaza, because the reality is that there will be no peace without justice, and there will be no justice until Gaza is reconstructed and Israel pays for it.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脜陆eljana Zovko (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, the announcement of an agreement on the Gaza peace plan proposed by President Trump came as a relief. Yet lasting peace requires clear commitment from all sides to address the real obstacles.

As long as Hamas remains the sole security authority in Gaza, reconstruction and a genuine peace will remain out of reach. Its actions continue to undermine the security of both Israelis and Palestinians.

The EU must show great determination to contribute to the success of President Trump's peace plan, with a view to facilitating the two-state solution and a peaceful coexistence.

Coordination with international partners will be key to ensuring that the current opportunity is not wasted. We must seize this chance to help bring peace to a region torn apart by decades of conflict and violence.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Matja脜戮 Nemec (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, the eyes of the world must not turn from Palestine. The promise of the ceasefire in Gaza has been broken at least 80脗聽times, at a cost of lives, hopes, and in law itself.

We need more than pauses in violence; we need an end of the occupation and all hostilities, including in the West Bank. Those responsible for the genocide and war crimes must be brought to justice in-line with international law. Accountability is non-negotiable 芒聙聯 sanctions must be imposed. Lasting and just peace can only be built on the foundation of justice. Urgent humanitarian aid must flow. The crossing must be open and the medical workers and journalists protected. We must protect the very lifeline that keeps children alive and gives families hope, such as UNWRA.

Let us choose courage over cruelty, law over impunity, and a future where every child, Israeli and Palestinian, can grow up free from fear. The time to act is now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Billy Kelleher (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, I welcome the recent ceasefire. Calling it a peace process may be very ambitious, but we must welcome the fact that the massive attacks on Gaza have stopped. But we also have to expect that humanitarian aid has to flow immediately unhindered into Gaza. We also need to see an international observation and international peacekeeping force deployed rapidly to ensure that there are no further breaches from a military perspective.

In any peace process, accountability is critically important in the longer term, but in the medium term what is significantly at issue is the recognition of Palestine as a state because, clearly, peace without hope will not last very long.

I welcome this change and the slight change in tone in many European actors 芒聙聯 particularly the Commission itself and some of the major European countries 芒聙聯 around the need to support a two-state solution. But if we are to see the European Union play a role in the EU, it must play a positive role. It must not be afraid to say to Israel that the trade association agreement will be suspended if it does not honour its full commitments in terms of the ceasefire, promoting the peace plan, allowing humanitarian aid flow, and 芒聙聯 just as importantly 芒聙聯 the longer-term acceptance of a two-state solution.

脗聽脗聽Chlo脙漏 Ridel (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, nous l'observons avec inqui脙漏tude, le cessez-le-feu 脙聽 Gaza est bien fragile. Ce weekend, 54脗聽Palestiniens ont 脙漏t脙漏 tu脙漏s, l'aide humanitaire a de nouveau 脙漏t脙漏 bloqu脙漏e, deux soldats isra脙漏liens ont aussi trouv脙漏 la mort. C'est pourquoi il est essentiel de rester fermes et lucides. C'est maintenant qu'il faut redoubler d'efforts. Il serait impensable que l'Union europ脙漏enne renonce 脙聽 suspendre l'accord d'association entre l'Union europ脙漏enne et Isra脙芦l, ou 脙聽 sanctionner les ministres messianiques isra脙漏liens qui encouragent la colonisation en Cisjordanie, ou encore 脙聽 soutenir la justice internationale pour que les criminels soient jug脙漏s.

Je veux vous alerter sur un projet de loi isra脙漏lien tr脙篓s dangereux, qui vise 脙聽 priver les ONG isra脙漏liennes de financements en les taxant 脙聽 des taux prohibitifs. 脗聽脗聽Reinhold Lopatka (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, at this moment, there is a chance for peace in the Middle East, but not more.

Thanks to President Trump, there is significant momentum, with a ceasefire and hostage-exchange deal, as well as commitments to humanitarian access and reconstruction in Gaza.

At the same time, the situation remains extremely fragile. Violations of the truce have already occurred, and the core issues 芒聙聯 the full disarmament of Hamas, the long-term governance of Gaza and a credible two-state solution 芒聙聯 are still unresolved.

The EU has to remain firm 芒聙聯 most urgently, for the full disarmament of the terrorist organisation Hamas and the end of their rule over Gaza.

We must also continue working towards a two-state solution, which is necessary for a viable Palestinian state alongside a secure Israel.

In all areas, we must work with the Arab world. A long-lasting solution is only possible with their support. The Abraham Accords show the way.

脗聽脗聽Christophe Gomart (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, Madame la Commissaire, l'Union europ脙漏enne n'a jamais eu les moyens r脙漏els de peser dans le conflit isra脙漏lo-palestinien. Sa rh脙漏torique volontariste entretient une illusion de puissance vite dissip脙漏e par la r脙漏alit脙漏 et par l'action am脙漏ricaine. Pr脙篓s de la moiti脙漏 des 脙聣tats membres de l'Union europ脙漏enne ont reconnu l'脙聣tat palestinien. C'est du symbolisme sans effet, une pens脙漏e magique qui ne prot脙篓ge ni les civils, ni la stabilit脙漏 r脙漏gionale, ni ne permet d'脙漏tablir la paix.

Pendant ce temps, profitant du cessez-le-feu, le Hamas continue d'脙漏liminer ses opposants et de faire r脙漏gner la terreur. La brutalit脙漏 de la Donald Trump nous a peut 脙陋tre choqu脙漏s, mais elle a produit des r脙漏sultats, des otages lib脙漏r脙漏s, des corps r脙漏cup脙漏r脙漏s, un cessez-le-feu, fragile mais r脙漏el.

Et l'Europe, que propose-t-elle? Elle veut r脙漏silier l'accord d'association UE-Isra脙芦l, appelle 脙聽 des sanctions. Quand elle envoie des armes 脙聽 l'Ukraine, mais refuse de soutenir Isra脙芦l face au terrorisme, elle se contredit. Car ce terrorisme n'est pas confin脙漏 脙聽 Gaza, il circule, il inspire, il s'impose, il infiltre nos soci脙漏t脙漏s 脙聽 travers des r脙漏seaux ou des loups solitaires, des discours radicaux ou insidieux des Fr脙篓res musulmans, via aussi les flux migratoires de clandestins incontr脙麓l脙漏s.

Ignorer ce lien c'est ignorer la menace. Quel est le r脙麓le de l'Union europ脙漏enne? Le r脙麓le de l'Union, c'est de coordonner davantage de diplomaties, de les mobiliser, de d脙漏finir une position cr脙漏dible et d'asseoir son autorit脙漏 au profit d'une v脙漏ritable paix au Moyen-Orient comme en Europe.

脗聽脗聽脙聳zlem Demirel (The Left), Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹. 芒聙聯 Dankesch脙露n, Herr Kollege, dass Sie die Zwischenfrage gestatten. Sie haben eben noch einmal betont, dass die Hamas ja den Waffenstillstand irgendwie brechen w脙录rde und Terror in Israel und Pal脙陇stina mit sich bringen w脙录rde. Wenn Sie es zur Kenntnis genommen haben, w脙录rden Sie dann bitte auch zur Kenntnis nehmen, dass das Selbstbestimmungsrecht der Pal脙陇stinenser entscheidend wichtig ist, um den Nahostkonflikt dauerhaft zu befrieden? 脗聽脗聽Christophe Gomart (PPE), r脙漏ponse carton bleu. 芒聙聯 Merci, ch脙篓re coll脙篓gue, pour votre question. Il me semble qu'avant de reconna脙庐tre l'脙聣tat de Palestine脗聽芒聙聯 et je suis pour la reconnaissance de deux 脙聣tats脗聽芒聙聯 il faudrait que les Palestiniens reconnaissent l'脙聣tat d'Isra脙芦l. Le Hamas, en effet, continue de tuer ses opposants. On a vu des images. Des services de renseignement l'ont prouv脙漏. 脗聽脗聽Costas Mavrides (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, the recent peace conference for the Middle East brought hope for peace, but took place without the European Union. If the EU wants to be a global player, we cannot be absent from shaping peace and stability in a region that lies in our immediate neighbourhood and is of strategic importance, as confessed by the Commissioner.

Strengthening our geopolitical role is essential for our future. With challenges ahead we should support and invest in this agreement as a long-due chance for stability and prosperity for the Middle East. Cyprus was at the conference 芒聙聯 as the EU Member State closest to the region with traditional external relations with key countries there 芒聙聯 and stands ready once again to contribute by presenting a complementary plan directed towards security, humanitarian aid and reconstruction. This complementary plan received a very warm welcome by the midnight conference yesterday, and I strongly believe that it deserves more attention and support from the European Commission.

脗聽脗聽Wouter Beke (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, collega芒聙聶s, het akkoord van Gaza en de vrijlating van de gijzelaars gaven een sprankeltje hoop. Maar vandaag zien we opnieuw hoe broos die hoop is en hoe broos ook het staakt-het-vuren in werkelijkheid is. Duurzame vrede is meer dan het tijdelijk zwijgen van de wapens. Zonder perspectief, zonder rechtvaardigheid en zonder heropbouw blijft elk bestand tijdelijk. En daar ligt nu de verantwoordelijkheid van Europa.

Europa moet het voortouw nemen door humanitaire hulp te versnellen, daar hebben wij de fondsen voor; door de heropbouw van Gaza te steunen; door druk te blijven uitoefenen op Hamas en Isra脙芦l, daar hebben we sanctiepakketten voor, en door te werken aan een geloofwaardige tweestatenoplossing, daarvoor hebben we de diplomaten. Het nieuwe pact voor de Middellandse Zee kan een nieuwe Europese koers inluiden, maar enkel met krachtige en concrete maatregelen. Wij waren afwezig bij het tot stand komen van het akkoord of van het bestand. Laat ons niet afwezig blijven bij de uitvoering ervan, en alle Europese middelen daarvoor inzetten.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hana Jalloul Muro (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, eight days have passed since the peace agreement for Gaza was signed, and yet the Israeli Government has already killed more than 100脗聽Palestinians and injured 80脗聽more. The Israeli Government has not fulfilled fully the agreement from the beginning.脗聽We also condemn any breach of the ceasefire by Hamas. The situation is very fragile.

We ask the Commission to closely monitor the situation and keep sanctioning the Israeli Government, who must be held accountable for its actions in Gaza and in the West Bank, where many civilians continue to be killed and are subject to the violence of the settlers.

We hope that the ceasefire can bring peace and the possibility of establishing the two-state solution with the '67 borders. For that to happen, the Rafah crossing must be opened and humanitarian aid properly delivered. We call for journalists to be allowed into Gaza and conditional support to the vital role of UNRWA.

We need to focus now on the day after: reconstruction, a future government elected by the Palestinians 芒聙聯 a horizon of hope, peace and security for Palestine and Israel.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ingeborg Ter Laak (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, collega芒聙聶s, een wankele vredesdeal en een kwetsbaar bestand, dat is de huidige situatie in het Midden-Oosten. We mogen hoopvol zijn over de weg naar vrede die lijkt te zijn ingezet, maar tegelijkertijd zijn we ook bezorgd over het tien dagen oude bestand dat alweer onder druk lijkt te staan. De rol van Europa was zeer beperkt. De tweestatenoplossing wordt aangemoedigd, maar vragen over een concrete aanpak, het ondersteunen van vrede en de rol die Europa hierin gaat spelen, blijven onbeantwoord.

De EU moet zich blijvend inzetten voor de doorgang van humanitaire goederen. Hamas moet ontwapend worden en de bombardementen moeten stoppen. Op lange termijn zal er actief naar een tweestatenoplossing gestuurd worden, waarbij Hamas geen enkele rol in een toekomstig bestuur heeft. Wel is er een nieuw bestuur nodig dat tot stand is gekomen door vrije verkiezingen. En de EU moet haar rol pakken bij de weg naar duurzame vrede.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marco Tarquinio (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, signora Commissaria, onorevoli colleghe e colleghi, a Gaza c'脙篓 un prezioso, fragile e contraddetto cessate il fuoco e gli ostaggi ancora vivi in mano di Hamas sono finalmente liberi.

Ma dov'脙篓 la pace? Il percorso 脙篓 tutto da sviluppare con tenacia, pazienza, visione e 芒聙聯 uso un'antica parola greca 芒聙聯 con parresia, la nuda forza della verit脙聽. E la verit脙聽 脙篓 che a Gaza, come in Cisgiordania, le sopraffazioni volute e consentite dal governo Netanyahu continuano. Il piano Trump ha allentato la stretta al collo di chi era mortalmente soffocato e percosso ma lascia intatti collari, guinzagli e bastoni.

L'ha gridato un'infinit脙聽 di persone, anche nelle piazze d'Europa: vediamo, ascoltiamo e agiamo per davvero noi europei, per il pieno rispetto del diritto internazionale, per l'autodeterminazione dei palestinesi accanto agli israeliani, per la liberazione, come 脙篓 gi脙聽 stato chiesto in quest'Aula, di Marwan Barghouti, il Nelson Mandela palestinese. Questa 脙篓 la via.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Se脙隆n Kelly (PPE). 芒聙聯 A Uachtar脙隆in, a chairde, the late John Hume, who won the Nobel Peace Prize for helping to broker the Good Friday Agreement, once said that we must spill sweat and not blood to bring about a brighter future. Just two weeks ago, we celebrated the ceasefire deal brokered by President Trump. Last weekend's events showed just how fragile it is.

There is a narrow path to lasting peace in Gaza. It will require enormous restraint from both Israel and Hamas. It will require international partners to apply pressure on them to do so, and to hold each accountable for breaches like those over the weekend. It will require the inclusion of democratic actors like the EU and the UN, to help deliver a surge of aid to Gaza, to set up systems of governance and policing, and to create a credible process towards a two-state solution.

There is a path to peace, but all sides 芒聙聯 us included 芒聙聯 must now spill sweat, not more blood.

脗聽脗聽Nicolas Bay (ECR). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, mes chers coll脙篓gues, on peut 脙漏videmment se r脙漏jouir des avanc脙漏es obtenues par Donald脗聽Trump au Moyen-Orient, des avanc脙漏es significatives vers la paix, la restitution des otages, la restitution des corps et un cessez-le-feu, certes fragile, mais r脙漏el. Et on peut a contrario regretter, s'inqui脙漏ter de voir l'Europe totalement absente alors que le Moyen-Orient est 脙聽 nos portes et que nous partageons avec lui les m脙陋mes sources civilisationnelles.

Il est regrettable que l'Europe soit totalement absente de ce conflit et surtout de sa r脙漏solution. Le combat contre l'islamisme, le combat contre le Hamas, contre Daesh, contre les Fr脙篓res musulmans, c'est une unit脙漏 de combat que nous devons mener et que nous devons gagner aussi bien au Moyen-Orient que sur le sol europ脙漏en.

Enfin, je voudrais dire que l'extr脙陋me gauche, les d脙漏put脙漏s europ脙漏ens d'extr脙陋me gauche participant 脙聽 la flottille pour Gaza font honte 脙聽 notre Parlement, font honte 脙聽 nos nations et font honte 脙聽 l'Europe par la bienveillance, la connivence qu'ils expriment envers les terroristes du Hamas.

Par ailleurs, je reviens de quelques jours au Liban et je voudrais dire qu'il ne faut pas oublier les chr脙漏tiens d'Orient. Ils sont les grands oubli脙漏s de ce conflit. Je me suis rendu au sud du Liban, des villages chr脙漏tiens ont 脙漏t脙漏 enti脙篓rement d脙漏truits dans le conflit opposant Isra脙芦l au Hezbollah. Nous devons soutenir le Liban et les chr脙漏tiens d'Orient.

脗聽脗聽Juan Fernando L脙鲁pez Aguilar (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, el acuerdo de Sharm el Sheij no es un acuerdo de paz: acomete la neutralizaci脙鲁n del terrorismo de Ham脙隆s, s脙颅, y abre paso al intercambio de rehenes y presos; pero no garantiza, en ning脙潞n caso, la soluci脙鲁n de dos Estados 芒聙聰que es la 脙潞nica forma de que los dos pueblos puedan convivir dentro de fronteras seguras y se reconozcan mutuamente芒聙聰; ni mucho menos la retirada de Israel de los territorios ocupados de Gaza, para empezar; pero tambi脙漏n de Cisjordania.

Por tanto, este debate, que habla del papel de la Uni脙鲁n Europea 芒聙聰y lo han calificado de irrelevante芒聙聰 no es, sin embargo, la 脙潞ltima palabra.

La Uni脙鲁n Europea puede todav脙颅a cumplir sus compromisos adquiridos en el debate del Estado de la Uni脙鲁n: suspender la parte comercial del Acuerdo con Israel 芒聙聰como as脙颅 le exigi脙鲁 la Resoluci脙鲁n aprobada por amplia mayor脙颅a en este Parlamento Europeo inmediatamente despu脙漏s芒聙聰, adem脙隆s de sancionar a los ministros y a los colonos y, por supuesto, garantizar que no habr脙隆 impunidad para los cr脙颅menes de guerra perpetrados en Gaza. Porque, en ning脙潞n caso, el acuerdo de Sharm el Sheij significa impunidad para esos cr脙颅menes.

脗聽脗聽Oihane Agirregoitia Mart脙颅nez (Renew). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, muy brevemente: estamos viendo la fragilidad de este proceso de paz. Creemos que es momento de trabajar mucho, y de hacerlo muy discretamente, para evitar que todas aquellas personas, agentes, Estados que quieren desestabilizar el proceso no lo consigan. Porque 芒聙聰no nos enga脙卤emos芒聙聰 tambi脙漏n son muchos los intereses contrapuestos que tenemos en este proceso de paz. Por ello, lo hacen realmente dif脙颅cil.

As脙颅 que no nos despistemos. Todos nuestros esfuerzos deben ir orientados a dar cumplimiento a lo acordado y a estabilizar la zona, garantizando que entra la ayuda humanitaria y se cubren todas las necesidades b脙隆sicas de la poblaci脙鲁n civil. Y todos nuestros esfuerzos deben destinarse a que nada, ni nadie, impida que podamos avanzar hacia una paz que sea justa, verdadera y duradera.

脗聽脗聽Jaume Asens Llodr脙聽 (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora comisaria, digamos la verdad: el plan de Trump es la victoria del nuevo orden imperial construido sobre las cenizas del holocausto palestino, y ni siquiera se va a cumplir en aquello que tiene de positivo, como ya advertimos.

Israel firm脙鲁 un alto el fuego y, al d脙颅a siguiente, bombardeaba Gaza 芒聙聰y ya van cien bombardeos, m脙隆s de cien muertos芒聙聰; Israel firmaba permitir la ayuda humanitaria y, al tercer d脙颅a, ya lo estaba impidiendo; Israel firm脙鲁 que se retirar脙颅a de Gaza y, al cuarto d脙颅a, uno de sus ministros ya dec脙颅a que pondr脙颅a all脙颅 asentamientos ilegales.

脗驴Alguien cree realmente que Israel va a retirarse de Gaza? 脗驴Alguien cree realmente que Israel va a parar? 脗驴Va a parar con su programa de limpieza 脙漏tnica, de supremacismo racial? Creer eso es no conocer el proyecto del sionismo.

Israel no va a parar hasta que los Estados Unidos y Europa no dejen de apoyarlo. Israel no va a parar hasta que no sea derrotada pol脙颅tica y militarmente, como sucedi脙鲁 con la Alemania de Hitler.

脗聽脗聽Estrella Gal脙隆n (The Left). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, en una semana Israel ha roto el alto el fuego m脙隆s de cincuenta veces, ha bloqueado la entrada de ayuda humanitaria, ha seguido matando y asesinando y est脙隆 consolidando la ocupaci脙鲁n y la colonizaci脙鲁n. 脗驴A qu脙漏 est脙隆 esperando la Uni脙鲁n Europea para reaccionar, para hacer algo?

Esto no es un plan de paz y lo saben. Ni tampoco, ni siquiera es un alto el fuego. Esto es una estrategia perfecta para continuar con la segunda fase del genocidio. Y ustedes siguen mirando a un lado. Ya no valen las excusas. Con bombas no hay paz. Sin justicia tampoco hay paz. Queremos a todos los responsables juzgados y condenados.

Se脙卤ora comisaria, 脗驴d脙鲁nde est脙隆n las medidas que el 17脗聽de septiembre la se脙卤ora von der Leyen present脙鲁 en este Parlamento? 脗驴D脙鲁nde est脙隆n las sanciones? 脗驴D脙鲁nde est脙隆n las suspensiones? 脗驴D脙鲁nde est脙隆n las consecuencias? Llevamos m脙隆s de un mes esperando y nada. El cero es el absoluto que ustedes han presentado.

La gente ya no cree en ustedes. Las calles siguen reclamando...

(el presidente retira la palabra a la oradora)

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, liebe Menschen Europas, liebe Menschen in Israel, liebe Menschen in Pal脙陇stina! Frieden! Frieden zwischen Ruinen, aus denen immer noch Panzerabwehrraketen abgefeuert werden und auf die immer noch Artilleriegranaten fallen. Frieden zwischen Menschen, die immer noch auf die 脙聹bergabe der Leichen ihrer Verwandten warten, und Menschen, die immer noch hungern. Frieden zwischen Kriegsverbrechern und Terroristen, die allesamt in Untersuchungshaft in Den Haag geh脙露ren. Frieden.

Was kann die Rolle der Europ脙陇ischen Union sein? Wir k脙露nnen ein Vorbild sein f脙录r einen Frieden, der Generationen 脙录berdauert. Denn mit der Zweistaatenl脙露sung allein wird es nicht getan sein. Zu gro脙聼 sind die Wunden, als dass diese beiden Staaten mit friedlichen Grenzen koexistieren k脙露nnten. Doch die Europ脙陇ische Union ist ein Modell, das irgendwann auch im Nahen Osten funktionieren kann: geteilte Zust脙陇ndigkeiten f脙录r gemeinsame Interessen unter Wahrung der Diversit脙陇t der V脙露lker. Ich wei脙聼, ich klinge naiv. Aber ich glaube an Frieden.

脗聽脗聽Katrin Langensiepen (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, Frau Kommissarin! Wir haben ja heute den Nahen Osten auf der Agenda stehen. Und hier w脙录rde ich gerne mal nach Gaza und Israel schauen, wo es berechtigt ist, eine Debatte dar脙录ber zu f脙录hren und ein Scheinwerferlicht darauf zu werfen.

Ich m脙露chte aber auch ein Scheinwerferlicht auf Syrien werfen. Das ist der Nachbarstaat von Israel, und Israel ist ja auch gerade milit脙陇risch in Syrien zugegen, sage ich mal. Seit dem Sommer dieses Jahres ist die Region um Suwaida, die Region der Drusinnen und Drusen, unter Belagerung. Den Menschen geht es schlecht. Es wird von Massakern, von Ermordungen gesprochen, die ja auch klar belegt sind. Was im Westen Syriens passierte脗聽芒聙聯 den Aleviten, aber auch den Christinnen und Christen 芒聙聯, ist belegt.

Vor 25 Jahren ist Baschar al-Assad an die Macht gekommen, und wir haben ihm den roten Teppich ausgerollt, weil man dachte: Ein junger Mann, ein Pr脙陇sident, der Englisch kann, das muss doch erneuernd sein f脙录r Syrien. Wo endeten wir? Jetzt haben wir vor zehn Monaten wieder einem jungen neuen sogenannten Pr脙陇sidenten den Teppich ausgerollt, und wir gucken, wie das neue Syrien aussehen kann. Ich m脙露chte nicht, dass wir uns in 25 Jahren wieder schuldig sprechen m脙录ssen, dass wir hier 芒聙娄

(Der Pr脙陇sident entzieht der Rednerin das Wort.)
