脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Grzegorz Braun (NI). 芒聙聯 Mr President, there is no peace process in the Middle East. We only have the normalisation of genocide. Israeli leaders, generals, rabbis, ministers keep referring to Palestinians as 'non-humans'. They keep bombarding everybody around. So let's move the Overton window a bit 芒聙聯 let's remember that the very existence of the Jewish state located in Palestine was voted over by the United Nations in the 1940s. Let's vote again 芒聙聯 let's vote over the fact that the experiment failed. And the two-state solution could be taken into account, as far as I am concerned, only in the case that we locate that other state somewhere over the Hudson or...

(The President cut off the speaker)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Reinier Van Lanschot (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, we are Europe. We should speak with one voice globally, especially when the topic is difficult. We should defend international law even when it interferes with our interests. And we should celebrate peace, no matter who helped achieve it.

However, the ceasefire has already been violated, and this deal is incomplete. So, what can we do to help turn an incomplete ceasefire into lasting peace?

Well, for peace, the root causes of the conflict need to be addressed, with Palestinians at the table. And peace requires justice 芒聙聯 that means, instead of standing ovations for Netanyahu, we bring him, Hamas terrorists and other war criminals before the International Criminal Court. And it means rebuilding Palestine and supporting an independent, democratic Palestinian State.

And we, Europe, can contribute to making that happen. Let's start with pressure on the Israeli Government to let humanitarian aid reach Gaza.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Br脛芦v脛聛 mikrofona uzst脛聛脜隆anos beigas.)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dubravka 脜聽uica, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, I was listening carefully to all your interventions, but I can agree with the last speaker: yes, I agree that we should speak with one voice, but let us clarify. First of all, everybody was mentioning the European Union; what is the European Union? All of us are aware the European Parliament is very important, but there is the Commission, which I represent here today, and there is also the European Council.

So the European Council 芒聙聯 yesterday, I was present at the Foreign Affairs Council together with HR/PV Kaja Kallas and, if I have enough time, I will try to summarise the discussion in the Council just to be on the safe side. All of the ministers insisted on the enhanced role of the EU, and that we need to have a role in the future peace plan and reconstruction effort. It was agreed to ensure more humanitarian aid and access to NGOs, as well as journalists, to Gaza 芒聙聯 both CSDP missions need to be properly utilised and it needs to be seen how their role can be enhanced.

As the European Union is the main supporter of the two-state solution, they stressed that it is up to the European Union to keep the Palestinian Authority alive, while there remains a need to support and push their reform efforts, as I said in my introductory remarks. In the dialogue with Israel, it needs to be stressed that Israel should not undermine the Palestinian Authority, notably linked to withheld tax revenues. Sanctions linked to Hamas are on the table, while restrictive measures against Israel will not be removed but kept on the table for now. These could be used either for NGOs, for media access or to push for tax revenues. Lastly, the European Union will look into how to use its leverage, how to be part of the Board of Peace and the European Union will continue diplomatic efforts with all sides. So everybody is doing what is under the remit of their role.

Now, before my concluding remarks, I will try to reply to some of your questions. On Syria 芒聙聯 although it is not the topic today, I have to reply to Ms Langensiepen. I was there four months ago, spoke with different components, I am still in contact with different components of society, not only with the government. My first meeting was with the civil society organisations, so we heard both sides 芒聙聯 it is not only about the government, we are very cautious. But at the same time we have to assist them, we have to help them. The new opportunity will be the days of dialogue, which are organised for 15脗聽November in Damascus and in some other cities in Syria. So we are trying our best, but it is not easy. Sorry 芒聙聯 this was not the topic, but everything is connected with the Middle East.

On supporting peace initiatives, I agree: peace remains to be built, as all of us know. The Commission suspended our bilateral support to Israel 芒聙聯 this is within our remit脗聽and we suspended this 芒聙聯 but at the same time, we continued working with Israeli civil society. We allocated EUR脗聽10脗聽million for 2021芒聙聯2024 under the NDICI programme and EUR脗聽5脗聽million for 2025芒聙聯2027 is in the pipeline now for supporting peacebuilding initiatives via Israeli and Palestinian civil society organisations.

On Hamas, we have to be clear, as I emphasised here on 7脗聽October, there is no role for Hamas in the future of Gaza and we need a strong Palestinian Authority. We do not need to repeat this, I hope all of you agree on this here. Hamas has been on the terror list, as all of us know, since 2001 with a dedicated sanctions regime. The Commission implements rigorous safeguards to ensure that no person or entity receives EU funding if they are involved in criminal and unethical practices, including terrorist financing and terrorist offences. We use our PEGASE programme that is under my remittance in DG MENA, so we are following this closely.

Someone asked: when will the Middle East strategy come out? It is part of my mission letter to work on the Middle East strategy together with the HR/VP, but, as you know, firstly we have to try to solve the situation on the ground. We are acting already and setting the base for peace through support to the Palestinian Authority. All of you know that our actions in the Middle East speak loudly; we stand for peace, I do not see anyone who is not in favour. We stand for stability and we stand for Palestinian and Israeli children. We gave EUR脗聽1.6脗聽billion to the Palestinian Authority for this year, next year and 2027. We gave EUR脗聽1脗聽billion to Lebanon脗聽芒聙聯 this is about the Middle East strategy. We gave EUR脗聽2.5脗聽billion to Syria, over the course of time, and we have given, at this moment, EUR脗聽500脗聽million to Jordan. So we are acting now before the Middle East strategy is on the table because that is only the document 芒聙聯 this brings stability.

On the future, on the Middle East strategy 芒聙聯 of course, first of all, consolidating durable stability, supporting inclusive democracy in Syria and Lebanon, which is connected. Of course, Gaza's reconstruction will be a priority and, of course, as I already said, we have to strengthen EU missions on the ground.

Now a few words on sanctions 芒聙聯 as you know, we will regularly assess how the situation evolves both in Gaza and the West Bank, and we will report in relevant Council formations on this. In Gaza, as the first phase of the ceasefire has been agreed, the second phase will be critical. All of you are prudent and, of course, it will be critical to lead to a permanent ceasefire and to offer guarantees or security and governance in Gaza. So details on the second phase are not clear yet. This is something which I have to say here, something which is very important for the Palestinian Authority too 芒聙聯 we urge Israel to release the clearance revenues collected from April脗聽2025 by Israel and due to the Palestinian Authority, they owe this money to them. So this is something which we urge them, as well as the continuation of correspondent banking services and the issue of access to Israeli shekels.

Just to make you aware 芒聙聯 if we were not the biggest payers for the Palestinian Authority, they would not have survived. Just to understand what the European Commission is doing at the moment. As I said when you mentioned the European Union, it is all of us. So we have to, as you said, speak with one voice, which is not the case at the moment, but we are coming closer to consensus, I think. This is the reason why I read my conclusions from the Foreign Affairs Council.

To finish with closing remarks, I know that I have exceeded my time, but since the topic is very important, I am trying to reply to all of your questions. I want to underline, and I think all of us agree, that the European Union is ready to have a strong role to play along the principles of the US peace plan. We are ready to support Gaza's recovery and reconstruction, and we know that it will require enormous international effort with strong coordination among partners.

What we would like to do with this 20-point plan is: to continue the reform of the Palestinian Authority; support the recovery and reconstruction of Gaza; ensure a neutral third-party presence at the Rafah crossing with Egypt; support the capacity of a future international stabilisation force; ensure Palestinian governance from day one; and mobilise all relevant Commission tools at our disposal.

To recall President von der Leyen's State of the Union speech when she announced a support package for Gaza 芒聙聯 we have not forgotten this. As I mentioned in my introduction, we will set up a Palestine Donor Group and the first meeting is convened for 20脗聽November at the margins or on the eve of the November Foreign Affairs Council 芒聙聯 I don't know now exactly. The objective is to have an EU-led Palestine Donor Group of like-minded partners, donors and so on together; I do not want to expand on this because I already talked about this. It will allow for discussions on the future engagement of Gaza, and we also invite Israel to join us because without them, we cannot have lasting stability. This is something which we have to admit, unfortunately, but this is true. In the meanwhile, we continue to emphasise the necessity of urgent humanitarian aid delivery 芒聙聯 this is what my colleague Lahbib is working on.

Achieving demilitarisation and the transitional governance will be vital and will require careful coordination of the engagement of all stakeholders. Who will convince Hamas? Let's talk about it 芒聙聯 they should be convinced to lay down arms. We don't want to use military force, so it is important to convince them to lay down arms. The European Union stands ready 芒聙聯 we as the Commission, but also, I hope, all components of the European Union 芒聙聯 to contribute to this effort. We will continue supporting the two-state solution, and this is our long-standing goal. We will continue to support the unity of the West Bank and Gaza, and we want a reformed Palestinian Authority to play a central role in the revival of Gaza.

To conclude, the recovery and reconstruction of the Strip must be a Palestinian-led process, and this is what we are aiming for. Once again, thank you for all your engagement, not only this morning but all the time. The European Union is ready to work with partners to provide support, and we will allocate a dedicated support package for Gaza's recovery and reconstruction 芒聙聯 this is part of my mission letter. Thank you for your for all your support and let us continue working together.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Thank you very much, Commissioner, for the substantive reply to our MEPs and colleagues.

The debate is closed.

Rakstiski pazi脜聠ojumi (178. pants)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Thomas Bajada (S&D), bil-miktub. 芒聙聯 Illum ninsabu f'mument kru脛聥jali fl-isforz internazzjonali lejn pa脛聥i fil-Medda ta' Ga脜录a u fil-Lvant Nofsani.

Il-ftehim ta芒聙聶 pa脛聥i, g脛搂alkemm po脜录ittiv, huwa fl-istess waqt fra脛隆li. Il-qtil g脛搂adu g脛搂addej, u b芒聙聶hekk je脛搂tie脛隆 impenn diplomatiku sostnut.

L-Unjoni Ewropea ilha siekta sa mill-bidu. Per脙虏 issa, BLA TLAQLIQ, irridu nappo脛隆脛隆jaw l-a脛聥脛聥ess umanitarju, nappo脛隆脛隆jaw governanza tran脜录izzjonali li tinkludi lill-Palestinjani, u ni脜录guraw li r-rikostruzzjoni ssir b'rispett s脛搂i脛搂 g脛搂al-li脛隆i internazzjonali.

L-Unjoni tag脛搂na g脛搂andha responsabbilt脙聽 morali. B芒聙聶hekk, almenu issa, g脛搂andna na脛隆ixxu b'mod de脛聥i脜录iv, b芒聙聶mod mag脛搂qud, u b芒聙聶mod uman biex in脛隆ibu pa脛聥i li hija sostenibbli.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nikola Bart脜炉脜隆ek (PfE), in writing. 芒聙聯 Peace in the Middle East is fragile, precious, and never the result of declarations made in Brussels. True peace is built every day 芒聙聰 through courage, through dialogue, and through respect for both sides. The recent agreement between Israel and Palestinian representatives is not a miracle, but a step forward 芒聙聰 a sign that hope still exists, even after the horror of October 7th. What the region needs now is not more moral lectures, but support for security, disarmament of terrorist groups, and real diplomacy. Europe must be a voice for peace, not an echo of others芒聙聶 agendas. We must stand by Israel芒聙聶s right to exist and defend itself 芒聙聰 while also recognising the dignity and aspirations of the Palestinian people. Unfortunately, Europe often speaks with many voices and no conviction. We have allowed ideology to replace realism, and bureaucracy to replace leadership. It is time for the European Union to find its own balanced, independent voice 芒聙聰 one that unites moral strength with strategic clarity. Only then can Europe truly contribute to peace, instead of merely commenting on it from afar.

脗聽

3. Changing security landscape and the role of police at the heart of the EU芒聙聶s internal security strategy (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Priek脜隆s脛聯d脛聯t脛聛js. 芒聙聯 N脛聛kamais darba k脛聛rt脛芦bas punkts ir debates par Komisijas pazi脜聠ojumu par main脛芦go dro脜隆脛芦bas vidi un policijas lomu 芒聙聰 Eiropas iek脜隆脛聯j脛聛s dro脜隆脛芦bas strat脛聯脛拢ijas centr脛聛lie elementi (2025/2939(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Magnus Brunner, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, honourable Members, good morning, first of all, let me thank you for putting this important topic on the agenda today. Security is the issue of our times. We are being threatened from the East and we have to rethink our relationships also to the West. We also have what you just discussed before: the conflicts in the Middle East, which actually feed the threat of radical terrorism and divide our communities at home.

Of course, technology also gives new tools to the criminal networks. Our law enforcement must learn to adapt to all these new kinds of threats we are facing, like when hostile states, for instance, employ criminal gangs or even prey upon our youth to carry out hybrid attacks. Only a few days ago, two boys of 17 were arrested in the Netherlands after allegedly being recruited by hackers from Russia, working for Russia also.

In such a world, we cannot just afford business as usual. We need to level up 芒聙聯 also when it comes to internal security. The most important way we can do that is by strengthening our cooperation between the law enforcement authorities. I think that is key. The Union has made good progress, I think, on cross-border practices especially, but there is still fragmentation, even as the need for cooperation actually increases.

This is where Europol comes in. Working closely with this Parliament, we have a unique opportunity, I think, to reinforce the EU's internal security architecture by bringing Europol into its full operational potential. Just one example to mention: the anti-smuggling regulation agreed this year. That's the first step we took. Once adopted, Europol will receive additional resources of EUR脗聽50脗聽million and 50 additional staff members.

But I think we must go further than that 芒聙聯 creating within Europol specialised capacities脗聽to support investigations, for example in areas like data analytics and the digital forensic part. So I think we have to take脗聽very important steps forward.

This brings me to a second aspect of empowering law enforcement: the improvement of access to data. As our societies continue脗聽to go digital, so do the criminals and, pretty obviously, we've got fraud, we've got cyberattacks, money laundering, recruitment, also planning violence 芒聙聯 they're all happening online. We have a duty to protect, but we can't fulfil that without the evidence that will put criminals behind bars.

We have already taken, I think, very important steps to ensure access to data and enable criminal investigations like the E-evidence package. But the simple fact is actually that with every passing year, we are losing ground in the race against crime. I think it's time to reset the clock on that race. In June this year, the Commission adopted the Roadmap for lawful and effective access to data for law enforcement, which actually sets out actions in key areas like retention of data, interception of communications and AI, of course, which plays an important role.

For next year, we are preparing a technology roadmap on encryption to set out solutions for law enforcement to accept and access encrypted data in a lawful manner, safeguarding cybersecurity and fundamental rights. On data retention, we have already launched the impact assessment and we plan to finalise it in the first quarter of next year.

A third area, honourable Members, is organised crime. Never before have these networks been so professional, so international, so cross-border, or so sophisticated as they are today. Next year, the Commission will propose a new legislative package on updating the definition of a criminal organisation as well as the penalties they face. We will also enhance investigative tools. We will reinforce the intelligence picture against organised crime and improve overall prevention.

Some things about criminal networks are changing, but in one respect I would say they have not changed: they are still doing it for the money and they are doing it for 芒聙聯 I would say 芒聙聯 a lot of money actually. That is why we have the 'follow the money' approach and that approach remains so important, in my view.

We need a full transposition and implementation of the Asset Recovery Directive. We are also taking a critical look at our gaps when it comes to tracing criminal finances, as we announced in our ProtectEU strategy, our security strategy. These are just some of the many initiatives we will be launching, including on drugs, on countering terrorism, on firearms, on human trafficking all together.

I know that there are different opinions in this House, which is fair enough when it comes to details. I respect the diversity we have in this House and let's have this debate, of course. But one thing I believe we can all agree on is that without security, there can be no freedom. Without security, there can be no prosperity. Without security, there can be no democracy. So that is why I always say that security is the issue of our time.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tomas Tob脙漏, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, Commissioner, colleagues, we need to make Europe more safe 芒聙聯 now and for future generations 芒聙聯 because organised crime is on the rise across Europe.

Our common task is quite clear. We need to crack down on the criminal networks. At the heart of this work are the brave men and women in law enforcement 芒聙聯 those who safeguard our citizens and communities, sometimes by even risking their own life and safety. It is high time that we acknowledge their dedication and sacrifices, making sure that police and law enforcement are better protected.

But, to be frank, sometimes when I listen to the left of this House, it seems that you are more engaged in criticising the police officers than actually combating the criminals. We as EPP instead say that we must provide law enforcement with the tools necessary to crack down on crime, turn Europol into a truly operational police agency and strengthen our cross-border cooperation between law enforcement.

We should pay tribute to those who have made the ultimate sacrifice and got killed in the line of duty with a European Police Day.

We must realise that criminality is a threat to the whole of society, to ordinary life, and to the internal market, to economic growth and to rule of law.

From EPP, European citizens can trust that we will make sure that we use the full power of European cooperation to push back on this, and we call on the Commission to deliver.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Birgit Sippel, im Namen der S&D-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Freiheit und Sicherheit stehen unter Druck. Da sind einerseits hybride Bedrohungen, Cyberangriffe, Extremismus und andererseits 脙聞ngste durch soziale Unsicherheit, Ausgrenzung, Hass und Hetze 芒聙聯 offline wie online. Mehr denn je brauchen wir Polizei und Beh脙露rden, die anlassbezogen arbeiten, die technisch, personell und in ihrer Ausbildung auf der H脙露he der Zeit sind. Dazu geh脙露ren auch vielf脙陇ltige F脙陇higkeiten und Kenntnisse, von Cyber- und interkultureller Kompetenz bis hin zur vertrauensvollen Zusammenarbeit mit zivilen Akteuren.

Vertrauen basiert dabei auch auf der Achtung von Freiheiten und Grundrechten, auf Vertraulichkeit der Kommunikation 芒聙聯 online wie offline. Deshalb: Immer mehr 脙聹berwachung ist keine Option, sondern verst脙陇rkt 脙聞ngste. Was wir aber brauchen, ist eine 脙聹berwachungs-Gesamtrechnung, damit wir Sicherheit, Freiheit und Grundrechte systematisch sch脙录tzen, das Vertrauen in Menschen und Beh脙露rden neu st脙陇rken k脙露nnen. Denn nur zusammen k脙露nnen wir Freiheit und Sicherheit st脙陇rken.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Matthieu Valet, au nom du groupe PfE. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, la police, pour moi, c'est une grande famille. C'est une passion et surtout une grande fiert脙漏 de rendre 脙聽 la France tout ce qu'elle m'a donn脙漏 en la prot脙漏geant durant vingt脗聽ans.

En 2023, j'脙漏tais fier de commander des femmes et des hommes qui, avec courage, ont d脙漏fendu la R脙漏publique face 脙聽 des racailles qui ont tout incendi脙漏, tout saccag脙漏 et qui ont bless脙漏 pr脙篓s de 1脗聽000脗聽policiers, gendarmes et sapeurs-pompiers en France. Avec nous, ils seront tous d脙漏cor脙漏s, tous r脙漏compens脙漏s et surtout tous mis 脙聽 l'honneur lors d'une c脙漏r脙漏monie bleu blanc rouge qu'Emmanuel脗聽Macron n'a pas organis脙漏e.

Avec nous, les policiers du RAID, de la BRIPP, de la brigade d'intervention, des BAC n'attendront pas dix ans apr脙篓s le Bataclan pour 脙陋tre d脙漏cor脙漏s pour leur courage face aux terroristes islamistes qui ont travers脙漏 l'Europe pour venir frapper mon pays.

Avec nous, tous leurs frais m脙漏dicaux seront pris en charge, sans retard, sans avances, sans qu脙漏mander, sans mendier. Avec nous, la prise en charge de l'avocat sera automatique. Ils seront syst脙漏matiquement prot脙漏g脙漏s par l'administration. Nous cr脙漏erons une pr脙漏somption de prise en charge de l'administration.

Soyez aussi assur脙漏s, devant ce Parlement europ脙漏en, que tous ces branleurs qui saccagent tout aux frais des honn脙陋tes gens qui n'en peuvent plus paieront jusqu'au dernier centime: finies les allocations, fini le logement social. Surtout, nous mettrons au pain sec ces voyous. Face aux sauvages qui fracassent un policier de la BAC 脙聽 Tourcoing, un fr脙篓re d'armes, la r脙篓gle sera simple: tu touches 脙聽 un flic, tu dors en prison et ce sera 脙聽 tes frais. Pareil si tu es un fid脙篓le client des policiers et gendarmes: en plus d'une carte de fid脙漏lit脙漏, fini le foutage de gueule devant les magistrats qui n'en peuvent plus. Ce sera aussi la prison d脙篓s la premi脙篓re r脙漏cidive.

Face aux narco-racailles qui gangr脙篓nent mon pays, nous redonnerons ses lettres de noblesse 脙聽 la police judiciaire qui a 脙漏t脙漏 d脙漏mantel脙漏e par le ministre de l'int脙漏rieur de l'脙漏poque, G脙漏rald脗聽Darmanin. Et nous sauverons tous ces enqu脙陋teurs qui sont en train de crever.

脙聙 ces 脙漏lus gauchistes, je leur dis d'arr脙陋ter de souiller mes fr脙篓res d'armes, policiers, gendarmes, avec 脗芦la police tue脗禄. Parlez plut脙麓t de Jean-Baptiste脗聽Salvaing et de Jessica脗聽Schneider, policiers 脙漏gorg脙漏s par un terroriste 脙聽 Magnanville devant leur enfant Mathieu, 脙垄g脙漏 de trois ans, du gendarme Arnaud脗聽Beltrame qui a sacrifi脙漏 sa vie pour sauver un otage face 脙聽 un islamiste, d'脙聣ric Masson, policier en France, ex脙漏cut脙漏 par un dealer 脙聽 Avignon, qui a laiss脙漏 deux petites filles derri脙篓re lui. Je dis aussi 脙聽 mes coll脙篓gues LFI: rendez-vous en 2026 pour le proc脙篓s de Florian dans l'affaire Nahel. Vous avez souill脙漏 son honneur.

Face 脙聽 vous, je n'aurai jamais peur, je ne reculerai jamais. Il en va de tous mes fr脙篓res d'armes tomb脙漏s pour la France, sous l'uniforme de la police nationale.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Assita Kanko, on behalf of the ECR Group. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear colleagues, dear Commissioner, in every European country you will hear horrifying stories about how police officers have been treated. You will hear their frustration about how their work and the effort and intention they put into it is less and less likely to lead to convictions and change in society. Motivation drops, and it starts making less sense for young people to commit to the mission of a police officer.

And this is at a time when the security landscape has fundamentally shifted: cybercrime, hybrid warfare, organised crime and persistent terrorism 芒聙聯 the lines between internal and external security are blurred by these global challenges. The EU must view these threats as a common European problem, demanding the implementation of a unified, robust and adaptable internal security strategy.

As a believer in EU security sovereignty and as rapporteur, I hailed the Entry/Exit System as a triumph of our common pragmatic resolve over bureaucratic apathy. It is a vital shield to strengthen our Schengen external borders and empower our border protection officers. Border guards and national law enforcement are at the forefront of integrating EES data into criminal databases for real-time alerts on watch lists, individuals and documents. This ties into protecting the EU's focus on preventive policing against terrorism and trafficking. I believe this is the path we must continue to follow.

At the heart of this strategy, the role of our police forces 芒聙聯 the primary gatekeepers of our democratic way of life 芒聙聯 is essential. We need to honour them. We need to support them. We need to address their needs by prioritising resources to improve their functioning and well-being, and by giving back a sense of purpose to their mission. And this is for the security of every European citizen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fabienne Keller, au nom du groupe Renew. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, cher脗聽Magnus Brunner, chers coll脙篓gues, l'Europe fait face 脙聽 une transformation profonde de son environnement s脙漏curitaire. Je crois qu'on le dit tous ici, les menaces se multiplient, se renforcent: terrorisme, crime organis脙漏, menaces hybrides, ing脙漏rence 脙漏trang脙篓re.

La protection de nos concitoyens est une priorit脙漏 europ脙漏enne. Nous devons renforcer la coop脙漏ration polici脙篓re et judiciaire 脙聽 l'脙漏chelle de l'Union. Cette coop脙漏ration existe d脙漏j脙聽 脙聽 une 脙漏chelle de proximit脙漏. Vous le savez, Monsieur le Commissaire, nous avons visit脙漏 ensemble le centre de coop脙漏ration de Kehl. Il existe 56 centres de ce type o脙鹿 coop脙篓rent les polices, les douanes de part et d'autre de la fronti脙篓re. Au niveau europ脙漏en, Europol, Eurojust et le parquet sont les piliers de cette coop脙漏ration. La r脙漏vision de leur mandat est pr脙漏vue en 26.

Je vous appelle, Monsieur le Commissaire, 脙聽 renforcer la capacit脙漏 de ces agences pour faciliter leurs enqu脙陋tes, les 脙漏changes de donn脙漏es que vous avez 脙漏voqu脙漏s. Je vous engage 脙聽 valoriser l'action de terrain qui se fait d脙漏j脙聽 dans ces centres, aux fronti脙篓res de nos 脙聣tats membres et l'esprit de coop脙漏ration entre les polices, les gendarmeries, les douanes nationales, l'ensemble des services qui conduisent les enqu脙陋tes pour avancer ensemble vers une Europe de la s脙漏curit脙漏, ferme sur les r脙篓gles et surtout respectueuse de l'脙漏tat de droit.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Saskia Bricmont, au nom du groupe Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, oui, il y a une v脙漏ritable urgence 脙聽 donner les moyens 脙聽 une police de proximit脙漏, 脙聽 une police et une justice outill脙漏es pour lutter contre toutes les formes de criminalit脙漏 organis脙漏e, trafic de drogue, violences sexuelles sur femmes et enfants, terrorisme. Mais nos dirigeants, majoritairement de droite, faut-il le rappeler, Monsieur Tob脙漏,脗聽ne le voient pas de cet 脜聯il-l脙聽. La police est l'outil au service d'un agenda s脙漏curitaire utilis脙漏 pour acc脙漏der 脙聽 toujours plus de nos donn脙漏es, pour faire taire aussi toute opposition 脙聽 leur politique d'aust脙漏rit脙漏 d'une violence sociale sans pr脙漏c脙漏dent.

Et c'est un sale r脙麓le que vous confiez aux policiers qui s'engagent au service de la soci脙漏t脙漏. Des personnes marchant pacifiquement pour leurs droits sociaux et 脙聽 la pension en ont fait les frais mardi dernier 脙聽 Bruxelles. Intimidations, violences, gaz lacrymog脙篓nes en pr脙漏sence d'enfants. Manifester est un droit. L'usage de la force 脙聽 Anderlecht pour expulser des familles qui r脙漏clament un toit face 脙聽 un 脙聣tat de droite, bien souvent, qui les abandonne et les exploite n'est pas quelque chose de normal. Avoir un toit est un droit. C'est la s脙漏curit脙漏 dont nous avons besoin face 脙聽 un avenir incertain, aux al脙漏as de la vie, aux factures 脙聽 payer et aux bouches 脙聽 nourrir 脙聽 la fin du mois. La s脙漏curit脙漏 de pouvoir marcher librement et sans crainte dans nos rues, au quotidien comme dans les manifs. Cela demande de restaurer la confiance entre forces de l'ordre et citoyens pour avancer ensemble.

(Le Pr脙漏sident retire la parole 脙聽 l'oratrice)

脗聽脗聽脙聳zlem Demirel, im Namen der Fraktion The Left. 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Sie wollen 脙录ber mehr Sicherheit in unseren St脙陇dten sprechen? 脙聹ber die wachsende Kriminalit脙陇t? 脗聽脗聽Lena D脙录pont (PPE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, Herr Kommissar! Manchmal hilft ein Blick auf das, was wir bereits erreicht haben. Der Raum der Sicherheit, der Freiheit und des Rechts 芒聙聯 die Idee von Tampere 1999 芒聙聯 wird heute mit Leben gef脙录llt 芒聙聯 mit Europols t脙陇glichem Kampf gegen Kriminalit脙陇t, wie j脙录ngst in Lettland zusammen mit 脙聳sterreich und Estland im Bereich Cybercrime. Frontex-Beamte, die jeden Tag an der Au脙聼engrenze stehen und gemeinsam mit den Mitgliedstaaten gemeinsam gegen organisierte Kriminalit脙陇t, gegen Drogenhandel, Waffenschmuggel, Zigarettenschmuggel, Menschenhandel vorgehen.

Allein diese ausgew脙陇hlten Beispiele machen deutlich: Polizeiliche europ脙陇ische Zusammenarbeit ist ein entscheidender Baustein unserer europ脙陇ischen Sicherheitsarchitektur. Es sind unsere Polizistinnen und Polizisten aus allen Mitgliedstaaten und in allen Mitgliedstaaten, die tagt脙陇glich f脙录r Sicherheit und Ordnung sorgen. Und deswegen ein herzliches Dankesch脙露n von hier aus.

Gleichzeitig 脙陇ndert sich die Lage dynamisch und teilweise dramatisch. Innere und 脙陇u脙聼ere Sicherheit sind untrennbar miteinander verbunden. Organisierte Kriminalit脙陇t, terroristische Gruppierungen, Drittstaatsakteure fordern uns heraus wie lange nicht. Wir brauchen Europas Sicherheitsarchitektur nicht neu erfinden. Wir m脙录ssen das st脙陇rken, was wir bereits haben. Das gilt f脙录r Frontex und Europol, das gilt f脙录r den Informationsaustausch.

Und weil vieles heute auch vom Kommissar schon gesagt worden ist脗聽芒聙聯 richtigerweise gesagt worden ist脗聽芒聙聯, lassen Sie mich einen Blick verst脙陇rkt auf die grenz脙录berschreitende Zusammenarbeit legen. Wir haben so wunderbare Beispiele in dem Bereich wie j脙录ngst das Jubil脙陇um der deutsch-franz脙露sischen Einsatzeinheit, die gemeinsamen Zentren in Kehl, die Fabienne Keller erw脙陇hnt hat. Das hei脙聼t, wir m脙录ssen uns darauf fokussieren, f脙录r sie praktische H脙录rden abzubauen, gemeinsame 脙聹bungen veranstalten zu k脙露nnen, Sprachkurse zu finanzieren, und vor allen Dingen anerkennen, was f脙录r einen hervorragenden Beitrag sie f脙录r unsere europ脙陇ische Sicherheitsarchitektur leisten.

Wir erwarten von Polizistinnen und Polizisten, dass sie uns, unsere Gesellschaft, unsere Demokratie sch脙录tzen. Tun wir das gleiche f脙录r sie.

(Die Rednerin ist damit einverstanden, auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹 zu antworten.)

脗聽脗聽Lena D脙录pont (PPE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 Thanks, colleague, for that very important question. I assure you that this is indeed part of our policies.

脗聽脗聽Juan Fernando L脙鲁pez Aguilar (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or comisario Brunner, s脙颅, libertad y seguridad van juntas en el art脙颅culo 6 de la Carta de los Derechos Fundamentales de la Uni脙鲁n Europea. Y no hay libertad sin seguridad. Como no hay seguridad sin libertad. Y este Parlamento Europeo hace esa apuesta cuando renueva la estrategia de seguridad interior para que la Uni脙鲁n Europea sea un actor global contra la criminalidad organizada y el terrorismo en ese nuevo frente que plantean las amenazas h脙颅bridas.

Y quiere tambi脙漏n el Parlamento Europeo ser parte de la renovaci脙鲁n del mandato de Europol 芒聙聰que usted ha prometido para 2026芒聙聰, porque hemos trabajado en el refuerzo de las capacidades tecnol脙鲁gicas de Europol. Pero hace falta tambi脙漏n el intercambio de informaci脙鲁n, el intercambio de inteligencia y la cooperaci脙鲁n efectiva entre todas las agencias de seguridad europeas, adem脙隆s de la cooperaci脙鲁n p脙潞blico- privada en la garant脙颅a de la seguridad de los europeos.

Pero el mensaje no est脙隆 completo si no decimos que la libertad est脙隆 representada tambi脙漏n en el control parlamentario, en el escrutinio que ejercen los Parlamentos nacionales y este Parlamento Europeo, sobre la seguridad interior, cumpliendo tambi脙漏n el mandato del art脙颅culo 12脗聽del Tratado de la Uni脙鲁n Europea.

脗聽脗聽Jorge Buxad脙漏 Villalba (PfE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, si solo en cuatro a脙卤os entran ilegalmente en Europa 1脗聽200脗聽000 extranjeros y no conoces su origen, antecedentes ni intenciones, tienes un problema de seguridad; si en Espa脙卤a se dictan por la polic脙颅a 51脗聽000 脙鲁rdenes de expulsi脙鲁n y, el Gobierno c脙鲁mplice, solo deporta a 3脗聽000, tienes un problema de seguridad; si m脙隆s del 50脗聽% de las personas que piden asilo ven rechazada su solicitud y, sin embargo, se quedan en Europa, tienes un problema de seguridad.

Si te preocupa m脙隆s un chaval tuiteando en X 脗芦las vidas blancas importan脗禄 que una manada de extranjeros violando a una joven en Alicante, si repartes dinero a entidades vinculadas a los Hermanos Musulmanes y tus puertos son un coladero a las drogas, tienes un problema de seguridad; si desde tu Gobierno indultas a terroristas antifa porque las v脙颅ctimas son de VOX, si justificas la violencia contra los patriotas, si desproteges a la polic脙颅a, si el terror tiene mejores armas... tienes un problema de seguridad. En realidad, el problema de seguridad eres t脙潞.

Se脙卤or脙颅as, solo hay una soluci脙鲁n: tolerancia cero, persecuci脙鲁n policial, juicios r脙隆pidos, penas altas, c脙隆rceles inc脙鲁modas y deportaciones masivas.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Charlie Weimers (ECR). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, colleagues, migrant-fuelled drug gangsters flaunt stolen wealth: Lamborghinis, watches, designer bling. Every time I see them, I think, 'They steal from us, they poison our children, they murder with impunity'. No longer.

In Sweden, if a gangster cannot prove legal income, the state takes it. Cars, watches, clothes, even villas 芒聙聯 over 200 cases this year, millions seized. Imagine the message it sends when a gang boss is pulled over in his Porsche and, minutes later, must walk home with his girlfriend after police seized the car. That is deterrence.

Police are the front line. They must be at the heart of Europe's strategy. And I've been waiting to say this: Sweden shows the way.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Malik Azmani (Renew). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, collega芒聙聶s, de wereld verandert snel en in deze turbulente tijd gaat er ook terecht veel aandacht uit naar internationale veiligheid. Maar terwijl we naar buiten kijken, mogen we onze interne veiligheid niet vergeten. Ook binnen Europa nemen de dreigingen toe, vaak ook gestimuleerd van buitenaf, van drugsgeweld tot ondermijning en terrorisme. Een versterkte Europese samenwerking is van groot belang en onze politieagenten spelen daarin een cruciale rol, en het is aan ons om ervoor te zorgen dat zij de steun en de middelen krijgen die nodig zijn. De interne veiligheidsstrategie is daarbij essentieel.

Maar woorden zijn niet genoeg. Ik roep de Europese Commissie op om snel met concrete voorstellen te komen die de positie van onze politiemannen en -vrouwen versterken, onder meer in de strijd tegen de productie van en de handel in drugs. Voor een Europa dat niet alleen sterk is buiten, maar ook veilig is binnen zijn eigen grenzen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jaume Asens Llodr脙聽 (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or comisario, hoy no podemos hablar del modelo policial europeo sin hablar de lo que sucede en nuestras calles. Y perm脙颅tame que le recuerde una efem脙漏ride: un d脙颅a como hoy, de 1971, en Par脙颅s, centenares de manifestantes contra el colonialismo en Argelia fueron detenidos, asesinados, torturados. Y esto no ha sido reconocido por Francia hasta el a脙卤o pasado. Eso tiene que ver con la visi脙鲁n que tenemos de la seguridad, una visi脙鲁n marcada por nuestra mentalidad colonial.

Ahora ya no es tanto Argelia, pero es Palestina, y lo vemos en la Ley Antiterrorista de Gran Breta脙卤a, que asocia la solidaridad con Palestina al terrorismo. Y lo vemos en el Madrid de Ayuso, del Partido Popular, que persigue los s脙颅mbolos palestinos porque cree que es un problema de seguridad. Y lo vemos tambi脙漏n en Berl脙颅n, en Budapest, cuando se proh脙颅ben o se disuelven manifestaciones por razones de seguridad. Y lo vimos la semana pasada cuando a un grupo de mujeres le pusieron multas por llevar una bandera palestina.

Hoy no podemos hablar de seguridad sin hablar de esa mentalidad colonial que est脙隆 presente todav脙颅a en nuestro modelo policial europeo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mary Khan (ESN). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Allein in Deutschland wurden im Jahr 2024 109脗聽000脗聽Angriffe auf Polizeibeamte registriert. Das sind mehr als 300 pro Tag, also alle f脙录nf Minuten ein Angriff auf einen Polizeibeamten. Ein Drittel der T脙陇ter hatte keinen deutschen Pass. Die Zahlen zeigen vor allem, wie sehr Migration und Gewalt l脙陇ngst auch die Sicherheit unserer Einsatzkr脙陇fte bedrohen. Die Konflikte, n脙陇mlich gerade vor allem bei uns in Deutschland, werden immer fremder, und der R脙录ckhalt unserer Polizei wird vor allem durch linke Ideologie immer d脙录nner. In Berlin kam es nach den Hamas-Angriffen zu 脙录ber 600脗聽Attacken auf Polizisten. Gleichzeitig nimmt aber auch die linksextreme Gewalt zu. Und was passiert? Linke Politiker relativieren, die CDU schweigt, und die Gr脙录nen versuchen, Spr脙录che wie All Cops are Bastards salonf脙陇hig zu machen.

Diese EU redet von innerer Sicherheit, doch sie l脙陇sst offene Grenzen, importierte Konflikte und Hass auf Uniformierte zu. Eine europ脙陇ische Sicherheitsstrategie muss endlich dort ansetzen, wo Sicherheit wirklich beginnt 芒聙聯 an unseren Grenzen. Wir haben jegliche Infrastruktur daf脙录r. Unsere Beamten brauchen deshalb keine politischen Parolen. Sie brauchen klare Gesetze, R脙录ckendeckung und vor allem auch Respekt. Wir m脙录ssen unserer Polizei wieder das Gef脙录hl der St脙陇rke und des R脙录ckhalts geben, damit sie ohne Angst ihre Auftr脙陇ge erf脙录llen kann. Denn am Ende gilt: Sch脙录tzt, wer uns sch脙录tzt.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jeroen Lenaers (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, een veilige Europese Unie vraagt meer dan alleen sterke grenzen of militaire paraatheid. Ze vereist ook robuuste politiecapaciteit en echte samenwerking. Grensoverschrijdende criminaliteit, cyberdreigingen en hybride aanvallen raken dagelijks onze vrijheid en daarom moet de interne en externe veiligheid beter worden ge脙炉ntegreerd. Geen gescheiden domeinen meer, maar samenwerking om hiaten te dichten en sneller te reageren. Co脙露rdinatie is geen luxe, maar bittere noodzaak.

We moeten investeren in meer politiecapaciteit, zodat korpsen effectief kunnen optreden, ook bijvoorbeeld in grensregio芒聙聶s waar de drugshandel floreert. En tegelijkertijd versterken we die Europese samenwerking. Die twee gaan hand in hand. Stevig op straat en verbonden in Europa. De versterking van Europol is daarbij cruciaal. Meer middelen, meer menskracht om de politiecollega芒聙聶s in de lidstaten effectief te kunnen ondersteunen.

Maar er is meer nodig. In veel lidstaten staat de politie onder druk: te weinig instroom, hoge werkdruk, toenemend geweld, beperkte loopbaanperspectieven, jong talent dat afhaakt, juist nu het zo hard nodig is, agenten die worden bespuugd, belaagd en aangevallen tijdens hun werk, wat onze rechtsstaat ondermijnt. Wie onze veiligheid garandeert, moet zelf veilig zijn.

Daar ligt ook een taak voor Europa. Ik roep de Commissie daartoe nogmaals op: ontwikkel een Europese aanpak om geweld tegen de politie systematisch in kaart te brengen met vergelijkbare data, een gericht beleid en betere bescherming van onze handhavers. Wat we niet meten, kunnen we ook niet oplossen en dit moeten we oplossen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 As I said, no blue cards, colleagues. Sorry about this. Please understand.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Evin Incir (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, security isn't just another cost; it is to protect our societies, communities, our homes and our businesses from deep harm 芒聙聯 from stopping crimes before they happen to fighting terrorism and safeguarding information.

We need strong systems and strong police cooperation as organised criminal gangs operate across borders. Over 70脗聽% of criminal gangs are active in at least three countries in parallel. And they are toxifying the lives of our children. Collaboration between EU Member States has never been more vital.

The Commission's proposal to make Europol stronger is welcome, but it must be based on real needs, and the needs of the Member States. Coordination must be strengthened within the EU and it must also be strengthened with global partners.

But with greater power must come greater responsibility. Intelligence-sharing must improve, yes 芒聙聯 but always with strong safeguards. Expanded surveillance must protect rights, because in the end, one truth remains clear: security matters to ensure safety, but it must be done in a secure way.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Joachim Stanis脜聜aw Brudzi脜聞ski (ECR). 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Panie Komisarzu! Strategia, kt脙鲁r脛聟 tutaj omawiamy, koncentruje si脛聶 na poziomie unijnym i wsp脙鲁脜聜pracy mi脛聶dzy pa脜聞stwami, ale mniej uwagi po脜聸wi脛聶ca si脛聶 integracji i wsparciu lokalnych i krajowych s脜聜u脜录b policyjnych, kt脙鲁re cz脛聶sto s脛聟 na pierwszej linii dzia脜聜a脜聞. Nale脜录y zwr脙鲁ci脛聡 szczeg脙鲁ln脛聟 uwag脛聶 na rol脛聶 policji w przeciwdzia脜聜aniu niepokojom i niebezpiecze脜聞stwom nasilonym wskutek 芒聙聯 by脜聜a ju脜录 o tym mowa 芒聙聯 masowej nielegalnej imigracji, powodowanym przez osoby, kt脙鲁re nie respektuj脛聟 porz脛聟dku publicznego i norm spo脜聜ecznych obowi脛聟zuj脛聟cych w naszych krajach.

Nale脜录y zwr脙鲁ci脛聡 uwag脛聶 na to, 脜录e pa脜聞stwa cz脜聜onkowskie powinny mie脛聡 pe脜聜n脛聟 autonomi脛聶 w tym, jak funkcjonuj脛聟 ich s脜聜u脜录by. Ale przera脜录a mnie wizja tej argumentacji, kt脙鲁ra r脙鲁wnie脜录 pada tutaj, w tej Izbie, 脜录eby policjanci w moim kraju zachowywali si脛聶 tak 芒聙聯 czego byli脜聸my 脜聸wiadkami w Wielkiej Brytanii 芒聙聯 脜录eby kl脛聶kali przed przest脛聶pcami, 脜录eby pl脛聟sali w r脙鲁脜录owych sp脙鲁dniczkach na marszach r脙鲁wno脜聸ci, 脜录eby mo脜录e zacz脛聶li zajmowa脛聡 si脛聶 nie 脜聜apaniem bandyt脙鲁w i przest脛聶pc脙鲁w, tylko wsadzaniem do wi脛聶zie脜聞 os脙鲁b, kt脙鲁re modl脛聟 si脛聶 pod klinikami aborcyjnymi. 脗聽脗聽Raquel Garc脙颅a Hermida-Van Der Walle (Renew). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, collega芒聙聶s, de heer Lenaers zei het net heel goed: onze agenten worden belaagd en bespuugd. Maar hoe worden ze belaagd? Aangevallen? Heel vaak gebeurt dat met explosieven die de kracht van een handgranaat hebben. Explosieven die legaal geproduceerd worden in de EU en vervolgens verkocht. Een Cobra脗聽6 is online te vinden voor een prikkie. En dat heeft niets te maken met vuurwerk. Criminelen maken graag en slim gebruik van de mogelijkheden van onze interne markt.

De recente evaluatie van de huidige vuurwerkregels toont aan dat het tijd is voor Europese actie. Dit bevestigt waar burgemeesters overal in Europa al lang voor waarschuwen. Commissaris, neem dit alstublieft serieus. Zorg ervoor dat onze agenten hun werk veilig kunnen doen. Geef de mensen onze straten terug. Neem deze reikende hand aan en we gaan samen aan de slag.

脗聽脗聽Javier Zarzalejos (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or comisario, este es un debate, el del papel central de la polic脙颅a en la estrategia de seguridad interior de la Uni脙鲁n, que ha tardado mucho tiempo en producirse y, por eso, tenemos que sacar conclusiones.

Con demasiada frecuencia tenemos la impresi脙鲁n de que, al definir las estrategias de seguridad, simplemente damos por supuesta a la polic脙颅a, damos por supuesto su compromiso profesional, que existe. Por eso tenemos que empezar haciendo honor a un imperativo pol脙颅tico y moral, y es el de proteger a quienes nos protegen.

Sin polic脙颅a, sin fuerzas de seguridad, sin agencias de cumplimiento de la ley, no hay Estado de Derecho ni hay ejercicio de las libertades. La polic脙颅a al servicio de la democracia es el Estado de Derecho. Y lo cierto es que nunca ha habido tantas agresiones f脙颅sicas, verbales y digitales contra los profesionales de la seguridad p脙潞blica.

Por lo tanto, si queremos una estrategia de seguridad eficaz, hay que fortalecer el instrumento primordial de esa estrategia, que son las fuerzas de seguridad. En este sentido, la polic脙颅a debe ser considerada como una profesi脙鲁n de riesgo, y estamos comprometidos con ello.

Tenemos que velar por la mejora general de las condiciones de trabajo y de los servicios de apoyo integral 芒聙聰incluido el psicol脙鲁gico芒聙聰 a los miembros de las fuerzas y cuerpos de seguridad; tenemos que avanzar en la armonizaci脙鲁n del tratamiento penal de la protecci脙鲁n a las fuerzas de seguridad y sus profesionales en el 脙隆mbito europeo; y es preciso, con urgencia, mejorar sustancialmente la dotaci脙鲁n de medios humanos, materiales y tecnol脙鲁gicos para que se cierre la brecha escandalosa entre la capacidad de la polic脙颅a y la ventaja de las organizaciones criminales.

脗聽脗聽Andr脙隆s L脙隆szl脙鲁 (PfE). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, we don't need fancy new strategies and even more bureaucracy to defend Europeans against crime. The European internal security strategy is nothing more than an attempt by Ursula von der Leyen to gaslight European citizens.

We could immediately halve violent crime in Europe by kicking illegal immigrants out. It would also almost entirely eradicate terrorism in the European Union.

We need to let police and border guards do their jobs, but most leaders won't. That's why we have migrant youth in Belgium plotting to bomb the prime minister with drones. That's why a football match between Belgium and Israel had to be organised in Hungary. And that's why we have migrant gangs fighting turf wars with Kalashnikovs in the Brussels metro.

This is the security situation in what many call 'the heart of the European Union', and this very House refused to waive the immunity of a violent Antifa member.

Instead of defending our borders, returning illegals and locking up criminals, the European Commission is doing the exact opposite. The EU migration pact will relocate migrants across the EU and it will only make things worse.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Elena Donazzan (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, signor Commissario, onorevoli colleghi, abbiamo un crescente problema di sicurezza interna dentro l'UE, nelle nostre citt脙聽, nelle nostre case.

Da un lato, serve un approccio alla sicurezza e alla difesa che coinvolga l'intera societ脙聽, come ci ricorda il rapporto Niinist脙露: rafforzando le nostre capacit脙聽 militari e ripensando il ruolo della leva, di un servizio, soprattutto per i giovani, e di una riserva pi脙鹿 ampia.

Dall'altro, dobbiamo riconoscere il ruolo fondamentale delle forze di polizia e di sicurezza. Negli ultimi giorni abbiamo visto cortei pseudo-pacifisti manifestare in favore di Gaza e trasformarsi in violenti attacchi contro chi, in divisa, stava solo garantendo ordine e legalit脙聽.

Qui, oggi, non posso non ricordare Valerio, Davide e Marco, i tre carabinieri che hanno perso la vita in provincia di Verona, in Italia, in modo tragico e insensato: a loro e a tutto il corpo dei carabinieri va il nostro pensiero, il nostro grazie.

E trovo inaccettabile che qualcuno, anche in quest'Aula, abbia tentato di giustificare questo vile atto farneticando sul capitalismo, invece di condannare senza esitazioni la violenza. Sto parlando della collega Salis, impunita anche in quest'Aula, e della sua disobbedienza solita. Per garantire sicurezza, prima dobbiamo rispetto a chi veste la divisa.

Is fadhbanna coiri脙潞lachta tras-Eorpacha iad. 脙聣il脙颅onn siad r脙漏itigh trasteorainneacha, faisn脙漏is a roinnt i bhf脙颅or-am, imscr脙潞duithe nasctha tr脙颅 Europol agus foirne comhoibritheacha imscr脙潞daithe a 脙潞s脙隆id chun an t-airgead a lean脙潞int trasna dl脙颅ns脙颅. 脙職s脙隆ideann coirpigh teicneola脙颅ocht chun gluaiseacht go tapa agus caithfidh 脙隆r bp脙鲁il脙颅n脙颅 gluaiseacht i bhfad n脙颅os tap脙潞la.

脗聽脗聽Verena Mertens (PPE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Wenn wir 脙录ber die Sicherheitslage in Europa sprechen, d脙录rfen wir eines nicht vergessen 芒聙聯 unsere Polizei. Sie ist unsere erste Verteidigungslinie脗聽芒聙聯 Tag und Nacht脗聽芒聙聯, wenn es darum geht, unsere B脙录rgerinnen und B脙录rger zu sch脙录tzen脗聽芒聙聯 vor Anschl脙陇gen, vor organisierter Kriminalit脙陇t und vor Cyberangriffen. Doch in vielen dieser Debatten, auch hier im Haus, ger脙陇t sie oft in Vergessenheit.

Aber diese Gefahren verschwinden nicht, nur weil wir weniger dar脙录ber sprechen. Gewaltbereite Extremisten sind europaweit vernetzt, organisierte Betrugsf脙陇lle nehmen zu, und Cyberkriminalit脙陇t kennt keine Grenzen. Als ehemalige Polizistin wei脙聼 ich, was es hei脙聼t, an dieser Front zu stehen. Deshalb setze ich mich ausdr脙录cklich daf脙录r ein, dass wir die Frauen und M脙陇nner w脙录rdigen, die unser Leben sch脙录tzen. Ich fordere einen europ脙陇ischen Gedenktag f脙录r Polizistinnen und Polizisten, insbesondere f脙录r die, die im Dienst ihr Leben verloren haben. Ich m脙露chte, dass wir endlich auch politisch deutlich machen: Wir stehen an der Seite derer, die uns sch脙录tzen, derer, die sich bei einem Amoklauf oder Anschlag mit ihrem eigenen Leben f脙录r uns ins Feuer stellen. Protect the protectors!

脗聽脗聽Christophe Bay (PfE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, depuis le discours de Mme脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen du 18脗聽juillet脗聽2024 voulant rendre Europol un service op脙漏rationnel, nous nous dirigeons clairement vers une police f脙漏d脙漏rale. Or, la s脙漏curit脙漏 int脙漏rieure rel脙篓ve avant tout de nos forces de s脙漏curit脙漏 nationales.

Bien entendu, nous sommes favorables 脙聽 une coop脙漏ration inter脙漏tatique. Elle est n脙漏cessaire 脙聽 travers Europol ou au niveau mondial avec Interpol. N脙漏cessaire en mati脙篓re d'脙漏changes d'informations, de formations communes, cette coop脙漏ration doit rester inter脙漏tatique, fond脙漏e sur la volont脙漏 des 脙聣tats et non sur des injonctions bureaucratiques.

Ce que nous refusons, c'est la tentation d'une Europe de la police, centralis脙漏e, o脙鹿 la Commission pr脙漏tendrait dicter 脙聽 nos forces de s脙漏curit脙漏 comment agir. Une fois encore, vous ne pensez qu'脙聽 limiter l'action des 脙聣tats. En mati脙篓re de s脙漏curit脙漏, l'Union europ脙漏enne doit laisser les 脙聣tats agir, car eux seuls sont l脙漏gitimes pour prot脙漏ger leurs citoyens.

脗聽脗聽Maciej W脛聟sik (ECR). 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Masowa migracja to ogromny wzrost przest脛聶pczo脜聸ci. Fa脜聜szywi azylanci masowo nap脜聜ywaj脛聟cy do Europy to zagro脜录enie dla naszych obywateli i rodzin i dzieci. Chcemy by脛聡 bezpieczni? Bro脜聞my granic, nie wpuszczajmy fa脜聜szywych azylant脙鲁w, a tych, kt脙鲁rzy dotarli, wy脜聸lijmy do kraj脙鲁w pochodzenia.

Pakt migracyjny nie stanowi 脜录adnego remedium dla nielegalnej imigracji. Trzeba i脜聸脛聡 drog脛聟 Polski. Twarda obrona granic wbrew lewicowym 脜聸rodowiskom. Zapora na granicy z Bia脜聜orusi脛聟 wbrew Ursuli von der Leyen. Zawracanie nielegalnych imigrant脙鲁w na granicy do kraj脙鲁w, gdzie ich pobyt jest legalny wbrew oficerom humanitarnym Frontexu. Odrzucenie paktu migracyjnego wbrew Komisji Europejskiej. Fa脜聜szywi azylanci to nie, jak m脙鲁wi脜聜a komisarz Ylva Johansson, ludzie poszukuj脛聟cy mi脜聜o脜聸ci w Unii.

Fa脜聜szywi azylanci to nie, jak m脙鲁wi脜聜 Donald Tusk, biedni ludzie poszukuj脛聟cy miejsca na ziemi. Fa脜聜szywi azylanci to ludzie 脜录eruj脛聟cy na wysokich 脜聸wiadczeniach socjalnych i dobrobycie pa脜聞stw Unii. M脙鲁wiono 芒聙聻Wpu脜聸脛聡cie ich, a potem sprawdzi si脛聶, kim s脛聟芒聙聺. To fa脜聜szywa strategia, kt脙鲁ra przynios脜聜a ogromny wzrost przest脛聶pczo脜聸ci i pot脛聶脜录ne zmiany spo脜聜eczne w pa脜聞stwach Europy.

脗聽脗聽Ana Miguel Pedro (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, poucas profiss脙碌es carregam tanto peso como a de um agente policial, muitas vezes com risco da sua pr脙鲁pria vida. A Europa mudou. O crime 脙漏 hoje transnacional, tecnol脙鲁gico e sem fronteiras. E quem est脙隆 na linha da frente das nossas pol脙颅cias sente essa mudan脙搂a todos os dias.

A nova estrat脙漏gia de seguran脙搂a interna, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, 脙漏 um passo decisivo. Os criminosos j脙隆 atuam sem fronteiras. 脙聣 tempo de a justi脙搂a e de as for脙搂as de seguran脙搂a tamb脙漏m o fazerem. A justi脙搂a europeia tem de ser t脙拢o 脙隆gil quanto o crime que combate. Isto significa, tamb脙漏m, dotar as for脙搂as policiais de meios modernos e eficazes. N脙拢o se combate o crime do futuro com instrumentos do passado.

Mas, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, nenhuma estrat脙漏gia vence se deixar para tr脙隆s quem a executa. Sem pol脙颅cias respeitados, formados e valorizados, n脙拢o h脙隆 seguran脙搂a europeia. Hoje, o n脙潞mero e a viol脙陋ncia dos ataques contra agentes da autoridade aumenta. O que antes era uma exce脙搂脙拢o tornou芒聙聭se rotina. O que antes chocava passou a ser tolerado. E uma Europa que se quer forte tem de come脙搂ar por respeitar quem a defende.

脗聽脗聽Dolors Montserrat (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or comisario, cuando un polic脙颅a se juega su propia vida por defender la libertad de todos, no pide privilegios, exige justicia. Reconocer su labor como profesi脙鲁n de riesgo no es un privilegio, es un deber moral y pol脙颅tico. Europa cre脙鲁 Frontex, Europol o CEPOL para responder al terrorismo y al crimen organizado. Pero ya no basta con reaccionar.

Hoy el Partido Popular Europeo propone algo m脙隆s: actuar antes de que el mal act脙潞e. La narcolancha que embisti脙鲁 a la Guardia Civil en Barbate no solo asesin脙鲁 a dos polic脙颅as, atent脙鲁 contra la libertad de toda la Uni脙鲁n, y ante eso solo cabe la firmeza por respuesta.

Debemos blindar el principio de autoridad, reconocer a la Polic脙颅a como profesi脙鲁n de riesgo en toda la Uni脙鲁n Europea y que los ataques a los polic脙颅as sean eurodelito para que los delincuentes entiendan este mensaje.

Las leyes se acatan y al polic脙颅a se le respeta en todos los rincones de Europa, porque cuando un polic脙颅a cae, cae nuestra libertad. Defenderlos a ellos es defender nuestra democracia.

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Colleagues, we will have a very short catch-the-eye procedure, and I will use as a criterion being present during the entire time of the debate. I will only give the floor to those colleagues.

脗聽脗聽Caterina Chinnici (PPE). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, signor Commissario, onorevoli colleghi, come figlia di un giudice, Rocco Chinnici, ucciso dalla mafia con gli uomini della sua scorta; come magistrato, che per molti anni ha lavorato a diretto contatto con la polizia giudiziaria; come madre di un ufficiale dei carabinieri e di un funzionario di polizia e ora come legislatore, credo fermamente sia nostro compito tutelare chi ci tutela.

Donne e uomini delle forze dell'ordine, compiendo il loro dovere, ci garantiscono sicurezza, diritti e libert脙聽, affrontando rischi e pericoli talvolta fatali. Come i tre carabinieri che la settimana scorsa in Italia hanno perso la vita nel corso di un'operazione e che, personalmente, in quest'Aula oggi desidero ricordare e omaggiare.

Assicurare alle autorit脙聽 di contrasto nazionali ed europee risorse umane, finanziarie e tecniche necessarie per affrontare la criminalit脙聽, soprattutto la criminalit脙聽 organizzata. Potenziare, come previsto nella strategia ProtectEU, la cooperazione operativa transfrontaliera nell'attivit脙聽 di contrasto e il sostegno di Europol agli Stati membri. Rafforzare la tutela sociale e giuridica degli appartenenti alle forze di polizia, riconoscendone il rischio professionale.

脗聽脗聽Oihane Agirregoitia Mart脙颅nez (Renew). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or vicepresidente, llevamos tiempo hablando de que debemos garantizar la seguridad de una forma hol脙颅stica y ser aut脙鲁nomos y soberanos, y solamente somos conscientes de ello cuando tenemos una situaci脙鲁n que nos deja en jaque, como la que vivimos ayer con Amazon Web Services.

Solo podremos vivir tranquilos y tranquilas si somos capaces de garantizar m脙颅nimamente la seguridad f脙颅sica, digital, aeroterrestre, mar脙颅tima y energ脙漏tica. Esto es cuesti脙鲁n de soberan脙颅a estrat脙漏gica. Sin seguridad no hay libertad.

En Euskadi 芒聙聰en mi pa脙颅s芒聙聰 lo tuvimos claro y por eso apostamos por una polic脙颅a integral como principio de autonom脙颅a. Pueden y deben contar con la Ertzaintza para desarrollar el modelo de seguridad europeo. (La oradora se expresa en una lengua no oficial).

脗聽脗聽Petras Gra脜戮ulis (ESN). 芒聙聯 Gerbiami europarlamentarai. Tik i脜隆rinkus mane 脛炉 Europos Parlament脛聟, apsistojau vie脜隆butyje Briuselyje ir vakare i脜隆脛聴jau pasivaik脜隆脛聧ioti. Nu脛聴jau nuo vie脜隆bu脛聧io tik keliasde脜隆imt metr脜鲁 ir buvau u脜戮pultas narkoman脜鲁 ir kitoki脜鲁 nusikalt脛聴li脜鲁. Europos sostin脛聴je! Man policija paai脜隆kino, kad negalima ten eiti. 脗聽脗聽Maravillas Abad脙颅a Jover (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, hace dos semanas, un agente recibi脙鲁 una pu脙卤alada que le atraves脙鲁 el casco. 脗驴Alguien ha pensado en su familia o en sus compa脙卤eros? Hace dos a脙卤os, dos guardias civiles fueron asesinados luchando contra el narcotr脙隆fico en una lancha.

脗驴Alguien ha pensado en quienes hoy siguen saliendo a proteger nuestras fronteras? 脗驴Cu脙隆ntos nombres m脙隆s no conocemos porque algunos Gobiernos se niegan a recoger los casos? 脗驴Cu脙隆ntos polic脙颅as dudan de si esto merece la pena? 脗隆Qu脙漏 dura es esa enfermedad silenciosa que tantos afrontan en soledad!

Por eso, este Parlamento tiene que decir alto y claro que s脙颅 merece la pena salir a defender la libertad y el Estado de Derecho. Merece la pena y tenemos que protegerles. El crimen organizado no respeta fronteras. Necesitamos una estrategia com脙潞n, datos fiables y equipos suficientes para garantizar su seguridad.

Debemos reconocer a las fuerzas y cuerpos de seguridad como profesiones de alto riesgo, porque defender a nuestros agentes es defender la libertad de todos los europeos.

脗聽脗聽Matthieu Valet (PfE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, je trouve dommage que, pour un d脙漏bat sur le r脙麓le de la police, qui joue un r脙麓le crucial dans nos d脙漏mocraties, vous ayez sabr脙漏 le d脙漏bat et qu'on ne puisse pas lever de cartons bleus pour pouvoir mener un d脙漏bat face 脙聽 une extr脙陋me gauche qui insulte nos policiers, qui les taxe de violences polici脙篓res, de contr脙麓les au faci脙篓s, et qui nous explique quand m脙陋me qu'ils bravent les interdictions de manifester pour d脙漏fendre les honn脙陋tes gens.

J'aimerais donc que, dans cette enceinte qui se dit 脗芦d脙漏mocratie en action脗禄, chaque sensibilit脙漏 puisse s'exprimer sur des positions qu'on ne partage pas, mais en tout cas, la d脙漏mocratie c'est 脙搂a. Vous distribuez 脙聽 longueur de journ脙漏e des brevets de respectabilit脙漏. Ce serait bien de vous les appliquer 脙聽 vous-m脙陋mes et de faire de ce temple la r脙漏elle d脙漏mocratie en action.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 I understand you, but as you remember, there were several rejections of blue cards because of time constraints; there was nothing political.

We are closing catch-the-eye, and I would like to reduce the noise in the room a bit.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Magnus Brunner, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, I will be short, I know you have other things to do, but just a couple of words 芒聙聯 and actually, it shows how important the topic of security is, which is great, if that's the case. Thank you very much for your contributions, to those who had contributions in this Chamber, it's great to see that there is such wide agreement on the main challenges: on security, on the police forces, on law enforcement altogether. I even believe there is also a strong consensus on where we must go, which direction we must go.

Security challenges, as most of you mentioned, are more cross-border, more sophisticated, more international, and we can only succeed by becoming also more European in our responses. That means better coordination and cooperation between law enforcement with Europol as a central hub; that means also more harmonised laws at EU level, combined, of course, always with room for regional cooperation based on what local needs there are; and, thirdly, also backed up with the resources we need to keep Europe safe in this changing world. On all of this, I look very much forward to working together with you to find solutions 芒聙聯 at the end of the day, that's what it's about.

Rakstiski pazi脜聠ojumi (178. pants)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mariusz Kami脜聞ski (ECR), na pi脜聸mie. 芒聙聯 Komisarz Brunner poruszy脜聜 szereg bardzo istotnych kwestii. Zgadzam si脛聶, 脜录e dost脛聶p do nowoczesnych technologii zapewnia przewag脛聶 w sektorze bezpiecze脜聞stwa wewn脛聶trznego. Zorganizowana przest脛聶pczo脜聸脛聡 - bez wzgl脛聶du czy mowa o handlu narkotykami, brutalnej przest脛聶pczo脜聸ci kryminalnej, czy skomplikowanych przest脛聶pstwach ekonomicznych - u脜录ywa nowoczesnych technologii. Do tego dochodz脛聟 obce wrogie nam s脜聜u脜录by, dla kt脙鲁rych jest to podstawowe narz脛聶dzie dzia脜聜ania. Komisarz poda脜聜 przyk脜聜ad z Holandii, ale w moim kraju dzi脛聶ki profesjonalizmowi s脜聜u脜录b uda脜聜o si脛聶 rozbi脛聡 wiele zadaniowanych przez Moskw脛聶 grup sabota脜录owych.

To na rz脛聟dach pa脜聞stw narodowych spoczywa odpowiedzialno脜聸脛聡 za bezpiecze脜聞stwo i zwalczanie przest脛聶pczo脜聸ci. Nie ulega jednak w脛聟tpliwo脜聸ci, 脜录e usprawni脛聡 mo脜录na, a nawet nale脜录y, wsp脙鲁脜聜prac脛聶 mi脛聶dzyrz脛聟dow脛聟 na poziomie rozpoznania i zwalczania przest脛聶pczo脜聸ci zorganizowanej.

Komisarz s脜聜usznie zauwa脜录y脜聜, 脜录e nie mo脜录emy skutecznie chroni脛聡 obywateli bez dost脛聶pu do dowod脙鲁w, kt脙鲁re pozwol脛聟 wsadzi脛聡 przest脛聶pc脙鲁w za kratki. M脙鲁wi脜聜 o pracach nad rozwi脛聟zaniami umo脜录liwiaj脛聟cymi organom 脜聸cigania dost脛聶p do zaszyfrowanych danych. To bardzo wa脜录ne kwestie, kt脙鲁re nale脜录y pilnie rozwi脛聟za脛聡, ale w kt脙鲁rych licz脛聟 si脛聶 szczeg脙鲁脜聜y. Czekam na konkretne propozycje.

Wreszcie nie zapominajmy o tych, kt脙鲁rzy codziennie stoj脛聟 na pierwszej linii- policjantach, kt脙鲁rzy s脜聜u脜录脛聟 pa脜聞stwu i spo脜聜ecze脜聞stwu, dlatego musz脛聟 czu脛聡 pe脜聜ne wsparcie pa脜聞stwa i obywateli. Musimy wzmacnia脛聡 policj脛聶, a nie j脛聟 os脜聜abia脛聡, bo os脜聜abiaj脛聟c policj脛聶, os脜聜abiamy nie tylko struktury pa脜聞stwa, lecz ca脜聜e spo脜聜ecze脜聞stwo.

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 We start with a few points. The Patriots for Europe Group has notified the President of decisions relating to changes to appointments within committees. These decisions will be set out in the minutes of today's sitting and take effect on the date of this announcement.

I would like to inform the House that I have received several requests for points of order, and we'll start with Sabine脗聽Verheyen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sabine Verheyen (PPE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Bevor wir heute beginnen, m脙露chte ich um einen Moment Ihrer Zeit bitten, um unserer ehemaligen Kollegin Petra Kammerevert zu gedenken, die am Wochenende nach schwerer Krankheit viel zu jung脗聽芒聙聯 im Alter von 59脗聽Jahren脗聽芒聙聯 verstorben ist.

Petra war eine langj脙陇hrige gesch脙陇tzte Kollegin und ehemalige Vorsitzende des Kulturausschusses, und sie wird uns als leidenschaftliche Verfechterin f脙录r Kultur, Bildung und Medienfreiheit in Europa in Erinnerung bleiben. Sie hat sich 脙录ber Parteigrenzen hinweg f脙录r kulturellen Zusammenhalt in Europa und Gerechtigkeit stark gemacht und die Idee europ脙陇ischer Identit脙陇t stets verteidigt. Sie hat ebenfalls hier im Haus entscheidend mitgewirkt mit ihrer Arbeit an der AVMD oder auch dem EMFA, einem Gesetz, das entscheidend f脙录r die Schaffung gleicher Wettbewerbsbedingungen und neue audiovisuelle Medien ist und auch die Freiheit von Journalismus in den Vordergrund stellt. Ohne ihren verl脙陇sslichen Einsatz w脙陇ren diese Gesetze sicherlich nicht zustande gekommen.

Man konnte sich politisch wunderbar in der Sache mit ihr streiten, aber auf der menschlichen Ebene ist sie einem immer mit gro脙聼em Respekt und W脙陇rme begegnet. Das kommt leider in der Politik viel zu selten vor. Ich werde im Auftrag der Pr脙陇sidentin das Parlament bei ihrer Beisetzung vertreten, und ich bitte darum, dass wir einen kurzen Augenblick innehalten, um Petra Kammerevert zu gedenken.

(Das Parlament erhebt sich zu einer Schweigeminute)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Petra was a wonderful colleague. Sincere condolences to her family.

The other point of order is from Marion Mar脙漏chal.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marion Mar脙漏chal (ECR). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, j'interviens sur le fondement de l'article dix du r脙篓glement int脙漏rieur. Hier, sur ses r脙漏seaux sociaux, Rima Hassan jubilait devant les images des ex脙漏cutions sommaires de civils commises 脙聽 Gaza par les terroristes du Hamas. Son commentaire satisfait et gla脙搂ant 脗芦One by one脗禄 r脙漏sonne comme une v脙漏ritable incitation au meurtre. Cette gauche d脙漏voile l脙聽 son vrai visage, celui du soutien 脙聽 un islamisme sanguinaire qui se drape dans les habits de la justice. Ne vous y trompez pas, dans l'esprit de Rima Hassan et de ses amis des Fr脙篓res musulmans, ce 脗芦One by one脗禄 ne s'adresse pas seulement aux suppos脙漏s tra脙庐tres gazaouis, mais 脙聽 tous ceux qui se dressent face au projet djihadiste de cette organisation. Il d脙漏signe tous ceux que les islamistes veulent combattre: les Juifs, les chr脙漏tiens, les immigr脙漏s assimil脙漏s et la civilisation europ脙漏enne, 脗芦One by one脗禄.

Madame Hassan, nous avons g脙漏n脙漏reusement accueilli votre famille comme r脙漏fugi脙漏s. Vous trahissez chaque jour notre g脙漏n脙漏rosit脙漏 en semant la haine et la discorde. Je n'ai qu'une chose 脙聽 vous dire: partez. Partez et rentrez l脙聽 o脙鹿 vous vous consid脙漏rez chez vous. Rejoignez donc ce Hamas que vous aimez tant et go脙禄tez, en tant que femme aux d脙漏lices amers d'脙陋tre une femme au milieu des islamistes et 脙聽 d脙漏faut au moins...

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Please respect the timing. The procedures are well known by everyone, including the timing.

The next point of order is from Nathalie Loiseau.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nathalie Loiseau (Renew). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, je voudrais faire un rappel au r脙篓glement, en vertu de l'article 10 et de l'article 183 de notre r脙篓glement. Ces articles pr脙漏cisent qu'un d脙漏put脙漏 europ脙漏en doit pr脙漏server la dignit脙漏 du Parlement, ne pas porter atteinte 脙聽 sa r脙漏putation et qu'il peut 脙陋tre sanctionn脙漏 s'il manque 脙聽 ses obligations. Nous savions que notre coll脙篓gue Rima Hassan n'avait montr脙漏 aucune compassion pour les victimes du pogrom du 7 octobre. Nous d脙漏couvrons d脙漏sormais qu'elle fait l'apologie des exactions commises par le Hamas contre des Palestiniens dans la bande de Gaza. On aurait pu esp脙漏rer que son groupe politique la sanctionne, mais je crains que cet espoir soit vain. Je vous demande donc, Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, de veiller 脙聽 la dignit脙漏 de notre Parlement et de prendre les mesures qui s'imposent.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Evin Incir (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr President, I am asking for the floor in accordance with Rule脗聽10(1), 10(2), 10(3) and 10(4). During the debate on Gaza, Patriot MEP Anders Vistisen was throwing out lies and accusations that I am an Hamas apologist. I have had enough!

Demanding an end to the genocide is standing up for international law. Unlike him, I can actually condemn the Israeli far-right government's war crimes and Hamas war crimes.

You know, you need to have two cells in your brain and make them function at the same time! If anything, he's making himself Hamas's and the Israeli far-right government's useful idiot.

Mr President, slander is not a political opinion. Lies are not a political opinion. And his ways of debating don't even fit in a playground. I therefore call for action according to Rule 182.

6.3. Union-wide effect of certain driving disqualifications (A10-0193/2025 - Matteo Ricci) (vote)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next vote is on Union-wide effect of certain driving disqualifications (see minutes, item 6.3).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

芒聙聯 Before the vote:

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Matteo Ricci, relatore. 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, oggi facciamo un passo importante per la sicurezza delle nostre strade, adottando gli ultimi due provvedimenti di un pacchetto che vuole proteggere davvero chi guida e vuole proteggere i pedoni. Nel 2024 abbiamo avuto ancora quasi 20.000 vittime per incidenti stradali, un numero troppo alto che dobbiamo assolutamente ridurre.

Questa nuova direttiva arriva dopo quasi 60 anni e finalmente mette ordine nelle regole sul ritiro delle patenti in tutta Europa: oggi, infatti, chi commette un'infrazione grave, in un Paese diverso da quello che ha rilasciato la patente, spesso rimane impunito in gran parte dell'Unione europea. Ben il 40% di queste infrazioni transfrontaliere restava senza conseguenze serie.

Con le nuove norme, invece, chi perde la patente in un Paese europeo per un'infrazione grave non potr脙聽 pi脙鹿 guidare in nessun altro Stato membro. La decisione di sospensione o ritiro della patente sar脙聽 riconosciuta e applicata ovunque in Europa. Voglio ringraziare l'onorevole Paulus e tutti i relatori ombra脗聽che hanno lavorato con impegno e spirito di collaborazione.

Questo pacchetto non 脙篓 solo un insieme di regole: 脙篓 un impegno concreto per rendere le nostre strade pi脙鹿 sicure, specialmente perch脙漏 abbiamo pi脙鹿 a cuore i nostri figli.

脗聽

6.9. Statute and funding of European political parties and European political foundations (A9-0223/2022 - Lor脙隆nt Vincze, Charles Goerens) (vote)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next vote is on the statute and funding of European political parties and European political foundations (see minutes, item 6.9).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lor脙隆nt Vincze, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 (in writing) The new rules for European parties and foundations are long overdue. I am grateful to my co-rapporteur, Charles Goerens, to the shadow rapporteurs, to the Polish Presidency, and to the Commission for their readiness to unblock this seemingly complicated file.

Let me highlight a few key elements of the regulation. It provides European parties and foundations with clear rules on governance, including the possibility to have associated members from third countries.

It strengthens their financial viability and facilitates their cooperation with national member parties through clearer provisions on organising joint political activities.

We have also included measures to reduce the excessive administrative burden on the parties and to increase their legal certainty. In addition, we addressed the new and growing risks of foreign interference.

Dear colleagues, you are about to vote on new, simplified rules governing our European parties and foundations. Let me remind you that these rules will benefit all European parties and foundations represented in this Hemicycle.

脗聽

6.10. Monitoring framework for resilient European forests (A10-0176/2025 - Emma Wiesner, Eric Sargiacomo) (vote)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next vote is on the monitoring framework for resilient European forests (see minutes, item 6.10).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

芒聙聯 Before the vote:

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Emma Wiesner, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Mr President, today our national forest monitoring systems speak different languages. With this law, we have an opportunity to build a system that would be able to translate those languages into a common European one, a language that could help us understand why forest fires grow more common, why bark beetle attacks become more severe, to help and assist our forest owners.

The Commission proposal was not perfect, but this was a perfect opportunity to guarantee the involvement of the Member States, to put the Member State authorities in the centre, and to ensure true subsidiarity in the field of forests 芒聙聯 values that many of you in this side of the House (speaker gestures to the right of the House)脗聽say that you cherish. I will warn you, because if this law is rejected, information will still be used, but without the right safeguards, without any way for the Member States to ensure that the data is used correctly.

Don't distrust information, colleagues, don't make this vote a lost opportunity to strengthen the Member States' role in forest-related policies.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Eric Sargiacomo, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, ch脙篓re Emma, depuis plusieurs mois, nous avons travaill脙漏 sans rel脙垄che pour chercher la voie du compromis, pour faire avancer ce texte 脙漏minemment perfectible, dans un contexte o脙鹿 la pression sur nos for脙陋ts n'a jamais 脙漏t脙漏 aussi forte. Nous pensions y arriver, nous pensions y arriver, tant les for脙陋ts sont une partie de la solution pour les d脙漏fis que nous nous devons de relever, aussi bien environnementaux qu'脙漏conomiques.

Le vote du rejet en commission est un 脙漏chec. Le n脙麓tre comme rapporteurs, mais surtout celui de notre assembl脙漏e, incapable de construire une position de compromis 脙漏quilibr脙漏 pour n脙漏gocier avec le Conseil et la Commission. En somme, nous avons d脙漏cid脙漏 collectivement de renoncer 脙聽 notre r脙麓le de l脙漏gislateur. Nos coll脙篓gues du PPE ont pr脙漏f脙漏r脙漏 la compromission plut脙麓t que le compromis. Je n'ose esp脙漏rer un sursaut en fid脙漏lit脙漏 脙聽 leur long h脙漏ritage politique.脗聽Alea jacta est, maintenant, votons.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

芒聙聯 After the vote on the proposal to reject the Commission proposal:

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Kos, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, honourable Members, I wish to thank all of you in this House, in particular the co-rapporteurs, for their work on this proposal. The Commission takes good note of this vote 芒聙聯 as you know, the Commission has already expressed its position and concerns on the Council's general approach on this file. I will immediately report back to the college so that the Commission can take appropriate action in light of the positions of both co-legislators. The European Parliament and the Council will be informed about the next steps of this file.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Based on the intervention of the Commissioner, the Commission maintains its proposal.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Eric Sargiacomo, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, nous prenons acte de ce vote de rejet et, avec ma corapporteure, sur la base de l'article 60, nous demandons de renvoyer le texte 脙聽 la commission comp脙漏tente pour r脙漏examen, en attendant la r脙漏ponse de la Commission.

脗聽

6.11. Standing Forest and Forestry Expert Group (A10-0175/2025 - Emma Wiesner, Pekka Toveri) (vote)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next vote is on the Standing Forest and Forestry Expert Group (see minutes, item 6.11).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

芒聙聯 Before the vote:

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Emma Wiesner, F脙露redragande. 芒聙聯 Herr talman! Skogarna erbjuder rekreation. De lagrar koldioxid, skyddar den biologiska m脙楼ngfalden och m脙露jligg脙露r f脙露r landsbygdssamh脙陇llen att leva och utvecklas.

Nationella experter 脙陇r de som k脙陇nner skogarna b脙陇st och m脙楼ste st脙楼 i centrum f脙露r EU:s politik som p脙楼verkar skogarna. Eftersom f脙露ruts脙陇ttningarna skiljer sig 脙楼t fr脙楼n Lappland till Alperna, mellan Finland och Spanien, 脙陇r det just den h脙陇r lokala expertisen som 脙陇r helt avg脙露rande.

I 脙楼rtionden har den st脙陇ndiga skogskommitt脙漏n fungerat som en viktig l脙陇nk mellan medlemsstaterna och EU-kommissionen. Och n脙陇r vi nu moderniserar den m脙楼ste vi s脙陇kerst脙陇lla att det h脙陇r samarbetet, och andan av samarbete, lever vidare.

Jag hoppas d脙陇rf脙露r att ni kommer att st脙露dja det h脙陇r f脙露rslaget och r脙露sta f脙露r att beh脙楼lla och bevara det h脙陇r viktiga samarbetet som s脙陇tter lokal expertis och nationella experter i centrum f脙露r EU:s arbete och EU:s skogspolitik.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pekka Toveri, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues, the report we are voting on today updates the decision establishing the Standing Forestry Committee. For decades, this forum has ensured that the Member States' knowledge and experience are taken into account in EU policy making. Throughout these negotiations, our goal has been to update what is necessary while focusing on the most important point: we need cooperation between the Member States and the Union, not further centralisation of power in the Commission. Subsidiarity, together with the practical knowledge of our forest owners and professionals must remain at the core of our decision making. We have found a balanced compromise that strengthens this cooperation.

Even though forests are linked to several EU policies, management remains a national competence. The Standing Forestry Committee helps the EU and the Member States work together constructively on these shared objectives. I want to thank the shadow rapporteurs and all colleagues who have worked on this file. Let us keep first legislation practical, balanced and respectful of national competences. Therefore, I invite you all to support the report in the final vote.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

芒聙聯 After the vote on the Commission proposal:

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pekka Toveri (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, in accordance with Rule 60(4), I request that the file be referred back to the committees responsible for interinstitutional negotiations.
