脗聽

9. Institutional consequences of the EU enlargement negotiations (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next item is the report by Sandro Gozi, on behalf of the Committee on Constitutional Affairs, on institutional consequences of the EU enlargement negotiations (2025/2041(INI)) (A10-0177/2025).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sandro Gozi, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear Commissioner, dear colleagues, first of all, I want to thank all the shadow rapporteurs for their constructive work and commitment throughout this process, and notably the intensive and good work on the compromise amendments. I also want to record the useful exchanges and cooperation with the AFET and SEDE committees.

This report starts from a simple conviction: the real question today is not whether the European Union should enlarge, but how to ensure that enlargement strengthens our Union. The cost of inaction would be too high. Reform is no longer a choice; it is a necessity. Continental unification and reform must go hand in hand.

Every enlargement in our history has been preceded by an internal reform. The Treaty of Nice prepared the 2004 enlargement to Central and Eastern Europe. The Treaty of Lisbon then made it possible to integrate Croatia and to equip the Union with institutions which should have been fit for 28脗聽Member States. And today, once again, if we want to welcome new countries, we must adapt our Union to this new dimension. We must reform the Union to unify Europe.

This report shows the way forward in a very pragmatic way. It offers a clear direction for a Union that acts faster and speaks with one voice. It turns the priorities set out in this very Chamber in September, in the State of the Union address, into concrete proposals.

Our objectives are three-fold. First of all, efficiency 芒聙聯 by extending qualified majority voting to more areas so we can move beyond paralysis and take decisions more quickly. Power and resources 芒聙聯 by reforming the EU budget, as we already decided together last May, so that Europe has the means to act. And democracy 芒聙聯 by strengthening the powers of the European Parliament.

But I want to be very clear on one point, to clarify to some colleagues. Our report proposes the use of qualified majority voting only for intermediate stages of the enlargement process: 150 cases of decisions on technical and sectoral methods that cannot continue to be dependent on veto. But we maintain unanimity when it truly matters: for decisions to open negotiations, to close them, and to approve a new Member State. These are historic and sovereign decisions, and they must remain unanimous. We must all agree to accept a new member of the club. We are not therefore taking power away from the Member States; we are giving Europe the capacity to act.

The text also calls for making full use of current Treaty flexibilities, pursuing targeted Treaty changes on issues such as rule of law, and advancing differentiated integration among willing Member States. In this sense, defence could be a great example. Tools such as Permanent Structured Cooperation allow willing Member States to move forward in the field of security and defence. We need, colleagues, a Europe of free political will. Those who want to move ahead faster must be allowed to do so without obliging the others to make the same choice.

Throughout the drafting process, we worked constructively across political groups to build a balanced and ambitious text. The spirit of cooperation gives me hope that the report will receive broad support in plenary, sending a strong signal of unity and determination to move Europe forward in view of the Commission's pre-enlargement strategy and in view of the discussion in the European Council.

I also want, Mr脗聽President, to stress the importance of dialogue with national parliaments. They have participated in our work. They have contributed to the whole process. They have presented amendments, and some of their amendments have reached our report. Why? Because we think that this debate must not be confined to Brussels. It must live and breathe in all capitals and in our societies.

So, there are unavoidable changes and unavoidable reforms. If we really want a strong and more united Europe, we must do our homework.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Kos, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear honourable Members, I would like to thank the rapporteur Sandro Gozi and all the shadow rapporteurs for this report. We take good note of all the points in this report, which covers a wide array of recommendations on enlargement, institutional reforms and the budget, of course.

Now, more than ever, enlargement and a larger and stronger European Union 芒聙聯 I agree with you Sandro 芒聙聯 is a strategic imperative. The EU must widen as it deepens.

As you very well know, President von der Leyen's Political Guidelines 2024-2029 stressed that enlargement must be used as a catalyst for progress in terms of the EU's capacity to act, its policies and its spending programmes.

This report is very timely. The Commission adopted a first communication on the pre-enlargement policy reviews in March 2024, covering four strands: values, policy, budget and governance. The Commission plans to adopt the policy reviews on 4脗聽November, and what we have announced in the Political Guidelines of President von der Leyen and in the Commission work programme for 2025. So the date is now known.

The policy reviews should help to set out a clear path for advancing on the parallel tracks of enlargement and EU reforms. A few weeks ago, and from this very place during the State of the Union speech, President von der Leyen stated, and I quote, 'We need to move to qualified majority in some areas, for example in foreign policy. It is time to break free from the shackles of unanimity'.

The position of the Commission is long standing, consistent and well known. Unanimity no longer makes sense if the Union wants to play its role quickly, effectively and therefore strategically, especially in the geopolitical situation today.

The Commission will focus on what can already be done under the current Treaties and on those areas where a broad consensus is emerging. We share the view, also expressed in the report, that it is key to use the tools available under the current Treaty. For example, one element already outlined in the Commission's communication from 24脗聽March, which is also addressed in Mr脗聽Gozi's report, is the activation of the passerelle clauses.

Next to governance, the policy reviews will also cover strands on sectoral policies, values and budget. They should be seen as an important next step within the broader EU reform agenda, with several related already delivered, or planned around or after the reviews.

We look forward to continuing discussions with you, honourable Members, once it is adopted 芒聙聯 meaning on 4脗聽November there will be an enlargement package and, at the same time, this report. I am looking forward to hearing your views now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebasti脙拢o Bugalho, em nome do Grupo PPE. 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria Kos, esta 脙漏 a primeira vez nesta legislatura que o Parlamento toma a iniciativa sobre o alargamento.

脙聣 um sinal que o fa脙搂amos come脙搂ando por falar no que temos de mudar enquanto Uni脙拢o e n脙拢o apenas no que t脙陋m de mudar os que querem fazer parte dela. O debate sobre a ades脙拢o de novos Estados芒聙聭Membros resume芒聙聭se a duas quest脙碌es: se vai acontecer e se deve acontecer. A nossa resposta, enquanto partido, 脙漏 clara: sim a ambas.

O alargamento n脙拢o s脙鲁 vai acontecer, como j脙隆 est脙隆 a acontecer hoje, e n脙拢o s脙鲁 vai refor脙搂ar a Uni脙拢o Europeia no futuro, como j脙隆 脙漏 um contributo chave para o nosso desenvolvimento.

Na seguran脙搂a, quando aprofundamos a integra脙搂脙拢o gradual dos pa脙颅ses candidatos, n脙拢o s脙拢o s脙鲁 os pa脙颅ses candidatos que ficam mais seguros, 脙漏 a Uni脙拢o como um todo que fica menos vulner脙隆vel. N脙拢o 脙漏 por acaso que os inclu脙颅mos no Livro Branco para a defesa e na estrat脙漏gia de seguran脙搂a interna. Mais alargamento tamb脙漏m 脙漏 mais seguran脙搂a. Quando integramos os pa脙颅ses da ades脙拢o no mercado interno, n脙拢o 脙漏 s脙鲁 a sua economia que ganha oportunidades, 脙漏 a Uni脙拢o como um todo que ganha competitividade. Mais alargamento tamb脙漏m 脙漏 mais economia.

Madam Commissioner, in our journey towards an ever-closer Union, the courage to reform, including 芒聙聯 not imposing 芒聙聯 is adamant. In the last year, few have done it with such grace and commitment as you did. At the beginning of this term, integration required 150脗聽unanimous decisions. If we want enlargement to be taken seriously by our partners, we need to do something about it within ourselves.

In 2029, besides an Enlargement Commissioner, we can also have an enlarged Europe and a stronger one, too. For those who have visited the candidate countries, it is clear today: for them, enlargement is no more than an act of faith. They believed in it, but they don't think they'll ever see it. Tomorrow, we also vote on that, on showing every European that, yes, they can be citizens of this Union, too.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marc Angel, on behalf of the S&D Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear Commissioner, dear colleagues, all EU enlargements were not just a success story 芒聙聯 enlargement is a beacon of hope, a testament to what we can achieve when we stand strong together. Past enlargements have transformed our continent, bringing peace, prosperity and stability to millions. A new wave of enlargement is now a necessity, but with new members joining, we face real institutional challenges that demand bold actions.

At the current stage, to be able to absorb new members, we must move away from the paralysis of unanimity in the Council, because Europe cannot afford to be held hostage by a single veto. We should fully utilise the flexibilities already offered by our current Treaties to act faster and to act smarter. But this is not enough. We need the courage to adopt targeted Treaty changes and reflect the ambitions of our citizens of a Union ready for the future. The Council must not shy away from this conversation, and I regret its absence here today. Silencing the debate on Treaty change is not an act of caution, but a dangerous retreat from responsibility.

Dear colleagues, the time to act is now. Let's move beyond paralysis and division, ignite the spirit of solidarity and justice and build a stronger Europe. I thank the rapporteur, Sandro Gozi, for his excellent work, and we must work hard together, because we must, before the end of this mandate, make sure that those candidate countries who advanced quickly, who are doing many efforts, become members of this European Union.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marieke Ehlers, namens de PfE-Fractie. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, volgens sommigen is de uitbreiding van de Europese Unie een doel op zich. Voor hen is de ever closer Union per definitie ook een ever expanding Union, een groot wereldrijk dat voortdurend macht, dominantie en invloed moet vergroten om relevant te blijven. De PVV en de Fractie Patriotten voor Europa zien dat echter anders. De EU heeft duidelijke politieke, culturele en natuurlijke grenzen.

Want een ever expanding Union betekent ongetwijfeld ook meer netto-ontvangers en een steeds kleinere groep nettobetalers. Deze herverdelingsmachine verzwakt de EU als geheel, maar ook de afzonderlijke lidstaten. Brussel geeft een feestje en de Nederlandse burger mag opdraaien voor de kosten.

We mogen dus wel steeds meer betalen, maar als het van de eurofielen afhangt, hebben we ook steeds minder te zeggen. Want volgens dit verslag zou een Unie van dertig of meer lidstaten politiek vastlopen als elke lidstaat zijn vetorecht zou behouden, en daarom willen ze het vetorecht afschaffen.

Uitbreiding is geen vooruitgang als het ten koste gaat van doeltreffendheid, soevereiniteit en het gezond verstand. We moeten stoppen met het verzamelen van lidstaten alsof ze postzegels zijn. Zowel bij migratie, Europese regelgeving en uitbreiding van de Unie geldt hetzelfde principe: genoeg is genoeg en less is more.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Charlie Weimers, on behalf of the ECR Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, Swedish voters did not elect me to give away their votes to Brussels. Yet that is exactly what this report proposes; removing national veto powers and centralising decision-making far from the people.

Here is why: can you explain to Swedish voters why we should grant EU membership to a country that hosts terrorists, fuels organised crime and drives migration into Europe. Can you convince them that EU citizenship should be handed to 85脗聽million Muslims? Can you persuade Swedish taxpayers that they must pay even more into Brussels? Of course not 芒聙聯 that is why you want to take away Sweden's sovereignty. I was sent here to defend Swedish democracy, not to dismantle it, and that is why I vote no. Sweden first 芒聙聯 always.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Charles Goerens, au nom du groupe Renew. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, l'Europe, telle une voiture, dispose d'un frein et d'un moteur. Pour lui permettre de suivre sa voie, nous avons int脙漏r脙陋t 脙聽 ce que ces deux fonctions soient conduites avec pr脙漏voyance et d脙漏termination. Sandro Gozi nous a 脙漏voqu脙漏 le prix du non-fonctionnement et du recours excessif au frein. Maintenant, le propri脙漏taire de la voiture Europe s'appr脙陋te 脙聽 augmenter d'un tiers le nombre de ses passagers.

Quiconque a une faible id脙漏e de ce qui se passe dans le monde en 2025 doit admettre que ladite voiture ne r脙漏pond plus aux attentes d'une Europe puissance. Elle doit donc passer au garage, non pas pour l'aider 脙聽 mieux ralentir, car nombreux sont ceux qui veulent freiner des quatre fers, mais si nous voulons atteindre le r脙漏gime de croisi脙篓re, nous devrions doper son moteur. Les garagistes Letta et Draghi nous ont fait savoir que, si nous laissons inchang脙漏 le fonctionnement de la voiture Europe, tr脙篓s bient脙麓t, la Chine et les 脙聣tats-Unis ne nous verraient d脙漏j脙聽 plus dans leur r脙漏troviseur.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Reinier Van Lanschot, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President,脗聽dear colleagues, I've been to Montenegro, Georgia, Albania, Moldova and Ukraine. For the people there, the EU is freedom, prosperity and democracy. For them, the EU equals hope.

New countries joining is also good for our economy and for our security, and some of these countries are reforming incredibly fast. But the EU itself has stayed still.

Colleagues, the EU is the biggest diplomatic achievement in history, but does not work well enough today. Good ideas are blocked. We live in a 'vetocracy'. We can't wait to welcome new members, but enlargement has always happened with big changes to how we work together.

The EU at the moment has two big problems. Firstly, a lack of action. Secondly, a lack of democratic legitimacy. Let's fix that. Let's give the EU the power to act, and citizens the power to decide how the EU should act.

Europeans don't want to live in the ruins of a vetocracy. We want to live in a house of a united European democracy 芒聙聯 a house where we can welcome new countries and where we can all say, 'We are Europe'.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Anthony Smith, au nom du groupe The Left. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, Madame la Commissaire, nous le disons devant cet h脙漏micycle, l'脙漏largissement ne sera un succ脙篓s que s'il est au service des peuples et de l'am脙漏lioration des conditions de vie, et non uniquement vu comme un outil g脙漏opolitique au service de logiques guerri脙篓res qui sont 脙聽 des ann脙漏es-lumi脙篓res des aspirations des peuples d'Europe.

Mais votre Union europ脙漏enne se r脙漏sume, dans les faits, 脙聽 un ensemble de moyens permettant une concurrence libre et non fauss脙漏e, au service de la pr脙漏dation des grands groupes capitalistes et d'une vision politique imp脙漏rialiste. Un mod脙篓le dans lequel la question sociale est encore et toujours absente.

Pourtant, l'accueil de nouveaux 脙聣tats devrait se concevoir et se r脙漏aliser autour d'un socle commun ambitieux de droits sociaux et de garanties collectives pour les travailleuses et les travailleurs: n脙漏gociations collectives, hausse massive des salaires et des cong脙漏s pay脙漏s, renforcement des l脙漏gislations sociales, notamment en mati脙篓re de respect de la sant脙漏 et de la s脙漏curit脙漏 au travail, des moyens renforc脙漏s pour les inspections du travail, 脙漏galit脙漏 r脙漏elle entre les femmes et les hommes au travail.

Autant dire que nous ne cessons de nous 脙漏loigner de cet objectif, comme en atteste le cours r脙漏actionnaire de la Commission europ脙漏enne et de ses relais au sein du Parlement, qui s'appr脙陋tent 脙聽 enterrer le devoir de vigilance des multinationales. Accueillir de nouveaux 脙聣tats devrait aussi permettre de prot脙漏ger la mobilit脙漏 des travailleurs en mettant fin au statut du travail d脙漏tach脙漏, pour que les travailleurs b脙漏n脙漏ficient de l'int脙漏gralit脙漏 des droits sociaux du pays o脙鹿 ils sont employ脙漏s.

Voil脙聽 un moyen de mettre fin aux pratiques de dumping social comme fiscal, qui d脙漏truisent la vie de millions de salari脙漏s et de leurs familles. L'脙漏largissement devrait 脙陋tre un levier de construction d'un continent plus juste, o脙鹿 les droits sociaux et les contre-pouvoirs d脙漏mocratiques soient la r脙篓gle, et qui rejette la concurrence g脙漏n脙漏ralis脙漏e, le dumping et la corruption. Ce n'est pas le sens du rapport qui nous est aujourd'hui propos脙漏.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sven Simon (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, Europe once again stands at a historic crossroads and, colleague Stoyanov, of course the decision has a geopolitical meaning and, we all know, colleagues, such windows of opportunity open and they also close again. Too often we have opened them only to close them half-heartedly afterwards, and each time the disappointment in the Western Balkans has grown. This is about our credibility, our stability and our ability to act in an increasingly dangerous world.

When Europe hesitates, others fill the vacuum, but not with our values, rather with their interests. Enlargement is not an act of idealism. It is an act of European self-assertion, yet a Union that grows without institutional renewal grows into its own incapacity to act. We need structures that make decisions possible, and institutions can be elected and unelected. Because the ability to act without democratic legitimacy would be a pyrrhic victory.

Colleagues, we need more federal mechanisms in key policy areas where no Member State can act alone, but we also know the appetite for treaty change is limited in many capitals, if not all. That is why we must proceed wisely, step by step, with concrete reforms that create the ability to act without breaching centralism in Brussels. Those who want a larger Union tomorrow must have the courage to renew it today, and must seize this historic window before it is too late.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Reinier Van Lanschot (Verts/ALE), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Thank you, I'm very happy to hear you say that you want to increase democratic legitimacy, that you also want to create a possibility for Europeans to elect a government 芒聙聯 or what exactly should they elect and how can we also unelect them? I hear you talk about the goals you want to achieve with these reforms, but what would these reforms look like exactly for you to make that happen and achieve that goal of more democratic legitimacy?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sven Simon (PPE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 Yes, indeed, thank you very much for this question. I think that a mistake in the construction of the institutional setting with the Lisbon Treaty is that we have a political union, fortunately, now, and the problem here is that the European elections do not have any effect on the composition of the Commission 芒聙聯 that should be changed. At the moment, national governments nominate a commissioner. Okay 芒聙聯 the European Parliament can vote or not vote for the European Commission president, but we should have a government in certain policy fields with federal structures, which can be voted in and voted out of office. This is my concrete proposal. Thank you.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Juan Fernando L脙鲁pez Aguilar (S&D). 芒聙聯 La historia de la Uni脙鲁n Europea es la historia de su crecimiento, de sus ampliaciones y 芒聙聰por tanto芒聙聰 es un hecho que la ampliaci脙鲁n ha hecho a la Uni脙鲁n Europea m脙隆s fuerte y 芒聙聰adem脙隆s芒聙聰 muestra que todav脙颅a est脙隆 viva. Muestra que, a pesar de sus crisis, de sus par脙隆lisis y 芒聙聰a veces芒聙聰 de su impotencia, contin脙潞a siendo un polo de atracci脙鲁n por su combinaci脙鲁n virtuosa de derechos, libertades, correcci脙鲁n de desequilibrios y oportunidades de prosperidad.

Pero para que la ampliaci脙鲁n siga teniendo esa capacidad motriz, es imprescindible que se atiendan las lecciones de este informe de la Comisi脙鲁n Constitucional.

Primera lecci脙鲁n: los costes de la no ampliaci脙鲁n son insoportables porque la Uni脙鲁n Europea entra en contradicci脙鲁n consigo misma. Para que la ampliaci脙鲁n sea coherente, es imprescindible tambi脙漏n que se produzcan reformas institucionales al tiempo que supera la din脙隆mica de vetos cruzados y, por supuesto, la exigencia de unanimidades.

La segunda lecci脙鲁n es que es imprescindible que se utilicen las pasarelas y las cooperaciones reforzadas.

La tercera es que seamos capaces de elegir aquellas reformas de los Tratados que resulten imprescindibles para acoger a nuevos miembros. La clave est脙隆 en las modificaciones espec脙颅ficas, no en una reforma indiscriminada ni en entrar en la batidora de una gran convenci脙鲁n que haga perder el control de la situaci脙鲁n.

Hay que saber elegir cu脙隆les son exactamente los mensajes porque es imprescindible que la Uni脙鲁n Europea siga aportando recursos propios, reforme el marco financiero plurianual y 芒聙聰sobre todo芒聙聰 refuerce su compromiso con el Estado de Derecho, la democracia y los derechos fundamentales de modo que ninguna nueva ampliaci脙鲁n deteriore 芒聙聰que ya lo hemos visto芒聙聰 el est脙隆ndar europeo de respeto al Estado de Derecho, a la democracia, a los derechos fundamentales, a la promoci脙鲁n del pluralismo y a la protecci脙鲁n de minor脙颅as.

脗聽脗聽Ern脜聭 Schaller-Baross (PfE). 芒聙聯 Eln脙露k 脙職r! Az Uni脙鲁hoz val脙鲁 csatlakoz脙隆s nem politikai alkuk k脙漏rd脙漏se, hanem vil脙隆gos, 脙隆tl脙隆that脙鲁 felt脙漏telek ment脙漏n t脙露rt脙漏nik. Minden egyes tagjel脙露lt orsz脙隆gnak ugyanazokat a szab脙隆lyokat kell teljes脙颅tenie. Nem lehet orsz脙隆gokat 脙露sszekapcsolni, csomagban kezelni 脙漏s politikai nyom脙隆sgyakorl脙隆sokkal 脙隆tnyomni d脙露nt脙漏seket.脗聽Az Eur脙鲁pai Uni脙鲁 b脜聭v脙颅t脙漏se nem ideol脙鲁giai j脙隆tsz脙鲁t脙漏r, hanem s脙潞lyos k脙录lpolitikai 脙漏s strat脙漏giai d脙露nt脙漏s, amely mindannyiunk k脙露z脙露s j脙露v脜聭j脙漏t 脙漏rinti. 脙聣ppen ez脙漏rt vil脙隆gosan ki kell mondani: k脙录l脙录gyi k脙漏rd脙漏sekben az egyhang脙潞s脙隆g az alap, 脙漏s ezen nem kell, s脜聭t nem is szabad v脙隆ltoztatnunk. Ez a tag脙隆llamok szuverenit脙隆s脙隆nak utols脙鲁 igazi garanci脙隆ja.

脗聽脗聽Gheorghe Piperea (ECR). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, 脙庐nainte de a ne g脙垄ndi la o extindere a Uniunii Europene, este cazul s脛聝 rezolv脛聝m propriile probleme ale Uniunii, care devine treptat una centralizat脛聝, de tip sovietic, de neinvidiat. Corup脠聸ia a invadat Uniunea, libertatea de opinie 脠聶i de alegere a devenit subiect de fractur脛聝 a rela脠聸iei cu Statele Unite ale Americii, c脛聝ci nu mai este o valoare comun脛聝 a Vestului.

Devenim o alian脠聸脛聝 militarist脛聝 care-脠聶i propune s脛聝 concureze cu NATO 脠聶i un spa脠聸iu al s脛聝r脛聝cirii, al inegalit脛聝脠聸ii economice 脠聶i al 脙庐nvechirii tehnologice. Temele marginale ale birocra脠聸iei Uniunii Europene, cum ar fi identitatea de gen sau schimbarea climatic脛聝, ecraneaz脛聝 adev脛聝ratele probleme ale Uniunii. Iat脛聝 una: consumatorii europeni sunt victime ale dezinform脛聝rii, zilnic, 脠聶i ale agresivit脛聝脠聸ii comerciale, dar Uniunea European脛聝 arunc脛聝 resurse 脙庐n gaura neagr脛聝 a scutului democratic european pentru a-脠聶i proteja politrucii 脠聶i aparatul birocratic neales de nimeni.

Nu mai avem o carte de vizit脛聝 onorabil脛聝, cum ne-am putea permite s脛聝 le solicit脛聝m statelor ter脠聸e care accept脛聝 ideea de a adera c脙垄ndva la Uniunea European脛聝 s脛聝 脙庐脠聶i fac脛聝 temele 脙庐n privin脠聸a valorilor libert脛聝脠聸ii, 脙庐n privin脠聸a valorilor onestit脛聝脠聸ii 脙庐n privin脠聸a valorilor stabilit脛聝脠聸ii? Eu zic s脛聝 ne facem mai 脙庐nt脙垄i curat 脙庐n propria ograd脛聝, 脙庐nainte de a ne preg脛聝ti s脛聝 primim al脠聸i colocatari.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Joachim Streit (Renew). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Ich danke Sandro Gozi f脙录r seinen wirklich hervorragenden Bericht und darf folgende Anmerkungen hinzuf脙录gen: Die Idee, ein Initiativrecht f脙录r uns Abgeordnete einzuf脙录hren, f脙录r das Europ脙陇ische Parlament, ist unabdingbar. Denn nur das macht das freie Mandat eines Abgeordneten aus, dass er auch selbst Dinge ver脙陇ndern kann, wo er erkennt, dass Gesetze schlecht laufen. Das hei脙聼t, wir m脙录ssen das Recht haben, Gesetze aufzuheben oder selbst eigene einzubringen.

F脙录r mich wichtig als ehemaliger Landrat und B脙录rgermeister: das Konnexit脙陇tsprinzip 芒聙聯 wer bestellt, muss auch bezahlen. Das sage ich auch als Mitglied des Haushaltsausschusses. Und wenn die EU Gesetze erl脙陇sst, die vor Ort, in den Gemeinden, in den St脙陇dten Geld kosten, dann muss man auch dieses Geld den Gemeinden mitgeben.

Und der dritte Punkt: Einstimmigkeitsprinzip. Es kann nicht sein, dass Regierungschefs wie Orb脙隆n uns mit dem Einstimmigkeitsprinzip am Ring in der Arena herumf脙录hren und sich dann ihre Stimme auch noch mit Geld abkaufen lassen. Deshalb ist es ganz, ganz wichtig, dass wir hier zum Mehrheitsprinzip kommen, mit Ausnahme der EU-Erweiterung: Dann sollten alle Mitgliedstaaten zustimmen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Vladimir Prebili脛聧 (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Gospod predsednik! 脛聦e mislimo resno z naslednjo 脜隆iritvijo, moramo na脜隆im partnerjem omogo脛聧iti aktivno sodelovanje v tem procesu. Kot nacionalni zakonodajalci morajo prevzeti odgovornost za implementacijo evropskega pravnega reda v dr脜戮avah kandidatkah.

To pomeni, da moramo 脜戮e danes v tem parlamentu na脜隆im kolegicam in kolegom z Zahodnega Balkana omogo脛聧iti status opazovalcev. 脛聦etudi z majhnimi koraki, je prav odpirati tudi dele na脜隆ega enotnega trga. Samo dvig blagostanja v dr脜戮avah kandidatkah bo dokaz, da Evropska unija vanje verjame.

Seveda se strinjamo, da ne smemo spu脜隆脛聧ati ve脛聧 novih Orbanov v Evropsko unijo, ne moremo pa ostati rigidni v postopku 脜隆iritve. Do leta 2030 mora Zahodni Balkan sedeti z nami za isto mizo, tudi 脛聧e sprva brez polnih glasovalnih pravic.

Poglejte, kaj je prinesla 脜隆iritev za srednjo in vzhodno Evropo. Evropa ni oslabela, postala je mo脛聧nej脜隆a. 脜聽iritev ni tveganje, je dokon脛聧anje na脜隆e Unije. Poka脜戮imo, da smo pripravljeni ne le odgovoriti o prihodnosti, ampak da jo tudi soustvarjamo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hans Neuhoff (ESN). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Wer die EU zu einem Zentralstaat machen m脙露chte, der sollte das den Unionsb脙录rgern auch so sagen. Wer die Hand an das Einstimmigkeitsprinzip legt脗聽芒聙聯 und genau das tut der vorliegende Bericht脗聽芒聙聯, legt die Hand an die Souver脙陇nit脙陇t der Mitgliedstaaten. Sie behaupten, dass nach Artikel脗聽49 ein 脙聹bergang zur Beschlussfassung mit qualifizierter Mehrheit auch ohne 脙聞nderung der Vertr脙陇ge m脙露glich sei. Hier sollen wir offenbar 脙录bert脙露lpelt werden, denn Passerelle steht in Artikel 48 Absatz 7 und gilt nicht f脙录r die Bestimmungen 脙录ber Mitgliedschaft und Beitritt nach Artikel 49 EUV.

Sie schlagen vor, die Einstimmigkeit bei Verfahren zum Schutz der EU-Werte abzuschaffen und den Gerichtshof zur Schiedsstelle f脙录r Verst脙露脙聼e zu machen脗聽芒聙聯 ausgerechnet den Gerichtshof, den Verfechter von Unionsmacht par excellence. Mit solchen Forderungen soll nicht Demokratie bef脙露rdert, sondern das Abstrafen von Staaten leicht gemacht werden.

Kolleginnen und Kollegen, die EU ist bereits 脙录berdehnt und leidet an den l脙陇hmenden Folgen dieser 脙聹berdehnung. H脙露ren Sie auf damit, Beitrittsverhandlungen zum Vorwand f脙录r die Aush脙露hlung staatlicher Souver脙陇nit脙陇t zu machen. Beherrschen Sie Ihren Erweiterungstrieb und kehren Sie zur脙录ck zur Ursprungsidee einer Zweckgemeinschaft, in der kein Mitglied gegen seine grundlegenden Interessen 脙录berstimmt werden kann.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fidias Panayiotou (NI). 芒聙聯 Mr President, the European Union is becoming bigger, and I think, in general, this is good news. In a world with big powers like United States, China, Russia and India, our small European countries must stay united to survive, develop and compete. But, of course, more countries joining the EU will mean slower decision-making.

Let's be honest, our bureaucracy and decision-making are huge and slow, so that we can't take decisions as fast as other world powers. Even our 27 leaders cannot manage to agree on important topics. How will we manage when we are 30 or 40 countries in the EU?

I believe that for the EU not to become even slower, we first need to reform it from the inside, but before more countries join the club. So this way we'll be able to compete and stay relevant in the most important battles, such as artificial intelligence. Thank you so much, I love you all.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lor脙隆nt Vincze (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, dear colleagues, as someone coming from central and eastern Europe, I can say with conviction that enlargement is not only a policy, but a promise fulfilled. EU integration transformed my country. It brought prosperity, democracy and, yes, a lot of common challenges to face. Today, we must ensure that the next wave of enlargement brings the same success, for both the candidates and the Union itself.

That is why I believe we should approach the next enlargement with confidence, not fear. Experience shows that we do not need a fundamental change of our institutional system. What we need is the political will to make existing mechanisms work better, to simplify, not to reinvent. Of course, decision-making can be improved, but efficiency cannot come at the expense of democracy. Smaller and medium-sized Member States, those at Europe's geographic and cultural frontiers, must continue to have a meaningful voice. The European Union is strong precisely because it balances unity with diversity, efficiency with equality.

On behalf of the EPP Group, I thank all those involved in the preparation of this report. Its content shows us that it is possible to adapt the institutions and their processes to new realities. To put it bluntly, when there is a positive decision on new countries joining the EU, the Treaty and our institutions are ready to work on them. We need to focus on what has always made enlargement a success: merit, fairness and respect among partners.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gabriele Bischoff (S&D). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Das ist doch mal eine interessante Debatte, die sich wirklich auf das Fundament Europas konzentriert: Erweiterung und Vertiefung der Europ脙陇ischen Union. Und wir konnten in dieser Debatte sehen, dass die gesamte Rechte 芒聙聯 von EKR bis nach ganz rechts脗聽芒聙聯 das Fundament dieser Europ脙陇ischen Union zerst脙露ren will. Und wir versuchen 芒聙聯 und da danke ich dem Berichterstatter und allen Schattenberichterstattern 芒聙聯, wir versuchen wirklich, gemeinsam in der Mitte dieses Hauses deutlich zu machen, dass sich die EU nat脙录rlich auch reformieren muss, wenn sie die Mitgliedstaaten aufnehmen will, denen sie es lange versprochen hat und die wirklich enorme Anstrengungen unternehmen, um auch bereit zu sein f脙录r die Erweiterung.

Genauso muss die Europ脙陇ische Union sich reformieren und bereit sein. Ob sie nun die Spielr脙陇ume in den Vertr脙陇gen nutzt oder Vertrags脙陇nderungen macht 芒聙聯 ohne diese Ver脙陇nderungen wird die Europ脙陇ische Union in der Bedeutungslosigkeit verschwinden. Denn wir werden entweder st脙陇rker, mehr und handlungsf脙陇higer, oder wir werden in der globalisierten Welt keine Rolle mehr spielen.

(Die Rednerin ist damit einverstanden, auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹 zu antworten.)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rihards Kols (ECR), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Madam Bischoff, some of your words were really deeply insulting. You referred to the ECR as fundamentally destroying the foundations of the EU. Could you please elaborate on this loud statement and give concrete examples of where the ECR is shattering the fundamental principles of the EU? Concrete 芒聙聯 not just some Polish sentences 芒聙聯 concrete cases!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gabriele Bischoff (S&D), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 You just need to listen to your speakers that spoke about the European Union as the Soviet Union here, etc. If Europe is not able to reform and is not able to enlarge here 芒聙聯 this is the foundation of our Union we are talking about.

If you have a different opinion, please convince me that there are also ECR colleagues that have a different position and are standing in the middle of the House. I would appreciate that.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fabrice Leggeri (PfE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, les discussions d'脙漏largissement men脙漏es avec l'Ukraine, la Moldavie, la G脙漏orgie, les pays des Balkans occidentaux et la Turquie traduisent une fuite en avant de la Commission europ脙漏enne. Toutes n'en sont pas au m脙陋me stade, certaines sont 脙聽 l'arr脙陋t, d'autres suspendues au respect de crit脙篓res que beaucoup ne remplissent pas. Mais tous ces 脙漏largissements, s'ils devaient aboutir, bouleverseraient en profondeur l'脙漏quilibre politique, en particulier au Conseil, mais aussi institutionnel et budg脙漏taire de l'Union europ脙漏enne.

Accueillir des pays fragiles sur le plan 脙漏conomique ou institutionnel, c'est redistribuer les si脙篓ges, revoir les r脙篓gles de vote et alourdir consid脙漏rablement la charge des 脙聣tats contributeurs, au second rang desquels la France, qui injecte 23脗聽milliards d'euros au budget europ脙漏en en 2025.

Derri脙篓re cet 脙漏largissement, pr脙漏sent脙漏 comme une n脙漏cessit脙漏 morale, la Commission poursuit un objectif politique: centraliser le pouvoir, affaiblir la voix des nations et diluer leur souverainet脙漏 dans un ensemble sans l脙漏gitimit脙漏 populaire. Or, comme l'脙漏crivait Stefan脗聽Zweig, quand les peuples cessent de croire 脙聽 leur propre destin, d'autres d脙漏cident 脙聽 leur place. C'est cela que nous refusons. L'脙漏largissement ne doit pas se faire au prix de la souverainet脙漏.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Petras Au脜隆trevi脛聧ius (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President,脗聽Commissioner, colleagues, significant geopolitical shifts and security threats in Europe and around the world must be harnessed to unify Europe, strengthen the European project and bolster the European Union's standing as a geopolitical actor.

Our shared vision for Europe encompasses political unity, securing of the continent, economic growth and a competitive single market. This requires a willingness and readiness to enlarge, and the determination to implement internal EU reforms.

Europe can no longer afford to delay making the necessary decisions. We must identify the optimal time to implement needed internal reforms, so that, by integrating new members, we can collectively become stronger and more effective.

This year must see the start of accession negotiations with Ukraine and the Republic of Moldova, turning our promises into action. This will practically illustrate our response to building a secure and united Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Daniel Freund (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Die europ脙陇ische Einigung, die Europ脙陇ische Union ist eine unfassbare Erfolgsgeschichte. Und ich glaube, dass sie vor allen Dingen auf zwei Prinzipien beruht: einer immer engeren Union脗聽芒聙聯脗聽der weiteren Integration脗聽芒聙聯, aber eben auch der Erweiterung 芒聙聯 dass L脙陇nder, die noch vor Kurzem unter Diktatur, unter Sowjetherrschaft gelebt haben, jetzt als starke Demokratien hier Teil dieses Europ脙陇ischen Parlaments, der europ脙陇ischen Institutionen sind.

Diesen Weg, den wollen wir weiter beschreiten, denn er macht uns st脙陇rker, er macht uns reicher, er macht uns alle gemeinsam demokratischer. Dass wir Konflikte heute hier und in Br脙录ssel in Verhandlungen und eben nicht mehr im Sch脙录tzengraben kl脙陇ren 芒聙聯 was f脙录r ein Erfolg!

Und wenn ich mir angucke, was die CDU, was die Konservativen dazu noch vor wenigen Jahren gesagt haben: Wir wollen die europ脙陇ische Einigung nicht nur festhalten, wir wollen voran auf dem Weg zum europ脙陇ischen Bundesstaat. Und jetzt sagt Friedrich Merz: Ich habe nie zu denen geh脙露rt, die das Wort der Vereinigten Staaten von Europa wollten, und ich habe selbst dazu beigetragen, dass es aus der Programmatik der CDU gestrichen wurde. Wie sollen wir so weiter Europa stark machen? Das ist der falsche Weg. Wir m脙录ssen in der demokratischen, proeurop脙陇ischen Mitte f脙录r Vertrags脙陇nderungen, f脙录r ein engeres Europa k脙陇mpfen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jan-Peter Warnke (NI). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, Frau Kommissarin! Zun脙陇chst m脙露chte ich Herrn Gozi f脙录r seinen Bericht danken. Der Bericht analysiert den verfassungsm脙陇脙聼igen Status der EU und zieht dann Schlussfolgerungen f脙录r die Struktur im Falle einer Erweiterung auf mehr als 30 Mitglieder.

W脙陇hrend ich diese Analyse teile, habe ich doch Bedenken hinsichtlich des Kurses der EU und der脗聽vorgeschlagenen Verfassungs脙陇nderungen. Der Bericht offenbart die schwere Verfassungskrise, in der sich die EU schon jetzt befindet. Denn eine Verfassung ist ja nie wirklich zustande gekommen. Wir handeln jetzt nach m脙录hselig verhandelten Lissabon-Vertr脙陇gen.

Wir m脙录ssen unsere B脙录rger fragen, wie viel Nationalstaat wir behalten und abgeben wollen. Und 脙录ber Friedensf脙陇higkeit oder Kriegst脙录chtigkeit m脙录ssen die B脙录rger in einem Alter bis 35 Jahre entscheiden, denn sie verbluten auf den k脙录nftigen Schlachtfeldern und sind meistens nicht in diesem Parlament vertreten. Einer europ脙陇ischen R脙录stungsindustrie stimme ich zu, wenn diese sich in gesellschaftlichem Eigentum befindet und sich einer Kontrolle dieser unterzieht.

Ich denke, im gegenw脙陇rtigen Zustand ist die EU nicht erweiterungsf脙陇hig.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Salvatore De Meo (PPE). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signora Commissaria, l'allargamento dell'Unione oggi 脙篓 un impegno storico ed un investimento strategico nel futuro del nostro continente. 脙聢 una scelta di responsabilit脙聽 verso i paesi che guardano all'Europa come una casa di libert脙聽 e democrazia ma, allo stesso tempo, 脙篓 anche una sfida che impone all'Unione di essere in grado di decidere, di agire e di difendere i propri valori e interessi nel mondo.

脙聢 chiaro che un'Unione pi脙鹿 grande non pu脙虏 continuare a funzionare con regole pensate per un'Europa a 27: serve un processo decisionale all'altezza delle sfide di oggi, un processo pi脙鹿 rapido e pi脙鹿 trasparente, capace di rispondere ai cittadini superando quei meccanismi che oggi limitano la nostra azione.

Questa relazione va di pari passo con la risoluzione del Parlamento del novembre 2023, dove 脙篓 stato detto chiaramente: 脙篓 tempo di riformare i trattati e di superare quelle regole che, troppo spesso, hanno paralizzato le decisioni europee e privato l'Unione della capacit脙聽 di reagire alle crisi in tempi rapidi.

Forse 脙篓 arrivato il momento che anche il Consiglio, purtroppo oggi assente, prenda atto di questa decisione e apra una fase costituente. La stessa Presidente von der Leyen, nel suo discorso sullo stato dell'Unione, ha sottolineato l'urgenza di superare l'unanimit脙聽 in alcune questioni. Serve un'Unione forte con un Parlamento dotato di piena iniziativa legislativa e con competenze rafforzate nelle politiche strategiche.

L'Europa deve rafforzare la sua autorevolezza e credibilit脙聽. C'脙篓 bisogno di una sola figura per la Commissione e per il Consiglio, una sola direzione politica. Solo cos脙卢 potremo avere un'unione coerente e determinata. Facciamo dell'allargamento l'occasione per ritrovare il coraggio di riformare i trattati, per un'Europa pi脙鹿 coesa e capace di contare davvero sulla scena internazionale.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Thijs Reuten (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear colleagues, 15 to 20脗聽years 芒聙聯 that's how long some accession countries are in the waiting room. Citizens in and outside the EU become sceptical about our intentions.

We need to restore the credibility of enlargement. It's a geopolitical necessity and it makes our Union stronger. It does not mean abandoning merit-based processes. It means we should invest efforts where it's merited.

We also need to change our paralysis by design. Hence I welcome the proposal of Ant脙鲁nio Costa: interim benchmarks should not require unanimity. Thank you for your strong stance.

Yes, we need reforms. But even without new members, we must adapt to new realities and move towards a more federalist Union, at a minimum on foreign affairs and defence.

Let's not use our flaws as an excuse not to live up to the promise of European unification and building that stronger Union.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Juan Carlos Girauta Vidal (PfE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, no nos preocupa tanto la ampliaci脙鲁n como los procedimientos de paulatino borrado de la soberan脙颅a con que quieren llevarla a cabo.

Primero nos dicen que quieren m脙隆s Europa. Luego descubrimos que eso significa menos soberan脙颅a, menos voz y menos libertad para las naciones. Lo llaman eficiencia, pero en realidad es miedo. Ustedes tienen miedo a los Gobiernos que no aceptan su agenda, miedo a los partidos patriotas que crecen en toda Europa y devuelven el poder a las naciones.

Maquillan este golpe a la independencia de las naciones 芒聙聰脙潞nica base de soberan脙颅a, 脙潞nica base de legitimidad芒聙聰 intentando nuevos foros como el A脤聛gora Europa, una especie de teatro participativo donde los ciudadanos podr脙隆n hablar. 脗驴Cu脙隆ntos se reunir脙隆n? 脗驴Trescientos millones? 脗驴Cuatrocientos millones? Parece que se han olvidado de que la verdadera 脙隆gora ya existe: se llaman elecciones.

Hay ventajas de la unanimidad que quisiera destacar: con la unanimidad se evita que una mayor脙颅a circunstancial imponga su voluntad a todos. Es la 脙潞ltima garant脙颅a de respeto a nuestra soberan脙颅a.

脗聽脗聽Dan Barna (Renew). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, dac脛聝 exist脛聝 o misiune fundamental脛聝 pentru Uniunea European脛聝, aceasta este unificarea continentului nostru. Dar pentru a primi noi membri trebuie mai 脙庐nt脙垄i s脛聝 ne facem ordine 脙庐n propria cas脛聝, s脛聝 facem renovare a脠聶a cum e necesar 脙庐n orice cas脛聝 dup脛聝 ni脠聶te zeci de ani.

Nu putem construi viitorul Europei cu uneltele trecutului. O uniune extins脛聝 cu peste 30 de state membre pur 脠聶i simplu nu 脙庐脠聶i poate permite s脛聝 fie paralizat脛聝 de dreptul de veto al unui singur guvern, iar procesul de extindere nu poate cere unanimitate la fiecare pas tehnic. Costul lipsei de ac脠聸iune este prea mare 脠聶i prea riscant.

De aceea, prin acest raport pentru care 脙庐l felicit pe colegul Sandro Gozi, propunem o cale c脛聝tre o Uniune European脛聝 mai eficient脛聝, mai democratic脛聝 脠聶i mai puternic脛聝, una capabil脛聝 s脛聝 ac脠聸ioneze mai rapid, s脛聝 vorbeasc脛聝 pe o singur脛聝 voce 脠聶i s脛聝-脠聶i capaciteze cet脛聝脠聸enii.

Extinderea necesit脛聝 reform脛聝 din partea statelor candidate precum Moldova, Ucraina sau statele din Balcanii de Vest, dar 脠聶i din partea noastr脛聝. Prin reformarea institu脠聸iilor 脠聶i procedurilor Uniunii ne preg脛聝tim pentru provoc脛聝rile 脠聶i oportunit脛聝脠聸ile secolului XXI.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tineke Strik (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, thank you, Commissioner, for being here. The foundation of our common project is the shared commitment to values, democracy and the rule of law. The requirements to become an EU Member State are high; we are asking candidate countries to implement significant changes, and rightfully so.

But we have to do our homework ourselves as well. We cannot allow enlargement to be obstructed by individual Member States just because of bilateral conflicts or interests. So it is crucial to remove national vetoes of all the internal steps towards enlargement, as Council President Costa also proposed. But we should also actively support candidate countries during the accession process by supporting their reform agenda on government level, but also by stepping up our support to civil society, free media and other actors promoting the enlargement agenda, sometimes against the atmosphere that is in their countries. So we should reward those countries meeting demands and punish those backsliding. If we are serious about renewed commitment to enlargement, please let us show it.

脗聽脗聽Vasile D脙庐ncu (S&D). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, stima脠聸i colegi, raportul dlui Sandro Gozi este un exemplu de proiect ra脠聸ional 脠聶i curajos 脙庐n acela脠聶i timp. Uniunea European脛聝 cu adev脛聝rat se afl脛聝 脙庐n fa脠聸a unui moment decisiv.脗聽Extinderea a fost privit脛聝 ca o promisiune politic脛聝, dar este un test al capacit脛聝脠聸ii noastre institu脠聸ionale. O Uniune extins脛聝 trebuie s脛聝 r脛聝m脙垄n脛聝 capabil脛聝 s脛聝 decid脛聝 rapid, s脛聝 protejeze valorile democratice 脠聶i s脛聝 ac脠聸ioneze unitar pe scena global脛聝. Extinderea 脠聶i reforma trebuie s脛聝 mearg脛聝 脙庐mpreun脛聝. Numai a脠聶a vom avea o Europ脛聝 mai puternic脛聝 脠聶i mai credibil脛聝.

脗聽脗聽Adrian-George Axinia (ECR). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, raportul Gozi ar trebui predat la cursurile de logic脛聝 din 脙庐ntreaga Europ脛聝. Este exemplul perfect de premise corecte 脠聶i concluzii halucinante, care nu au cum s脛聝 decurg脛聝 din asump脠聸iile ini脠聸iale.

脙聨n loc s脛聝 discute despre cum s脛聝 facem s脛聝 ajut脛聝m statele candidate s脛聝 脙庐ndeplineasc脛聝 mai repede condi脠聸iile pentru aderarea la UE, raportul recomand脛聝 ni脠聶te abera脠聸ii, ca de exemplu eliminarea veto-urilor statelor membre, ceea ce echivaleaz脛聝 cu dorin脠聸a unui sistem autocratic. Eu, ca europarlamentar rom脙垄n, sus脠聸in extinderea Uniunii Europene. Sunt state care au 脙庐nceput acest proces, au f脛聝cut reforme 脠聶i sacrificii 脠聶i merit脛聝 s脛聝 fie al脛聝turi de noi. Nu sunt de acord s脛聝 existe cuplaje, a脠聶a cum se 脙庐nt脙垄mpl脛聝 cu Ucraina 脠聶i Republica Moldova. Cred c脛聝 fiecare stat trebuie evaluat individual, abordarea fiind mai eficient脛聝 dec脙垄t una pe valuri. 脗聽脗聽脜陆eljana Zovko (PPE). 芒聙聯 Dear President, Madam Commissioner, Russian aggression against Ukraine, as well as the end of the wars in the western Balkans, has made us aware that the only true tool for bringing peace and security to the borders of the European Union is the enlargement process.

The western Balkan countries have been dragged through the labyrinth of bureaucratic procedures of the European Union and the philosophy of whether we can accept more countries in our EU family or whether we must first change the rules of decision making. So this report goes along this line as well, dear Mr Gozi, although I really appreciate your role in bringing Croatia to the table as well at a time when no one wanted to discuss these issue. But it's not the time to discuss treaty changes.

Countries that have rejected the hegemonic approach of the big brothers in the unions of the past, those who escaped Russian hegemony in the Soviet Union, or Serbian dominance in former Yugoslavia, will never accept being placed in an unequal position in the decision-making process of further enlargement. That's the philosophy of further enlargement 芒聙聯 we all have to be equal. The countries that are entering this Union are entering under the condition that they will be equal. So it's not about mercy, it's about the honesty to admit whether or not we want the further enlargement process.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Brando Benifei (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, l'allargamento dell'Unione europea 脙篓 una precisa scelta politica.

Oggi i processi di adesione hanno ripreso slancio ma se vogliamo accogliere nuovi membri non possiamo solamente aggiungere delle sedie al tavolo: prima di allargarsi, l'Unione ha bisogno di riforme istituzionali profonde per un processo decisionale pi脙鹿 efficace, semplificato e mirato. Bisogna andare di pari passo.

Anche il rapporto Draghi, spesso evocato in queste aule, lo dice chiaramente: serve modificare i trattati per superare il vincolo dell'unanimit脙聽. E se non si pu脙虏 farlo subito, usiamo strumenti che gi脙聽 abbiamo, come le clausole passerella e le cooperazioni rafforzate. Ma dico di pi脙鹿: chi 脙篓 pronto a fare di pi脙鹿 insieme, lo faccia, senza paura di un'integrazione differenziata.

Serve un lavoro serio, perch脙漏 ogni nuovo ingresso non diluisca l'unit脙聽 dell'Europa ma la renda pi脙鹿 unita, pi脙鹿 forte, pi脙鹿 vicina ai suoi cittadini. Possiamo farcela, lavorando insieme con impegno e determinazione.

脗聽脗聽Ant脙鲁nio T脙垄nger Corr脙陋a (PfE). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, n脙鲁s somos a favor do alargamento, obviamente, principalmente naquilo que se relaciona com os pa脙颅ses dos Balc脙拢s Ocidentais, porque h脙隆 um buraco negro, que est脙隆 ali no meio da Europa, que 脙漏 fundamental preencher.

No entanto, o alargamento n脙拢o deve ser um pretexto para destruir a unanimidade, esmagar os pa脙颅ses pequenos ou diluir a soberania.

E, j脙隆 agora, queria dizer芒聙聭vos: cuidado com as declara脙搂脙碌es do Sr. Ant脙鲁nio Costa, porque ele destruiu Portugal e eventualmente poder脙隆 vir a destruir a Uni脙拢o Europeia tamb脙漏m.

Neste caso, concretamente, n脙鲁s afirmamos sempre a preced脙陋ncia dos Estados sobre Bruxelas. E mais, n脙拢o s脙拢o as institui脙搂脙碌es que est脙拢o desatualizadas, 脙漏 a burocracia que complica a vida das institui脙搂脙碌es em Bruxelas. S脙拢o os burocratas a mais, a burocracia a mais, e n脙拢o a parte pol脙颅tica, que prejudicam as institui脙搂脙碌es europeias. 脙聣 a m脙隆quina burocr脙隆tica, esse monstro que foi criado ao longo dos anos.

脗聽脗聽Ond脜聶ej Krut脙颅lek (ECR). 芒聙聯 Pane p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, pan脙颅 komisa脜聶ko, roz脜隆脙颅脜聶en脙颅 nen脙颅 jenom n脙隆脜隆 vnit脜聶n脙颅 probl脙漏m, ale m脙隆 i geopolitick脙陆 rozm脛聸r. 脗聽脗聽Csaba D脙露m脙露t脙露r (PfE). 芒聙聯 Eln脙露k 脙職r! V脙漏gre itt van egy vita, amelyen a b脜聭v脙颅t脙漏s k脙露vetkezm脙漏nyeir脜聭l eshet sz脙鲁. Az脙漏rt kell ezt megbecs脙录ln脙录nk, mert az elm脙潞lt h脙鲁napokban az Eur脙鲁pai Bizotts脙隆g 脙潞gy haladt el脜聭re turb脙鲁 temp脙鲁ban az ukr脙隆n b脜聭v脙颅t脙漏ssel, hogy az alapvet脜聭 k脙漏rd脙漏sekre sem adtak v脙隆laszt. Mi脙漏rt kell 20%-kal cs脙露kkenteni az agr脙隆rt脙隆mogat脙隆sokat, hogy azt a b脜聭v脙颅t脙漏si 脙漏s a h脙隆bor脙潞s politik脙隆ra k脙露lts脙漏k?

Vagy p脙漏ld脙隆ul abba bele se megyek, hogy a biztos asszony szerint teljesen rendben vannak a kisebbs脙漏gi jogok Ukrajn脙隆ban, mik脙露zben ennek az ellenkez脜聭je igaz, a nyelvhaszn脙隆lati jogokat korl脙隆tozz脙隆k. 脗聽脗聽Rihards Kols (ECR). 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues, imagine being Moldovan: reforming, resisting Russian pressure and watching the door to the EU stay shut. Or being Ukrainian: bleeding on the battlefield for European values while the Union debates 'absorption capacity'.

Many in this Chamber do not remember what EU accession actually means. I do. I remember Latvia's path clearly 芒聙聯 it was demanding. We reformed, we adapted, we compromised, because there was a goal within reach. And because it was credible, it was empowering. We believed in Europe because Europe honoured its word.

That is what is now at stake, not just institutional capacity, but the very credibility of the Union's work. A candidate country can do everything right and still be blocked, not because of its failures, but because of ours. If institutional reform is needed, then do it 芒聙聯 not to delay enlargement, but to enable it. Reforms should serve enlargement, not the other way around. Enlargement isn't about sending a message, it's about shaping reality.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kristian Vigenin (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, Commissioner, colleagues, this report reminds us that Europe has both the responsibility and the capacity to continue its enlargement with those partners who have already met the criteria.

Enlargement and institutional reform must advance together 芒聙聯 not as competing goals, but as mutually reinforcing processes. The prospect of EU membership remains one of the strongest engines for transformation, stability and reconciliation across our continent.

Completing the Union towards the south-eastern and eastern edges is therefore not only a matter of geography, but of fulfilling the European project's promise of unity and stability.

Our Union's institutions are already capable of welcoming the enlargement frontrunners, provided we continue to strengthen their efficiency, transparency and legitimacy.

The ongoing reflection on institutional reform is not a precondition to enlargement, but a necessary preparation, ensuring that, when new members join, our decision-making remains effective and our values intact.

Europe's strength lies in its ability to adapt without hesitation and expand without division. Let us therefore move forward with confidence.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alexandre Varaut (PfE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, il est tr脙篓s fr脙漏quent, au sein de l'Union europ脙漏enne, de voter des rapports dont le titre est s脙漏duisant, mais dont le contenu l'est moins. Aujourd'hui, nous d脙漏battons des changements que provoquerait, dans le fonctionnement des institutions, l'entr脙漏e de nouveaux pays dans l'Union europ脙漏enne.

Le Rassemblement national s'oppose 脙聽 l'脙漏largissement syst脙漏matique. Il s'y oppose d'autant plus que les dix pays candidats souffrent d'importantes carences de d脙漏veloppement 脙漏conomique, de culture d脙漏mocratique et de stabilit脙漏 politique. Le rapport, d'ailleurs, en fait lui-m脙陋me l'aveu. Cependant, nous pouvions, 脙聽 titre d'hypoth脙篓se, chercher 脙聽 imaginer quelles seraient les cons脙漏quences de l'adh脙漏sion de ces pays sur le fonctionnement de l'Union.

Ce n'est malheureusement pas ce que fait ce rapport, qui consid脙篓re l'adh脙漏sion de ces pays comme une certitude et qui se consacre, d脙篓s lors, pour l'essentiel, 脙聽 identifier comment l'Union europ脙漏enne peut en tirer pr脙漏texte pour accaparer toute une s脙漏rie de nouvelles comp脙漏tences. Tout y passe: l'Europe de la d脙漏fense, fin de l'unanimit脙漏 des 脙聣tats au Conseil, ressources propres europ脙漏ennes, c'est-脙聽-dire imp脙麓t europ脙漏en, et bien s脙禄r r脙漏vision des trait脙漏s vers encore plus de f脙漏d脙漏ralisme, presque dans le meilleur des cas, et pourquoi pas un 脙聣tat unique?

L'脙漏largissement n'est ici que le cheval de Troie de l'autoritarisme europ脙漏en. Nous esp脙漏rions que les le脙搂ons des 脙漏lections europ脙漏ennes de 2024 auraient 脙漏t脙漏 tir脙漏es. Il semble que l'Union europ脙漏enne veuille rester aveugle, ne pas entendre la rupture voulue par les peuples. Le Rassemblement national, d脙漏sormais pl脙漏biscit脙漏, portera leur voix.

(L'orateur accepte une question carton bleu)

脗聽脗聽Alexandre Varaut (PfE), r脙漏ponse carton bleu. 芒聙聯 Cher coll脙篓gue, peut-脙陋tre n'avons-nous pas la m脙陋me opinion de ce qui est bon pour la France. Et peut-脙陋tre suis-je mieux plac脙漏 que vous pour juger de mon pays.

脗聽脗聽Sun脛聧ana Glavak (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovani predsjedavaju脛聡i, po脜隆tovana povjerenice, kolegice i kolege, pro脜隆irenje Europske unije nije tek administrativni proces niti skup tehni脛聧kih poglavlja, to je politi脛聧ki 脛聧in,脗聽izraz na脜隆e vizije i na脜隆e odgovornosti, ali i odgovornosti dr脜戮ava 脛聧lanica koje bi 脜戮eljele pristupiti Europskoj uniji.

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Russian troops have been halted on Ukrainian soil. They have not stopped because they were following orders from Putin. Behind Ukraine lies Moldova 芒聙聯 Putin's next target. Russia is already conducting aggressive propaganda and other forms of interference in Moldova. Does this sound familiar? Ukraine faced the same onslaught before Russian tanks rolled in. Why? Because Putin wants a more Russian Europe.

We can prevent this by supporting countries like Moldova on their European path. I want to see a more European Europe 芒聙聯 Europe without Russian propaganda, without Russian interference and without Russian tanks.

脗聽脗聽Se脙隆n Kelly (PPE). 芒聙聯 A Uachtar脙隆in, I've always been in favour of enlargement. I think when you join a club, you shouldn't close the door behind you, lock it and keep everyone else out. My country has benefited hugely from joining the European Union way back in 1973.

Then, of course, we had Brexit. I always felt that the best answer to Brexit is enlargement, and there are many countries that want to join the European Union rather than leave it. But joining also has responsibilities, and I think that is one of the key aspects that we need to emphasise 芒聙聯 learning from the obnoxious behaviour of Viktor Orb脙隆n in Hungary, utilising the benefits of the European Union, then making himself popular at home by criticising everything the European Union does and voting against anything the European Union wants.

So Ursula von der Leyen is right when she says we need to shake off the shackles of unanimity. This is something we should do on a gradual basis 芒聙聯 not in everything, because there will be some areas that will be very sensitive for Member States, but certainly you cannot have a situation where Orb脙隆n has abused the situation. So we need to look at that.

脗聽脗聽Matej Tonin (PPE). 芒聙聯 Gospod predsednik! Kak脜隆ne so posledice 脜隆iritve za obstoje脛聧e 脛聧lanice in za nove? Pozitivne v vsakem primeru in je resni脛聧no neverjetno, koliko 脛聧asa se porabi za razpravo o tem, kak脜隆ne bodo posledice. Vsekakor pozitivne.

Nove 脛聧lanice dobijo seveda dodatna sredstva, ker lahko okrepijo svoj razvoj, ampak obstoje脛聧e 脛聧lanice pa dobijo predvsem nove trge, kjer lahko prodajajo svoje odli脛聧ne proizvode. In ekonomske analize so pokazale, da po 脜隆iritvi leta 2004, ko se je Evropski uniji pridru脜戮ilo deset novih 脛聧lanic, so v neto izplenu najve脛聧 pridobile obstoje脛聧e starej脜隆e 脛聧lanice.

Tako da argumenti francoskega poslanca, kako je Francija neto pla脛聧nica in da je to slabo za Francijo, enostavno ne zdr脜戮ijo, ker zaradi 脜隆iritve je Francija in vse starej脜隆e 脛聧lanice predvsem pridobila. Pridobila ve脛聧 kot vse nove 脛聧lanice. 脜聽iritev je pozitivna.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Kos, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear Members of the European Parliament, thank you for all your contributions. I have taken good note of your remarks.

Looking ahead, it is really important to keep in mind that a lot of work is still to come. Yes, we should enlarge, but we should also reform. Difficult discussions will take place and they are likely to drag on for a long time. But, we must not lose sight of our main goal. As Mr脗聽Gozi's report states, EU enlargement is a long-term geostrategic investment which will benefit all of our citizens. In President Von der Leyen's words, 'only a united 芒聙聯 and a reunited 芒聙聯 Europe can be an independent Europe', but this Europe should be stronger and also more effective.

Once we give a candidate country the status of candidate, it is our responsibility to help them on the way. It is also about our credibility: that once they come to the end, we will be able to take them in. I have heard from two or three of you saying that enlargement for many candidate countries is hope. Indeed, hope is very important, but it's not enough. But it can play a decisive role as a motivator when the countries have to do the reforms, and this takes time 芒聙聯 it's not happening from today to tomorrow.

I have heard that we are following the geopolitical needs. No, we are not. If we would have done this, then we would take all the candidate countries into the European Union tomorrow. But we are not doing this. It is on a merit-based principle, meaning that we have clear criteria and sometimes we demand more from our candidate countries than we demand from the Member States.

After the adoption of the upcoming communication, the Commission will continue to engage with this Parliament and the Council and closely follow all developments. I will continue, dear Sandro, to be online with you to discuss this very important topic.

When I say that it is a merit-based principle: among the candidate countries, sometimes, when we are in a good mood, we say that only the merit-based principle can lead us to a marriage-based unity at the end.

To the question of dear Micha脜聜 Kobosko: the Commission is aware of the informal suggestions for the voting rights for new Member States from some countries, but we haven't discussed this yet at all in the Commission.

My wish for all of us is that we go on with the enlargement process as a very important 芒聙聯 or the most successful 芒聙聯 foreign policy, as well as that we also follow and really, really keep our European Union strong for the future. There are many, many challenges, and we will only be able to answer those challenges if we really stick to the principles and if we stay united. For me, this is one of the most important issues we should follow in all the debates which are in front of us.

Thank you, Sandro, for your contribution. Thank you all for all the contributions.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sandro Gozi, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner Kos, thank you, and thank you all, because I think that this debate made things very clear. I mean, there is the extreme right which is against everything: 'We don't need to reform because we are against enlargement'. It's fine, it's not madness, but at least it's a clear position.

Then there are those who haven't read the report properly, because I heard that there are Greek delegations and delegations from Cyprus, from different groups, who say, 'We cannot accept that we lose the unanimity vote to decide whether T脙录rkiye should become a member or not'. You don't lose it 芒聙聯 you don't lose it. Because what we say in the report is that to start the negotiation, to assess the country and to decide whether a country must become a member, we keep unanimity. I would like to emphasise this again for the Cypriot and Greek delegation of different groups, and also for our Croat friends: you don't want Serbia in the European Union, you can keep unanimity, but during the enlargement process, we cannot be exposed to 150脗聽vetoes on technical or sectoral issues.

We want to accelerate, that is the first point that I wanted to explain, the second is that it is clear, several colleagues said, it is a question of credibility. Colleagues, if we want to unify the continent, we must reform the Union, because we perfectly know, if we are in good faith, that without reforming the Union, without using the flexibility in the Treaty, without some targeted Treaty reform on the rule of law, for example, the Union is already hampered today and it will not work with the enlargement. So either we are lying to the Ukrainians, we are lying to the Western Balkans and we say, 'Of course we want you in the European family,' but in the meantime we don't prepare the house, so it is a lie, or we are serious about enlargement.

If we are serious about enlargement, we must do our homework as we ask the candidate country to do their homework. Because the consequence 芒聙聯 last point, Mr President 芒聙聯 is exactly what Micha脜聜 Kobosko raised. There are Member States that, instead of tackling the issue of reform, they say, we create two leagues: league one, the current Member States, and league two, the second league. So the new members will not have voting rights until when? Until we have done what we have to do today.

We have to prepare the Union to become more efficient, to become more powerful and to become more democratic. And we must do it because we owed it to our citizens, and we must do it because also we must be serious and credible when we say that we want to unify the continent.

And to the colleague of the extreme right, I would like to recall that the European project, since the very beginning, was meant to unify the continent. Only at the beginning there was the Berlin Wall, there was a dictatorship in Spain and Portugal, and this is why we started with six Member States. But since the very beginning, it was a project for all the citizens of our continent, and we must make it happen.

脗聽

脗聽

The vote will take place tomorrow, 22 October 2025.

脗聽

10. Delayed justice and rule of law backsliding in Malta, eight years after Daphne Caruana Galizia芒聙聶s assassination (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next item is the debate on the Commission statement on delayed justice and rule of law backsliding in Malta, eight years after Daphne Caruana Galizia芒聙聶s assassination (2025/2931(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Henna Virkkunen, Executive Vice-President of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, honourable Members, thank you for including in the Plenary agenda this debate to mark the eighth anniversary of the assassination of Maltese investigative journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia. The Commission has repeatedly condemned Daphne's murder, which was a horrible crime. Attacks on our journalists are attacks on press freedom and democracy itself.

This eighth anniversary should serve as a stark reminder that protecting journalists' safety in the European Union and around the world needs to have all our attention.

The Commission praises once more the courage of Daphne's family and Maltese civil society in fighting for the safety of journalists. We expect the Maltese authorities and courts to continue to do everything necessary to ensure that all those involved in this crime face justice.

With regard to the rule of law situation in Malta, I would like to express our appreciation for the Maltese authorities for their spirit of cooperation with the Commission. The Rule of Law Report is the result of very broad engagement with national authorities and stakeholders, as well as civil society and other actors, including professional associations and journalists. It is rooted in dialogue and shared responsibility.

The 2025 Rule of Law Report includes in-depth analysis of the main developments concerning the rule of law in Malta. Our conclusion is that Malta has taken steps to solve certain issues. However, additional efforts are needed.

Regarding justice, the Report notes that discussions are ongoing in the parliament on a comprehensive constitutional reform relating to the justice sector. The reform would provide further involvement of the judiciary in the procedure for appointment of the Chief Justice, in line with our recommendations.

However, the Report also highlights that despite substantial recruitment efforts and proposals to expand court premises, there are still concerns over the need to increase resources, the length of the proceedings and overall efficiency of justice.

Efficiency of justice is crucial for ensuring that the rule of law is upheld and that citizens can trust the justice systems. Therefore, we have recommended to Malta to take steps to improve the efficiency of justice.

As regards anti-corruption, the Report notes that the implementation of the National Anti-Fraud and Corruption Strategy continues. While new tools have been developed to reduce the length of investigations of high-level corruption cases, a robust track record of final judgments is still needed. Similarly, the capacity of the Permanent Commission Against Corruption to achieve concrete results needs to be improved.

Moreover, more work is still needed to implement important anti-corruption recommendations issued following the public inquiry into the assassination of Daphne Caruana Galizia. These include, in particular, rules of lobbying, code of conduct and integrity measures of high-level politicians and officials.

Malta has also been asked to improve the regime for the protection of whistleblowers, to actively encourage reporting persons to speak up and notify the authorities of potential corruption. In terms of transparency, only limited steps were taken to improve access to official documents.

With regard to media freedom more generally, we are committed to strengthening the support to journalists and improving their protection against pressure and threats. In August, Malta adopted a legal notice which aims to align Maltese legislation with several aspects of the European Media Freedom Act. Now we are monitoring closely this and also other developments concerning the independence of public service media and transparency in state advertising.

As to other institutional issues, reforms to establish the national human-rights institutions and to introduce a formal framework for public participation in the legislative process remain pending, although certain progress has been made on the latter. The Report stresses recommendations on these points.

In conclusion, while Malta has made progress on certain issues, we continue to insist on the need to make further progress on the different pending issues highlighted in the recommendations of this year's Rule of Law Report. We stand ready to support these efforts.

Honourable Members, crimes against our journalists anywhere in the world strike at the very heart of our freedom of expression and media freedom everywhere. On Thursday, 16脗聽October, eight years to the day from Daphne's murder, Sigfrido Ranucci, an Italian journalist working for Rai, was targeted by a bomb placed under his car outside his family home. An investigation is ongoing into this matter. The Commission condemns the attack and expresses its solidarity with Mr脗聽Ranucci, his family and his colleagues.

Let me add here that under the upcoming European Democracy Shield, we are looking at ways to further support our journalists and improve their protection against pressure and threats, building on the anti-SLAPPs package and the 2021 Commission recommendation on the safety of journalists.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽David Casa, f'isem il-grupp PPE. 芒聙聯 Sur President, Kummissarju, irrid nibda billi nirringrazzja lil dan il-Parlament. Grazzi talli g脛搂al tmien snin s脛搂a脛搂 kontu fuq in-na脛搂a tal-poplu Malti u G脛搂awdxi. U grazzi talli qeg脛搂din hawnhekk illum, tkomplu tonoraw il-memorja ta' Daphne Caruana Galizia. Il-g脛搂aliex dak li rnexxielna niksbu s'issa huwa wkoll ri脜录ultat tal-iskrutinju ta' din il-Kamra. Huwa skorrett, naturalment, illi ng脛搂idu illi ma kienx hemm kambjamenti. Pere脜录empju, il-kriminali ta' livell baxx li wettqu l-assassinju 脛隆ew ikkundannati l-脛搂abs. Il-mod ta' kif tin脛搂atar il-脛隆udikatura f'pajji脜录na nbidel u da脛搂lu fis-se脛搂脛搂 li脛隆ijiet Ewropej li jipprote脛隆u lill-脛隆urnalisti.

Imma g脛搂ad fadal 脛搂afna x'isir. Pere脜录empju, il-familja ta' Daphne g脛搂adha qieg脛搂da tistenna l-e脜录itu tal-ka脜录 fil-konfront tal-allegat mandant. Hemm rakkomandazzjonijiet tal-inkjesta pubblika, inkjesta li dan il-Parlament kemm-il darba sostna li g脛搂andha ssir illi, sal-lum, g脛搂adhom ma twettqux. L-ewwel fosthom hija li dak kollu li kellu jinbidel biex neg脛搂lbu l-klima ta' impunit脙聽, sal-lum g脛搂adhom ma twettqux, g脛搂adhom ma nbidlux.

Il-g脛搂aliex, kollegi, 脛隆ustizzja g脛搂al Daphne tfisser 脛隆ustizzja g脛搂all-kriminalit脙聽 li 脛隆iet maqtula biex iwaqqfuha milli tesponi. Imma tmien snin wara l-assassinju, g脛搂ad m'hemmx kundanna wa脛搂da li hi wa脛搂da fuq dik il-korruzzjoni kollha li kienet kixfet. U lanqas ma tg脛搂allimna l-lezzjonijiet tal-importanza tal-脛隆urnali脜录mu f'demokrazija. Bi脜录脜录ejjed ng脛搂idu illi, il-脛隆img脛搂a li g脛搂addiet, fl-anniversarju tal-assassinju, impjegat mill-uffi脛聥脛聥ju tal-Prim Ministru mar jarmi l-fjuri li t脛搂allew biex ifakkru lil Daphne fil-mafkar tag脛搂ha fil-Belt Valletta. 脛娄a脛隆a imbarazzanti illi 脛隆iet ikkundannata minn diversi ambaxxati Ewropej i脜录da mhux mill-Prim Ministru.

U allura, iva, fadal 脛搂afna x'nag脛搂mlu f'pajji脜录na, imma anke madwar id-dinja. Min ma jemminx illi g脛搂ad g脛搂andna problema tal-脛隆urnalisti fid-dinja, in脛搂arsu x'脛隆ara f'Ga脜录a u n脛搂arsu x'脛隆ara anke fl-Italja. Ejja niddefendu l-脛隆urnalisti biex ikomplu jag脛搂mlu xog脛搂olhom u ji脜录velaw il-verit脙聽.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alex Agius Saliba, f'isem il-grupp S&D. 芒聙聯 Sur President, nixtieq nibda billi nikkwota parti mid-diskors tal-Prim Im脛搂allef Malti illi g脛搂amel 脛隆imag脛搂tejn ilu. Huwa qal hekk, "Nixtieq nirringrazzja lill-Ministru tal-脛聽ustizzja li dejjem sema' s-su脛隆脛隆erimenti tieg脛搂i f'isem il-脛隆udikatura Maltija. Su脛隆脛隆erimenti sabiex jissa脛搂脛搂u l-qrati tag脛搂na. Kien hemm dejjem rispett re脛聥iproku bejnietna, rispett abba脜录i tal-irwoli kostituzzjonali rispettivi tag脛搂na." Kliem il-Prim Im脛搂allef Malti.

U g脛搂alhekk nistaqsi. G脛搂alfejn dan l-attakk ie脛搂or fuq Malta tag脛搂na? Qalulna, fit-titlu ta' dan id-dibattitu, illi g脛搂andna dewmien sabiex issir 脛隆ustizzja. Dewmien sabiex issir 脛隆ustizzja meta g脛搂andna 脛搂ames akku脜录ati fil-qtil ta' Daphne Caruana Galizia illi kollha g脛搂addew 脛隆uri, bl-a脛搂脛搂ar 脛隆uri jintemm ftit tal-脛隆img脛搂at ilu?脗聽Fadal l-a脛搂脛搂ar 脛隆uri wie脛搂ed biss pendenti li jinsab f'idejn il-qrati tag脛搂na. U jiena nistaqsi. X'qed jipproponi l-Parlament Ewropew? Illi l-Prim Ministru, Robert Abela, imur fuq il-qrati tag脛搂na u jg脛搂idilhom sabiex i脛搂affu l-pro脛聥ess? Kieku tkunu l-ewwel illi tippuntaw subg脛搂ajkom lejna u tg脛搂idulna li qeg脛搂din tinda脛搂lu fix-xog脛搂ol tal-qrati tag脛搂na.

G脛搂idtulna li r-rule of law marret lura, f'dan id-dibattitu. Marret lura, ir-rule of law, meta g脛搂addejna mill-Parlament Malti li脛隆i, medja ta' li脛隆i fix-xahar, sabiex insa脛搂脛搂u r-rakkomandazzjonijiet illi kellna? Marret lura, ir-rule of law, meta g脛搂andna l-ikbar ammont ta' 脛隆udikanti fl-istorja ta' pajji脜录na, u tajniehom aktar g脛搂odod sabiex ja脛搂dmu a脛搂jar? Marret lura, ir-rule of law, meta ne脛搂脛搂ejna l-poteri minn idejn il-politi脛聥i sabiex tin脛搂atar il-脛隆udikatura, u g脛搂addejniehom f'idejn kummissjoni indipendenti? Marret lura, ir-rule of law, meta 脛搂loqna l-avukat tal-Istat?

Taf min jikser ir-rule of law? Il-Partit Nazzjonalista, parti mill-Grupp tal-EPP, li dawn, wara li rre脜录istew g脛搂al snin twal illi jkollna li脛隆i tal-finanzjament tal-partiti, ilhom 脛搂ames snin s脛搂a脛搂 jiksru din il-li脛隆i billi ma jippubblikawx il-finanzi awditjati tag脛搂hom. Suppost, skont il-li脛隆i Maltija, il-partit tal-EPP hawnhekk, lanqas biss huwa partit rikonoxxut f'pajji脜录na.脗聽Allura jiena nappella lill-EPP illi, qabel jippuntaw subg脛搂ajhom lejn 脛搂addie脛搂or, jaraw illi jnaddfu daqsxejn darhom.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Veronika Cifrov脙隆 Ostriho脜聢ov脙隆, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, dear colleagues, eight years have passed since Daphne Caruana Galizia was assassinated. For her sons, this is perhaps one-fifth of their lives. Imagine that 芒聙聯 is this how a modern state treats justice and fairness? What message do we send to our citizens when so little follows such a crime?

As a former journalist, as a mother and as someone who is passionate about the truth, this feels truly personal to me because justice and delays are just incompatible. We still need, as we have heard, thorough prosecutions for the corruption that Daphne exposed, and we need effective reforms to end the climate of impunity that enabled her assassination 芒聙聯 preferably delivered yesterday, but the next best thing is as soon as possible.

There is one more thing I wish to add: this debate is held in solidarity with the Maltese people. We want you to know that there are voices, European voices, who stand firmly and strongly by your side. And now it's time for local decision makers to step up.脗聽Grazzi 脛搂afna.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Daniel Freund, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues, right in the capital of Malta, there is a monument where every year flowers are laid down to commemorate the work and life of Daphne Caruana Galizia and, of course, her brutal assassination eight years ago. How petty do you have to be to throw those flowers into the bin?

But more importantly, let's look at what political consequences have been drawn by the government after the assassination. Well, the alleged mastermind has been released on bail 芒聙聯 there is still no trial date set as of today. The reforms colleagues have spoken about are going slow 芒聙聯 GRECO has found only 4 out of the 32脗聽recommendations on anti-corruption being followed. The rule of law report speaks of no progress, also no progress on the protection of journalists and on the corruption that Daphne actually investigated. There is not a single prosecution, not a single conviction, and the magistrates suggesting prosecution are framed by the Prime Minister as political terrorists. I do not think this is the way to go. If we want to honour Daphne, we need to implement the necessary reforms, make the rule of law work in Malta.

脗聽脗聽Javier Zarzalejos (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora vicepresidenta, hoy no solo recordamos el asesinato de Daphne Caruana. Recordamos asimismo que el Estado de Derecho es la columna vertebral de la Uni脙鲁n Europea y, por tanto, tenemos que insistir en que el Estado de Derecho es un deber y una responsabilidad de la Uni脙鲁n.

Si levantamos el velo del lenguaje siempre medido de la Comisi脙鲁n en su informe sobre el Estado de Derecho, vemos que se ha avanzado muy poco. Se脙卤or脙颅as, han pasado ocho a脙卤os ocho a脙卤os 芒聙聰脗隆ocho a脙卤os!芒聙聰 y, tras ocho a脙卤os, el instigador y autor intelectual del crimen se encuentra en libertad, y altos cargos que han obstaculizado la investigaci脙鲁n no han sido procesados.

Ocho a脙卤os despu脙漏s de las treinta y dos recomendaciones del Grupo de Estados contra la Corrupci脙鲁n del Consejo de Europa solamente se han adoptado cuatro. Ocho a脙卤os despu脙漏s 芒聙聰脗隆ocho a脙卤os despu脙漏s!芒聙聰 la Comisi脙鲁n nos dice que no se han producido avances en la protecci脙鲁n de los periodistas ni en la libertad de los medios.

Ocho a脙卤os despu脙漏s 芒聙聰脗隆ocho a脙卤os despu脙漏s!芒聙聰 del asesinato, cuando los jueces act脙潞an, se les acusa de terrorismo pol脙颅tico. Y ocho a脙卤os despu脙漏s, personas vinculadas a la Oficina del Primer Ministro han destruido la ofrenda floral en memoria de Daphne Caruana. Ocho a脙卤os.

Podemos hablar, por tanto, de lo que se ha hecho, pero hablemos de lo que no se ha hecho en estos ocho a脙卤os, porque ese ser脙隆 nuestro compromiso y el mejor homenaje a la memoria de Daphne Caruana.

(El orador acepta responder a una pregunta formulada con arreglo al procedimiento de la 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄)

脗聽脗聽Alex Agius Saliba (S&D), mistoqsija karta blu. 芒聙聯 Sur President, nixtieq nag脛搂mel mistoqsija li脛聥-Chair tal-LIBE Committee.

Ji脛隆ifieri inti fid-diskors tieg脛搂ek g脛搂idt illi g脛搂adhom g脛搂addejjin il-pro脛聥essi 脛隆udizzjarji. Fadal 脛隆uri wie脛搂ed biss f'dan il-ka脜录 tal-persuni li huma akku脜录ati bl-involviment fil-qtil ta' Daphne Caruana Galizia, u hemm pro脛聥ess illi g脛搂addej, li l-Gvern totalment m'g脛搂andux x'jaqsam mieg脛搂u.

Ji脛隆ifieri inti qed tipproponi, b脛搂ala Chair tal-LIBE Committee, li l-Gvern Malti, il-Prim Ministru, il-Ministru tal-脛聽ustizzja jitkellem direttament mal-qrati sabiex il-pro脛聥ess, il-脛隆uri, isir b'mod mg脛搂a脛隆脛隆el, immedjat u jinkisru d-drittijiet illi l-akku脜录at, f'dan il-ka脜录, qieg脛搂ed imexxi 'l quddiem, drittijiet illi jo脛搂or脛隆u mil-li脛隆i u mill-kostituzzjoni Maltija?

脗聽脗聽Javier Zarzalejos (PPE), respuesta de 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or Agius Saliba, muchas gracias por su pregunta, porque me da la oportunidad de exponer lo siguiente.

Primero, me alegro mucho de todos los avances que se est脙隆n produciendo en el fortalecimiento de la independencia judicial. Se脙卤alo todav脙颅a los muchos avances que est脙隆n pendientes.

Adem脙隆s, creo que el respeto por la independencia judicial es incompatible con los ataques que los jueces est脙隆n sufriendo en Malta por investigar estos casos de corrupci脙鲁n.

脗聽脗聽Daniel Attard (S&D). 芒聙聯 Sur President, dan id-dibattitu, fil-fehma tieg脛搂i, mhu xejn g脛搂ajr tentattiv ie脛搂or biex jittappan isem Malta. G脛搂aliex, jiena, sin脛聥erament ma nafx kif 脛聥ertu nies ma jist脛搂ux jinqdew bil-qtil ta' 脛隆urnalista biex iservu l-a脛隆enda politika tag脛搂hom. Meta jkun hemm gvern differenti, meta jkun hemm gvern Nazzjonalista? G脛搂aliex s'hemm jafu min i脛隆ib il-poter qabel il-pajji脜录.

脗聽脗聽Raquel Garc脙颅a Hermida-Van Der Walle (Renew), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Thank you for taking my blue card.

脗聽脗聽Daniel Attard (S&D), reazzjoni "karta blu". 芒聙聯 Sur President, mill-脛隆did, nistrie脛搂 fuq il-fatti illi f'pajji脜录na saru riformi kbar f'diversi setturi, mhux l-inqas fil-脛隆udikatura u anki fil-ka脜录 li qed nittrattaw illum, fil-ka脜录 ta' Daphne Caruana Galizia u l-qtil kundannabbli tag脛搂ha, fejn il-pro脛聥ess 脛隆udizzjarju 脛搂a l-perkors tieg脛搂u u jonqos 脛隆uri wie脛搂ed biss.
