脗聽脗聽Moritz K脙露rner (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, eight years after the murder of Daphne, her fight for truth and the rule of law is still unfinished. The European Parliament cannot be silent about it. We owe Daphne this justice.

But what really makes me angry in this debate is actually one thing: that not even one speaker of the PfE and the ECR is even here speaking in this debate. Not one.

This is actually telling us a lot, because normally the right-wing groups always say, 'You are just attacking conservative governments like Orb脙隆n'. Now there is a debate on a socialist government and they are not here. This is actually 芒聙娄

(Heckling)

No, I have not! Where's your speaker on the list? You are the only one here. No speaker at all in the list. You don't care about it!

That's really what makes the difference. We care about the rule of law. We care about the rule of law when there's a conservative government, when there's a socialist government.

We care about all different European countries, and we protect the rule of law everywhere. That defines us and that differentiates us from you and your friends Orb脙隆n and Co.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Leoluca Orlando (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, signora Commissaria, onorevoli colleghi, in piazza a La Valletta e oggi in quest'Aula, 脙篓 doverosa la memoria per l'uccisione della coraggiosa giornalista Daphne Caruana Galizia e siamo con i familiari e tanti cittadini maltesi qui per chiedere forte verit脙聽 e giustizia.

Il sospettato mandante, arrestato nel 2019, 脙篓 stato messo in libert脙聽 dietro cauzione all'inizio del 2025 e, alla data odierna, non 脙篓 ancora fissata la data del processo, dopo otto anni da quel terribile delitto. Rischia di prevalere un disarmante clima di rassegnazione all'illegalit脙聽, alla corruzione, alla violenza e all'impunit脙聽.

Va ricordata la necessit脙聽 di interventi legislativi e di scelte politiche soltanto in parte sin qui realizzate. La relazione 2025 della Commissione europea sullo Stato di diritto a Malta riassume lacune e necessit脙聽 di riforme. 脙聢 necessario 芒聙聯 riporta ufficialmente la relazione 2025 芒聙聯 garantire sicurezza, indipendenza e libert脙聽 dei giornalisti e una riforma dei media.

E ancora, testualmente, la relazione 2025 ricorda che per la piena attuazione delle raccomandazioni anticorruzione, restano da intraprendere azioni chiave per quanto riguarda la trasparenza, le attivit脙聽 di lobbying, un codice di condotta 芒聙聯 e io aggiungo con convinzione 芒聙聯 anche per il controllo di movimenti e di origine dei patrimoni.

Se non ora, signora Commissaria, quando?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Giuseppe Antoci (The Left). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, oggi non celebriamo un anniversario: ricordiamo una ferita che non si rimargina. Daphne Caruana Galizia 脙篓 stata uccisa perch脙漏 cercava la verit脙聽, perch脙漏 non accettava il compromesso, perch脙漏 aveva il coraggio di guardare in faccia il potere.

Una bomba ha provato a cancellarla, ma non c'脙篓 bomba che possa cancellare la verit脙聽. La stessa bomba che in Italia, qualche giorno fa, poteva uccidere il giornalista d'inchiesta RAI, Sigfrido Ranucci. Ne parleremo pi脙鹿 tardi.

Chi uccide un giornalista non elimina una voce ma ne accende altre 100. Perch脙漏 la libert脙聽, come la verit脙聽, non si pu脙虏 mettere a tacere. Oggi le campane suonano, le marce avanzano silenziose: ma la promessa di verit脙聽 脙篓 ancora incompiuta.

Gli esecutori sono stati condannati ma i mandanti no. In piazza, a Malta, risuonano tre parole: verit脙聽, memoria, libert脙聽. 脙聢 un monito che non smette di interrogarci. Daphne aveva ragione. Daphne non cercava gloria, cercava giustizia; non scriveva per s脙漏, ma per i cittadini, per la democrazia, per un Paese migliore. Ed 脙篓 per questo che oggi la sua voce parla ancora in ogni giornalista che non si piega, in ogni persona che crede che la libert脙聽 di stampa sia il fondamento della libert脙聽 di tutti.

Ricordarla non basta: bisogna agire, proteggere chi racconta, garantire la sicurezza di chi indaga, combattere l'impunit脙聽 di chi minaccia e di chi corrompe, s脙卢, corrompe! Ed 脙篓 per questo che bisogna approvare la direttiva anticorruzione con la specifica prevista sull'obbligatoriet脙聽 dell'abuso d'ufficio. Lo dobbiamo per Daphne, perch脙漏 dobbiamo fare non solo gesti simbolici: abbiamo bisogno di scelte coraggiose.

Daphne ci ha lasciato un messaggio semplice e potente: non avere paura. E allora il modo migliore per onorarlo 脙篓 proprio questo, cio脙篓 non avere paura di fare e di dire la verit脙聽. Perch脙漏 la verit脙聽 pu脙虏 essere colpita ma non sar脙聽 mai sconfitta.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tomas Tob脙漏 (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, colleagues, eight years have passed since the brutal assassination of Daphne Caruana Galizia, and I think it is fair to say that impunity has not ended. Yes, there have been some reforms and yes, it is positive that the Commission is engaging with the Maltese authorities, but it is also clear that this is not enough. That is also clear in the recommendations that we can see for Malta. But perhaps the main question is: where is the justice for Daphne's family? That is the main question that needs an answer in the end.

The current government in Malta needs to understand that belonging to the European Union means upholding the core values: democracy, accountability, and also rule of law. Without that, Malta will continue to risk not only your credibility, but the very democratic soul. And to our S&D colleagues from Malta, I think you actually could be also proud of EPP colleagues from the nationalist parties. One of them is even president of this House 芒聙聯 this House that actually is the best House, I would say, for democracy and rule of law. Be proud of that instead and give Daphne Caruana Galizia, give her the answer, give her family the answer that you are, in a way, sweeping under the carpet.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 No, I will not take blue cards coming from previous speakers, taking into account the time restrictions that we have because we have the debate until 15:00.

脗聽脗聽Thomas Bajada (S&D). 芒聙聯 Sur President, sena wara l-o脛搂ra, dan il-Parlament intalab jiddibatti dan il-qtil makabru. Fl-istess waqt, fost l-o脛搂rajn, se脛搂脛搂 l-attentat b'karozza bomba fuq Sigfrido Ranucci f'Ruma u l-qtil ta' mitejn tnejn u sebg脛搂in 脛隆urnalist f'Ga脜录a. Dan kollu jkompli juri illi l-脛隆urnali脜录mu, ji脛隆ifieri t-tfittxija tal-verit脙聽, jinsab ta脛搂t theddida, kullimkien.

Nemmen li kul脛搂add jixtieq il-脛隆ustizzja. B脛搂alma 脛搂dimna g脛搂aliha a脛搂na b脛搂ala Maltin, issa wasal i脜录-脜录mien li, b脛搂ala Ewropej, na脛搂dmu g脛搂all-脛隆ustizzja g脛搂al kull 脛隆urnalist. G脛搂aliex jekk verament irridu nag脛搂tu 脛隆ustizzja lil Daphne, irridu nag脛搂tu 脛隆ustizzja lill-脛隆urnalisti kollha u mhux bir-rule of law biss meta jaqbel.

Ejja mmorru lil hinn mill-politika parti脛隆脛隆jana, lil hinn mill-EPP kontra gvernijiet So脛聥jalisti, lil hinn min-Nazzjonalisti u l-Laburisti u, minflok, ejjew nag脛搂tu t-tama lin-nies, u mhux il-pika u l-konfrontazzjoni. Ejja nipprote脛隆u lill-脛隆urnalisti kollha fit-tfittxija tal-verit脙聽. Ejja nkunu dejjem flimkien u mhux biss meta jaqblilna, sabiex verament niksbu l-脛隆ustizzja Ewropea.

脗聽脗聽Irena Joveva (Renew). 芒聙聯 Gospod predsednik! 芒聙聻There are crooks everywhere you look.芒聙聹 To je citat Daphne Caruane Galizie in ta citat dodobra opi脜隆e razmere, ki leta po njenem umoru 脜隆e vedno prevladujejo na Malti.

Govorila je resnico, razkrivala korupcijo, zahtevala odgovornost, vse tisto, kar naj bi bilo v demokraciji samoumevno. In kot so bila odmevna njena dejanja, je odmeven tudi njen umor, ampak kljub temu pravice ni nikjer.

Tisti, ki so pritisnili na spro脜戮ilec, so v zaporu. Tisti, ki so dali ukaz, so na prostosti in tisti, ki so preiskavo ovirali, 脜隆e vedno na svojih polo脜戮ajih. In kaj po脛聧ne vlada? Umika ro脜戮e z njenega spominskega mesta.

Ugotovitve Komisije ka脜戮ejo, da napredka ni, kar je popolnoma, popolnoma nesprejemljivo. Noben korupcijski primer se ni kon脛聧al s pravnomo脛聧no obsodbo, novinarji pa 脜隆e vedno delajo v sovra脜戮nem in nevarnem okolju.

Gre za evropski problem zato, ker brez kaznovanosti trpi vsa Unija. Zato je skrajni 脛聧as za ukrepe, za spremembo razmer, na katere nas je opozorila Daphne, in naj lopovov ne bo tam, kjer se odlo脛聧a o na脜隆ih 脜戮ivljenjih.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ana Miguel Pedro (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, today we remember Daphne Caruana Galizia, eight years after her brutal assassination. Eight years of struggle, eight years of promises, eight years of illusions. Each year we return to this Chamber, each year, the same reality confronts us. In Malta, justice is still delayed. The rule of law is still eroding. The system remains captured by power. The culture of impunity that made her death possible still governs the country. The Commission's latest rule of law report flags no real progress on convictions, no effective protection for journalists and blocked media reform in Malta.

Eight years on, justice is still incomplete. Justice for Daphne means more than arrests, it means dismantling the corruption she exposed and ending the impunity that killed her. Anything less is complicity, anything less weakens the rule of law at the heart of our Union. When a journalist is silent, society is blinded. When impunity prevails, democracy decays. Daphne was murdered for telling the truth but the truth does not lie. It waits, it resists, it returns. Our duty, this Parliament's duty is clear to make truth impossible to kill 芒聙聯 for Daphne, for her sons, for every journalist who still dares to write. Let Europe prove that courage cannot be killed.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Temido (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, 脗芦n脙拢o estamos interessados em justi脙搂a sem mudan脙搂as脗禄, escreveram nas redes sociais os filhos de Daphne Galizia, na primeira publica脙搂脙拢o que fizeram depois do seu brutal assassinato, h脙隆 oito anos.

Sabemos que foram feitos avan脙搂os, que dois homens foram condenados 脙聽 pris脙拢o perp脙漏tua por fornecerem o carro芒聙聭bomba, que dois outros foram acusados de colocar e detonar o dispositivo, estando a cumprir 40 anos de pris脙拢o. Mas, sim, os mandantes do crime continuam por punir.

E o melhor contributo que podemos prestar a Daphne Galizia 脙漏 continuar a exigir justi脙搂a e reformas do sistema que garantam a confian脙搂a nas institui脙搂脙碌es e no Estado de direito.

Oito anos depois deste assassinato, o trabalho dos jornalistas est脙隆 cada vez mais amea脙搂ado dentro e fora da Uni脙拢o Europeia. A desinforma脙搂脙拢o, a censura, a intimida脙搂脙拢o, mas tamb脙漏m os assassinatos em contexto de conflito tornam esta profiss脙拢o um trabalho de risco, quando este 脙漏 um pilar da nossa vida democr脙隆tica. A corrup脙搂脙拢o 脙漏 apenas um dos seus muitos inimigos.

脙聙 sociedade civil cabe a den脙潞ncia. A n脙鲁s cabem芒聙聭nos as mudan脙搂as que a defendam, doa a quem doer.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Isabel Wiseler-Lima (PPE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Daphne脗聽Caruana脗聽Galizia: chaque ann脙漏e, m脙陋me point de d脙漏part, chaque ann脙漏e, nous devons constater qu'une journaliste dans l'Union europ脙漏enne a 脙漏t脙漏 assassin脙漏e et que des coresponsables au plus haut niveau continuent 脙聽 jouir de l'impunit脙漏.

Daphne脗聽Caruana脗聽Galizia a 脙漏t脙漏 assassin脙漏e il y a maintenant huit ans. Huit ans! Sa famille m脙篓ne un combat admirable.

Au Parlement europ脙漏en, nous multiplions les appels pour que le gouvernement maltais prenne enfin ses responsabilit脙漏s et fasse en sorte que l'impunit脙漏 prenne fin. Les groupes de d脙漏fense d'une presse libre aussi, et ils demandent les r脙漏formes l脙漏gislatives n脙漏cessaires 脙聽 la libert脙漏 des m脙漏dias et 脙聽 la s脙漏curit脙漏 des journalistes. Mais la lenteur des autorit脙漏s maltaises fr脙麓le la provocation.

Il faut esp脙漏rer que la consultation publique sur la r脙漏forme des m脙漏dias, initi脙漏e par le minist脙篓re de la justice maltais, n'est pas qu'une nouvelle man脜聯uvre pour retarder des actions visant 脙聽 garantir une presse libre, une presse d'investigation 脙聽 m脙陋me de d脙漏voiler la corruption et donc de lutter contre celle-ci. C'est le combat de Daphne, celui qui lui a co脙禄t脙漏 la vie.

Nous demandons que tous, j'insiste, tous les responsables de l'assassinat de Daphne脗聽Caruana脗聽Galizia soient jug脙漏s.

脗聽脗聽Peter Agius (PPE). 芒聙聯 Sur President, f'Malta kien hemm min ma ridniex nitkellmu fuq Daphne llum. Qalu li dan id-dibattitu kien tradiment pjanat mal-Kap tal-Oppo脜录izzjoni. Iddefendew lil min 脛隆ie jne脛搂脛搂i x-xemg脛搂at minn fuq il-mafkar ta' Daphne.

G脛搂ax Daphne skomda wisq g脛搂alihom. G脛搂ax Daphne qalet il-verit脙聽, u l-verit脙聽 tag脛搂ha tnigge脜录. Tnigge脜录 il-kuxjenza ma脛搂mu脛隆a ta' min ma jridx jimplimenta l-inkjesta pubblika, l-inkjesta pubblika li qaltilna li g脛搂andu jkun hemm reat g脛搂all-unexplained wealth. G脛搂aliex ma triduhx dan ir-reat fil-li脛隆i Maltija? Il-verit脙聽 tag脛搂ha tibqa' tnigge脜录. Tnigge脜录 lil dawk li jridu jag脛搂mlu pipeline tal-gass mas-17 Black, tnigge脜录 lil dawk li jridu jisirqulna erba' mitt miljun mill-isptarijiet. Lilna Daphne ma tbe脜录脜录ag脛搂niex, g脛搂ax a脛搂na vera n脛搂obbu lil Malta, b脛搂alma 脛搂abbitha Daphne Caruana Galizia.

脗聽脗聽Juan Fernando L脙鲁pez Aguilar (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr President, President von der Leyen, Vice-President Virkkunen, for eight years in a row we have paid respect to the memory of Daphne Caruana Galizia regularly every year, and rightly so. But it's not fair to use that commemoration and pay tribute to Daphne Caruana to attack Malta, because Malta has not challenged the rule of law framework. Malta has not challenged the ruling of the European Court of Justice.

There has been an investigation, there have been prosecutions, there have been indictments and there have also been convictions. The Venice Commission and the European Commission have certified that Malta has endorsed the Media Freedom Act and rules on SLAPPs 芒聙聯 strategic lawsuits against public participation 芒聙聯 in order to better protect journalists.

So all respect to the memory of Daphne Caruana and all investigative journalists 芒聙聯 including Sigfrido Ranucci, who was subject to an attempt just yesterday 芒聙聯 and, of course, full speed to the rule of law, fundamental rights and democracy framework of the European Union.

Lokakuisena p脙陇iv脙陇n脙陇 Daphne astui autoonsa ja hetke脙陇 my脙露hemmin autopommi repi pienen Peugeotin ja Daphnen riekaleiksi. Korruption vastainen 脙陇脙陇ni sammui.

T脙陇m脙陇 on j脙陇rkytt脙陇v脙陇 esimerkki siit脙陇, miten vahva asema korruptiolla voi viel脙陇 t脙陇n脙陇 p脙陇iv脙陇n脙陇kin olla Euroopassa. Ne, jotka taistelevat v脙陇sym脙陇tt脙陇 ja pelk脙陇脙陇m脙陇tt脙陇 korruptiota vastaan, ovat todellisia oikeusvaltion puolustajia. Daphne tutki ty脙露ss脙陇脙陇n muun muassa Maltan h脙陇rski脙陇kin h脙陇rskimp脙陇脙陇 kultaisten passien j脙陇rjestelm脙陇脙陇, jossa EU-kansalaisuus olisi kaupan rahaa vastaan. H脙陇n ei ollut en脙陇脙陇 n脙陇kem脙陇ss脙陇, kun se vihdoin t脙陇n脙陇 kev脙陇脙陇n脙陇 tuomittiin laittomaksi j脙陇rjestelyksi. Terveisi脙陇 Daphnelle, ty脙露 v脙陇脙陇ryytt脙陇 vastaan ei lopu.

脗聽脗聽Raquel Garc脙颅a Hermida-Van Der Walle (Renew). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, iba a decir otra cosa, pero, se脙卤or L脙鲁pez Aguilar, me sorprende y me apena mucho que la acusaci脙鲁n impl脙颅cita de su contribuci脙鲁n sea que estamos utilizando incorrectamente脗聽el homenaje a la se脙卤ora Caruana Galizia para acusar al Gobierno de Malta de algo de lo que no es culpable. No, no. Estamos aqu脙颅 denunciando que no se ha hecho lo suficiente para rendir justicia a Daphne, a sus hijos y al pueblo de Malta. Estamos aqu脙颅 para defender el Estado de Derecho en toda la Uni脙鲁n Europea, independientemente de consideraciones pol脙颅ticas o de partido.

As脙颅 que vamos a ser serios y vamos a dejar las cosas muy claras. Aqu脙颅 no se est脙隆 haciendo un mal uso脗聽de nada. Recordamos a la se脙卤ora Caruana Galizia y su trabajo contra la corrupci脙鲁n en Malta, que es el trabajo contra la corrupci脙鲁n en toda la Uni脙鲁n Europea.

J脜芦s k脛聟 tik prie脜隆 kelet脛聟 savai脛聧i脜鲁 apsaugojot ir nepanaikinot imuniteto saviems. Tie, kurie Vengrijoje organizavo miting脛聟, smurto aktus mitingo metu,脗聽kurie gyn脛聴 脜隆eim脛聟. K脛聟 j脜芦s? Pirmininkaujanti net neleido atsistoti europarlamentarams. Apie k脛聟 j脜芦s kalbate? 脛聦ia g脛聴dos institutas! O pa脜戮i脜芦r脛聴kit, kas yra. Kas vyksta 脜隆itoj Europoje?

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Henna Virkkunen, Executive Vice-President of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, honourable Members, thank you, all of you, for your contributions during this very important debate and for this opportunity to present developments in the rule of law situation in Malta, in the light of the 2025 rule of law report. I count on the Maltese authorities to continue with the reforms already underway and also to address our recommendations, and that they will launch reform processes on issues that still require a response.

For our part, as we have said, we are fully prepared to support the Maltese authorities in the interests of its citizens. I would like to personally thank Maltese civil society, which has played a very crucial role in advancing policy changes since Daphne was assassinated eight years ago. Developments in the rule of law in Malta and in all Member States will be analysed as part of the new annual cycle, which will be launched very shortly leading up to the 2026 rule of law report.

More generally, let me also flag two upcoming important initiatives: the European democracy shield will include actions to promote, protect and strengthen our democracies in the long run, and it will also be accompanied by the civil society strategy, recognising the key role of civil society organisations in promoting our European Union values.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The debate is closed.

12. Commission Work Programme 2026 (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next item is the debate on the Commission statement on the Commission Work Programme 2026 (2025/2848(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ursula von der Leyen, President of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear Roberta, honourable Members, our Europe must be an independent Europe. These words have resonated in the Parliament before. They were pronounced by the great Simone Veil, in her first speech, in the first plenary, of the first elected European Parliament. That was almost 50 years ago. Today, these words are more true than ever, and we simply cannot afford to wait another 50 years to turn them into reality. Because the world of today is unforgiving. The outcome of Russia's war will shape Europe's security order for decades to come. There is hope that the war in Gaza can finally come to an end. Our regional and global order is being redrawn, and Europe must fight for its place in the world, where some major powers are either ambivalent or hostile to us.

The same is true about the global economy. A global AI race has begun. The clean industrial race is faster and fiercer than ever before, and the impact is felt on our factory floors, in our family budgets and in the way we work and live. It is a world of constant change, and Europe must rise to the moment. We must do more for our own defence and security. We must take control over the sensitive technologies and energy sources that will power our economies. And we must take responsibility for the kind of society we want to live in. This is the choice we all face. We can either be shaped by events, or claim the freedom and the power to determine our own destiny. And we know what we want to be: we want to be the masters of our own destiny.

Honourable Members, this is why the entire work programme for the year ahead will be focused on Europe's independence moment. It delivers on the political guidelines you have voted on, updated for today's new realities. Of course, I won't touch on every single initiative, but I would like to address the main priorities, starting with competitiveness and jobs. Europe's independence will depend on its ability to compete in a changing global economy. But I want to be frank, the challenges we face today are even greater than a year ago, with the exponential growth of artificial intelligence, with the rising use of export controls and with global uncertainty straining public finances and private investments alike. So we must stay the course on implementing the Draghi report, but I think it has to be a 'Draghi plus' that we have to fulfil now.

Let me make three points on this. First, speed is of the essence. Companies across Europe demand that we make business easier for them. Harmonisation cannot wait 芒聙聯 we need to complete the single market. And this is why we are setting clear political deadlines to unleash the potential of the single market by 2028. We have a full programme of upcoming initiatives, let me mention some of them. From the ambitious 28th regime, to completing the savings and investment union, to a new fleet of simplification omnibuses, the sense of urgency on our side couldn't be greater. Our companies and workers cannot wait. We have to deliver on their needs, and we have to do it fast.

My second point is that we must double down on reducing our dependencies. Just last week, the association of our aerospace and defence industries issued a stark warning: a new wave of Chinese export controls could disrupt production and increase costs. And I take this warning very seriously. So on one hand, we have to, and will keep, diversifying our supply chains with new trade deals and new partnerships. But on the other hand, we will place new emphasis on recycling raw materials from scrap or other materials with our circular economy act, because circularity and recycling goes to the core of European independence.

Beyond raw materials, Europe must be in control of the critical technologies that will shape the economy of tomorrow. So in the year ahead, we will propose new initiatives on batteries, on cloud services and AI, on quantum, on advanced materials, and much more. These sectors are central to our economic security, but they are also central to keeping and creating good jobs here in Europe, so strategic industries like these deserve all our support. This is why we will introduce 'made in Europe' criteria for sensitive products in public procurement. We will foster lead markets for clean and innovative products, and we will keep protecting our industries from unfair competition 芒聙聯 we like competition, but it has to be fair 芒聙聯 like we just did now with the steel safeguards, because the industries and jobs of the future must be here in Europe.

My third point has to do with affordability. How can Europe be competitive if people working full time cannot make a living? If they cannot afford to live where the good jobs are because they don't find housing? Or if business revenues are eroded by unaffordable energy prices? So affordability is a main subject of this Commission work programme for 2026, with our work on affordable energy, the affordable housing plan, the small affordable electric cars initiative, but also, most importantly, the quality jobs act and the skills and fair labour mobility package. So across all sectors, my point is the same: our economy must work for people and businesses alike, and if we modernise the business and the industry and the workplace, we also have to modernise the labour market and the working conditions. Europe must deliver for all of its people. For instance, it must deliver for our farmers and fishers. They work so hard for the quality food we eat, but they are often forced to sell below production costs 芒聙聯 this is completely unacceptable. We've discussed it before, now we're tackling this point in the work programme for 2026 because their hard work must pay off.

Honourable Members, a new international order will emerge in this decade. When we talk about Europe's independence, we talk about our ability to shape this new order and starting, of course, from our immediate neighbourhood. This is true in the Middle East, where Europe must play a role in rebuilding Gaza, in relaunching the two-state solution, as well as in encouraging the birth of a new Syria. But of course, this is also true on our continent. The war in Ukraine needs to end with a just and lasting peace. So we will continue to step up the pressure on Russia, to support Ukraine's resistance in every way we can.

I want to be very clear on this one. Even if the war stops, Putin's war economy and imperial delusions will not go away. We must be ready for the threats that Europe may face, today and tomorrow, from the eastern flank to our southern border, from drones to hybrid warfare, but also from terrorism to the security consequences of climate change. That means we must continue to strengthen our defence and our deterrence, and this is the heart of our defence roadmap 2030. It sets very clear milestones towards a clear goal. It's a readiness roadmap. So Europe's defence must be mainly developed in Europe, we are very clear also about the content of European capabilities that has to be procured together. Because for decades we've talked about building a Europe of defence 芒聙聯 now it's time to make it happen, honourable Members.

At the heart of our independence is a strong democracy that can provide solutions to people's legitimate concerns, and nowhere is this more evident than in the context of migration. Yes, the number of irregular arrivals continues to decline. That is good, our policies are working. But there is a sense of frustration among Europeans. The accumulated pressure over the years has put a strain on Member States' resources, and I want to be very clear that our rules are also very clear. Legal migration is welcome, and we will need more of it. But illegal migration is not, and we cannot tolerate the smugglers and traffickers working against our rules, and there is a feeling that our rules are not always enforced. So we all must do something against this.

First, we must ensure that the Pact on Migration and Asylum is fully implemented. It balances fairness, responsibility and solidarity among all 27 Member States. The pact has been agreed by all 27; all of us have agreed to the pact, and now we must all follow the rules. The work programme also includes new action, from a stronger Frontex, to the digitalisation of returns, to sanctions against smugglers and traffickers. But we also need to agree quickly to the common European system of returns. Let me give you again one figure, I think this is telling: out of those migrants that are applying for asylum and that have a negative asylum decision, 80脗聽% do not leave, but stay here in Europe, and this cannot be. Everyone must abide to our rules and they must be enforced. That is a principle in a democracy that is working.

Our democracy must also deliver to those who suffer the impacts of climate change. We must protect people and nature from devastating wildfires. We must preserve our precious water, that is the most vital of all natural resources, and we must deploy the best science to protect our ocean. Because to all of us, our children and grandchildren will never forgive us if we do not rise to the challenge right now and right here.

Our democracy must also deliver on our children's safety. Social media is changing the way children are growing up on an unimaginable scale. It is completely rewiring childhood. It's true that social media has many benefits for connecting people, without any question, but it is also often designed to create addiction and dependence, specifically in vulnerable children and teenagers. And there is now growing scientific evidence about the enormous harm that a screen-based childhood poses that is replacing the play-based childhood.

So we should take action. Some Member States are leading the way in setting a digital minimum age. We are now setting up a panel of experts that will soon deliver its recommendations, and then we will act on this in the coming year, and I really hope for your support. Because we should be absolutely clear that it is parents that raise their children and not algorithms. This has to be a rule for all of us.

Honourable Members, last month I told you that Europe is in a fight and this is, first and foremost, a fight for our values. Some say we should give up on our deepest beliefs to keep pace with a changing world. I fundamentally disagree. As the world gets more troubled, we must, even more, hold tight to the ideas, the freedoms, the rights that have made us who we are. We must find new ways to incarnate our age-old values. We must find new ways to uphold the rule of law, to protect independent media from being captured or dismantled, new ways to fight corruption in our democracies and to shield democracy from foreign interference. This is also what an independent Europe means. Our values are enshrined in the marble of our constitutions and declarations. They are the living heritage of our turbulent, yet tenacious, history. And we will not let any foreign autocrats tell us what to believe in 芒聙聯 this is our choice.

Honourable Members, Europe's independence moment is the moment to come together, above and beyond our legitimate differences, of course. And this is my final message to you today: I will always be open to finding common solutions. I will always be ready to listen and to engage. We must all be able, and ready, to find common ground. And, most importantly, then really to take action for Europe.

Thank you very much, and long live Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jeroen Lenaers, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Madam Commission President, Commissioner, dear colleagues, the EPP welcomes the Commission's 2026 work programme as an important blueprint for a stronger, more independent and more united European Union, and the emphasis on unity, speed and ambition must guide us in addressing today's challenges. For us, the key priorities remain Europe's competitiveness, security and migration, although I admit that on migration, the proposals are on the table and it is up to us in this House to finally deliver.

On the other two, though, we expect the Commission to be more courageous, determined and ambitious than ever. Competitiveness must come first and let me point out three points. One, our companies, especially our SMEs, need breathing space in order to innovate and grow. Simplification, reducing bureaucracy is crucial and must go hand-in-hand with investments and a positive competitiveness agenda. We need to indeed unleash the full potential of the single market and the 'Draghi plus' sounds very welcoming. We like steps like the 28th regime, GDPR simplification, the savings and investments union next to supporting our industrial sectors, bringing down energy costs and strengthening the position of our farmers.

Secondly, we must learn from the past. Much of this mandate has been spent in improving or correcting already existing legislation. This is necessary, but when it comes to new legislation, we must get it right from the start. We also must not be afraid to withdraw existing proposals or legislation.

The third point 芒聙聯 new legislation must be carefully considered. Simplifying with one hand while piling on new rules with the other makes no sense. We will also scrutinise the 2026 programme through this lens, and there is work to do.

Next, competitiveness security remains a top priority both internally and externally, and I am very happy that now the Commission work programme explicitly also mentions the Drone Defence Initiative. I missed it in earlier leaks and this is why it's always good to prepare also with the newest proposals, but it is very important. For the EPP, flagship projects like an EU drone wall must be fully and rapidly developed. The clock is ticking and is not running in our favour.

When it comes to internal security, we also fully support strengthening Europol and Frontex with better resources, improved data access and enhanced operational capabilities. We must stand shoulder to shoulder with those who protect us everyday 芒聙聯 police officers, they deserve our support and our protection so that they can perform their duties in keeping all of us safe.

Above all, and you already mentioned this Commission President, we must fiercely protect our most vulnerable 芒聙聯 our children. The online world can be a dangerous place, filled with threats like cyberbullying and exploitation. This is why our EPP initiatives to combat cyberbullying and to keep children safe online are not just policies, they are a solemn promise to create a safe digital environment where every child can grow, learn and thrive without fear.

The EPP stands ready to work closely with the Commission and with all colleagues in this House to keep Europe competitive, secure and united. Together, we must build an independent EU fit for today's challenges and for tomorrow's opportunities.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Iratxe Garc脙颅a P脙漏rez, en nombre del Grupo S&D. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, presidenta Von der Leyen, hoy podemos decir con claridad que una parte importante de este programa de trabajo de la Comisi脙鲁n Europea lleva el sello socialdem脙鲁crata, y es la prueba de que el di脙隆logo, la cooperaci脙鲁n y el compromiso entre las fuerzas europe脙颅stas dan resultados concretos para los ciudadanos. Frente al ruido y la divisi脙鲁n, hemos elegido el camino de la responsabilidad y de la acci脙鲁n.

Gracias al trabajo conjunto entre la Comisi脙鲁n y el Parlamento y al impulso de nuestro Grupo pol脙颅tico, la Uni脙鲁n Europea contar脙隆 con un plan europeo de vivienda asequible. Sabemos que el hogar no es un bien de lujo, sino un derecho. Revisaremos las normas sobre ayudas de Estado para que los municipios puedan construir vivienda p脙潞blica sin miedo a ser penalizados. Y avanzaremos con una propuesta legislativa sobre los alquileres de corta duraci脙鲁n para garantizar que nuestras ciudades sigan siendo habitables y no parques de inversi脙鲁n.

Esta es una victoria de la pol脙颅tica que pone a las personas por delante del mercado, pero no hay dignidad sin empleos y sin derechos. Por eso, hemos logrado incluirlo en una estrategia de la UE de lucha contra la pobreza con objetivos concretos y financiaci脙鲁n real, incluyendo la garant脙颅a infantil reforzada para que en la Uni脙鲁n Europea ning脙潞n ni脙卤o ni ninguna ni脙卤a crezca sin oportunidades. Y porque el futuro del trabajo se est脙隆 definiendo ahora.

Impulsamos un acta de empleo de calidad con propuestas legislativas para regular el uso de la inteligencia artificial en el lugar de trabajo con el objeto de garantizar el derecho a la desconexi脙鲁n y el teletrabajo.

Este es el verdadero rostro del progreso europeo. Y ese programa tambi脙漏n exige mirar al futuro con visi脙鲁n transformadora. La econom脙颅a europea no solo ser脙隆 fuerte si genera empleos verdes. Por eso, defendemos un Pacto Verde Europeo con justicia social.

La Uni脙鲁n Europea debe mantener el rumbo y asegurar el objetivo clim脙隆tico del 90脗聽% para 2040 con legislaci脙鲁n concreta. No basta con reducir emisiones, debemos adaptarnos. Por eso 芒聙聰adem脙隆s de aumentar los recursos del Fondo Social para el Clima芒聙聰 impulsaremos una ley europea de adaptaci脙鲁n y una directiva de transici脙鲁n justa. Porque la justicia clim脙隆tica y la justicia social van de la mano.

Tambi脙漏n hemos dado un paso decisivo en competitividad sostenible. Revisar la Directiva sobre contrataci脙鲁n p脙潞blica para introducir criterios sociales y medioambientales es una forma de reforzar asimismo el papel de las pymes y de los contratistas regionales. Tambi脙漏n lo es la creaci脙鲁n de un programa europeo para el leasing social de veh脙颅culos para incentivar una movilidad limpia y accesible, y as脙颅 lo pidi脙鲁 nuestro Grupo pol脙颅tico hace una semana.

Perm脙颅tanme terminar poniendo en valor la referencia que se ha hecho a la protecci脙鲁n de los menores en las redes sociales. Creo que significa poner encima de la mesa una gran preocupaci脙鲁n de las familias europeas de c脙鲁mo protegemos a nuestros menores frente a los desaf脙颅os que hay en estos momentos.

Se脙卤or脙颅as, este programa es la demostraci脙鲁n de que la Uni脙鲁n Europea avanza cuando dialoga, no cuando se bloquea.

Pero ahora viene lo m脙隆s importante: convertir estas propuestas en realidad. Ha llegado el momento de elegir las alianzas que trasladamos aqu脙颅 en el Parlamento para trabajar. Podemos trabajar juntos para cumplir ese programa o pueden seguir mirando hacia otros lados con agendas totalmente distintas.

La verdad es que nuestra Uni脙鲁n no puede permitirse en estos momentos ning脙潞n error. El Grupo socialdem脙鲁crata elige seguir construyendo porque creemos en una Europa que protege y que progresa, en una Europa que une generaciones y territorios, en una Europa que no se encoge ante el futuro, sino que lo conquista con valent脙颅a. Sigamos las fuerzas europe脙颅stas construyendo esa Europa, una Europa que sigue siendo la mayor promesa de esperanza en nuestro tiempo.

脗聽脗聽Val脙漏rie Hayer, au nom du groupe Renew. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, enfin, enfin, ce document qu'on attendait avec le groupe Renew, un agenda de travail pour l'ann脙漏e 2026, un agenda positif, un agenda de puissance, d'ind脙漏pendance, de souverainet脙漏 脙聽 conqu脙漏rir et 脙聽 pr脙漏server pour l'Europe. Enfin, nous allons pouvoir avancer, produire des r脙漏sultats pour les citoyens europ脙漏ens. Et je le dis, il est plus que temps.

Alors oui, chers coll脙篓gues, quand 脙搂a va dans le bon sens, il faut le dire et on le dit. Sur la table, on a maintenant un agenda de travail sur la comp脙漏titivit脙漏, avec la mise en 脜聯uvre du rapport Draghi: 28e脗聽r脙漏gime, innovation, pr脙漏f脙漏rence europ脙漏enne, simplification du march脙漏 unique ou encore Union des march脙漏s de capitaux. Un agenda de travail pour r脙漏pondre 脙聽 la crise de l'脙漏nergie qui p脙篓se tant sur notre pouvoir d'achat. Un agenda de travail sur une vraie d脙漏fense europ脙漏enne, le renforcement du mandat d'Europol et l脙聽 encore, enfin, le recrutement des 30脗聽000脗聽gardes-fronti脙篓res qu'on attendait depuis 2019.

Un agenda de travail sur le logement, sur la protection de nos enfants sur les r脙漏seaux sociaux ou encore la d脙漏fense de nos agriculteurs contre les pratiques commerciales d脙漏loyales. Un agenda de travail aussi sur la simplification en direction des citoyens. On a fait beaucoup sur la simplification en direction des entreprises, mais on n'avait rien fait r脙漏ellement en direction des citoyens. Avec le groupe Renew, on y a travaill脙漏 ces derni脙篓res semaines. On vous a fait des propositions la semaine derni脙篓re et merci, Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, de les avoir int脙漏gr脙漏es dans votre agenda de travail pour 2026, c'est un signal tr脙篓s important.

Chers coll脙篓gues, je ne vais pas ici faire la liste de tous les textes qui nous attendent, mais je voudrais vous dire une chose, vous redire une chose: merci, Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, d'avoir jou脙漏 le jeu de l'脙漏coute. D'avoir 脙漏cout脙漏 le groupe Renew et d'avoir jou脙漏 le jeu d'une r脙漏elle concertation. Je voudrais souligner aussi votre pr脙漏sence aujourd'hui, le fait que vous pr脙漏sentiez vous-m脙陋me ce programme de travail de la Commission europ脙漏enne. C'est un signal tr脙篓s fort de votre leadership politique, en particulier pour cette ann脙漏e qui sera capitale pour l'avenir de l'Union europ脙漏enne. Un tr脙篓s grand merci 脙漏galement pour 脙搂a.

Cet agenda, il est bon, il est bon pour les Europ脙漏ens et on le soutient 脙聽 1脗聽000脗聽%. Et il faut le dire aussi entre nous 芒聙聯 脙搂a a 脙漏t脙漏 脙漏voqu脙漏 par certains de mes coll脙篓gues 芒聙聯 il va nous redonner de la dynamique dans ce Parlement. Quand je dis nous, je pense 脙聽 votre majorit脙漏, madame von der Leyen. Depuis notre 脙漏lection en juin 2024, cette majorit脙漏 a connu quelques dysfonctionnements, il faut le dire, et j'ai eu l'occasion plusieurs fois dans cet h脙漏micycle, de rappeler l'importance de cette majorit脙漏. C'est pour 脙搂a que le groupe Renew s'est toujours acharn脙漏 脙聽 la faire vivre. Parce que pour que cet agenda sur l'ind脙漏pendance europ脙漏enne vive, eh bien ce n'est pas sur l'extr脙陋me droite ou sur l'extr脙陋me gauche que nous devons compter. C'est nous-m脙陋mes, nous les forces proeurop脙漏ennes, qui allons le mettre en 脜聯uvre.

Alors je n'aurai ici qu'un mot d'ordre: la responsabilit脙漏. Nos 脙漏lecteurs, chers coll脙篓gues, nous regardent et partout dans nos 脙聣tats membres, je vois que nos d脙漏mocraties sont en crise. Je vois que les Europ脙漏ens ont l'impression que les choses leur 脙漏chappent, que leurs repr脙漏sentants n'agissent pas dans leur int脙漏r脙陋t, que le pouvoir est ailleurs, dans d'autres pays ou m脙陋me aux mains de certaines multinationales. Chers coll脙篓gues, cette responsabilit脙漏 dont je vous parle, c'est celle de l'action. C'est assumer que l'Europe est une 脙漏chelle fondamentale pour r脙漏pondre 脙聽 des probl脙篓mes qui deviennent trop grands pour nos 脙聣tats membres. C'est maintenant que 脙搂a se joue. Les 脙漏lections, c'脙漏tait il y a un an et demi. Alors on y va.

脗聽脗聽Marie Toussaint, au nom du groupe Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, il n'y a pas d'amour, il n'y a que des preuves d'amour. Et si je commence aujourd'hui par ce po脙篓me fran脙搂ais assez connu, c'est pour poser la question, Madame la Pr脙漏sidente: quelle preuve d'amour est ce nouvel affaiblissement de la loi sur la d脙漏forestation que vous nous avez propos脙漏 aujourd'hui, malgr脙漏 toutes vos promesses. Et je veux ici rappeler que, comme l'amour, la confiance doit se d脙漏cliner par des actes.

Mais venons-en au programme de travail que vous nous proposez pour l'ann脙漏e prochaine. Et voyez-vous, le premier point de bascule environnementale a 脙漏t脙漏 d脙漏pass脙漏 la semaine derni脙篓re, nous ont dit les scientifiques, avec le d脙漏p脙漏rissement g脙漏n脙漏ralis脙漏 des coraux, tandis que la pauvret脙漏 continue de condamner des millions d'Europ脙漏ennes et d'Europ脙漏ens. Notre conviction, c'est donc, cela ne vous surprendra pas, que ce programme doit porter une action r脙漏solue pour la protection de ce que nous avons de plus pr脙漏cieux: la vie, la justice, la dignit脙漏. Non pas d脙漏r脙漏guler avec une nouvelle vague d'omnibus, mais acc脙漏l脙漏rer la transformation 脙漏cologique dans la justice sociale.

Nous nous r脙漏jouissons d'y voir figurer un projet d'adaptation au d脙漏r脙篓glement climatique et d'acc脙漏l脙漏ration des actions en faveur de l'efficacit脙漏 脙漏nerg脙漏tique ou encore des renouvelables, ou enfin, enfin, un plan de sortie des subventions aux 脙漏nergies fossiles. Ce sont l脙聽 des mesures qui prot脙篓gent. Mais encore nous faut-il nous donner les moyens financiers d'atteindre nos objectifs. Et de ce c脙麓t脙漏, le b脙垄t blesse un peu. Vous nous annoncez un omnibus sur la taxation, mais visera-t-il 脙聽 taxer 脙漏quitablement les multinationales? Visera-t-il 脙聽 demander aux milliardaires de contribuer 脙聽 la transformation 脙漏cologique dans la justice sociale?

Et nous sommes aussi inquiets, inquiets de ce 28e r脙漏gime que vous annoncez, dont nous n'avons aucune garantie qu'il ne participera pas une nouvelle fois 脙聽 la d脙漏r脙漏gulation. Inquiets parce que, si nous accueillons avec beaucoup d'espoir la strat脙漏gie de lutte contre la pauvret脙漏, comment comprendre qu'elle ne soit suivie d'aucun acte l脙漏gislatif? Comment comprendre que, dans la cat脙漏gorie 脙漏quit脙漏, il y ait trois textes, mais aucun qui soit contraignant?

Alors nous vous appelons 脙聽 r脙漏sister aux sir脙篓nes de l'union des droites, qui ne sont d'ailleurs pas l脙聽 aujourd'hui, qui veulent mettre 脙聽 terre le mod脙篓le de protection sociale et environnementale et 脙聽 choisir la solidarit脙漏 plut脙麓t que la peur, la justice plut脙麓t que la r脙漏signation, l'Europe simplement.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Li Andersson, on behalf of The Left Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, what were the main reasons Europeans voted in the last European parliamentary election? It was rising prices and the cost of living. And what are the issues that they think the European Parliament should address as the main priority? Inflation, rising prices and the cost of living.

I think we have been given a pretty clear message, and a very clear mandate to act. That is why, last year, it was a shock to see zero legal initiatives in the work programme in the field of employment and social policy. This year, luckily, this has been corrected, with several legal and non-legal initiatives for employment and social policy.

The quality jobs act will be especially important. We want to see restrictions on subcontracting and rules for using algorithms and AI in the workplace. We want to secure workers' right to training and address psychosocial risks. And we also want to ensure that while we take one step forward, we do not take two steps back. The 28th regime and the citizens' omnibus, whatever that will mean, can in no way mean lowering labour standards.

The EU cannot afford to disregard the clear message and mandate from the people. I think this mandate should not be about deregulating for big business. This should be about the rights and the livelihood of the European people.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Christine Anderson, on behalf of the ESN Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Ms von der Leyen, it's me again. I'm sorry. But looking at your work programme, I have to say I haven't seen a document this full of ridiculous promises since you, Ms脗聽von der Leyen, swore to save all of your emails.

But you have the audacity to call it 'Europe's independence moment'. Independence from what? Common sense? Well, congrats, you nailed that one.

Common sense would mean scrapping the Green Deal, smashing the ban on combustion engines, stopping the boats, shredding the DSA, cutting red tape and taxes, recognising there are only two sexes 芒聙聯 men and women 芒聙聯 and, most of all, cutting back on the EU's power.

Instead of trusting the Member States to make their own choices, you micromanage them into oblivion.

The truth is, you can't even be trusted with running a lemonade stand. You would spend three years writing a lemon strategy, only to find out that you don't have any lemons 芒聙聯 for which, of course, you would then blame Trump, Putin and Orb脙隆n.

What Europe really needs is for you to step down 芒聙聯 the sooner, the better.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Markus Ferber (PPE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Frau Kommissionspr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen, liebe Kollegen! Das neue Arbeitsprogramm der Kommission tr脙陇gt ja einen sehr ambitionierten Titel: 芒聙聻Europas Unabh脙陇ngigkeitsmoment芒聙聺. Aber wir m脙录ssen diesem Titel auch gerecht werden. Wir stehen in einer Phase wirtschaftlicher Unsicherheit, und deswegen brauchen wir jetzt auch ein Programm, das Wachstum, Wettbewerbsf脙陇higkeit und Investitionen in den Mittelpunkt stellt. Wenn Sie sagen, Europa soll wieder ein attraktiver Standort f脙录r Unternehmer und f脙录r Unternehmen werden, dann muss das auch in den Vorschl脙陇gen erkennbar sein.

Es gibt einige positive Elemente: der Fokus auf dem Binnenmarkt. Hier haben wir wirklich einen Schatz zu heben. Aber wir m脙录ssen uns auch mal etwas zutrauen. Wir m脙录ssen auch mal etwas wagen, auch wenn die Mitgliedstaaten vielleicht Widerstand leisten. Wir waren gerade mit einer Delegation des Wirtschaftsausschusses bei der Jahrestagung des Internationalen W脙陇hrungsfonds. Hier wurde uns ein Spiegel vorgehalten: Euer Binnenmarkt ist kein Binnenmarkt. Da m脙录ssen wir wirklich rangehen. Da m脙录ssen wir ambitioniert sein.

Wir m脙录ssen aber auch schauen, dass wir wettbewerbsf脙陇hig sind im wirtschaftlichen Bereich, aber auch im Bereich der Finanzen und der Banken. Deswegen h脙陇tte ich mich gefreut, wenn auch ein Finanz-Omnibus auf diese Strecke der Omnibusse gestellt wird, damit wir hier mit einfacheren Regeln etwas erreichen k脙露nnen. Das hei脙聼t nicht Deregulierung, sondern Simplifizierung, die Dinge einfacher machen脗聽芒聙聯 dann sind wir auf dem richtigen Weg. 2026 sollte das Jahr werden, in dem wir in Europa vom Regelmacher zum Erm脙露glicher werden. Wir m脙录ssen viel mehr erm脙露glichen und nicht immer nur alles versuchen zu regulieren. Wenn wir das miteinander hinbekommen, dann wird Europa wirklich in eine gute Zukunft gehen.

脗聽脗聽Camilla Laureti (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, alcune priorit脙聽 dell'agenda socialdemocratica trovano spazio nel programma per il 2026: penso alle iniziative sul lavoro di qualit脙聽, il costo dell'energia, la lotta alla povert脙聽, le garanzie per l'infanzia. Sono strumenti coerenti con il pilastro europeo dei diritti sociali e con le nostre richieste, ma non basta.

La casa 脙篓 diventata ormai una vera emergenza sociale. Bene intervenire su affitti brevi e aiuti di Stato ma serve una risposta strutturale, un fondo europeo per l'edilizia sociale e un bilancio dedicato.

E poi c'脙篓 il nodo cruciale delle risorse: senza una capacit脙聽 di prestito comune, senza un PNRR europeo prorogato, senza un bilancio pluriennale che protegga le politiche sociali, la coesione, la politica agricola comune con fondi dedicati, l'Europa non potr脙聽 rispondere alle sfide che ha davanti.

Oggi pi脙鹿 che mai serve coraggio politico, serve un'Europa come un solo Stato: coraggio per scegliere la solidariet脙聽, per investire nelle persone e per costruire un'Unione che non lasci davvero indietro nessuno.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pascale Piera (PfE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, n'ayons pas peur des mots, le programme de travail de la Commission pour 2026 est schizophr脙篓ne et d脙漏lirant. Schizophr脙篓ne, parce qu'apr脙篓s avoir 脙漏touff脙漏 les entreprises et les citoyens europ脙漏ens dans une fr脙漏n脙漏tique ivresse normative, les m脙陋mes technocrates proposent d脙漏sormais de revenir sur leurs propres textes au pr脙漏texte gonfl脙漏 d'une simplification.

En r脙漏alit脙漏, les paquets omnibus sur l'脙漏nergie, la fiscalit脙漏 et la cr脙漏ation d'un 28谩碌聣脗聽r脙漏gime et tous les autres programmes du m脙陋me acabit donnent le vertige. Avoir tout compliqu脙漏 pour d脙漏sormais tout simplifier, quelle est cette folie? En politique, cela s'appelle du r脙漏trop脙漏dalage. En psychiatrie, cela s'appelle de la schizophr脙漏nie.

Ce programme est 脙漏galement d脙漏lirant. Non contente d'aspirer m脙漏thodiquement la souverainet脙漏 des 脙聣tats, la pieuvre europ脙漏enne va d脙漏sormais s'en prendre franchement 脙聽 la vie des citoyens. En v脙漏rit脙漏, c'est toujours le m脙陋me discours mensonger et l脙漏nifiant en vue du bien de tous.

Nous attendent donc au menu une strat脙漏gie d'脙漏quit脙漏 interg脙漏n脙漏rationnelle, un passeport europ脙漏en de s脙漏curit脙漏 sociale et, pour la bonne bouche, une initiative pour la r脙漏silience sanitaire mondiale. La Commission appelle avec culot ce programme 脗芦le moment de l'ind脙漏pendance de l'Europe脗禄. Au final, ne nous attendent que dumping fiscal, dumping social, cr脙漏ation d'un imp脙麓t europ脙漏en et enfermement toujours plus grand dans un Titanic fou qui se heurtera au mur de la dette. C'est un monde de la d脙漏mesure, un monde de la vanit脙漏, une structure qui ne peut qu'脙漏chouer.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Francesco Torselli (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, Presidente von der Leyen, onorevoli colleghi, ascoltando le sue parole oggi sulla concorrenza sleale, sulla difesa dell'indipendenza e della produttivit脙聽 europea, si capisce bene come Lei, al di l脙聽 del ruolo che le impone di non dirlo apertamente, sia ben consapevole dei danni che cinque anni di脗聽Green Deal hanno causato alle imprese e agli agricoltori europei.

Ecco perch脙漏 oggi siamo a farle una semplice richiesta: l'Unione europea sia pragmatica nel mettere a terra gli aiuti e gli incentivi necessari a far sopravvivere le nostre imprese: snellimento burocratico, formazione del personale all'utilizzo delle nuove tecnologie, regole chiare e 芒聙聯 come ha detto Lei 芒聙聯 omogenee. Questo ci chiedono i nostri imprenditori e i nostri agricoltori. E non si sono mai sottratti 芒聙聯 badate bene 芒聙聯 alle sfide delle innovazioni tecnologiche e della transizione.

Lei per脙虏, dopo quello che ha detto oggi, deve guardarli in faccia e fornire loro gli strumenti necessari per difendersi da chi non rispetta quelle stesse regole che l'Europa negli ultimi anni si 脙篓 autoimposta.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Billy Kelleher (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, I welcome the publication of the work programme for 2026. I also welcome many of the aspects that are included from the Renew Group.

You speak about competitiveness, cost of living, jobs, growth and investment, and research and innovation. Of course, the most fundamental issue that we need to address is the harmonisation and the deepening of the single market, coupled with the 28th regime and bringing forward the savings and investment union. If we do not achieve those things, we undermine all that we are trying to do in the context of this work programme, but more broadly. So over the next number of months, my group will play its part in ensuring that the work programme can move forward with speed, to react to the issues that you have outlined as being very serious.

'Draghi Plus' has been mentioned. Of course, Draghi is the Bible across Europe at the moment, but we do need to accept that there are many things in Draghi that need to be done quickly. Most fundamentally, the only way we can untap the single market and the private market is by bringing forward that savings and investment union. I hope that you put strong emphasis on that particular project.

On an issue that is political,脗聽President von der Leyen, the people in this Chamber who are primarily supportive of this work programme sit from here to here, and it doesn't always work. You have an obligation, President, to ensure that the platform does work, and that we try and work from the centre out on the policies that you have outlined in your programme. Otherwise, you will have significant challenges in this Chamber in trying to get through a work programme that is critical to the citizens of Europe. So I do hope that strong emphasis will be put on the platform and ensuring that this programme goes ahead smoothly.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Damian Boeselager (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, colleagues, imagine a little boy, Omar, who was born to Palestinian parents in Jordan. When Omar turned 15, he emigrated to the US, where he arrived with a very basic level of English.

Now, for some in this room, this triggers an almost instinctive negative reaction. In your minds, Omar doesn't just arrive as Omar, but is a dangerous threat to our primarily white, Christian culture.

Just last week, actually, German Chancellor Merz dangerously connected deportations with the idea of improvement of the image of our cities. Omar, it is implied, doesn't belong next to our cathedrals and half-timbered houses.

Of course, if you go further to the right, the AfD openly flirts with the idea of remigration 芒聙聯 the unconstitutional idea of explicitly kicking 25脗聽% of Germans out of our country.

President, in your plans, your work programme, we can sadly see how much of this negative discourse has also made it here on the European level. We don't see a single positive, comprehensive proposal for labour migration. And we really need it 芒聙聯 our businesses need it on all skill levels.

So, President, we need your loud voice to speak the truth about migration. There is no competitiveness without international talent.

Fifteen-year-old immigrant Omar turned into 60-year-old Professor Omar Yaghi, one of the six Nobel Prize laureates from the US this year 芒聙聯 half of whom, by the way, were immigrants.

Will you, President, finally stand up for the real stories of the millions of immigrants in Europe, and for those who still need to join us every year to make us competitive? Will you ensure that the motto 'United in Diversity' is true for the Philips but also for the Omars in this country, and in this Union?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Leila Chaibi (The Left). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Madame la Pr脙漏sidente de la Commission europ脙漏enne, chers coll脙篓gues, je voudrais vous rappeler quelque chose d'important. Le Parlement europ脙漏en est compos脙漏 de l脙漏gislateurs, autrement dit de personnes qui ont 脙漏t脙漏 脙漏lues pour construire des lois, des r脙篓gles qui permettent d'am脙漏liorer la vie des Europ脙漏ens. Cela semble 脙漏vident, mais il me semble fondamental de vous le rappeler, car, depuis seize mois et le d脙漏but de ce nouveau mandat, j'ai personnellement l'impression que la Commission attend de nous de d脙漏tricoter les lois plut脙麓t que de l脙漏gif脙漏rer.

Devoir de vigilance, pacte vert, r脙漏gulation du num脙漏rique, tous ces textes que nous avons adopt脙漏s pour prot脙漏ger les Europ脙漏ens, vous les d脙漏tricotez, vous les contournez, vous les sabordez avec vos nombreux omnibus et d脙漏sormais un 脗芦citizen omnibus脗禄. Qu'est-ce que c'est? On ne sait pas. On s'attend au pire.

Vous appelez cela simplification? Ce travail de sape, nous le subissons au quotidien ici. Il y a encore quelques mois, il existait un consensus pour des directives sur le droit 脙聽 la d脙漏connexion, sur l'intelligence artificielle au travail, sur la sous-traitance et aujourd'hui tout cela para脙庐t remis en question. Au royaume de la simplification, r脙漏guler est quasiment devenu un outrage.

Madame脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, je vous propose qu'on aille jusqu'au bout de votre logique. On va simplifier la d脙漏mocratie, on va supprimer le Parlement europ脙漏en et on va vous laisser gouverner toute seule, tranquille, l'Union europ脙漏enne, une Union europ脙漏enne sans foi ni loi.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Karlo Ressler (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovani predsjedavaju脛聡i, povjereni脛聧e, kolegice i kolege, radni program Komisije za sljede脛聡u godinu dolazi u trenutku kada Europa prakti脛聧ki nema druge alternative nego u praksi dokazati da mo脜戮e djelovati u脛聧inkovito i odlu脛聧no.

Pred nama su jasni izazovi: naru脜隆ena sigurnost, uzdrmano gospodarstvo, a sve vi脜隆e i naru脜隆eno povjerenje gra脛聭ana u europske institucije. Zato pozivam Komisiju da temeljem ovog programa fokus u sljede脛聡oj godini stavi na tri klju脛聧na cilja. Prvo, sigurnost Europe u rastu脛聡oj neizvjesnosti ratnih sukoba. Drugo, ja脛聧anje konkurentnosti, uklju脛聧uju脛聡i jednostavnijim pravilima javne nabave i energetsku neovisnost i tre脛聡e, smanjenje birokratskog tereta koje gu脜隆i inovacije i poduzetni脜隆tvo, pa i kroz uvo脛聭enje 28. re脜戮ima. Pri planiranju i dono脜隆enju bilo kakvih novih europskih pravila pozivamo na krajnje konzervativan i oprezan pristup, da se 脛聧esto u dobroj namjeri na kraju ne nastave stvarati nova dodatna optere脛聡enja. Novu regulaciju treba svesti na ono 脜隆to je zaista krajnje nu脜戮no, a naglasak sada staviti na pojednostavljivanje, evaluaciju u脛聧inka i pametnu provedbu, osobito klju脛聧nih akata kao 脜隆to su Pakt za migracije i azil ili Akta za umjetnu inteligenciju. Konkretnih rezultata ne脛聡e biti ni bez smislenog dugogodi脜隆njeg prora脛聧una usmjerenog na prave prioritete i ravnomjerni razvoj svih europskih krajeva.

Vrijeme je za vratiti se osnovama. Vrijeme je za vratiti se zdravorazumskim politikama, ali isto tako i za suradnju svih onih koji 脜戮ele dobro Europi.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mohammed Chahim (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr President, the question today is: will we invest in our future or keep pretending that short-term thinking and lowering ambition will save us? Let me give you an example with the car industry, because every expert agrees 芒聙聯 the future of the car industry lies with the electric vehicle, and we are behind. A single American or Chinese manufacturer produces more electric vehicles than all European car makers together. Instead of focusing on catching up, instead of using common sense by investing in social leasing and investing in battery infrastructure, some here are asking to subsidise expensive synthetic fuels or biofuels. Mind you, these limited fuels are needed for aviation, for maritime and for our defence.

Then there are the rumours about lowering safety standards for small cars. Just imagine that your grandmother or mother is driving that car and then has an accident. Is that European competitiveness? I truly hope that these are just rumours because I'm disgusted by them. When companies choose profit over our safety, over our lives, then this Parliament, whether it is about cars, chemicals or agriculture, should stand firm. That's the Europe I believe in.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Dear colleague, you have a blue card from Mr脗聽Damian Boeselager. Do you accept it?

脗聽脗聽Damian Boeselager (Verts/ALE), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 I didn't expect such a formal 'yes'. Dear Mr Chahim, you talked about battery infrastructure and, I was just wondering, it seems that we are ten years behind in our technology when it comes to batteries and previous industrial policy cases, for example with Northvolt, didn't seem to work so well. So what is your idea of how to make Europe competitive when it comes to batteries? Thank you.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mohammed Chahim (S&D), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 I call it the 'Airbus approach', you know, when our aviation sector was having trouble 芒聙聯 also with unfair competition coming at that time from the US, I think our car industry has the same but now it's about China. I think we have to stick our brightest heads together, invest together in European reuse of batteries, European battery infrastructure, European software and components. All the car manufacturers are competing with each other by having their own Chinese company that they cooperate with 芒聙聯 that does not help. I think the European electric car has become more affordable, but social leasing schemes are also very helpful. You help the sector and you make a product acceptable and accessible for all Europeans, and this is something we should all support.

脗聽脗聽Nicolas Bay (ECR). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Mme脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen vient de nous parler pour 2026 de prosp脙漏rit脙漏 et de comp脙漏titivit脙漏. Mais c'est elle qui a 脙漏touff脙漏 notre comp脙漏titivit脙漏 avec son pacte vert. Il devait verdir l'脙漏conomie europ脙漏enne, il ne la fait que p脙垄lir. Comme Macron qui c脙篓de sur sa r脙漏forme des retraites pour rester en place, elle d脙漏tricote aujourd'hui ses propres r脙漏alisations pour sauver la face. Mais elle ne va ni assez loin, ni assez vite.

Il faut les lib脙漏rer du carcan r脙漏glementaire et pr脙漏tendument 脙漏cologique. 150脗聽milliards d'euros de co脙禄ts administratifs p脙篓sent aujourd'hui sur nos entreprises europ脙漏ennes. Cela leur co脙禄te jusqu'脙聽 2,5脗聽% de leur chiffre d'affaires. 60脗聽% des entreprises consid脙篓rent que la r脙漏glementation entrave leurs investissements. 55脗聽% des PME estiment que la r脙漏glementation environnementale europ脙漏enne est un obstacle majeur 脙聽 leur d脙漏veloppement.

De m脙陋me, il faut laisser tranquilles nos agriculteurs. Ils sont assomm脙漏s de normes et, dans le m脙陋me temps, des accords de libre-脙漏change avec le monde entier sont sign脙漏s, au moment m脙陋me o脙鹿 le monde entier r脙漏tablit des protections douani脙篓res.

脗聽脗聽Morten L脙赂kkegaard (Renew). 芒聙聯 Hr. formand! Det er ikke s脙楼 tit, man st脙楼r her p脙楼 talerstolen og skal g脙楼 i gang med at rose. Men jeg m脙楼 jo sige, at der kom mange rigtig, rigtig gode toner fra pr脙娄sidenten her i dag.

Jeg synes, at man skal rose, f脙赂rst og fremmest, at der er kommet et initiativ med, der handler om s脙楼kaldte "citizens omnibus", alts脙楼 et borgerinitiativ. Jeg kan forsikre venstrefl脙赂jen, som straks var ude og sige, at det her kunne n脙娄sten ikke v脙娄re sandt, med at det er faktisk sandt, og at der kommer en r脙娄kke gode forslag ud af det initiativ.

Ellers vil jeg sige, at von der Leyen jo betonede som det f脙赂rste i sin tale, og n脙娄ppe tilf脙娄ldigt, at det er konkurrenceevnen, vi skal satse p脙楼. Ogs脙楼 helt efter mit hjerte. Det handler om tre afg脙赂rende ting;

Det 28.-regime, som vi har manglet i flere 脙楼rtier nu, vi har talt meget om det. Vi k脙娄mper stadig med 27 forskellige regels脙娄t. Det er et af hovedproblemerne. Det er simpelthen ikke godt nok. Vi er ved at blive kvalt i bureaukrati. Vi siger det alle. Nu skal vi g脙赂re noget ved det.

S脙楼 er der den annoncerede Business Wallet, som jeg ogs脙楼 gerne vil betone. Det handler nemlig ikke kun om at reducere papirarbejdet. Det handler ogs脙楼 om at skaffe nogle ordentlige digitale v脙娄rkt脙赂jer, som virksomheder rent faktisk kan bruge, som kan forbedre konkurrenceevnen.

Og s脙楼 som det tredje vil jeg ogs脙楼 godt fremh脙娄ve biosolutions. Biosolutions har et enormt potentiale. Der investeres to-tre gange mere i det i USA, end der g脙赂r i EU, b脙楼de offentligt og privat. Vi skal turde investere mere. Vi skal satse. Og det initiativ hilser vi ogs脙楼 rigtig velkommen nu. I kan regne med os, kommiss脙娄r 芒聙聯 vi skal nok v脙娄re der, men husk nu ogs脙楼, at det er vennerne, man skal hj脙娄lpe, og ikke yderfl脙赂jene.

脗聽脗聽Sara Matthieu (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, colleagues, this Commission made us a bold promise: to secure both economic prosperity and the climate and environment. So the clean industrial deal is what we need to protect us against bullies like Trump, unfair competition and climate change.

But what I have seen lately is predominantly the dismantling of climate and environmental policies. Do we really think that Europe is going to catch up with China by simply deregulating?

Listen to what European businesses are asking us to do. They want urgent leadership to translate the 90脗聽% climate target into action, with strategic investment programmes in circular and in cleantech, with demand creation using public procurement or the greening of corporate fleets, with affordable, clean energy and electrification to cut costs and boost our independence, and, of course, with fairness to ensure that this benefits all workers.

Now, we expect a lot from the upcoming proposals of the Commission, like the IDAA, like the circular economy act, like the quality jobs roadmap. But, dear Commission, it is time to deliver.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pablo Arias Echeverr脙颅a (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, el 8脗聽de septiembre de脗聽2024 se public脙鲁 el Informe Draghi sobre la competitividad europea. Todos lo aplaudimos, pues pon脙颅a negro sobre blanco los problemas que tenemos y propuestas para resolverlos. El mismo Draghi, un a脙卤o despu脙漏s, lanz脙鲁 la voz de alarma sobre la inacci脙鲁n como amenaza para nuestra competitividad y soberan脙颅a. Vamos muy lentos.

Si bien es cierto que este primer a脙卤o de legislatura no se ha materializado en grandes cambios, tambi脙漏n lo es que para que estos se produzcan se necesita tiempo para prepararlos. Hoy la Comisi脙鲁n presenta su programa de trabajo para 2026, que va en la buena direcci脙鲁n y transmite una clara intenci脙鲁n: pasar a la acci脙鲁n. Sin embargo, una vez m脙隆s, nos enfrentamos a la principal barrera del mercado 脙潞nico: el proteccionismo de los Estados miembros. Necesitamos tambi脙漏n el compromiso del Consejo para ir un paso m脙隆s all脙隆 en la implementaci脙鲁n de estas recomendaciones. Impulsar un cambio implica ser disruptivos en las medidas que proponemos y rigurosos en su aplicaci脙鲁n. La misma f脙鲁rmula que nos permiti脙鲁 crear el euro y el mercado 脙潞nico: unidad, visi脙鲁n y coraje.

En concreto, necesitamos un mercado 脙潞nico s脙鲁lido que inspire confianza a los j脙鲁venes muchas veces desencantados con el proyecto europeo, que ofrezca seguridad jur脙颅dica a las empresas y pymes, que atraiga y retenga el talento, que sea atractivo para la inversi脙鲁n, la innovaci脙鲁n y el crecimiento y que se regule con normas sencillas y armonizadas: una norma y no veintisiete. El r脙漏gimen 28 puede ser una v脙颅a adecuada para simplificar y agilizar.

La Comisi脙鲁n ya he escrito la partitura, pero 芒聙聰como dicen los ingleses芒聙聰 it takes two to tango. La mayor脙颅a de este Parlamento est脙隆 preparada y comprometida. La pregunta es: 脗驴lo est脙隆 tambi脙漏n el Consejo?

(El orador acepta responder a una pregunta formulada con arreglo al procedimiento de la 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄)

脗聽脗聽Petras Gra脜戮ulis (ESN), pak脛聴lus m脛聴lyn脛聟j脛聟 kortel脛聶 pateiktas klausimas. 芒聙聯 Gerbiamas prane脜隆脛聴jau. Europa buvo 芒聙聻u脜戮lipusi ant didelio gr脛聴blio芒聙聹, t.脗聽y. rusi脜隆k脜鲁 duj脜鲁 ir naftos. 脗聽脗聽Pablo Arias Echeverr脙颅a (PPE), respuesta de 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Le agradezco mucho la pregunta, se脙卤or Gra脜戮ulis, pero la verdad es que yo no he hablado de energ脙颅a, ni verde ni de ning脙潞n otro tipo. 脗聽脗聽Ana Catarina Mendes (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, caros colegas, tenho tr脙陋s notas muito breves.

A senhora presidente da Comiss脙拢o falou sobre competitividade e eu gostava de deixar um alerta para que esta competitividade n脙拢o seja feita 脙聽 custa dos nossos valores fundamentais, mas que seja guiada por uma b脙潞ssola social e uma b脙潞ssola democr脙隆tica claras. 脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira (The Left), Pergunta segundo o procedimento 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Senhora Deputada Ana Catarina Mendes, o seu grupo pol脙颅tico disse estar satisfeito com o programa de trabalho que 脙漏 apresentado pela Comiss脙拢o Europeia.

脗聽脗聽Ana Catarina Mendes (S&D), Resposta segundo o procedimento 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Senhor Deputado Jo脙拢o Oliveira, eu acabei de dizer que este 脙漏 um programa com boas inten脙搂脙碌es e poucas concretiza脙搂脙碌es.

Em Portugal, temos uma crise neste momento muito grande. O aumento do pre脙搂o da habita脙搂脙拢o 脙漏 de 35 % no 脙潞ltimo ano, o que gera muitas desigualdades. Por isso, mais uma vez, assim como em 2022 apel脙隆mos em Portugal para que a Europa tivesse um programa de habita脙搂脙拢o, volto a reiterar, aqui, esse apelo para que haja um programa de habita脙搂脙拢o a s脙漏rio para todos os Estados芒聙聭Membros.

脗聽脗聽Roberto Vannacci (PfE). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, questa Commissione si sforza di presentarci oggi un programma che 脙篓 l'emblema del suo fallimento negli ultimi sei anni.

Al Green Deal, che ha distrutto la nostra economia, e a una politica immigratoria totalmente inadeguata, che ha eroso il nostro stato sociale e che ha minato la gi脙聽 vacua identit脙聽 del vecchio continente, si aggiunge un ReArm Europe che fa gli interessi degli Stati Uniti e della finanza internazionale.

Non solo: ma questa Commissione si ostina nell'assurda dittatura di regole ottuse, invece di lavorare a supporto degli Stati e per il benessere dei popoli e delle nazioni.

Basta con il Green Deal! Basta con l'immigrazione selvaggia! Basta con l'islamizzazione della nostra societ脙聽 e basta con l'erosione progressiva della sovranit脙聽 nazionale! Questo 脙篓 l'unico modo per salvare questa Europa, che sta gi脙聽 sprofondando nel mare della povert脙聽, dell'autoritarismo e della burocrazia.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Stefano Cavedagna (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, Presidente von der Leyen, onorevoli colleghi, l'Europa ha bisogno di una svolta radicale e muscolare.

Bisogna rivedere il regolamento sulle cosiddette auto elettriche, come diciamo da tempo, e apprezziamo l'apertura che c'脙篓 stata nei confronti dei cosiddetti biocarburanti.

Dobbiamo difendere le imprese da scelte insostenibili, in nome di un ambientalismo da salotto che troppo spesso era ecologista e talvolta ideologico, senza portare a delle risposte concrete.

Dobbiamo rivedere il sistema per lo scambio di quote di emissioni: l'idea che, per produrre, le nostre imprese debbano acquistare dei titoli per poter inquinare 芒聙聯 un sistema che non sta in piedi e ci sta portando al declino economico.

Il declino economico non pu脙虏 essere il destino dell'Europa: l'ha detto il presidente Draghi, l'ha detto persino il commissario socialista del precedente mandato, Paolo Gentiloni. Lo chiedono gli europei e i principali partiti della nostra Europa: cambiamo passo, perch脙漏 altrimenti andremo verso il declino.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Svenja Hahn (Renew). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, Kollegen! Made in Europe muss wieder f脙录r Exzellenz statt f脙录r B脙录rokratie stehen. Gut, dass die Kommission endlich Ideen aus dem Draghi芒聙聭Bericht aufgreift. Das 28.脗聽Regime ist 脙录berf脙陇llig. Auch Initiativen zur St脙陇rkung von Innovationen sind wichtig. Wir brauchen Tempo bei k脙录nstlicher Intelligenz, Cloud-Infrastruktur oder Quantencomputern. Ich finde es sehr schade, dass Frau von der Leyen anscheinend nicht der gesamten Plenardebatte lauschen kann, aber ich m脙露chte es ihr trotzdem sehr offen sagen: Frau von der Leyen, ich h脙陇tte mir mehr Mut gew脙录nscht von Ihnen, mehr Mut zur Marktwirtschaft und mehr Mut zum Rotstift. Sie wollen 38 neue Vorschl脙陇ge auf 111 offene draufsetzen, aber nur 25 Vorschl脙陇ge und ein Gesetz streichen und planen nur drei neue Omnibus芒聙聭Vereinfachungen.

Wenn Europa es ernst meint mit Wettbewerbsf脙陇higkeit, m脙录sste es genau andersherum sein. Wir brauchen mehr Abbau als Aufbau von Regulierungen, denn die bisherigen Omnibusgesetze kommen kaum voran. Und es w脙陇re doch fatal, wenn sie zum Symbol europ脙陇ischer Reformunf脙陇higkeit werden. Frau von der Leyen, haben Sie den Mut, gro脙聼 zu denken f脙录r Wettbewerbsf脙陇higkeit, Energieunabh脙陇ngigkeit und einen echten Binnenmarkt. Denn nur ein wirtschaftlich starkes Europa ist ein starkes Europa in der Welt.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Mandl (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, what a pity the Commission President left our plenary debate about the Commission work programme. But I'm more than happy that the most experienced commissioner, Vice-President Maro脜隆 脜聽ef脛聧ovi脛聧, is with us this afternoon, because it is an important matter we have to discuss. I kindly ask the Vice-President to convey the messages of this plenary debate to the Commission President. I have to say, I voted for her in her first election six years ago, in her second election one year ago 芒聙聯 even while the Member States didn't even provide us with more than one candidate for this position, but this is all a future perspective 芒聙聯 and I have defended, as has the majority of this House, the Commission President against the confidence vote we had here.

Having said that, I really want to ask the Commission President to not allow herself to get distracted by these confidence votes from the extremists of this House. Because when I was thinking in the beginning of this mandate that now the right policies will be taken from the side of the European Commission in terms of deregulation for our economy and, of course, also in terms of security and to tackle the crises of our times, then I think it was a positive, optimistic approach of the beginning of a mandate. We must not allow ourselves to be distracted because one extreme wants to destroy our economy, the other extreme wants to destroy our security. We have to take care of both in Europe 芒聙聯 our economy and our security 芒聙聯 because both are existential. We need a Europe with more freedom to the inside and more strength to the outside. Therefore, I kindly ask the Commission President to remain a friend of freedom in that sense, and a leader in terms of security for Europe.

脗聽脗聽Christophe Clergeau (S&D). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, la sant脙漏, la sant脙漏, la sant脙漏, voil脙聽 la grande absente de ce programme de travail pour l'ann脙漏e 2026. C'est pourtant une des principales priorit脙漏s des Europ脙漏ens. C'est un droit fondamental, le socle de leur qualit脙漏 de vie. Mais c'est aussi le socle de la comp脙漏titivit脙漏 europ脙漏enne, car sans population en bonne sant脙漏 il n'y a pas de comp脙漏titivit脙漏. Alors oui, il est urgent d'agir, d'agir face au danger qui menace la sant脙漏.

Nous attendions une nouvelle l脙漏gislation sur le tabac, un paquet l脙漏gislatif sur l'information des consommateurs dans l'alimentation et la tr脙篓s importante r脙漏vision du r脙篓glement脗聽REACH. Malheureusement, pour le moment, rien de tout cela.

Nous attendions aussi que l'Europe prenne le tournant de la pr脙漏vention, enclenche un grand plan europ脙漏en de pr脙漏vention dans le domaine de la sant脙漏 et que, dans ce cadre, il y ait un plan pour la sant脙漏 mentale, notamment des jeunes, un plan pour la sant脙漏 cardiovasculaire, un plan pour la sant脙漏 des femmes.

Notre interrogation aujourd'hui, c'est: pourquoi tout cela est-il absent? Cette clarification est encore possible. Des am脙漏liorations sont encore possibles. Nous attendons une action forte de la Commission europ脙漏enne sur la sant脙漏.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marieke Ehlers (PfE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, the work programme of the Commission is called 'Europe's independence moment'. A more apt description would be 'Operation more bureaucracy in fancy wrapping paper', because nothing says freedom like being buried under a mountain of new rules dressed up as simplification. The Commission promises 'new ambition' for Europe 芒聙聯 you promise to cut red tape and then you wrap us in even more of it.

But people have had enough of pacts, visions, plans, shields and reports. This endless cycle of document production is the very definition of competence creep 芒聙聯 the steady expansion of EU control without accountability or measurable results. This culture of paperwork can be found in almost every sphere of EU influence. The truth is that the EU never simplifies 芒聙聯 it multiplies. It multiplies rules and regulations until progress grinds to a complete halt.

The people of Europe do not need more bureaucratic window dressing or another glossy brochure. What they need is real deregulation and simplification. Fewer obstacles for entrepreneurs, simpler rules for citizens and the freedom to compete and create. Because people act on incentives and Brussels keeps offering the wrong ones. The dream of the EU federalists has become the worst nightmare of ordinary people, and it is time to wake up.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Carlo Ciccioli (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, il programma di lavoro 2026 ci parla di difesa, resilienza, grandi trasformazioni.

Bene, ma mi chiedo: esiste un piano B per l'Ucraina nel caso in cui, auspicabilmente, tornasse la pace? Va bene il riarmo, indispensabile per ridare dignit脙聽 e sicurezza agli europei: ma non possiamo continuare a pianificare solo lo scontro. Serve anche una strategia per la ricostruzione, la diplomazia, la pace. Altrimenti, rischiamo di trovarci impreparati nel momento in cui la dovremo guidare noi.

In quest'Aula sento parlare solo di grandi progetti e transizioni. Tutto molto bello ma senza risorse concrete restano sogni. Se davvero vogliamo un'Europa capace di realizzare ci脙虏 che si promette, dobbiamo attrarre grandi capitali internazionali. E questo sar脙聽 possibile solo riducendo la burocrazia, semplificando le procedure e rendendo il mercato europeo competitivo, come sostenuto nel rapporto Draghi. E questo lo abbiamo capito tutti.

Infine, sul tema del riarmo: bene la mobilit脙聽 militare ma 脙篓 cruciale investire in infrastrutture ad uso duale, completare ad esempio le reti ferroviarie europee, rafforzando non solo la difesa ma anche la logistica.

脗聽脗聽Gabriele Bischoff (S&D). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Nach dem Mann-im-Mond芒聙聭Moment jetzt der Moment der Unabh脙陇ngigkeit. Aber ich glaube, die B脙录rgerinnen und B脙录rger haben nicht vergessen, dass unter dem Label der Wettbewerbsf脙陇higkeit 脙录ber Jahrzehnte eines verfolgt wurde: m脙露glichst viel zu verlagern in andere L脙陇nder, Just-in-time-Produktion. Ganz viele gute Arbeitspl脙陇tze wurden zerst脙露rt, und es hat genau in diese Abh脙陇ngigkeit gef脙录hrt. Wenn wir jetzt den Draghi芒聙聭Bericht haben und sagen: Ja, wir wollen sogar Draghi plus, dann aber den essenziellen Teil von Draghi, n脙陇mlich dass wir viel mehr Investitionen brauchen脗聽芒聙聯 das hat er genau mit Zahlen unterlegt脗聽芒聙聯 und dass wir eben auch europ脙陇ische Player brauchen. Da springen wir hier viel zu kurz. Wenn wir diesen Teil nicht hinkriegen, die Illusion zu haben, dass 脙录ber Entb脙录rokratisierung Wettbewerbsf脙陇higkeit geschaffen w脙录rde脗聽芒聙聯 da wei脙聼 jeder 脙聳konom und jede 脙聳konomin, dass das Unsinn ist. Von Digitalsteuer zum Beispiel und Steuergerechtigkeit zur Finanzierung sehe ich nichts.

Ich begr脙录脙聼e das Paket zur Qualit脙陇t der Arbeit. Da ist der Einsatz, und da zitiere ich die Kommissionspr脙陇sidentin: Es geht da um Geschwindigkeit von k脙录nstlicher Intelligenz am Arbeitsplatz. Da brauchen wir konkrete Regelungen, genau wie beim Recht auf Abschalten, und da muss noch erheblich konkretisiert werden. Ein Europa, das sch脙录tzt, unterst脙录tzen wir. Ein Europa, das Rechte runterdr脙录ckt und den Schutz wegnimmt, lehnen wir ab.
