16. Stepping up funding for Ukraine芒聙聶s reconstruction and defence: the use of Russian frozen assets (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Predsedaj脙潞ci . 芒聙聯 脛聨al脜隆脙颅m bodom programu je vyhl脙隆senie Komisie: Zintenz脙颅vnenie financovania, obnovy a obrany Ukrajiny: vyu脜戮itie zmrazen脙陆ch rusk脙陆ch akt脙颅v (2025/2932(RSP)). Na 脙潞vod rozpravy by som poprosil p脙隆na komis脙隆ra Dombrovskisa za Komisiu.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Valdis Dombrovskis, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, honourable Members, first of all, I would like to thank the European Parliament for its strong and unwavering support for Ukraine. Russia continues its war of aggression against Ukraine with brutal attacks targeting not only Ukrainian military infrastructure, but also its civilian population and energy systems. Europe has been at the forefront of supporting Ukraine since the first day of the aggression. In total, the European Union has provided close to EUR脗聽178脗聽billion in support since the start of the war, and this is much more than any other global partner.

This is not only a demonstration of solidarity. It also reflects a deep conviction that Ukraine's security is Europe's security. Through the Ukraine Facility, we are playing a pivotal role in supporting Ukraine's European ambitions, providing the necessary backing to implement crucial reforms and bringing them closer to their goal of EU accession.

We also continue supporting Ukraine in meeting its defence needs. The EUR脗聽62.3脗聽billion in military assistance measures have been disbursed by the European Union and Member States to date, including through the Peace Facility. The Act in Support of Ammunition Production (ASAP) was instrumental in increasing the production of artillery shells from 300脗聽000 in 2023 to two million planned this year. In addition, approximately 80脗聽% of the two million rounds of large calibre ammunition pledged for Ukraine has been delivered, reflecting strong progress towards our 2025 goal.

Last week, with your help, we successfully concluded negotiations on a European defence industry programme, securing final agreement that will also serve to provide crucial support to Ukraine's defence sector. With EUR脗聽300脗聽million in funding under the Ukraine defence instrument, EDIP will help modernise Ukraine's defence capabilities, but also paves the way for closer integration with our European defence ecosystem and architecture. It will also help reinforce our cooperation with Ukraine on key defence capabilities. This includes on drone-related technologies, as you called for in your resolution of 8 October, responding to the incursions into Member States' airspace by Russia.

This partnership is beneficial for both Ukraine and Europe. However, as President von der Leyen said in her State of the Union address, it should not only be the European taxpayers who bear the brunt of this support. This is Russia's war, and Russia is under obligation to compensate Ukraine for the damages it has caused. This is why, under the European Union's leadership in 2024, G7 members unlocked EUR脗聽45 billion in additional funding for Ukraine under the Extraordinary Revenue Acceleration initiative. These G7 loans are being, and will be, repaid using profits generated by the immobilised Russian Central Bank assets in the EU. The implementation of the existing ERA loans is progressing well, with EUR脗聽25.3脗聽billion already disbursed by the G7 partners and the EU.

It also marks an important step. Russia is already beginning to pay for the destruction it has caused in Ukraine, but the significant increase in military expenditures driven by Russia's ongoing attacks has put substantial strain on Ukraine's resources. As a result, additional support is needed to sustain Ukraine's defence efforts. This is why President von der Leyen proposed in her State of the Union address to provide Ukraine with a reparations loan, funded by cash balances of the immobilised Russian Central Bank assets held in the EU. Ukraine will help to repay this loan only once Russia pays for the damages. This initiative will be fully compliant with international law, which remains our guiding principle on our actions. This reparation loan would mobilise significant new funding for Ukraine, and help send a clear message to Russia that its war of aggression will not succeed.

We are working towards a collaborative effort with international partners to ensure fair burden sharing on supporting Ukraine also going forward. Should European leaders agree to take this work forward in their meeting on 23-24脗聽October, the Commission stands ready to come forward with a proposal in the coming weeks. Thank you and I look forward to hearing your views on this matter.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

IN THE CHAIR: CHRISTEL SCHALDEMOSE
Vice-President

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽David McAllister, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, as we have just heard, the debate on the use of Russian frozen assets requires a very careful balance between political responsibility, legal feasibility and financial stability.

In my view, the new proposal on the table is both bold and necessary. We can provide Ukraine with an interest-free loan of nearly EUR脗聽140脗聽billion, using assets of the Russian central bank that have been frozen here in the European Union.

Politically, this plan leaves no doubt: the European Union stands with Ukraine, and the Kremlin will only regain access to its funds once it has fully compensated Ukraine for the destruction it has caused.

Legally, this mechanism can be designed with a clear EU legal framework, provided the political will is there. A decision at the European Council on Thursday would enable legislation to be put forward in order to start payments next year.

Financially, the mechanism safeguards the integrity of the financial system, because to use the frozen Russian assets for so-called reparation bonds leaves the Russian assets technically untouched. Ukraine must be in the strongest possible position before, during and after any ceasefire. We must ramp up the pressure on Russia's economy until Putin is ready to make peace.

To use the full value of Russia's immobilised sovereign assets would provide Ukraine with the resources it needs. Thank you. Go for it, Commissioner.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Thijs Reuten, on behalf of the S&D Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, colleagues, I'm quite disappointed that the Council is not here, but I am sure they are listening carefully. Now is the time to support Ukraine and to show European leadership towards a durable peace. Now is the time to support the Commission's proposals for the reparations loan that is legally sound, avoids confiscation, is morally right, and you have broad support here, Council, to do it.

Russia's aggression and responsibility for reparations are indisputable. The process to render binding decisions will take time; time that neither Ukraine nor we have. The strategic moment is now, and I urge you, the Council, to act fast, sending a very clear message to Russia, to the United States and to our Ukrainian friends. We stand with Ukraine for as long as it takes. We want peace 芒聙聯 fair, just, durable.

But when we and you, Council, were sending these very clear messages, it was a bold message. But bold messages must be accompanied with bold actions to make them a reality. A reparations loan offers the best path forward to strengthen Ukraine's defence, increase the pressure on Russia, and put Europe in a leading position, and to enforce peace. Because it is an advance on reparations that Russia will be obliged to pay; if Russia fails to pay, the reparations that have been agreed or awarded assets should remain frozen or be seized. If Russia pays, Ukraine can pay back.

And those still worried about risk, imagine, the long term economic and security costs for our continent if Ukraine fails for lack of money. The reparation loan is a game changer. Now is not the time for short term national interest, but for a strong Union action. Now is the time for the loan.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pierre-Romain Thionnet, au nom du groupe PfE. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, l'engagement de l'Europe 脙聽 soutenir l'Ukraine dans sa lutte pour la souverainet脙漏 est non seulement juste, mais 脙漏galement conforme 脙聽 nos int脙漏r脙陋ts de s脙漏curit脙漏 et chercher la mani脙篓re de poursuivre ce soutien tout en ne portant pas gravement atteinte 脙聽 la situation budg脙漏taire de nos pays est une d脙漏marche salutaire.

Cependant, l'id脙漏e d'un emprunt 脙聽 taux z脙漏ro, dont le remboursement reposerait sur d'脙漏ventuelles r脙漏parations russes, interroge profond脙漏ment. Imaginer que la Russie, au bout de trois ans d'invasion, indemniserait sans difficult脙漏 son agression rel脙篓ve moins de la strat脙漏gie que d'un pari et m脙陋me d'un fantasme, celui qu'un r脙漏gime qui n'a montr脙漏 jusqu'ici que m脙漏pris pour le droit international se mette soudainement 脙聽 se montrer docile.

L'脙漏chec de ce pari aurait une cons脙漏quence claire. La dette subsistera. Elle retombera sur les 脙漏paules de l'Union et des 脙聣tats membres. En bref, les Europ脙漏ens paieront les Russes 脙聽 hauteur de 140 milliards脗聽d'euros. Cette proposition vient affaiblir les Europ脙漏ens dans le rapport de force n脙漏cessaire des n脙漏gociations. Nous allons transformer ce qui 脙漏tait jusqu'alors un instrument de pression potentielle en une obligation financi脙篓re envers la Russie. Nous troquons un levier diplomatique pour un fardeau financier.

Aider l'Ukraine et pr脙漏parer sa reconstruction sont une n脙漏cessit脙漏, nous en sommes bien conscients, mais pr脙漏f脙漏rons les m脙漏canismes solides, politiquement tenables, et les aides militaires et mat脙漏rielles concr脙篓tes dont les Ukrainiens ont besoin plut脙麓t que les emprunts hasardeux.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adam Bielan, on behalf of the ECR Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, since our last debate on the frozen Russian assets, Moscow has fired thousands more rockets and drones at Ukraine, with incidents affecting European airspace and critical infrastructure. Each one of them is a reminder that this is not a distant war, but a direct challenge to European security.

Today, as we debate stepping up support for Ukraine's reconstruction and defence through the use of frozen assets, Russia's aggression has already cost hundreds of thousands of lives and devastated Ukraine's economy, but it has also tested Europe's resolve and credibility.

Poland, as one of Ukraine's closest allies, knows all too well the costs of aggression and the danger of hesitation, but also the strength that comes from solidarity. We therefore strongly welcome the Commission's determination to act swiftly and decisively.

Using Russian assets sends an unmistakable message: aggression has consequences, and those who wage illegal wars will pay for the damage they cause.

Ukraine needs support now for a defence system that protects civilians, for rebuilding energy infrastructure before winter, for de-mining agricultural land. Speed is decisive. While we debate safeguards and procedures, Ukraine is fighting for survival. Europe's credibility and the meaning of our solidarity depend on implementation within months, not years.

Europe must act together and Europe must act now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Petras Au脜隆trevi脛聧ius, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President,脗聽Commissioner, colleagues, Europe must finally move beyond the frozen stage of consideration regarding the use of Russia's state assets in the West. For four years, we have been going around in circles on this issue, neglecting our legal, political and moral obligation to support Ukraine. Europe, just do it!

Having crossed the Rubicon of international law and humanity, Russia must compensate Ukraine and Ukrainians for their losses. In this regard, the decision made by the European Court of Human Rights in July this year should be welcomed, as it provides a legal basis for compensating Ukrainians for the damage and consequences caused by the aggressor.

The idea of a reparation loan for Ukraine, which is currently under consideration, will ensure the country's financial stability amid unprecedented military pressure in general.脗聽It is crucial to hold Russia accountable and ensure that it pays a high price for its bloody aggression.

I have to admit, I am deeply disappointed by the Trump administration's announced decision to abstain from participating in the Ukrainian reparation loan, thereby weakening Western unity and decreasing pressure on Russia.

Dear colleagues, Ukraine is not for sale. Ukraine's future is to be decided by its people. And again, Russia must pay, compensate and accept legitimate terms.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Virginijus Sinkevi脛聧ius, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Commissioner, colleagues, when we speak about Ukraine, we are not only speaking about a nation under attack. We are speaking about the very foundation of international law and human dignity. Every destroyed home, every child displaced, every mother grieving 芒聙聯 they remind us that justice delayed can become justice denied.

The aggressor must pay. Russian-state and oligarch芒聙聭linked assets frozen in Europe are the lawful way to do it. These assets are not ours to appropriate; they are instruments of accountability under the rule of law. International law, including the principles of counter芒聙聭measures, under the law of state responsibility, that gives us a very clear path: those who destroy must pay for what they have destroyed.

We need one solid mechanism, clear legal basis, due process and independent oversight. We are not seizing property in vengeance. We are ensuring that aggression does not yield impunity. Using frozen Russian assets within a legal framework for reconstruction for hospitals, for schools in Ukraine transforms financial punishment into moral restoration. It strengthens the credibility of our sanctions regime and shows that Europe's justice has both heart and a spine.

Let us remember, the rebuilding of Ukraine is not a charity. It is a restitution necessary and righteous. The aggressor must bear the cost of its aggression. If we truly believe in a rule芒聙聭based order, then it is time for the rules to serve the victims, not the violator.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Merja Kyll脙露nen, The Left-ryhm脙陇n puolesta. 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, tuemme Ukrainaa niin kauan kuin on tarpeen. Me kaikki olemme kuulleet ja toistaneet t脙陇m脙陇n lupauksen, mutta pelk脙陇t sanat eiv脙陇t pys脙陇yt脙陇 ohjuksia, ne eiv脙陇t maksa tarvittavia asej脙陇rjestelmi脙陇, ne eiv脙陇t pelasta ihmishenki脙陇, ne eiv脙陇t rakenna kaupunkeja uudelleen. Yksi vahvimmista keinoistamme on yh脙陇 k脙陇ytt脙陇m脙陇tt脙陇: 250 miljardia euroa Ven脙陇j脙陇n valtion rahoja, jotka on j脙陇脙陇dytetty ymp脙陇ri Eurooppaa. N脙陇m脙陇 eiv脙陇t ole vain numeroita. Ne ovat sodan aloittaneen valtion varoja, jotka lep脙陇脙陇v脙陇t pankeissamme samaan aikaan kun ukrainalaiset puolustavat maataan ja niit脙陇 periaatteita, joille Eurooppa rakentuu.

Jotkut sanovat, ett脙陇 n脙陇iden varojen k脙陇ytt脙陇minen voisi horjuttaa Euroopan uskottavuutta. Mutta ollaan rehellisi脙陇. Paljon suurempi riski on se, ett脙陇 Ukraina kaatuu ja sen mukana kaatuu my脙露s Euroopan uskottavuus. T脙陇m脙陇 ei ole juridinen kysymys. T脙陇m脙陇 on poliittisen tahdon testi. Jos me pystymme j脙陇脙陇dytt脙陇m脙陇脙陇n n脙陇m脙陇 varat, me pystymme my脙露s k脙陇ytt脙陇m脙陇脙陇n ne Ukrainan puolustamiseen, Ukrainan j脙陇lleenrakentamiseen ja my脙露skin rauhan tulevaisuuden rakentamiseen Euroopassa. Tarvitsemme tahtoa toimia.

Tam 脛聧lensk脙漏mu 脜隆t脙隆tu 芒聙聯 Ma脛聫arsku, ktor脙漏 plat脙颅 do 脙職nie, do rozpo脛聧tu 脙職nie pr脙颅spevok, eurofondy blokujete, ale na Ukrajinu, ktor脙隆 nie je 脛聧lensk脙陆m 脜隆t脙隆tom 脙職nie, stovky mili脙隆rd eur posielate. Rodin脙隆m na pomoc s b脙陆van脙颅m alebo mlad脙陆m na to, aby mali v Eur脙鲁pe bud脙潞cnos脜楼, peniaze ned脙隆vate, ale Zelensk脙漏mu ich na脛聫alej posielate. Toto nie je ukrajinsk脙隆 脙潞nia, pri v脜隆etkej 脙潞cte, toto nie je ukrajinsk脙隆 脙潞nia. Toto je Eur脙鲁pska 脙潞nia, ktor脙隆 sa m脙隆 venova脜楼 eur脙鲁pskym probl脙漏mom. Ukrajina nie je st脙隆le 脛聧lensk脙陆m 脜隆t脙隆tom Eur脙鲁pskej 脙潞nie a dlho ani nebude. Ja si mysl脙颅m, 脜戮e by sme mali ove脛戮a v脙陇脛聧脜隆iu prioritu kl脙隆s脜楼 na probl脙漏my Eur脙鲁panov a obyvate脛戮ov Eur脙鲁pskej 脙潞nie, a nie neust脙隆le rie脜隆i脜楼 probl脙漏my cel脙漏ho sveta. Tak脜戮e p脙隆ni komis脙隆ri, pros脙颅m v脙隆s, u脜戮 naozaj sta脛聧ilo. Za脛聧nite kone脛聧ne rie脜隆i脜楼 probl脙漏my Eur脙鲁py, a nie cel脙漏ho sveta.

脗聽脗聽Pekka Toveri (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, as already mentioned here, every day that the war continues, innocent Ukrainian soldiers and civilians die. Therefore, we have to act fast.

However, there are some who want the United States to support the use of frozen Russian assets in the G7. Well, what we have learned about President Trump is that the only predictable thing about him is that he is unpredictable. Therefore, we can't wait for President Trump to, maybe, at one point somewhere in the future, agree with the EU. We have to grow up and take independent action now, without waiting for the United States.

Some people are afraid of the possible legal actions against us. At the same time, Ukrainians are afraid, every night, of losing their lives in Russian attacks. We owe it to the Ukrainians to act fast.

Slava Ukraini!

脗聽脗聽Hel脙漏ne Fritzon (S&D). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Kriget i Ukraina 脙陇r inte 脙露ver. Varje dag k脙陇mpar det ukrainska folket f脙露r sin frihet, f脙露r demokratin och f脙露r hela Europas s脙陇kerhet. Deras kamp 脙陇r faktiskt ocks脙楼 v脙楼r. Barnen i Ukraina v脙陇xer upp med bomber och missiler. Kriget har p脙楼g脙楼tt i 脙露ver tre och ett halvt 脙楼r 芒聙聯 och det 脙陇r l脙楼ng tid i ett barns liv.

Vi m脙楼ste forts脙陇tta st脙楼 vid Ukrainas sida, humanit脙陇rt, milit脙陇rt och ekonomiskt. Och vi socialdemokrater, vi kr脙陇ver att varje medlemsstat uppn脙楼r minst en halv procent av BNP i sitt st脙露d till Ukraina. F脙露r att 脙楼teruppbygga Ukraina, det handlar inte bara om byggnader och v脙陇gar. Det handlar ocks脙楼 om att ge hopp, framtid och v脙陇rdighet, om att ge m脙陇nniskor m脙露jlighet att faktiskt leva i fred.

H脙陇r m脙楼ste EU visa ledarskap och st脙楼 fast i sitt st脙露d till Ukraina. De kommande veckorna f脙楼r inte bli en uppg脙露relse d脙陇r Putin och Trump dikterar villkoren. Det handlar ju faktiskt om Ukraina. Slava Ukraini!

脗聽脗聽Anders Vistisen (PfE). 芒聙聯 Fru formand! 75 mia. danske kroner er, hvad vi i Danmark indtil videre har bidraget til Ukraines frihedskamp. Statsminister Mette Frederiksen fort脙娄ller os, at vi skal g脙楼 foran, s脙楼 vi har givet mest af alle i hele verden. Vi g脙楼r foran for at vise et eksempel for Europa.

S脙楼 lad os se, hvad det er for et eksempel, vi har vist. Der findes kun to andre socialdemokratiske regeringschefer tilbage i EU; Spanien og Malta.脗聽Danmark har givet 25 gange mere end Spanien. 250 gange mere end Malta.

Og n脙楼r vi ser p脙楼 NATO-bidraget, er Spanien det eneste NATO-land, som ikke er villig til at bruge fem procent af sit BNP p脙楼 at forsvare vores forsvarsalliance. N脙楼r det kommer til de frosne russiske aktiver, er det den belgiske regering, der s脙娄tter foden ned i R脙楼det. En belgisk regering, der ogs脙楼 har socialdemokratisk deltagelse.

S脙楼 k脙娄re Mette Frederiksen, f脙赂r du peger fingre af danskerne, s脙楼 peg fingre af dine venner i EU, fordi det er der, det virkelige problem er, n脙楼r det kommer til at hj脙娄lpe Ukraines frihedskamp.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Johan Van Overtveldt (ECR). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, collega芒聙聶s, het spreekt voor zich dat de brute aanvallen op Oekra脙炉ne, en eigenlijk op onze westerse vrijheden en waarden, niet onbestraft mogen blijven. We moeten het Russische regime via gerichte sancties blijven raken waar het pijn doet en Oekra脙炉ne en zijn samenleving onverkort en vastberaden steunen.

Wat het inzetten van bevroren tegoeden voor een reparatielening betreft, wil ik oproepen tot enige voorzichtigheid. Aan een dergelijke operatie, een operatie zonder precedent, zijn juridische, financi脙芦le en institutionele risico芒聙聶s verbonden, en die moeten we als dusdanig ook erkennen. Het gaat niet over een berg geld die klaarligt en waar we zomaar in kunnen graaien.

Er moeten harde garanties komen over eventuele juridische en financi脙芦le gevolgen en over de manier waarop bepaalde risico芒聙聶s gedragen zullen worden. Ik spreek niet enkel over de positie van mijn thuisland, maar ook over bevroren tegoeden die zich in andere landen bevinden en waarover trouwens weinig transparantie bestaat. Het kan ook niet de bedoeling zijn dat bepaalde financi脙芦le instellingen of, bij uitbreiding, het financi脙芦le weefsel in Europa in gevaar zouden komen door een onbesuisde actie zonder een duidelijke rechtsgrond. De aard en omvang van deze operatie vraagt om een maximum aan garanties.

Onze volle steun aan Oekra脙炉ne is de evidentie zelf, maar die goede wil is geen vrijgeleide voor maatregelen die onaangename economische en financi脙芦le gevolgen kunnen hebben voor de gehele Europese economie.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hilde Vautmans (Renew). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, ja, we moeten meer doen om Oekra脙炉ne te helpen winnen, zowel op militair als op financieel vlak. Oekra脙炉ne heeft de komende twee jaar 140脗聽miljard脗聽EUR extra nodig om zich staande te houden tegen het Russische gebeuk. Maar laat ons dat doen zonder Belgi脙芦 op te zadelen met een financieel risico ten grote van, hou u vast, 脙漏脙漏n derde van zijn bbp. Dat is pure waanzin. Dus ja, laat ons de Russische tegoeden mobiliseren om Oekra脙炉ne te steunen, maar doe het verstandig: niet door de tegoeden zelf aan te slaan, maar wel door ze als onderpand te gebruiken voor een massieve gezamenlijke lening.

En ten tweede, zoals collega Van Overtveldt ook zei: iedereen moet mee in bad. Dat wil zeggen dat we ook kijken naar de tegoeden op Franse, Duitse en andere Europese banken, en dat er spijkerharde garanties komen van alle lidstaten dat, als het ooit tot een rechtszaak komt, de kosten gedeeld worden en niet alleen door Belgi脙芦 gedragen worden. Mijnheer Gahler, u knikt. Dat is Europese solidariteit. Dat is de Europese eenheid die wij hadden naar Oekra脙炉ne toe. Dat is de Europese eenheid die we ook nu moeten hebben.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Saskia Bricmont (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, la solidarit脙漏 europ脙漏enne face 脙聽 la guerre en Ukraine est sans pareille. Paquets de sanctions contre la Russie accueil des r脙漏fugi脙漏s ukrainiens, en passant par la criminalisation du contournement des sanctions et le gel et la confiscation des avoirs. Et utiliser les avoirs russes saisis en Europe au profit du peuple ukrainien est un engagement que nous avons pris, un engagement moral pour la reconstruction des 脙漏coles, des h脙麓pitaux et des infrastructures publiques de l'Ukraine, et un脗聽engagement l脙漏gal, afin que les criminels de guerre ne puissent plus b脙漏n脙漏ficier de l'impunit脙漏 dans notre espace commun. C'est 脙聽 l'agresseur de payer.

Aujourd'hui, il faut passer de la parole aux actes et montrer notre ferme volont脙漏 europ脙漏enne commune de soutenir l'Ukraine et de lui accorder un pr脙陋t pour sa reconstruction. Mais la solidarit脙漏 et le partage des risques financiers et juridiques li脙漏s 脙聽 l'utilisation des avoirs russes gel脙漏s doivent aussi 脙陋tre europ脙漏ens. Un 脙聣tat membre, en l'occurrence la Belgique, ne devrait pas assumer seul ces risques. L'Union europ脙漏enne doit faire preuve de coh脙漏rence et de solidarit脙漏. Et ce n'est qu'ensemble que nous pourrons rendre justice aux victimes ukrainiennes et contribuer 脙聽 la r脙漏elle reconstruction du pays.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ivan David (ESN). 芒聙聯 Pan脙颅 p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, Ukrajina m脛聸la b脙陆t podle mezin脙隆rodn脙颅 dohody neutr脙隆ln脙颅 zem脙颅, jej脙颅 neutralita byla ze strany Ruska respektov脙隆na. Po barevn脙漏m p脜聶evratu na Majdanu nov脙隆 vl脙隆da odm脙颅tla neutralitu a NATO usilovalo o jej脙颅 p脜聶ijet脙颅 a za脛聧aly b脙陆t budov脙隆ny vojensk脙漏 objekty NATO na Ukrajin脛聸. Po rusk脙漏 intervenci byly ignorov脙隆ny Minsk脙漏 dohody i dohody z Istanbulu. Ukrajina je vykraden脙隆 a rozbit脙隆, zcela z脙隆visl脙隆 na zahrani脛聧n脙颅 finan脛聧n脙颅 pomoci, padlo v脙颅ce ne脜戮 milion ukrajinsk脙陆ch voj脙隆k脜炉, miliony obyvatel p脜聶ed b脙颅dou uprchly. Vojensky je Ukrajina v bezv脙陆chodn脙漏 situaci. N脙隆zev t脙漏to rozpravy ukazuje, 脜戮e se nepo脛聧脙颅t脙隆 s realistick脙陆m diplomatick脙陆m 脜聶e脜隆en脙颅m konfliktu, ale s pokra脛聧ov脙隆n脙颅m v脙隆lky. Zmrazen脙颅 aktiv ciz脙颅 zem脛聸 a dokonce i jejich pou脜戮it脙颅 povede k definitivn脙颅 ztr脙隆t脛聸 v脛聸rohodnosti finan脛聧n脙颅ch operac脙颅 v Evropsk脙漏 unii. Doporu脛聧uji obrat k realistick脙漏 politice.

(脜聵e脛聧n脙颅k souhlasil s t脙颅m, 脜戮e 脜戮e odpov脙颅 na ot脙隆zku polo脜戮enou zvednut脙颅m modr脙漏 karty)

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Dear colleague, I was wondering if you are aware of the international treaties between Russia and Ukraine in the 90s, where Russia promised to respect Ukraine's sovereignty and borders in exchange for Ukraine giving up its nuclear arsenal?

And also, did I understand you correctly that once a state breaks international agreements, that's a reason to wage an illegal war of aggression against that state?

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ivan David (ESN), odpov脛聸脛聫 na ot脙隆zku polo脜戮enou zvednut脙颅m modr脙漏 karty. 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙陆 kolego, sou脛聧脙隆st脙颅 t脙漏 dohody bylo tak脙漏 to, 脜戮e Ukrajina bude neutr脙隆ln脙颅 zem脙颅. Toto bylo jednozna脛聧n脛聸 poru脜隆eno. J脙隆 nesouhlas脙颅m s t脙颅m, aby reakc脙颅 na takov脙漏 poru脜隆en脙颅 byla vojensk脙隆 intervence. Ale na druh脙漏 stran脛聸 je pot脜聶eba si polo脜戮it ot脙隆zku, jak脙漏 mo脜戮nosti existuj脙颅 pro akceptov脙隆n脙颅 t脙漏 situace tak, aby do脜隆lo k urovn脙隆n脙颅. Nic takov脙漏ho se nestalo. Cel脙隆 podpora p脜聶edstavitel脜炉 Evropsk脙漏 unie na Majdanu tady byla proto, aby mohla b脙陆t vyprovokov脙隆na v脙隆lka s Ruskem.

脗聽脗聽Michael von der Schulenburg (NI). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Herr Kommissar! Ich habe das Gef脙录hl, dass diese Debatte eher ein Beispiel f脙录r die gescheiterte Ukrainepolitik ist. Seit ich hier bin, sind den Ukrainern so viele Versprechungen gemacht worden, und ich habe den Eindruck, wir haben keine davon gehalten. Die Zerst脙露rung der russischen Armee hat nicht stattgefunden. Die Sanktionen werden die russische Wirtschaft nicht sanktionieren. Die 0,5脗聽Prozent des Bruttosozialprodukts, die wir den Ukrainern versprochen haben jedes Jahr, sind wohl auch nicht 脙录berwiesen worden. NATO芒聙聭Friedenstruppen wird es auch nicht in der Ukraine geben, und ich bef脙录rchte, dass eine Artikel芒聙聭5芒聙聭Garantie ebenfalls eine Illusion bleiben wird. Die russischen Guthaben, die wir hier planen, werden wohl den gleichen Weg gehen.

Was wir damit machen? Wir schaden uns nur selbst damit, und vor allen Dingen schaden wir der Ukraine. Ich bin immer noch davon 脙录berzeugt, dass unsere vorrangige Aufgabe sein muss, die Ukraine als einen souver脙陇nen, funktionierenden europ脙陇ischen Staat zu halten, damit wir mit ihren Nachbarn 芒聙聯 und das bedeutet auch mit Russland 芒聙聯 einmal in Frieden leben k脙露nnen. Das kann nur dadurch erreicht werden, dass wir verhandeln, verhandeln, verhandeln und nicht irgendwelches Geld klauen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael Gahler (PPE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Wie wir nicht erst in den letzten Tagen erleben mussten, reichen Russlands Aktivit脙陇ten 脙录ber die Ukraine hinaus. Die Vorstellung, dass Wladimir Putin seine Aggression auf L脙陇nder der Europ脙陇ischen Union richten k脙露nnte, wenn er seinen Krieg gew脙陇nne, das ist keine weit hergeholte theoretische M脙露glichkeit. Es handelt sich um ein existenzielles Risiko f脙录r Europa. Gl脙录cklicherweise gibt es neben den bisherigen vielf脙陇ltigen Unterst脙录tzungsma脙聼nahmen eine weitere einzigartige M脙露glichkeit, der Ukraine zu helfen. Und das ist, Moskaus Verm脙露genswerte gegen Russland selbst einzusetzen, indem sie f脙录r einen Reparationskredit an Kyjiw verwendet werden.

Meine Aufforderung an die Mitgliedstaaten ist klar: Auch wenn Sie jetzt nicht hier sitzen, tun Sie das Richtige. Tun Sie es schnell. Die Ukraine braucht weitere Unterst脙录tzung, und das Reparationsdarlehen bietet eine sichere und solide M脙露glichkeit, diese zu finanzieren. Dieser Kredit ist praktisch eine Vorauszahlung auf die v脙露lkerrechtliche Verpflichtung des Kreml, Reparationszahlungen zu leisten. Wenn Russland sich hierzu weigert, bekommt es das Geld nicht zur脙录ck. Und wenn sie tats脙陇chlich bereit w脙陇ren, dann w脙录rde das Geld mit den zu zahlenden Reparationen verrechnet. F脙录r uns besteht also auch kein Haftungsrisiko.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Robert Biedro脜聞 (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, the message from this House must be clear: Russia will pay for the destruction of Ukraine. Law experts have confirmed that the confiscation of the frozen assets from Russia would be legal. But the reparations loan proposed by the Commission is the second best option.

Either way, one thing must be clear: those who violate international law, commit war crimes and destroy civilian infrastructure must bear the consequences of their actions. Of course, we have to guarantee that Ukraine continues to have the right military equipment, opposite to the friends of Putin from the right part of this Parliament.

But we also cannot forget about the reconstruction of Ukraine, which is estimated to cost around USD脗聽1脗聽trillion. This reparations loan would be an opportunity to lay the foundation for a more inclusive and resilient society. Imagine Ukrainians going to a newly built or reconstructed school or a hospital, and seeing a sign that says 'financed from Russian funds thanks to the European Union'. This is how Europe should act.

Prieks, ka beidzot ir vismaz kaut k脛聛da kust脛芦ba, un es saprotu da脜戮u dal脛芦bvalstu ba脜戮as par juridiskaj脛聛m sek脛聛m. Atbild脛芦bai ir j脛聛b脜芦t solid脛聛rai, un tam risin脛聛jums ir j脛聛atrod. Tas, ko es nevaru saprast, ir ba脜戮as par investoru uztic脛芦bas zaud脛聯脜隆anu. K脛聛du investoru? Diktatoru un agresoru? Cik mums t脛聛di v脛聯l ir?

Man 脜隆脛路iet, ka tie脜隆i t脛聛du zi脜聠u mums tiem ar脛芦 j脛聛dod. Ja gras脛聛ties iebrukt neatkar脛芦g脛聛 valst脛芦, j脜芦s zaud脛聯siet visu. Un v脛聯l. Tad, kad m脛聯s beidzot non脛聛ksim l脛芦dz Krievijas akt脛芦vu nodo脜隆anai Ukrainai 芒聙聰 un es ticu, ka m脛聯s non脛聛ksim 芒聙聰, mums j脛聛nodro脜隆ina, lai tas patie脜隆脛聛m ir papildin脛聛jums, nevis aizvietojums jau eso脜隆ajam atbalstam Ukrainai.

脗聽脗聽Marie-Agnes Strack-Zimmermann (Renew). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Herr Kommissar, meine Damen und Herren! Auf was warten wir eigentlich noch? Angesichts der brutalen Angriffe auf die Ukraine wird es Zeit, dass wir endlich das tun, was Putin h脙露ren und sehen sollte. Putin ist ein Kriegsverbrecher. Er geh脙露rt 脙录brigens auch nicht nach Budapest, sondern direkt nach Den Haag, und zwar vor Gericht. Denn das nennt man Gerechtigkeit. Gerechtigkeit ist auch, die eingefrorenen russischen Verm脙露genswerte zu nutzen, um die Ukraine zu unterst脙录tzen, um zu 脙录berleben und um ihr Land wieder aufzubauen 芒聙聯 und da reichen keine Zinserl脙露se, sondern alles, was geht.

Meine Damen und Herren, Putin muss daf脙录r bezahlen, was er der Ukraine antut. Es ist 脙录brigens auch keine Destabilisierung des Euroraums, wie immer kolportiert wird. Es belastet nicht unsere nationalen Haushalte, und es geschieht vor allen Dingen in einem rechtlichen Rahmen. Meine Damen und Herren, es wird wirklich Zeit, dass wir weniger debattieren, schnell handeln, denn in der Ukraine brennt wirklich alles, und es wird Zeit, dass wir nicht nur reden, sondern alles tun, was in unserer Macht steht. Und das geh脙露rt auch dazu.

脗聽脗聽Marie-Agnes Strack-Zimmermann (Renew), Antwort auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹. 芒聙聯 (Beginn des Redebeitrags bei ausgeschaltetem Mikrofon) 芒聙娄 gerade so aus, dass es doch nicht (nicht zu verstehender Passus). 脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Dear Mr脗聽Sieper, this was the last blue-card question you have for this debate.

脗聽脗聽Ville Niinist脙露 (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Russia's war didn't just attack Ukraine, it attacked the future of European democracy and the rules芒聙聭based international order. Russia has committed atrocious war crimes one after another 芒聙聯 stealing children, using all kinds of violence against Ukrainian civilians, including systematic torture, rape and bombing of civilian targets. Justice demands that the aggressor pays for its crimes.

The European Commission's reparations loan up to EUR脗聽140脗聽billion, backed by the Russian frozen assets in Europe, is a concrete change Ukraine needs now. We must ensure legal clarity but not hide truth and justice behind excuses. The reparation loan is a down payment of reparations that Russia must pay based on an international tribunal. The frozen assets themselves are still waiting there for that process; the ownership of the money has not changed.

Now let us deliver for Ukraine: step up defence support, accelerate reconstruction and hold Russia accountable, and ensure Europe's values and security. Stand firm.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hans Neuhoff (ESN). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Die geplante Beschlagnahme russischer Verm脙露genswerte ist kein Ausdruck von Rechtsstaatlichkeit. Sie ist deren Aush脙露hlung. Die Staatenimmunit脙陇t ist ein Grundpfeiler der internationalen Ordnung. Will die EU diese Ordnung zum Einsturz bringen? Sie alle sprechen von einem Risiko f脙录r die Finanzstabilit脙陇t, von Kapitalflucht und Vertrauensverlust.

脗聽脗聽Arkadiusz Mularczyk (ECR), pytanie zadane przez podniesienie niebieskiej kartki. 芒聙聯 Szanowny panie po脜聸le, z wielkim zdumieniem wys脜聜ucha脜聜em pana wyst脛聟pienia, w kt脙鲁rym kwestionuje pan prawo Ukrainy do reparacji polegaj脛聟cych na przej脛聶ciu zamro脜录onych rosyjskich 脜聸rodk脙鲁w. 脗聽脗聽Hans Neuhoff (ESN), Antwort auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹. 芒聙聯 Herr Kollege, der Irrtum, von dem Sie ausgehen, besteht darin, dass Russland jemals Reparationszahlungen leisten w脙录rde. Das wird Russland schlicht und einfach nicht tun. Wie wollen Sie denn Russland zwingen, solche Reparationszahlungen zu leisten? Sie k脙露nnen es nicht. 脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Der Kollege Neuhoff ist ein ganz besonderer T脙录nnes, das habe ich noch nicht gesehen.

Madam President, dear people of Europe, for me the situation is clear: we need to make Russia pay. But I also really do not understand the legal problem that some people are having here, because when you listen to Putin, then Russia goes all the way until Lviv. So even if we listen to those people, what we are doing is taking Russian money and investing it in what Putin calls Russia.

I was very surprised when my colleague Uhr脙颅k said that we have European problems and we need a European solution for that. That's something that I've never heard from the ESN. So that's also something that goes for Putin.

But I also want to answer the question that he gave to us: what else do we want to give to Ukraine and for how long do we want to give it? It's quite simple. We will support Ukraine with whatever it takes and however long it takes.

脗聽脗聽Markus Ferber (PPE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Russische Milliarden liegen eingefroren in europ脙陇ischen Tresoren, w脙陇hrend in der Ukraine tagt脙陇glich H脙陇user, Schulen und Krankenh脙陇user zerst脙露rt werden. Die Mittel ungenutzt liegen zu lassen, w脙陇re moralisch falsch. Russland hat diesen Krieg begonnen. Russland muss f脙录r die Folgen seines Handelns geradestehen.

Aber wir sollten bei der Nutzung der Gelder auch nicht un脙录berlegt handeln. Ich w脙录rde mir eher vorstellen, dass wir dieses Geld als Sicherheit, als Garantie nehmen, um daraus 脙录ber eine Konstruktion, eine Aufbaubank, was auch immer, entsprechende Mittel an die Ukraine zur Verf脙录gung zu stellen. Das w脙录rde die finanziellen Risiken deutlich abfedern und w脙录rde die gleiche Form der Hilfe f脙录r die Ukraine bedeuten.

Die Ukraine braucht unsere Unterst脙录tzung, aber wir brauchen auch eine L脙露sung, die wirtschaftlich und langfristig tragf脙陇hig ist. Bei dem jetzigen Vorschlag habe ich da noch meine Zweifel. Es w脙陇re besser, wenn die Kommission noch einmal ans Rei脙聼brett geht und sich die Dinge sauber durch脙录berlegt, damit am Ende kein Milliardenrisiko f脙录r den europ脙陇ischen Steuerzahler entsteht.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tobias Cremer (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, for too long, Putin has escalated this war because he believed that time is on his side. For too long, he has mistaken our readiness to negotiate as weakness, and responded only with more escalation, more bombs and more drones of our own cities. Colleagues, this vicious cycle has to end now! We must finally change Putin's calculus about this war and show him that time is not on his side.

The good news is that we have the tool to do that right at our hand. We have EUR脗聽200脗聽billion of Putin's own money frozen in European bank accounts. And by using these assets to create a reparations loan for Ukraine, we can put Putin's money into a leverage for peace, we can give Ukraine the means to defend itself as long as it needs and we can create a clear incentive for Putin to end the war quickly. Because the longer he drags it out now, he not only pays with the lives of soldiers that he doesn't care about, but with money that he does care about.

Colleagues, this is Putin's war: he wanted it; he prolongs it; he escalates it. It's time he starts paying for it!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kosma Z脜聜otowski (ECR). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Wykorzystanie zamro脜录onych rosyjskich pieni脛聶dzy na obron脛聶 Ukrainy to oczywi脜聸cie dobry, ale sp脙鲁脜潞niony pomys脜聜. M脙鲁wimy dzi脜聸 o 19. pakiecie sankcji. Dziewi脛聶tnastym! Mo脜录e skuteczny oka脜录e si脛聶 dopiero czterdziesty pi脛聟ty albo pi脛聶脛聡dziesi脛聟ty sz脙鲁sty? Ju脜录 dawno powinni脜聸my przekaza脛聡 te 脜聸rodki Ukrainie. Nie musieliby脜聸my wtedy udziela脛聡 tak wielu po脜录yczek, a Kij脙鲁w walczy脜聜by z Putinem za rosyjskie pieni脛聟dze.

Ale mo脜录e problem w tym, 脜录e cz脛聶脜聸脛聡 europejskich przyw脙鲁dc脙鲁w od pocz脛聟tku wola脜聜a sankcje na p脙鲁脜聜 gwizdka, licz脛聟c, 脜录e szybko mo脜录na b脛聶dzie wr脙鲁ci脛聡 do business as usual. Mo脜录e dopiero po wysadzeniu Nord Stream 2 niekt脙鲁rzy zrozumieli, 脜录e drugiego resetu z Moskw脛聟 nie b脛聶dzie.

Oby dzisiejszy plan nie oznacza脜聜 tylko zastrzyku got脙鲁wki dla niemieckich, francuskich, zachodnich firm zbrojeniowych, kt脙鲁re jeszcze niedawno robi脜聜y z Rosj脛聟 interesy. Bo je脜聸li tak ma wygl脛聟da脛聡 europejska solidarno脜聸脛聡, to ta wojna ju脜录 jest przegrana.

脗聽脗聽Urmas Paet (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, colleagues, perhaps a decision will finally be made to use the frozen Russian assets to support Ukraine's reconstruction. It has taken far too long to reach this point. It has been sad to hear arguments claiming that such a step cannot be taken because it would undermine the European Union's credibility as a trusted guardian of criminals' money. In reality, what damages the EU's credibility is when it fails to provide Ukraine with adequate support, allowing the aggressor state to continue unpunished and unbothered.

History also offers perspective: after the Second World War, Germany was required to pay reparations. Lots of European countries, including Belgium, received substantial reparations from Germany to compensate war damages. On what basis do some now wish to apply a different logic to Russia? The aggressor must pay for the destruction it has caused. EU Member States should finally show resolve and make that decision.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Villy S脙赂vndal (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Jeg meldte mig ind i et demokratisk, gr脙赂nt venstreorienteret parti for snart halvtreds 脙楼r siden. F脙赂rst og fremmest fordi kampen for retf脙娄rdighed og frihed var vigtig for mig. N脙楼r vi ser uretf脙娄rdighed, skal vi sige fra. Det ser jeg som vores demokratiske pligt.

En af de st脙赂rste uretf脙娄rdigheder, vi ser i 脙赂jeblikket, er det, der sker i Ukraine, hvor det russiske brutale angreb p脙楼 den ukrainske civilbefolkning nu er p脙楼 vej i sit fjerde 脙楼r. I fire 脙楼r har Rusland fors脙赂gt at tvinge det ukrainske folk i kn脙娄.

Men der er folk i dette Parlament, der g脙赂r, hvad de kan for at hj脙娄lpe aggressorerne p脙楼 vej. Som altid modarbejder ethvert skridt, der holder Rusland ansvarligt. Og det kan godt v脙娄re, at de kr脙娄fter her i Parlamentet ikke ser det som n脙赂dvendigt at kalde uretf脙娄rdigheden, n脙楼r man ser dem. Men det g脙赂r jeg. N脙楼r man har lavet et voldeligt overgreb p脙楼 et land, kommer regningen. Derfor b脙赂r det ikke komme som nogen overraskelse for hverken de s脙楼kaldte patrioter eller andre, at aggressorerne skal betale for deres overgreb. Og det er upatriotisk at svigte et land, der bliver angrebet.

脗聽脗聽Ruth Firmenich (NI). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Mit dem Zwangskredit f脙录r die Ukraine auf Basis russischen Verm脙露gens in H脙露he von 140 Milliarden脗聽Euro plant die EU einen V脙露lkerrechtsbruch mit unabsehbaren Konsequenzen f脙录r die eigene Wirtschaft. Dazu drei Bemerkungen: Was die EU vorhat, wird den Finanzplatz EU nachhaltig sch脙陇digen. Davor hat sogar die Europ脙陇ische Zentralbank gewarnt. Wer legt sein Geld gerne dort an, wo es sp脙陇ter enteignet wird?

Das finanzielle Risiko der Kriegskredite tr脙陇gt allein der europ脙陇ische Steuerzahler. F脙录r Deutschland hei脙聼t das: 35 Milliarden脗聽Euro f脙录r den neuen Schattenhaushalt. Und es steht zu erwarten, dass die faktische Enteignung russischer Verm脙露gen im Gegenzug mit der Beschlagnahme europ脙陇ischer Verm脙露gen beantwortet werden wird.

Statt sich Taschenspielertricks auszudenken, um neues Geld f脙录r den Krieg zu generieren, sollte die EU endlich Gehirnschmalz darauf verwenden, wie sie einen konstruktiven Beitrag zu einer Friedensl脙露sung in der Ukraine leisten kann. Doch das ist bei Frau von der Leyen und ihrer Kommission nicht zu erwarten. Es ist h脙露chste Zeit, dass sie geht.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nicol脙隆s Pascual de la Parte (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, nuestra prioridad absoluta debe ser llevar a Putin a la mesa de negociaciones y, para eso, tenemos que alterar su c脙隆lculo. 脙聣l piensa que nosotros nos vamos a cansar antes o que nos van a faltar los medios y los recursos financieros, militares y pol脙颅ticos para ayudar a Ucrania, y en esa equivocaci脙鲁n de su c脙隆lculo tenemos que incidir.

Tenemos que asegurarnos de dar a Ucrania toda la ayuda y el soporte financiero que necesite en los pr脙鲁ximos a脙卤os y, por eso, la Comisi脙鲁n ha decidido 芒聙聰y nosotros aprobamos芒聙聰 no que se confisque, sino que se utilicen 140脗聽000 millones de los activos rusos retenidos en Europa como garant脙颅a para un pr脙漏stamo a Ucrania. Como sabemos que Rusia no va a pagar nunca la reparaci脙鲁n, posiblemente perder脙隆 esa financiaci脙鲁n.

Somos conscientes de que existen riesgos legales en cuanto al prestigio de las inversiones extranjeras en Europa, a la confiscaci脙鲁n de los activos europeos no repatriados en Rusia y a la venta de divisas europeas en los mercados internacionales. Pero todos estos riesgos 芒聙聰que son ciertos芒聙聰 palidecen ante la necesidad de apoyar a Ucrania con todos los recursos financieros a nuestro alcance, y esto es una garant脙颅a de ello.

脗聽脗聽Arkadiusz Mularczyk (ECR). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Panie Komisarzu! Ja mam zatem pytanie, Panie Komisarzu: jak to jest, 脜录e Unia Europejska, Komisja Europejska nie stosuje tych samych standard脙鲁w do w脜聜asnych cz脜聜onk脙鲁w? Bo przecie脜录 Polska dwa lata temu wyst脛聟pi脜聜a o reparacje wojenne do Niemiec. Grecja cztery lata temu wyst脛聟pi脜聜a r脙鲁wnie脜录 o reparacje wojenne do Niemiec.

脗聽脗聽Anna-Maja Henriksson (Renew). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Kommission脙陇r. Vem var det som startade kriget mot Ukraina? Jo, det var Putin. Vem 脙陇r det som har bombat, orsakat oerh脙露rd f脙露r脙露delse, skada, om脙楼ttligt lidande? Jo, det var och 脙陇r Putin. Vem 脙陇r det som har brutit mot internationell r脙陇tt? Det 脙陇r Putin.

D脙陇rf脙露r 脙陇r fr脙楼gan enkel n脙陇r man st脙陇ller den: Vem ska betala? Det 脙陇r klart att det 脙陇r Putin och hans Ryssland som ska betala.

Vad g脙露r EU? Inte 脙陇nnu tillr脙陇ckligt. Men v脙楼rt momentum 脙陇r nu. Nu 脙陇r det dags att leverera. Nu 脙陇r det dags att visa att vi i EU inte vill sitta i publiken, utan vi vill vara ocks脙楼 en del av l脙露sningen, och d脙陇rf脙露r m脙楼ste vi v脙楼ga anv脙陇nda de frysta ryska medlen f脙露r Ukraina. Och det br脙楼dskar.

Det 脙陇r dags att visa att vi menar allvar d脙楼 vi s脙陇ger att Ukrainas kamp f脙露r frihet ocks脙楼 脙陇r v脙楼r kamp f脙露r frihet, demokrati och m脙陇nskliga r脙陇ttigheter.

脗聽脗聽Siegbert Frank Droese (ESN). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, verehrte Kollegen! Was die Kommission hier plant, ist ein politischer Dammbruch. Das Recht am Eigentum ist keine Dekoration, sondern ein fundamentales Recht in Demokratien. Die Kommission fordert st脙陇ndig Rechtsstaatlichkeit, bricht aber selbst fundamentale Regeln, wenn es ihr opportun erscheint.

Wer so handelt, ruiniert das Vertrauen in Europa als Finanzstandort auf Generationen. Kapital flie脙聼t 脙录blicherweise nur dorthin, wo Rechtssicherheit herrscht, doch die Kommission sendet ein fatales Signal. Ihr sind politische Interessen wichtiger als das Recht. Die EU w脙录rde damit zum finanziellen Schurkenstaat. W脙陇hrend die Kommission russische Milliarden ergaunern will, schweigt man zu rechtsstaatlichem Versagen in den eigenen Mitgliedstaaten. Beispiel: Von einem polnischen Gericht wird ein mutma脙聼licher Nord-Stream芒聙聭Bomber freigesprochen und Staatsterrorismus legitimiert脗聽芒聙聯 von der Kommission bisher kein Ton dazu.

Meine Damen und Herren, Frieden entsteht nicht durch Rechtsbruch, und Sicherheit entsteht nicht durch Heuchelei. Der heilige Antonius sagte bereits vor 脙录ber 1脗聽600 Jahren: 芒聙聻Nimm das Recht weg 芒聙聯 was ist dann ein Staat noch anderes als eine gro脙聼e R脙陇uberbande?芒聙聺 Da sollten Sie mal dr脙录ber nachdenken, Herr Kommissar, und auch dar脙录ber, dass die USA bei diesem Rechtsbruch nicht mitmachen.

(Der Redner ist damit einverstanden, auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹 zu antworten.)

脗聽脗聽Karin Karlsbro (Renew), fr脙楼ga ("bl脙楼tt kort"). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Kollegor. Det talas om tillit, men jag undrar h脙陇r varf脙露r man st脙陇ller sig p脙楼 f脙露r脙露varens sida.

Om en pyroman br脙陇nner ner ditt hus, tycker ni d脙楼 att ni sj脙陇lv ska betala f脙露r att bygga upp det igen? Vem 脙陇r skyldig f脙露r f脙露rst脙露relsen av Ukraina om inte Ryssland? Varf脙露r ska d脙楼 inte Ryssland ocks脙楼 betala?

脗聽脗聽Siegbert Frank Droese (ESN), Antwort auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹. 芒聙聯 Frau Kollegin, ich wei脙聼 nicht, ob Sie die Frage vom German Marshall Fund oder von Soros pers脙露nlich aufgeschrieben bekommen haben. Ich wei脙聼 auch nicht, ob Sie gerade schon da waren. Wenn Sie mir zugeh脙露rt h脙陇tten: Ich habe niemals davon gesprochen, sondern mir ging es eindeutig ums Recht. Wir m脙录ssen das Recht erhalten, sonst investiert einfach niemand mehr in unser sch脙露nes Europa. Und das haben Sie doch hoffentlich richtig verstanden.

脗聽脗聽Rasa Juknevi脛聧ien脛聴 (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, my main message today is simple: let's take this step decisively. It is a crucial decision. The European Parliament's position has been clear since April脗聽2022, when we first called for Russia to pay for the destruction it inflicted, including through the confiscation of Russian frozen assets, in full accordance with international law. Putin's Russia, which continues to harm Ukraine, must not get its money back until it has fully compensated for the damage it caused.

I want to congratulate the European Commission and Council on finding a sound legal solution. We must stand in solidarity with Belgium and other Member States, and share the responsibility for this decision together. We are on the right path. Let's stand firm. Let's fight evil together.

脗聽脗聽Karin Karlsbro (Renew). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Kommissionen. Kollegor. Det 脙陇r dags att Ryssland f脙楼r betala f脙露r kriget i Ukraina. EU m脙楼ste ta n脙陇sta steg och anv脙陇nda de frysta ryska tillg脙楼ngarna fullt ut som ett f脙露rskott p脙楼 v脙陇ntande krigsskadest脙楼nd.

Men varf脙露r har vi inte redan n脙楼got beslut p脙楼 plats? Jo, f脙露r att det finns en oro fr脙楼n medlemsl脙陇nder som tj脙陇nat p脙楼 att ha tillg脙楼ngarna i f脙露rvar. En oro f脙露r att Ryssland aldrig kommer att betala n脙楼got krigsskadest脙楼nd. En oro f脙露r konsekvenserna f脙露r eurons st脙陇llning.

Den h脙陇r st脙陇ndiga oron m脙楼ste f脙楼 ett slut. Det finns en sak att oroas f脙露r: att Ukraina skulle f脙露rlora, att vi l脙楼tit Putin vinna kriget. S脙楼 slakta h脙露nan. Make Russia pay!

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Wouter Beke (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, Oekra脙炉ne is vandaag onze Europese eerstelijnsdefensie en verdient alle steun. Whatever it takes. Maar er is een verleiding: de snelle en gemakkelijke oplossing, namelijk grijpen naar de bevroren Russische tegoeden. Ze lijken een beetje op kroonjuwelen die in een al dan niet beveiligde glazen kast zitten. Maar dat zijn ze niet, want in tegenstelling tot kroonjuwelen brengen de bevroren tegoeden intresten op. Die intresten kunnen gebruikt worden om G7-leningen voor Oekra脙炉ne te betalen. Vergeet niet dat we dat geld maar 脙漏脙漏n keer kunnen gebruiken: ofwel voor militaire steun, ofwel straks voor de wederopbouw van Oekra脙炉ne.

Daartegenover staat een gedurfde keuze, namelijk een voorstel voor een structureel Europees Oekra脙炉nefonds. Als we 2脗聽% van onze defensie-uitgaven europeaniseren, dan kunnen we 150脗聽miljard脗聽EUR mobiliseren. 150脗聽miljard脗聽EUR, waarbij we zowel de intresten blijven gebruiken voor Oekra脙炉ne als Oekra脙炉ne op militair en financieel vlak kunnen steunen en de frozen assets kunnen gebruiken voor de wederopbouw van Oekra脙炉ne.

脗聽脗聽Micha脜聜 Szczerba (PPE). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Zbli脜录a si脛聶 kluczowa decyzja dotycz脛聟ca Ukrainy. Apeluj脛聶 do Rady Europejskiej o odwa脜录ne dzia脜聜ania w sprawie zamro脜录onych rosyjskich aktyw脙鲁w pa脜聞stwowych. Ukraina potrzebuje ich dzi脜聸. Komisja jest gotowa.

I to jest pytanie, panie komisarzu: czy Komisja jest gotowa do opracowania wniosku prawnego dotycz脛聟cego wykorzystania aktyw脙鲁w do sfinansowania po脜录yczki dla Ukrainy?

Uwolnienie tych 140 miliard脙鲁w euro pozwoli na sfinansowanie obrony przez najbli脜录sze dwa, trzy lata. Historyczna odpowiedzialno脜聸脛聡 spoczywa na Belgii 芒聙聯 kraju, kt脙鲁ry jest siedzib脛聟 Euroclear. To nie czas na p脙鲁脜聜脜聸rodki. Zbyt d脜聜ugo dyskutowali脜聸my o kwestiach prawnych. To jest bezprecedensowy, ale konieczny krok, kt脙鲁ry nale脜录y podj脛聟脛聡. Nie mo脜录emy by脛聡 bezczynni. Rosja nie ma woli zako脜聞czenia wojny, nie tylko morduj脛聟c cywili, ale tak脜录e niszcz脛聟c infrastruktur脛聶, za脜聜amuj脛聟c gospodark脛聶 ukrai脜聞sk脛聟 i powoduj脛聟c ogromny kryzys demograficzny. To czas decyzji. Bardzo o nie prosimy.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sandra Kalniete (PPE). 芒聙聯 Cien脛芦t脛聛 s脛聯des vad脛芦t脛聛ja! Kol脛聯脛拢i! Ukrainai 2026. gad脛聛 ir nepiecie脜隆ami vismaz 40 miljardi 脛聛r脛聯j脛聛s pal脛芦dz脛芦bas, lai t脛聛 iztur脛聯tu gaid脛聛mo gr脜芦to ziemu apst脛聛k脛录os, kad Krievija pastiprin脛聛ti izn脛芦cina Ukrainas ener脛拢脛聯tikas infrastrukt脜芦ru.

M脜芦su izv脛聯le ir vienk脛聛r脜隆a 芒聙聰 vai nu pal脛芦dz脛芦bai izmantot savu nodok脛录u maks脛聛t脛聛ju naudu, vai agresora naudu. Pareizi b脜芦tu izmantot abas. Eiropas valstis ir pietiekami bag脛聛tas, lai no sava bud脜戮eta sniegtu Ukrainai liel脛聛ku atbalstu. T脛聛pat ir steidzami j脛聛konfisc脛聯 Krievijas iesald脛聯tie akt脛芦vi un tie j脛聛izmanto Ukrainas apbru脜聠o脜隆anai un atjauno脜隆anai.

N脛聛kamaj脛聛 Eiropas Padom脛聯 ir j脛聛atbalsta Eiropas Komisijas ierosin脛聛tais aizdevuma meh脛聛nisms, kur脛聛 脜聠emtas v脛聯r脛聛 visu dal脛芦bvalstu ba脜戮as. Pietiek aizbildin脛聛ties ar 芒聙聹riskiem makroekonomiskajai stabilit脛聛tei芒聙聺. Bez tam Eiropas Savien脛芦bai ir steidzami j脛聛pie脜聠em 19. sankciju pakete. Maksim脛聛ls spiediens uz Krievijas kara ekonomiku ir vien脛芦gais ce脛录脜隆 uz mieru.

M脛聯s nedr脛芦kstam pie脛录aut Krievijas uzvaru, jo t脛聛 b脜芦tu Eiropas sak脛聛ve.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andrey Kovatchev (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear Commissioner, I warmly welcome today's statement by President Zelensky, European leaders and the Prime Minister of Great Britain, which underlined the ongoing work to prepare measures 'to use the full value of the Russian immobilised sovereign assets, so that Ukraine has the resources it needs now'.

As a crucial part of these efforts, I would like to call on the European Member States and the Commission to quickly approve the proposal to create a Ukrainian reparation loan. This loan would use the cash balance created by the Russian assets as loans. It is a legally sound and morally just way to put these resources to work for the Ukrainian people.

This mechanism would not confiscate Russian assets; it will use them to hold Moscow accountable. Russia will remain the owner of the assets but will only recover them after it honours its obligation to pay reparations to Ukraine. It will provide Ukraine with vital funds now, while respecting international law and without straining European national budgets. It demonstrates the ability of Europe to lead independently.

All European Union Member States have the moral obligation to back this proposal without delay. We need to act now, Commission, thank you.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

Catch-the-eye procedure

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ond脜聶ej Kol脙隆脜聶 (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, ladies and gentlemen, I think that without exaggeration it can be said that the fate of Ukraine's defence capabilities and reconstruction possibilities now lies in Belgian hands. We are running out of cash, and the only way to effectively continue helping Ukraine is to use frozen Russian assets 芒聙聯 not just the interest, but the assets themselves.

Speaking of military aid, 60脗聽% of all costs are now covered by five members of the so-called 'coalition of the willing': Germany, Denmark, the Netherlands, the United Kingdom and Norway. With the withdrawal of US financial participation, Europe must look for new funds if we do not want the aid to collapse next year. Using this Russian money would mean securing funding for this purpose not only for 2026, but also for 2027. And honestly, I don't see a single reason why the European taxpayer should carry this load rather than the one who is responsible for the war.

After years of endless reports of attacks on civilians and atrocities committed by the Russian army, we can no longer protect the assets of a criminal regime. So, dear Belgian colleagues, I urge you: do not protect Russia's attack assets. Protect Ukraine!

(The President cut off the speaker)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Juan Fernando L脙鲁pez Aguilar (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or comisario Dombrovskis, este Parlamento Europeo ha apoyado todos los paquetes de sanciones, empezando por la congelaci脙鲁n de los activos del Banco Central ruso, hasta sumar en sucesivas medidas sancionadoras trescientos mil millones de euros rusos congelados en la Uni脙鲁n Europea. Y es triste que a estas alturas estemos discutiendo sobre la viabilidad de una soluci脙鲁n jur脙颅dica y la cobertura que puede prestar una resoluci脙鲁n de las Naciones Unidas para reparar las responsabilidades contra脙颅das con Rusia, con las v脙颅ctimas y con la destrucci脙鲁n causada por su guerra de agresi脙鲁n.

Pero m脙隆s preocupante todav脙颅a es nuestra falta de unidad, que tengamos que asistir a que un jefe de Gobierno europeo, Viktor Orb脙隆n, se apreste a recibir en la Uni脙鲁n Europea a Putin, violando nuestros compromisos con el Tribunal Penal Internacional y los suyos todav脙颅a vigentes.

Y, desde luego, es mucho m脙隆s preocupante todav脙颅a que tengamos que soportar que el futuro de Ucrania se decida haciendo a la Uni脙鲁n Europea irrelevante, situ脙隆ndola fuera completamente de la escena, y sin ni siquiera escuchar a Ucrania. Que el futuro de Ucrania puedan decidirlo entre Putin y Trump es absolutamente inaceptable.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dainius 脜陆alimas (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear Commissioner, dear colleagues, Ukraine needs predictability and stable, multiyear support that America no longer guarantees and that Europe has yet to deliver at full scale.

The Commission's proposal to use frozen Russian assets for reparation loans is a welcome first step. Russian state money must be used for Ukraine's self-defence, not for the aggressor's future.

It also has a sound international legal basis. Reparation loans are legitimate third-party countermeasures to Russia's ongoing war of aggression, which is the gravest breach of the UN Charter.

They meet the requirements of proportionality and temporariness under international law. They also are of an exceptional nature, as applied only in respect of the aggressor and only for the aggression.

Europe has the duty to assist the victim by all possible means. There is no other choice to secure our common European future.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Reinier Van Lanschot (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, it is time to make the aggressor pay. I have been calling for it for years and now the moment seems to finally be there: EUR脗聽250脗聽billion of Russian State money is frozen in European banks. It's so much money 芒聙聯 using it can change the outcome of the war!

This is our chance to help Ukraine rebuild what Russia destroyed without European taxpayers having to pay a single cent. This is our chance to make sure that Ukraine stands strong in upcoming peace talks. This is our chance to show Putin that Europe has no war fatigue 芒聙聯 that when the US retreats, Europe doubles down.

Opponents of this plan say that it's too risky, that it sets a dangerous precedent if we use money from an aggressive dictatorship. But this is exactly the precedent we need to set. If you don't start a war, destroy millions of homes, bomb hospitals, torture soldiers, kill civilians and abduct children, then you have nothing to fear.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Grzegorz Braun (NI). 芒聙聯 (start of speech off mic) ... to others, you don't like done to yourself. The very fact that you're discussing here prospects and possibilities of bold, brazen theft is an outrage.

Don't behave like Bolsheviks 芒聙聯 I know you are as far as your attitude towards property is concerned, and you present your Bolshevik attitude very often 芒聙聯 but don't be fools also, because it's foolish. It's not only wrong 芒聙聯 morally wrong, legally wrong 芒聙聯 it's politically stupid!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Valdis Dombrovskis, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, first of all, thank you all for this very engaging and substantive discussion. This debate has once again shown the resolve of the European Parliament to continue supporting Ukraine, so I want to thank you for this political support.

We must continue to intensify our efforts across all fronts to bolster Ukraine's resilience and strengthen our support. This includes providing additional assistance, remaining committed to Ukraine's European accession path, and seizing every opportunity to bring a swift end to Russia's war of aggression.

To do so, we will continue using all the instruments we have at our disposal. First, we will continue to deliver financial support to Ukraine under the G7 Extraordinary Revenue Acceleration initiative, through which the EU has already disbursed EUR脗聽14脗聽billion this year.

Second, through the Ukraine facility, we will continue to provide significant support to Ukraine as it works to implement crucial reforms.

Thirdly, we will continue to support Ukraine in meeting its defence needs, notably through the Ukraine Support Instrument in the European defence industry programme. Ukraine will be treated as an EU Member State under the instrument and will receive direct support for procurement and ramp-up actions.

Similarly, under the recently established Security Action for Europe (SAFE) instrument, the Member States also expressed interest in involving Ukraine under SAFE.

To address Ukraine's financing needs in 2026, 2027 and beyond, we are working on additional solutions such as the reparation loan. This would provide Ukraine with a stable and predictable source of funding, leveraging the value of immobilised Russian assets while ensuring full compliance with our international law obligations.

Maybe here I'll come to some of the points which were raised during the debate on the support of other partners: we obviously continued engagement with other partners, including our G7 partners, who are providing their share for Ukraine's financing. And it's worth noting that this reparation loan initiative, in a sense, is scalable. So other countries, which also have Russian assets in their jurisdiction can use a similar approach. The UK and Canada have actually signalled their readiness to do so.

Second, on some of the legal aspects, it's worth noting that this proposal stops short of confiscating Russian assets, while at the same time providing much needed financing to Ukraine.

On the arguments for financial stability, it's worth noting that we already took a fundamental decision on immobilising Russian assets in our territory, and it has not led to any financial stability issues or any issues with the international role of the euro.

Also, both the European Council and the G7 have already taken decisions that Russian assets should stay immobilised for as long as Russia does not pay reparations.

So basically, what we are doing with this reparation loan initiative is operationalising these decisions and providing, so to say, for how those cash balances associated with the Russian assets can be used in the meanwhile, so until Russia actually pays reparations.

So once again, I wanted to thank you for your broad support, which was here during this debate. I look forward to continuing engaging with you on this matter in the coming weeks and months. And we certainly must continue to provide our strong support and be very clear and unwavering on this.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The debate is closed.

Written Statements (Rule 178)

脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Vilija Blinkevi脛聧i脜芦t脛聴 (S&D), ra脜隆tu. 芒聙聯 Netrukus bus keturi metai nuo karo Ukrainoje prad脜戮ios ir Ukraina ir jos 脜戮mon脛聴s ry脜戮tingai tebekovoja u脜戮 savo laisv脛聶 ir ateit脛炉. Rusijos sukeltas karas padar脛聴 nei脜隆matuojam脛聟 脜戮al脛聟 , tod脛聴l m脜芦s脜鲁 vis脜鲁 pareiga yra prisid脛聴ti prie Ukrainos atstatymo ir jos gynybini脜鲁 paj脛聴gum脜鲁 stiprinimo. Tarptautin脛聴 bendruomen脛聴 privalo padidinti finansavim脛聟, kad pad脛聴t脜鲁 Ukrainai atsigauti ir u脜戮tikrinti saugum脛聟. Viena perspektyvi ir teisinga priemon脛聴 脜隆iam tikslui pasiekti yra panaudoti Rusijos 脛炉脜隆aldytus turtus. 脜聽ie turtai, laikomi u脜戮sienio s脛聟skaitose d脛聴l sankcij脜鲁, sudaro milijardus, kurie gal脛聴t脜鲁 b脜芦ti nukreipti Ukrainos infrastrukt脜芦ros atstatymui, pragyvenimo 脜隆altini脜鲁 atk脜芦rimui ir gynybos sistem脜鲁 stiprinimui. Toks po脜戮i脜芦ris ne tik pad脛聴t脜鲁 Ukrainai, bet ir ai脜隆kiai parodyt脜鲁, kad agresija ir tarptautin脛聴s teis脛聴s pa脜戮eidimai turi savo kain脛聟. 脜聽i脜鲁 脛炉脜隆aldyt脜鲁 l脛聴脜隆脜鲁 panaudojimas n脛聴ra vien tik baud脜戮iamoji priemon脛聴, tai yra teisingumas ir atsakomyb脛聴. Tai u脜戮tikrina, kad su karu susijusios l脛聴脜隆os yra panaudojamos taikai ir stabilumui atkurti. Ukrainos atstatymas n脛聴ra tik Ukrainos prioritetas 芒聙聯 tai yra ,vis脜鲁 pirma, Europos prioritetas, ir kartu globali b脜芦tinyb脛聴, siekiant i脜隆laikyti demokratij脛聟, taik脛聟 ir tarptautin脛聶 tvark脛聟. Europos s脛聟junga privalo rodyti tikr脛聟 lyderyst脛聶 ne tik padidinant finansavim脛聟 Ukrainai, bet ir randant politin脛聶 vali脛聟 panaudoti 脛炉脜隆aldytas Rusijos l脛聴脜隆as, kad sustiprintume Ukrain脛聟, geban脛聧i脛聟 u脜戮tikrinti savo pa脛聧ios ateit脛炉.

脗聽

17. Polarisation and increased repression in Serbia, one year after the Novi Sad tragedy (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next item is the debate on the Commission statement on polarisation and increased repression in Serbia, one year after the Novi Sad tragedy (2025/2917(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Kos, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear Members of the European Parliament, here we are again. I appreciate the opportunity to hold another debate on Serbia, as we approach one year since the Novi Sad tragedy. I wish to start by paying tribute to the 16 lives lost in this tragic canopy collapse at the railway station on 1 November last year, in Novi Sad. That moment of sorrow touched all of us, and it has especially shaken Serbia.

The tragedy and the aftermath have further deepened the polarisation in Serbian society. For the past 11 months, Serbian citizens, led by students, have been protesting across Serbia's towns and cities, calling for accountability for this tragedy. Corruption and the perceived lack of accountability were the main two triggers of protests. Also, the motives for discontent of the people are primarily the lack of democracy, media capture and impunity of politicians.

The Commission has been following the situation in Serbia very closely, as I mentioned in this House during the last debate, not even two months ago. Overall, the police have respected the principle of the freedom of assembly. However, recently several incidents and violence against students, peaceful protesters and journalists have been recorded as well, and there have been instances of suspected excessive use of force by the Serbian police. I wish to reiterate, once again, that we expect police action to be proportionate and to respect fundamental rights. The EU expects these cases to be investigated as a matter of priority. We also condemn all acts of hate, vandalism and violence from protesters and continue to call on all sides to de-escalate tension.

The EU accession process addresses many of the key issues that are at the heart of the debate in Serbia today. As a candidate country, we expect Serbia to respect the key values that are at the foundation of our Union. And as you know, our Union is founded on the values of respect for human dignity, freedom, democracy, equality, the rule of law and respect for human rights. Respect for fundamental rights and values, including freedom of peaceful assembly, media and academic freedom are key elements of Serbia's EU path and need to be adhered to by everyone. The Serbian Government should create an enabling environment for civil society. Intimidation and pressure on civil society is not the way we operate in the Union and must not be tolerated.

As underlined on previous occasions, it is imperative for Serbia to create conditions for a renewed dialogue between stakeholders across the political spectrum and across society. In our view, this should be based on a renewed societal consensus on key reforms, so I welcome the readiness to take it forward. In this context, let me welcome the recent progress made on the law on the unified voter register, and on the council of the regulatory body for electronic media (REM), achieved together with the civil society and the opposition. This comes in addition to the recent creation of a joint monitoring committee for the growth plan, which includes representatives of civil society.

There are concrete first steps towards re-engagement with all parts of the society and across the political spectrum. These agreements have now to be implemented properly, especially the OSCE ODIHR recommendations to ensure a level playing field for all contenders at elections, a long-standing commitment of Serbia's that shall be implemented and respected. A successful election of the new council of media regulator REM, in accordance with the legal requirements, and the new council implementing properly its function, could be the next step. This would serve as an example that progress is possible when there is a will and a constructive approach by all. It will also address an important request of the citizens who protested on the streets.

Dear honourable Members, it is in both Serbia's and our interests to bring a democratic Serbia into the EU. And I want to make it clear once again: I am truly devoted to it. We will continue with our engagement and will do everything possible to help Serbia move forward on its accession path. But as I have already stressed, for this to happen, concrete steps on democratic principles and reforms are needed.

We live in a fractured world 芒聙聯 now is the time for strategic choices. The EU is very clear on where it stands. Serbia needs to make its strategic choice loud and clear too. Close relations with Moscow and Beijing, accompanied with critical statements towards the EU, including relating hostile Russian narratives, is not what is expected from a candidate country. Hostile political rhetoric, including against the EU, its citizens and especially Members of the European Parliament, runs contrary to EU values and standards.

The same goes for disinformation against the EU, spread by some media yesterday and today. Namely, yesterday, the Council adopted its negotiation position on REPowerEU, which includes, for Member States, a ban on importing Russian gas from 2028 onwards. This decision does not imply that EU Member States will have to stop transit of Russian gas to third countries, such as Serbia, so the EU is not cutting any supplies to Serbia. These are lies. Instead 芒聙聯 and I really stress this 芒聙聯 the EU is supporting Serbia, and other West Balkan countries, for diversifying their energy supplies, and for closer integration into the EU's energy networks, both for electricity and gas, as well as through strategic investment. And let me reiterate President Vu脛聧i脛聡's remark in the press conference with President von der Leyen stating Serbia's strategic objective to diversify its gas supplies. Yesterday's decision is fully consistent with this, and the EU is ready to support it.

At the same time, I have to say that we have noted recent progress on Serbia's alignment with the EU's common foreign and security policy, and this approach should be maintained, strengthened and consistently sustained. Much more is needed to reach the CFSP脗聽alignment rate of a country that declares EU membership its strategic goal. We need Serbia to reaffirm its choice clearly and decisively. The EU's doors are wide open and what we have to offer cannot be matched. Serbia's commitment to the EU path can only be proven with its political will to credibly implement the required reforms, and the EU orientation has to be manifested via public narrative and concrete actions, both domestically and externally.

Democratic changes can only come from within the country through internal democratic processes. Citizens call for more rule of law and democratic norms, so the Commission will engage with the competent authorities and other stakeholders to reassure progress on reforms, particularly on the electoral framework, in the judiciary, on the fight against corruption, and media and academic freedom, including completing the election of the new council of media regulator REM.脗聽Now is the moment for the authorities to demonstrate Serbia's commitment to its EU path, both in words and deeds. I look forward to hearing your views on the domestic situation in Serbia.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Reinhold Lopatka, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, colleagues, it is in the hands of President脗聽Vu脛聧i脛聡 to take Serbia towards Europe. Their neighbour Montenegro is showing the path, or 芒聙聯 like Georgia 芒聙聯 take the opposite direction.

Serbia's European future remains open, of course, but it depends on respect for our common values. That means an independent judiciary, free media and political pluralism. It is crucial to have much closer alignment with the European Union in foreign and security policy, including towards Russia.

We want Serbia to succeed, of course, but that requires determined reforms, rejection of nationalist narratives from political leaders as well as all sections of civil society, and we have also seen it through the demonstrators. Serbia has made progress, of course, in certain areas, including economic growth, regional infrastructure, connectivity and cooperation with the EU on migration management and energy security. But there are many other fields where there is no progress, not at all.

Our long-standing commitment to Serbia's European perspective is essential, and it always depends on full respect for the rule of law and fundamental rights. Serbia needs a democratic dialogue, de芒聙聭escalation and inclusive political participation. It is Serbia's decision whether it comes back to the European track.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

VORSITZ: SABINE VERHEYEN
Vizepr脙陇sidentin

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tonino Picula, u ime kluba S&D. 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, gospo脛聭o povjerenice, kolegice i kolege, najbolji na脛聧in iskazivanja po脛聧asti 脜戮rtvama pada nadstre脜隆nice u Novom Sadu je razotkrivanje korijena problema koji su doveli do tragedije.

Tragedija u Novom Sadu dokazuje da korupcija nije obi脛聧an kriminal. Korupcija uni脜隆tava institucije i na脜戮alost, odnosi 脜戮ivote. Ova nesre脛聡a reflektira neke od glavnih strukturnih problema u Srbiji- netransparentnu uklju脛聧enost stranih aktera u projekt i izostanak kontrola koje bi omogu脛聡ile kori脜隆tenje objekta jer sigurnosni standardi nisu po脜隆tovani, ometanje istrage i neovisnosti pravosu脛聭a nakon tragedije te represiju prema gra脛聭anima koji tra脜戮e odgovornost i promjene. Dok vlast u Srbiji ponavlja da 脜戮eli u脛聡i u Europsku uniju svakim danom sve vi脜隆e odstupa od njezinih temeljnih vrijednosti. Napredak se mjeri konkretnim reformama, a ne obe脛聡anjima bez pokri脛聡a. Neovisne institucije, slobodni mediji i gra脛聭ani trebaju biti partneri vlasti, a ne mete raznih teorija zavjera. Ova rasprava i rezolucija, naravno, nisu napad na Srbiju.

Nije Europa iznevjerila Srbiju. Vlast u Srbiji iznevjerava svoje gra脛聭ane.

脗聽脗聽Harald Vilimsky, im Namen der PfE-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, meine sehr geehrten Damen und Herren! Gestern hat in Budapest ein aus meiner Sicht sehr wichtiges Treffen stattgefunden. Der serbische Staatspr脙陇sident Aleksandar Vu脛聧i脛聡 wurde eingeladen vom ungarischen Premierminister Viktor Orb脙隆n. Und beide haben, glaube ich, etwas gemeinsam, sie haben n脙陇mlich beide einen sehr starken R脙录ckhalt aus ihren jeweiligen Bev脙露lkerungen. Beide sind stolze Patrioten, und beide treten auch auf gegen den woken Unfug, der sich hier zurzeit in vielen anderen L脙陇ndern entwickelt. Aber beide sind deswegen wahrscheinlich auch im Visier der Br脙录sseler Eliten und der Br脙录sseler Nomenklatura. Wenn ich da etwa an Ungarn denke, wo man diese Kunstfigur, diesen P脙漏ter Magyar, geschaffen hat, nur um Viktor Orb脙隆n zu schaden bei den Wahlen. Ich kann sagen: Das wird nicht funktionieren. Viktor Orb脙隆n ist popul脙陇rer denn je, und der Versuch, ihn zu st脙录rzen, wird nicht gelingen.

Und genauso ist es in Serbien zurzeit der Fall, wo man mit inszenierten Massenprotesten 芒聙聯 wahrscheinlich zum Teil aus dem Ausland finanziert 芒聙聯 versucht, Vu脛聧i脛聡 zu schaden in seinem erfolgreichen Kurs. Ich frage mich: Was hat denn ein Staatspr脙陇sident mitzuverantworten, wenn 芒聙聯 so tragisch das Ungl脙录ck auch sein mag 芒聙聯 hier Menschen zu Schaden kommen? Ich bedauere das aus tiefstem Herzen. Aber was kann denn er daf脙录r? Das w脙陇re doch genauso, wie wenn hier in Stra脙聼burg am Bahnhof ein Ungl脙录ck passiert und ich das Pr脙陇sidium des Hauses hier daf脙录r verantwortlich machen w脙录rde. Nein, das kann nicht sein. Wenn Sie hier Medien- und Meinungsfreiheit beklagen in Serbien, mag ich Ihnen antworten: Digital Services Act, Media Freedom Act, das, was Sie mit Chatkontrollen planen, da ist Nachholbedarf, das zu regeln. Aber nicht in Serbien. Die Serben sind ein sehr stolzes Volk 芒聙娄

(Die Pr脙陇sidentin entzieht dem Redner das Wort.)
