脗聽脗聽Irena Joveva, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 (The speaker spoke in a non-official language)

It's in Serbian, but something should sound familiar. That's what the regime in Serbia called the people on the streets: Nazis. Let that sink in.

Do you know that Novi Sad literally means 'new now'? The protesters' wish is simple: a new future. Not tomorrow. Not after another round of no doings. Now.

So now get this grotesque irony, this theatre of power: those ordering the beatings called the beaten 'Nazis'. They invent fake students, '脛聠acis', while the real students bleed for democracy. And here comes the truth: the real students are the ones teaching us all the real lesson of courage. It's our turn to teach one back.

To the students: we stand with democracy, with you. To the autocrats in Belgrade, your time of impunity is ending. Targeted sanctions should come to every single one responsible.

Oh, and don't rush with those textbooks you promised, because your lesson by force is over. History will grade you.

(The speaker spoke in a non-official language).

We will see soon enough.

I choose justice, I choose Novi Sad, new and now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Vladimir Prebili脛聧, v imenu skupine Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Gospa predsednica! Spo脜隆tovana komisarka. Korupcija ubija. In nikjer ni to bolj o脛聧itno kot v Novem Sadu.

16 nedol脜戮nih ljudi je umrlo, ker je nekdo preprosto ukradel denar, namenjen njihovi varnosti. To ni malomarnost. To je umor zaradi korupcije. Ulice Srbije danes glasno kli脛聧ejo po spremembi. Najve脛聧ji 脜隆tudentski protesti v zgodovini te dr脜戮ave zahtevajo eno samo stvar - pravico. To lahko pri脛聧akujemo samo v primeru resni脛聧no svobodnih in po脜隆tenih volitev. Edino te lahko prekinejo vpliv korupcije in organiziranega kriminala.

Pozdravljamo stro脜戮ji ton Evropske komisije. Morda malo pozno, a vendar zelo, zelo pomembno. Toda besede, seveda, same ne bodo dovolj. Evropski parlament mora v Srbijo poslati tudi opazovalno misijo in to takoj. Naj bom jasen: nobenega evra iz na脛聧rta za rast, dokler ne pride do popolne uveljavitve neodvisnega delovanja medijskega regulatorja REM, preglednega volilnega imenika, svobode za vse politi脛聧ne zapornike ter konca ustrahovanja medijev.

In moje sporo脛聧ilo kolegom iz Evropske ljudske ljudske stranke. 脛聦e 脜戮e lansko leto ni bilo dovolj, da bi prekinili sodelovanje s SNS, potem je danes zadnji 脛聧as, da ukrepate in se postavite na pravo stran zgodovine.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Danilo Della Valle, a nome del gruppo The Left. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, signora Commissaria, onorevoli colleghi, 脙篓 passato un anno dal terribile incidente alla stazione di Novi Sad e il mio pensiero va alle vittime e ai loro familiari che, insieme a centinaia di migliaia di serbi, sono scesi in piazza per chiedere a gran voce che sia fatta giustizia e che chi ha sbagliato paghi.

Questa tragedia ci ha anche mostrato che la societ脙聽 serba 脙篓 viva, che la sua parte migliore, quella su cui si riversano le speranze per il futuro del Paese, 脙篓 capace di attivarsi e chiedersi un cambiamento reale contro la corruzione. Le imponenti manifestazioni che hanno portato in piazza centinaia di migliaia di persone sono un segno di speranza. Abbiamo il dovere di stare al fianco degli studenti e del popolo serbo ma abbiamo anche l'obbligo di rispettare la loro autodeterminazione.

Spesso in quest'Aula le proteste nei Paesi non allineati con la politica estera dell'Unione europea sono state occasioni per fare pressioni sull'opinione pubblica, affinch脙漏 si cambiasse dall'alto ci脙虏 che non andava bene a Bruxelles o a Washington. Anche sulle proteste in Serbia vedo che qualcuno vorrebbe utilizzare lo stesso metodo disastroso visto a Maidan, che ha contribuito a portare la guerra alle porte dell'Europa.

Nelle manifestazioni degli studenti non vi erano bandiere dell'Unione europea. Il popolo serbo non ha perso la memoria, non ha dimenticato i bombardamenti NATO del '99 e l'atteggiamento coloniale di tanti Paesi europei, compreso il mio, l'Italia, che hanno reso i Balcani una polveriera dove spesso regnano criminalit脙聽 e corruzione e, in alcuni casi, la democrazia 脙篓 solo uno slogan.

Stiamo molto attenti a non gettare benzina sul fuoco: i Balcani sono una pentola a pressione pronta a scoppiare. La storia, si sa, si ripete sempre due volte. Abbiamo gi脙聽 visto la tragedia e non vorrei assistere alla farsa.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tomasz Froelich, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Was wollen Sie eigentlich von Serbien? Wollen Sie Serbien zu einer zweiten Ukraine machen? Wollen Sie einen Regime Change? Den Eindruck muss man mittlerweile haben. Legitime Studentenproteste gegen Korruption werden f脙录r geopolitische Zwecke missbraucht. Sie stacheln Schl脙陇ger an, die auf serbische Polizisten einpr脙录geln. Sie destabilisieren ein Land, das mit westlicher Interventionspolitik sehr, sehr schlechte Erfahrungen gemacht hat.

Warum das alles? Weil Serbien pragmatische Beziehungen zu China und Russland unterh脙陇lt. Das ist Serbiens gutes Recht, denn Serbien ist ein souver脙陇ner Staat, und die Serben sind ein stolzes Volk脗聽芒聙聯 ein stolzes Volk, das Ihre arroganten Belehrungen nicht will. Und wenn Sie Zweifel an Ihrer eigenen Unbeliebtheit haben, dann schauen Sie sich doch einfach die neuesten Zahlen von Eurobarometer an: Nur noch jeder dritte Serbe ist f脙录r einen EU芒聙聭Beitritt 芒聙聯 Rekordtief. Gleichzeitig sind 60脗聽Prozent脗聽der Serben f脙录r einen Beitritt zu den BRICS. Sie treiben Serbien aus Europa. Sie treiben Serbien in die Arme Asiens. Ein fataler Fehler, denn wir brauchen Serbien als strategischen Partner, etwa im Kampf gegen illegale Migration, aber in Europa begreifen das nur Fico und Orb脙隆n. Finger weg von Serbien!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kathleen Van Brempt (S&D). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, het is al eerder gezegd: volgende week is het precies 脙漏脙漏n jaar geleden dat de verschrikkelijke tragedie in Novi Sad plaatsvond, waarbij 16脗聽mensen het leven lieten. Sindsdien zijn de moedige studentenprotesten voor transparantie, tegen corruptie en voor gerechtigheid voor de slachtoffers uitgegroeid tot een ware nationale beweging voor de democratie 芒聙聰 de grootste in de geschiedenis van Servi脙芦.

Ondertussen doen de Raad en de Commissie alsof er niets aan de hand is. En ik richt me niet persoonlijk tot u, mevrouw de commissaris, maar tot uw voorzitter, Ursula van der Leyen. Zij verscheen niet zo lang geleden opnieuw op de foto met Aleksandar Vu脛聧i脛聡, zonder enige kritiek te uiten. Dat beeld heeft de Servische bevolking diep teleurgesteld. Zij voelt zich daardoor in de steek gelaten. Dergelijke beelden wakkeren ook de anti-democratische gevoelens in de regio aan.

Collega芒聙聶s, mijn fractie 芒聙聰 en velen met ons in dit parlement 芒聙聰 kijken uit naar de toetreding van Servi脙芦 tot de Europese Unie. Maar dat moet dan wel een democratisch Servi脙芦 zijn. Om dat te bereiken, moeten we komen tot een goede resolutie, die jullie aanzet om een duidelijke keuze te maken: de keuze v脙鲁脙鲁r de Servische bevolking.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ant脙鲁nio T脙垄nger Corr脙陋a (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Commissioner, I was serving in Serbia in 1999. I was instrumental in downing Milo脜隆evi脛聡. You know one of the reasons why? Because I promised the Serbs a future in Europe. Eight years afterwards, I came back as a special representative of the European Union. And what had been done? Zero by us! So we are complaining about Serbia, but we are not complaining about us 芒聙聯 for what we did not do, for how we did not give any future to Serbia. And now we are complaining.

Yes, Vu脛聧i脛聡 is not perfect. Yes, the Serbs are not perfect. And yes, there is a complicated situation. But the Russians are there and the Chinese are there because we left the void. We left an empty space and they came in. It's normal, it's chemical, it's like in physics; it's like that also in politics.

So let's work a little bit harder to bring Serbia into the European Union: less talk and more action!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alessandro Ciriani (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, signora Commissaria, onorevoli colleghi, a un anno dalla tragica vicenda di Novi Sad, ci troviamo davanti a una situazione complessa che richiede innanzitutto responsabilit脙聽.

La Serbia attraversa mesi di polarizzazione con proteste diffuse e un confronto tra governo e opposizione che si 脙篓 irrigidito. 脙聢 fuori dubbio che la risposta dello Stato debba rimanere ancorata allo Stato di diritto. Tuttavia sarebbe un errore leggere questa crisi solo come un problema d'ordine pubblico: essa 脙篓 un chiaro segnale di un equilibrio che si sta incrinando.

L'ultimo sondaggio Eurobarometro riporta che l'adesione della Serbia all'Unione europea 脙篓 sostenuta solamente dal 33% dei cittadini serbi, il livello pi脙鹿 basso tra i Paesi candidati. Questo vuol dire che se l'Europa perde terreno politico a Belgrado, a trarne vantaggio sono attori con interessi a noi concorrenti, come Cina e Russia, con ricadute negative per la stabilit脙聽 regionale.

Per questo l'Unione deve mantenere un approccio equilibrato e costruttivo, rilanciando un'offerta europea di sviluppo e dialogo inclusivo. Solo cos脙卢 potremo evitare che la delusione si trasformi in distacco e che il distacco diventi allontanamento geopolitico: sarebbe una sconfitta per gli interessi europei.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Eugen Tomac (Renew). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝 Kos, vreau s脛聝 v脛聝 felicit pentru munca extraordinar脛聝 pe care o face脠聸i, pentru c脛聝 脙庐ncuraja脠聸i statele candidate s脛聝 r脛聝m脙垄n脛聝 optimiste, dar 脙庐n acela脠聶i timp s脛聝 respecte regulile care stau la baza Uniunii Europene.

Se 脙庐mpline脠聶te un an de la tragedia produs脛聝 脙庐n Novi Sad, o tragedie care a rupt societatea s脙垄rb脛聝 脙庐n dou脛聝 脠聶i care a scos 脙庐n eviden脠聸脛聝 o lupt脛聝 dreapt脛聝 pentru valori, nu pentru interese politice.

Ceea ce oamenii reclam脛聝 脙庐n Serbia sunt lucruri de bun sim脠聸 脠聶i sunt aspira脠聸iile unei genera脠聸ii care 脙庐脠聶i dore脠聶te ca Serbia s脛聝 fie parte a Europei, care 脙庐脠聶i dore脠聶te mai pu脠聸in脛聝 corup脠聸ie, care 脙庐脠聶i dore脠聶te un stat de drept 脠聶i o societate angajat脛聝 pe calea european脛聝. Dublul standard aplicat de Belgrad nu func脠聸ioneaz脛聝 脠聶i ar trebui descurajat 脠聶i v脛聝 脙庐ncuraj脛聝m s脛聝 merge脠聸i pe aceast脛聝 linie.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rasmus Nordqvist (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Commissioner, for one year the people of Serbia have been demanding change. People have gone to the streets because they saw that their own government values the financial gain of a selected few allies over the safety of their own people. Corruption is swept under the rug, critical voices are silenced, protesters are met with brutal violence.

Vu脛聧i脛聡 is right now dismantling the Serbian society with disinformation and hateful narratives in the media, which, of course, he is controlling. The free media and civil society are being harassed and silenced.

Last month I was in Novi Sad. I joined a peaceful protest that began with 16 minutes of silence commemorating the 16 people who died. The silence was haunting. All that could be heard was the crows in the air, and then people crying for the people lost.脗聽Let them not cry in vain, and let them not protest in vain.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Everding (The Left). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Seit vielen Monaten protestieren B脙录rger und Studierende in Serbien gegen Korruption und die zunehmende Autokratie in ihrem Land. Es scheint nicht so. Und w脙陇hrenddessen trifft sich die Kommissionspr脙陇sidentin mit ihm in Belgrad, als w脙陇re alles in bester Ordnung. Dieses Bild sendet das falsche Signal. 脗聽脗聽脛陆ubo脜隆 Blaha (NI). 芒聙聯 Po tom, 脛聧o v脙隆m nevy脜隆iel Majdan na Slovensku a v Gruz脙颅nsku, sna脜戮脙颅te sa o Majdan v Srbsku? Ve脛戮mi dobre viete, 脜戮e v Srbsku nie je 脜戮iadna rast脙潞ca represia. 脗聽脗聽Tomislav Sokol (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, povjerenice, kolegice i kolege, tragedija u Novom Sadu nije bila izolirani incident, ve脛聡 podsjetnik na moralnu i politi脛聧ku krizu u kojoj se Srbija danas nalazi, a koje nam pokazuje da ovakva kakva je danas nije joj mjesto u Europskoj uniji.

Iskustvo nedavnih prosvjeda, na脜戮alost, pokazuje da se duh velikosrpske ideologije jo脜隆 uvijek osje脛聡a i u redovima onih koji se nazivaju promjenom. Dok god je tako, bilo kakva ozbiljna transformacija ove zemlje ostat 脛聡e samo prazna fraza. Danas je Beograd, na脜戮alost, glavni izvor nestabilnosti u regiji. Dugoro脛聧ni opstanak suverene Crne Gore je u pitanju, a to je ne脜隆to 脜隆to je izuzetno opasno za cijelu jugoisto脛聧nu Europu.

Dame i gospodo, dok Srbija ne pro脛聭e dubinsku katarzu i ne odrekne se destruktivne velikosrpske politike ulazak u Europsku uniju ne dolazi u obzir.

脗聽脗聽Alessandra Moretti (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, promozione della violenza, arresti arbitrari, propaganda antieuropeista e pro-Russia, controllo dei media e dei discorsi d'odio, minacce di morte e violenza, sorveglianza illegale, licenziamenti ingiustificati: cos脙卢, nell'ultimo anno, il governo serbo ha risposto alle proteste che hanno riempito le strade di tutto il Paese in seguito al crollo di un tetto alla stazione di Novi Sad.

Dietro a questo disastro sembrano esserci vicende di corruzione, di appalti pilotati, vicende su cui i manifestanti chiedono di fare luce e di verificarne le responsabilit脙聽. La risposta da parte delle autorit脙聽 serbe nei confronti dei manifestanti non 脙篓 degna di un Paese candidato ad aderire all'Unione europea, n脙漏 tanto meno di uno Stato di diritto. La pericolosa polarizzazione creatasi nel Paese rischia di normalizzare la violenza e indebolire le istituzioni democratiche.

Serve un cambio di passo. L'impegno serbo verso il percorso europeo non pu脙虏 essere fatto solo di parole: servono progressi concreti, in particolare sullo Stato di diritto, la lotta alla corruzione, l'indipendenza dei media e della magistratura, un sistema elettorale libero e l'allineamento alla politica estera europea.

脗聽脗聽Gordan Bosanac (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, po脜隆tovana povjerenice, uo脛聧i godi脜隆njice novosadske tragedije, jo脜隆 jednom 脜戮elim izraziti su脛聡ut obiteljima stradalih, koji do dan danas nemaju odgovore na uzroke tragedije kao ni adekvatnu kompenzaciju.

脗聽脗聽Rasa Juknevi脛聧ien脛聴 (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, for the first time ever, I have decided to speak in a debate about Serbia 芒聙聯 and not because my country, Lithuania, has long been Serbia's rival in basketball. I wanted to speak today to offer a message of encouragement. Serbia's choices matter 芒聙聯 not only for its people, but for all of Europe.

I fully support the students who are protesting and fighting for a democratic Serbia. Only a genuine and stable democracy built on openness can heal the pain and scars left by history. The completion of democratic Europe is not just a political goal; it's our shared existential mission. Democracies in Europe are the only way to lasting peace in our continent. I truly hope that the Serbian nation will find its future on the European path.

脗聽脗聽Matja脜戮 Nemec (S&D). 芒聙聯 Gospa predsenica! Spo脜隆tovana komisarka, spo脜隆tovani visoki zbor.

脛聦ez nekaj dni bomo obele脜戮ili leto dni od tragedije v Novem Sadu, ko je pod ru脜隆evinami nadstre脜隆ka 脜戮ivljenje izgubilo 16 ljudi.

Tragedija je dokon脛聧no razgalila razkroj upravljanja dr脜戮ave, kjer odgovornost izginja pod te脜戮o politi脛聧ne propagande in la脜戮i. Zgodovinski protesti po vsej Srbiji v zadnjem letu dni so jasen znak, da se narod ne boji ve脛聧. Ti protesti niso izraz kaosa, temve脛聧 klica po pravici, svobodi in demokraciji.

Namesto iskrenega dialoga oblast odgovarja z nasiljem, s tiso脛聧imi aretacijami, s politi脛聧nimi procesi proti 脜隆tudentom, u脛聧iteljem in ostalim javnim uslu脜戮bencem. Namesto odgovornosti, teorije zarote in manipulacije. Namesto preiskav, molk in脜隆titucij. Srbska oblast je ujela institucije, pravosodje, medije v mre脜戮o strahu in korupcije.

Toda v Srbiji obstaja alternativa, proevropska opozicija in na tiso脛聧e dr脜戮avljank in dr脜戮avljanov, ki verjamejo v Evropo, v demokracijo in v svobodno prihodnost. Njihov in na脜隆 skupni poziv je jasen. 脛聦as je za pred脛聧asne in svobodne in po脜隆tene volitve. To je edini miren in demokrati脛聧en izhod iz sedanje krize.

脗聽脗聽Stephen Nikola Bartulica (ECR). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, kolegice i kolege, godinu dana nakon tragedije u Novom Sadu odr脜戮avamo jo脜隆 jednu raspravu o stanju u Srbiji, ali nema pomaka, kao da se vrtimo u krugu.

Dakle, Srbija u zadnjih godinu dana nije napravila nijedan zna脛聧ajan napredak. Vlast i dalje odbija priznati temeljne vrijednosti. 脜聽to se ti脛聧e Hrvatske i dalje odbija priznati agresiju iz 90-ih godina. Srbijansko dru脜隆tvo 脛聧ini se da se nalazi zarobljeno u starim mitovima o Velikoj Srbiji. Nisu odustali od toga, na脜戮alost. Nedostaje suradnje i transparentnosti u pronalasku nestalih osoba. A 脜隆to se ti脛聧e Hrvata u Srbiji nemaju ni pribli脜戮no prava, kao 脜隆to Srbi imaju u Hrvatskoj. Vrijeme je da se to kona脛聧no promijeni.

Dakle, Hrvatska treba suvereno se pona脜隆ati kao pobjednik iz pro脜隆lih godina i treba za脜隆tititi bolje prava Hrvata u toj zemlji. Ja 脛聡u uporno se zalagati za to.

脗聽脗聽Judita La脜隆脜隆脙隆kov脙隆 (NI). 芒聙聯 Drah脙颅 kolegovia, v脙隆脜戮en脙隆 pani predsedaj脙潞ca, v脙隆脜戮en脙隆 pani komis脙隆rka. 脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Thijs Reuten (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, colleagues, Commissioner, one year after Novi Sad, we first and foremost commemorate the victims. We are reminded that corruption is not a victimless crime. In fact, in Serbia today, people are worse off because of endemic corruption and state capture. Civil society and the opposition are criticised time and time again.

One year after, we recall violent crackdown, intimidation of peaceful protesters, inappropriate pressure on free media and independent judges, and manipulation of facts for political survival. This is not a government following a path towards脗聽the EU. Quite the opposite. And the EU should support the reasonable demands of those in Serbia demanding democracy, transparency and justice.

But finally, President von der Leyen changed her language towards the leadership. Well done! But no business as usual at the next meeting, please! I warn the Commission: this should be the start of a more realistic and honest approach, as you outlined, Commissioner. Without that, we won't see the needed reforms and we will not restore the EU's credibility. Bad news for Serbia and for Europe!

脗聽脗聽Veronika Vrecionov脙隆 (ECR). 芒聙聯 Pan脙颅 p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, rok od trag脙漏die na n脙隆dra脜戮脙颅 v Nov脙漏m Sadu nad n脙颅 st脙隆le z脜炉st脙隆v脙隆 脜聶ada otazn脙颅k脜炉. Vl脙隆d脛聸 prezidenta Vu脛聧i脛聧e se o脛聧ividn脛聸 necht脛聸lo 脜聶脙隆dn脛聸 vy脜隆et脜聶ovat trag脙漏dii, ke kter脙漏 do脜隆lo za 脙潞脛聧asti 脛聧脙颅nsk脙陆ch firem v r脙隆mci projektu Nov脙漏 hedv脙隆bn脙漏 stezky. To vyvolalo rozmach masov脙漏ho protestn脙颅ho hnut脙颅 v 脛聧ele se studenty. Kdy脜戮 srbsk脙隆 prokuratura obvi脜聢uje vysok脙漏 p脜聶edstavitele vl脙隆dy, 脙潞to脛聧脙颅 na ni prezident Vu脛聧i脛聧 a tvrd脙颅, 脜戮e prokur脙隆to脜聶i jednaj脙颅 pod vlivem jak脙陆chsi Evropan脜炉. 脜陆脙隆dn脙陆 Evropan ale nem脜炉脜戮e zav脙颅rat o脛聧i nad p脜聶脙颅pady st脙隆tem organizovan脙漏ho n脙隆sil脙颅 proti 脙潞脛聧astn脙颅k脜炉m pokojn脙陆ch demonstrac脙颅, kdy vl脙隆da plat脙颅 protidemonstrace a v ulic脙颅ch proti demonstrant脜炉m brut脙隆ln脛聸 zasahuje i vojensk脙隆 policie. Nyn脙颅 mus脙颅me zejm脙漏na pozorn脛聸 sledovat p脜聶脙颅pravu a regul脙漏rnost budouc脙颅ch srbsk脙陆ch voleb, proto脜戮e ty rozhodnou o budoucnosti t脙漏to evropsk脙漏 zem脛聸.

脗聽脗聽Karlo Ressler (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, godinu dana od novosadske tragedije srpsko dru脜隆tvo i dalje klju脛聧a, a Vu脛聧i脛聡ev re脜戮im generira sada ve脛聡 jednu grotesknu politi脛聧ku psihozu i permanentno izvanredno stanje.

Umjesto reformi, umjesto osloba脛聭anja od mitolo脜隆kih narativa pro脜隆losti, umjesto raskidanja privilegiranih veza s Rusijom i uskla脛聭ivanja s europskom ste脛聧evinom, europskom vanjskom i viznom politikom, Srbija se sve vi脜隆e odmi脛聧e i od europskih vrijednosti i od vladavine prava. Promatramo re脜戮im koji se uru脜隆ava unutra, u sjeni masovnih prosvjeda, nasilja i najdubljih podjela od doba Milo脜隆evi脛聡a. Istodobno, proces pristupanja Europi je odavno postao prazna fraza i to se vi脜隆e ni ne poku脜隆ava prikriti. Bizarno je da je podr脜隆ka srbijanskom ulasku u Europsku uniju danas ve脛聡a u Hrvatskoj nego 脜隆to je to u samoj Srbiji.

Politika europskog popu脜隆tanja do脜戮ivjela je svoj potpuni krah, a vjerodostojnost se sada mo脜戮e vratiti jedino suo脛聧avanjem s te脜隆kom realno脜隆脛聡u, podr脜隆kom pravim proeuropskim snagama, ali isto tako i 脛聧vr脜隆脛聡im stavom prema svima onima koji podrivaju europske vrijednosti.

脗聽脗聽Miriam Lexmann (PPE). 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙隆 pani komis脙隆rka, kolegovia. Trag脙漏dia v Novom Sade je jasn脙陆m pr脙颅kladom toho, ako sa nekvalitn脙漏 脛聧脙颅nske invest脙颅cie podp脙颅脜隆u spolu s kombin脙隆ciou korupcie a netransparentn脙陆mi praktikami pod ohrozovanie 脜戮ivotov eur脙鲁pskych ob脛聧anov. Je v脜隆ak aj symbolom toho, kam sa Srbsko v posledn脙陆ch rokoch uber脙隆. Namiesto pokroku smerom k Eur脙鲁pe vid脙颅me postupn脙陆 pr脙颅klon k Moskve a Pekingu. Tento pr脙颅klon je mi a脜戮 n脙隆padne zn脙隆my. Vid脙颅me narastaj脙潞cu agresiu vo脛聧i vlastn脙陆m ob脛聧anom, ktor脙颅 proti pomerom v krajine vy脜隆li do ul脙颅c. Vid脙颅me narastaj脙潞cu agresiu aj vo脛聧i in脙陆m skupin脙隆m, ako napr脙颅klad vo脛聧i slovenskej men脜隆ine. Tak ako asi ka脜戮d脙陆, aj Srbi chc脙潞 脜戮i脜楼 v slu脜隆nej a prosperuj脙潞cej eur脙鲁pskej krajine, v ktorej vl脙隆dne pr脙隆vny 脜隆t脙隆t, a nie korupcia. Ver脙颅m, 脜戮e bud脙潞cnos脜楼 demokratick脙漏ho Srbska je v demokratickej Eur脙鲁pe.

脗聽脗聽Sun脛聧ana Glavak (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, po脜隆tovana povjerenice, kolegice i kolege, jasno je, u Srbiji ne vidimo demokratski, ve脛聡 despotski narativ, koketan prema potrebi prema Europskoj uniji, a s druge strane vrlo koketan prema Rusiji i Kini.

脜陆elim jasno poru脛聧iti gra脛聭anima Srbije: mi ovdje ne mijenjamo vlast, mi ovdje tra脜戮imo demokratske promjene i tra脜戮imo da se ispune reforme za svaku zemlju koja 脜戮eli postati 脛聧lanice Europske unije. Posljednjih godinu dana u Srbiji svjedo脛聧imo zastra脜隆ivanju prosvjednika, privo脛聭enju novinara, pritiscima na civilno dru脜隆tvo i poku脜隆ajima da se svaka kritika prika脜戮e kao prijetnja dr脜戮avi. To nije put prema Europi, to je put unatrag prema strahu i 脜隆utnji.

Vladavina prava, sloboda izra脜戮avanja i neovisno pravosu脛聭e nisu roba kojom se trguje. Oni su preduvjet 脛聧lanstva.脗聽Srbija ne mo脜戮e istodobno tra脜戮iti otvaranje poglavlja i zatvarati usta vlastitim gra脛聭anima.

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, se kertoo paljon hallinnosta, miten se k脙陇sittelee mielenosoituksia. Serbian esimerkki kertoo t脙陇st脙陇 ik脙陇v脙陇脙陇 kielt脙陇脙陇n. Serbiassa rautatieaseman rakenteiden sortuminen vei 16 ihmisen hengen ja oli viimeinen laukaiseva tekij脙陇 j脙陇ttim脙陇isille mielenosoituksille maan hallintoa vastaan. T脙陇m脙陇n vuoden aikana maassa on n脙陇hty jo yli kymmenentuhatta mielenosoitusta. Hallinto on vastannut kovalla v脙陇kivallalla. Hakkaamista, kyynelkaasua, 脙陇脙陇niaseen k脙陇ytt脙露脙陇, kidutusta, jopa seksuaalista v脙陇kivaltaa. Tukea toimilleen hallinto on hakenut Ven脙陇j脙陇lt脙陇.

Samaan aikaan Serbia on EU-ehdokasmaa. Jos mielenosoitusten k脙陇sittelytapa kertoo paljon hallinnosta, t脙陇ss脙陇 tapauksessa se kertoo my脙露s sen, ett脙陇 Serbia on kaukana EU-kelpoisuudesta. EU-varojen n脙陇k脙露kulmasta t脙陇m脙陇n tulisi tarkoittaa sit脙陇, ett脙陇 t脙陇llaiselta hallinnolta vedet脙陇脙陇n EU-rahahanat kiinni.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sandro Gozi (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Serbia has chosen a European path. It was a free European choice 芒聙聯 a choice we strongly support and a choice which ensures a clear benefit, but demands clear commitments. Commitment to democracy, rule of law and freedom first of all.脗聽The freedom of hundreds of students to protest and to express criticism of President Vu脛聧i脛聡 one year after a terrible tragedy in Novi Sad. A protest against corruption, a protest for free information, a protest against propaganda, fake news and manipulation by the Serbian authorities. A protest against Russian interference. A call for democratic accountability, which does not seem to exist in the vocabulary of President Aleksandar Vu脛聧i脛聡.

Thank you, Commissioner脗聽Kos. Thank you, because it was time that we hear the right word from the European Commission. We stand by this commitment. We stand by the European students. And we stand by the European future of the Serbian people.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Ende der spontanen Wortmeldungen)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Kos, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, the Commission has taken good note of your views and concerns as regards the developments in Serbia and their impact on Serbia's EU path.

It is precisely Serbia's EU path that offers solutions to many of the issues that are at the heart of the discussion in Serbia today, notably in the field of the rule of law. This is why it remains the European Commission's priority, and my priority, to to support the Serbian people and their EU aspirations.

The people in Serbia deserve a European future, and the perspective of EU membership offers the transformation of societies in a comprehensive and sustainable way, based on democratic values and principles.

We will continue with our strong engagement with the competent Serbian authorities, parties across the political spectrum and civil society to ensure that it remains on its EU path and overcomes the current political impasse. Trust among all stakeholders has to be restored, but also trust in the EU. We have seen the first steps in this direction in the past days, and we will support Serbia to continue on this path.

Today's debate has once again shown the importance of adherence to EU values in our candidate countries. This is, and will, remain at the heart of the EU accession process. The Commission will continue engaging with Serbia, and we count on the European Parliament 芒聙聯 on you 芒聙聯 to continue supporting the Commission in bringing a democratic Serbia into our European family. We are living in the times where we have to make strategic choices. Serbia needs to make its strategic choice, clearly, too.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Thank you very much, Commissioner, for the clear words.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Zum Abschluss der Aussprache wurden gem脙陇脙聼 Artikel 136 Absatz 2 der Gesch脙陇ftsordnung acht Entschlie脙聼ungsantr脙陇ge eingereicht.

Die Aussprache ist geschlossen.

Die Abstimmung findet morgen, Mittwoch, 22.脗聽Oktober 2025, statt.

脗聽

18. General budget of the European Union for the financial year 2026 芒聙聯 all sections (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Als n脙陇chster Punkt der Tagesordnung folgt die Aussprache 脙录ber den Bericht von Andrzej Halicki und Matja脜戮 Nemec im Namen des Haushaltsausschusses 脙录ber den Standpunkt des Rates zum Entwurf des Gesamthaushaltsplans der Europ脙陇ischen Union f脙录r das Haushaltsjahr 2026 芒聙聯 alle Einzelpl脙陇ne (11216/2025 - C10芒聙聭0206/2025 - 2025/0210(BUD)) (A10-0192/2025).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andrzej Halicki, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear Minister, dear Commissioner, dear colleagues, welcome to this debate on the position of Parliament on the budget of the European Union for 2026.

The EU budget is an investment budget designed to provide the necessary resources to all European citizens, who can benefit from common action. Experts agree in saying that the EU budget is a crucial tool for a robust and stronger Europe, and this is also recognised by our citizens, who rightfully expect more and more from the EU budget. The latest Eurobarometer 2025 survey highlights Europeans' expectations for security, unity and more resources to address global challenges. In the face of geopolitical uncertainty, citizens across the Union are calling for stronger protection and greater solidarity.

This is exactly what Parliament's position is about. Back in April, Parliament adopted a strong set of guidelines for the financial year ahead of us, underlining the importance of strategic preparedness and security, as well as economic competitiveness and resilience. We made it clear that we expect the 2026 budget to provide people in the European Union with a robust ecosystem to deliver on their priorities in a prosperous Europe.

Since then, the Commission submitted its draft budget. Unfortunately, it does not come as a great surprise to the European Parliament that the Council cuts the Commission's proposal. The Council proposed to make unjustified cuts across a series of programmes 芒聙聯 I would like to say flagship programmes 芒聙聯 without proper justification. We oppose all these cuts. If it is expected from the European Union to enhance its action, it is not acceptable to cut the funding of the Union where it is needed.

Overall, we support the draft budget of the European Commission and, at Parliament, we have looked in a very responsible way at areas where funding has proven insufficient and more is needed. We are deeply aware of the critical situation of the Union budget, which is a victim of its success and provides for too limited availabilities compared to all the challenges ahead.

This is why our position is a reasonable one, which only proposes essential and targeted increases. I would like to underline a few priorities.

We need to support our businesses, especially small and medium enterprises and our researchers who work for a better future in Europe. Therefore, we propose to reinforce Horizon Europe and the Connected Europe Facility 芒聙聯 some of our most successful programmes.

We need to ensure the protection of our citizens, so we also confirm Parliament's strong support to further strengthen the Union Civil Protection Mechanism. Agriculture is another key pillar of our Union. Our farmers 芒聙聯 and particularly young farmers 芒聙聯 need continued support for the promotion of their products and to maintain profitability. This is also a widely shared priority in this House.

Security is another core concern of EU citizens, along with the need to strengthen our defence capabilities. We propose to reinforce key programmes in these areas, namely on asylum, migration, border management and military mobility.

As we are living in a global world, we are directly affected by what is happening outside our borders, including the continued illegal war of aggression by Russia in Ukraine. For this reason, we put forward essential increases of funds allocated to our southern and eastern neighbourhood, as well as humanitarian aid.

Dear colleagues, we have a strong support from several groups. I would like to emphasise our very good cooperation with the shadow rapporteurs. We achieved this majority on the committee level. Now I expect that it will be adopted by the whole Parliament and I am looking forward to the cooperation during our negotiations also with the European Commission and with the Council.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Matja脜戮 Nemec, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear Minister Wammen, dear Commissioner Serafin, dear colleagues, today we will debate on our priorities for next year芒聙聶s budget of the Union.

This is an important moment for the Parliament where we set our policy priorities, and how to finance them.脗聽Our common approach reflects our common values. In addition, it sets the course for the future of Europe as we see it.脗聽We are of course part of the globalised world, with many ongoing conflicts, including on our borders.脗聽We are also exposed to geopolitical events. We also have to respond to the urgent challenges, including cyber security.

In the last years, the EU adopted several ground-breaking legislative initiatives, such as the Digital Services Act, the Digital Market Act, or the Artificial intelligence Act, just to name some of them.

Consequently, we tasked the EU institutions to implement these initiatives and monitor their application.脗聽But to be able to do that, the EU institutions must be able to do their job. And for them to be able to do so, we must give them sufficient resources.脗聽If we can talk the talk, but do not walk the walk, we put all our institutions in a difficult situation. And the Union at risk.

Dear colleagues, our key priorities are reflected in our budget proposal.

And considering the difficult overall financial situation of the EU budget, we are being very responsible in planning it.脗聽And I believe that our proposal demonstrates just that. Our position is very focused, very reasonable and targeted to real needs.

However, certain difference between our institutions remain.脗聽But we have a common task to deliver for our citizens.脗聽It is them we serve. Not ourselves.

As the rapporteur for other sections, my message is clear.脗聽We must enable our institutions to have enough staff to deal with their increased tasks and responsibilities, and mandatory expenses.脗聽But also they need to be able to protect themselves from the new realities, such as cybersecurity issues.脗聽More efficient and safe EU institutions are in our common interest.

So, dear Minister脗聽Wammen, dear Commissioner Serafin,脗聽I am sure we will be able to find a common ground that will be based on the real needs for each of our institutions.脗聽They are the backbone of the EU, working for our citizens, but also the Member States.脗聽And that the result in the end will be a good one.

I look forward to today芒聙聶s debate and I thank you for your attention. Thank you also to my colleagues.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nicolai Wammen, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, honourable Members, rapporteurs, I am honoured to attend the European Parliament's debate on its position on the draft budget for 2026. It is clear that the European Parliament and the Council share many key priorities for next year's budget. However, unsurprisingly, there are also notable differences in expenditure levels. The Council's position aims to safeguard key EU priorities by ensuring an appropriate level of funding. This includes enhancing Europe's defence readiness and preparedness, as well as managing migration pressures.

We live in difficult times, and, therefore, we must prioritise and ensure that the budget for 2026 allows the Union to act effectively, without unnecessarily increasing the pressure on national budgets and tax payers. We need margins and flexibility for the EU budget to cope with war, crisis and challenges, notably Ukraine and the Middle East, but also the unforeseen ones. If almost all margins and special instruments are consumed, how can we then guarantee the flexibility to cope with unforeseen challenges?

Dear colleagues, the Council would not be able to approve all the European Parliament's amendments, should you vote them through tomorrow. In addition, the Council remains fully committed to the agreement on the fifty-fifty split for EURI interest costs that was reached last year. With this in mind, I find it difficult to understand the idea from Parliament of excluding liquidity costs from the fifty-fifty benchmark. These costs have been a part of the interest cost since the very beginning.

To conclude, we share many priorities, but we also have serious work to do in the coming weeks. Dear colleagues, tonight I reaffirm that the Council's willingness to reach a balanced agreement, and I am sure that the Commission will assist us in finding sound and reasonable compromises. Let me, therefore, conclude by expressing my sincere hope that by working together, we will be able to find the best answers for Europe. And the good news is, we have done it together before, and I am convinced we can do it together again.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Piotr Serafin, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, dear rapporteurs, dear Minister, first, I would like to congratulate both rapporteurs, Mr Halicki and Mr Nemec, for their work. You have reached a compromise on all amendments and prepared a strong position of the European Parliament.

It is not surprising that at this point of the budgetary process, the position of the European Parliament and the position of the Council diverge from each other substantially. In a week, we will start the conciliation and hopefully these divergences will start to shrink.

But today, let me thank you for the amendments proposed by the Parliament that reinstate the expenditure at the level proposed in the draft budget by the European Commission. I have been clear and consistent that the Commission does not consider the Council's cost cuts as adequately justified.

Of course, I also note that the Parliament proposes reinforcements in a number of programmes across all headings that go on top of the draft budget. We will need to treat such reinforcements very cautiously. The remaining financial resources are limited and we will have to prioritise. We must focus on programs where meaningful adjustments can be made and concessions on both sides will be necessary.

We should remember that reinforcements in this year's budget will compromise our capacity to respond to foreseeable and, more importantly, unforeseeable needs in the budget for 2027. We have to preserve some budgetary space to avoid a risk of cuts in the spending programs in the last year of the current Multiannual Financial Framework. I urge you to consider this issue with utmost prudence.

I would like to ask the Parliament and the Council to consider the amending letter of the European Commission as a starting point in the negotiating process. That letter addresses some concerns of both institutions, in particular the payment appropriations, agriculture and the EU Juri interest line. I'm confident that the negotiations will continue in a positive spirit, and that the Parliament and the Council will manage to reconcile their positions.脗聽As actually was mentioned also by Minister Wammen, that experience is already there.

Let me assure you again that I and my team will spare no effort to support your work throughout the conciliation to reach a good compromise.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael Gahler, Verfasser der Stellungnahme des AFET-Ausschusses. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Kolleginnen und Kollegen, Herr Kommissar, Ratspr脙陇sidentschaft! Aus Sicht des Ausschusses f脙录r ausw脙陇rtige Angelegenheiten m脙录ssen wir darauf hinweisen, dass die internationale Lage zunehmend komplexer wird und gepr脙陇gt ist durch geopolitische Spannungen, strategischen Wettbewerb und globale Herausforderungen. Die EU ist in der unmittelbaren europ脙陇ischen Nachbarschaft immer st脙陇rker gefordert.

Die K脙录rzungen der globalen Au脙聼enhilfe der Vereinigten Staaten und anderer L脙陇nder bieten jedoch eine Gelegenheit, als EU global eine st脙陇rkere Rolle zu spielen. Insbesondere verbessern m脙录ssen wir die Finanzierung der Politik f脙录r die 脙露stliche und s脙录dliche Nachbarschaft angesichts der N脙陇he und geostrategischen Bedeutung dieser Regionen f脙录r die Sicherheit der EU selbst. Unsere Bem脙录hungen um die Unterst脙录tzung der Republik Moldau sollten eine spezifische Haushaltslinie erhalten. Sie sollten die Transparenz des Haushalts erh脙露hen und die parlamentarische Kontrolle verbessern. Insgesamt ben脙露tigen wir 110 Millionen Euro mehr f脙录r die Rubrik脗聽6 des Haushalts 芒聙聯 Nachbarschaft und die Welt 芒聙聯, als der Rat f脙录r diese Herausforderungen angesetzt hat.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie-Agnes Strack-Zimmermann, Verfasserin der Stellungnahme des SEDE-Ausschusses. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, meine Damen und Herren! Ja, es wird eine riesengro脙聼e Herausforderung, denn wir leben in instabilen geografischen Verh脙陇ltnissen 芒聙聯 vergessen wir es nicht 芒聙聯 aufgrund des brutalen Angriffs Russlands auf die Ukraine. Der ganze Kontinent ist ber脙录hrt, und wir brauchen ein flexibles Budget. Wir m脙录ssen immer reagieren auf diese reale Welt. Das hei脙聼t: Investitionen in Verteidigung, Resilienz, in Fragen gemeinsamer Beschaffung, Interoperabilit脙陇t, milit脙陇rische Mobilit脙陇t 芒聙聯 und ja, das kostet viel Geld.

Aber auch mal eine gute Nachricht in diesem Haus: Ich freue mich, dass wir das European Defence Industry Programme, kurz EDIP genannt, auf den Weg gebracht haben. Es ist der erste Schritt in mehr europ脙陇ische Unabh脙陇ngigkeit. Meine Damen und Herren, ich danke allen, die dabei geholfen haben, den Kolleginnen und Kollegen, Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeitern, der Administration und auch Rat und Kommission 芒聙聯 ein herzliches Dankesch脙露n. Ich habe die letzten 14脗聽Sekunden, um Folgendes zu sagen: W脙陇hrend der US芒聙聭Pr脙陇sident den Ostfl脙录gel des Wei脙聼en Hauses einrei脙聼t, um einen Ballsaal f脙录r sein Vergn脙录gen aufzubauen, werden wir das EU芒聙聭Haus ert脙录chtigen, um die Sicherheit f脙录r unsere B脙录rger zu gew脙陇hrleisten.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Charles Goerens, rapporteur pour avis de la commission DEVE. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, la commission du d脙漏veloppement propose des amendements budg脙漏taires d脙漏passant les montants fix脙漏s par le Conseil. Parall脙篓lement, la commission des budgets sugg脙篓re d'augmenter les cr脙漏dits allou脙漏s 脙聽 l'aide humanitaire, qui manque cruellement de moyens.

En effet, la multiplication des crises constitue un vrai casse-t脙陋te pour le d脙漏veloppement et l'action humanitaire. Le retrait de USAID a cr脙漏脙漏 un vide de 60脗聽milliards de dollars. L'Europe est incapable de compenser ce montant gigantesque, selon le principe des vases communicants. Nous pouvons tout au plus red脙漏finir nos priorit脙漏s en tant que premier acteur humanitaire et de d脙漏veloppement, ensemble avec les quelques rares pays qui restent toujours attach脙漏s 脙聽 la dimension compassionnelle de la politique.

L'internationale des radins remporte des succ脙篓s qui, h脙漏las, donnent une fin de non-recevoir aux enfants, aux femmes, aux r脙漏fugi脙漏s qui, sans notre solidarit脙漏, sont vou脙漏s 脙聽 la disparition. Que chacun prenne ses responsabilit脙漏s.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tom脙隆脜隆 Zdechovsk脙陆, zpravodaj v脙陆boru CONT. 芒聙聯 Pan脙颅 p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, v脙隆脜戮en脙颅 a mil脙颅 kolegov脙漏, v脙隆脜戮en脙陆 pane komisa脜聶i, 脜聶ekl bych, 脜戮e jste m脛聸l brilantn脙颅 脜聶e脛聧, kdybyste nezapomn脛聸l na aspekt V脙陆boru pro rozpo脛聧tovou kontrolu, kter脙陆 se vlastn脛聸 tady sna脜戮脙颅, aby ka脜戮d脙漏 euro, kter脙漏 dost脙隆vaj脙颅 ob脛聧an脙漏 Evropsk脙漏 unie, 脜隆lo pr脙隆v脛聸 nikoliv oligarch脜炉m, ale 脜隆lo na v脛聸ci, kter脙漏 jsou spojeny s Evropskou uni脙颅. My jsme si ve V脙陆boru pro rozpo脛聧tovou kontrolu 脜聶ekli, 脜戮e mus脙颅 b脙陆t takov脙漏 t脜聶i mechanismy, kter脙漏 mus脙颅 fungovat. Za prv脙漏, mechanismus podm脙颅n脛聸nosti pr脙隆vn脙颅m st脙隆tem. Ten mus脙颅me vyu脜戮脙颅vat bez v脙隆h脙隆n脙颅. Nefunguje pr脙隆vn脙颅 st脙隆t, zastavujeme pen脙颅ze. Te脛聧ka. Mus脙颅me b脙陆t chyt脜聶ej脜隆脙颅 i proti podvod脜炉m. To znamen脙隆, n脙隆stroje jako EDES a ARACHNE mus脙颅 b脙陆t povinn脙漏. Nem脙隆脜隆 tyto n脙隆stroje, nedost脙隆v脙隆脜隆 pen脙颅ze z Evropsk脙漏 unie. A mus脙颅me 脜聶脙颅ci, 脜戮e evropsk脙隆 prokuratura mus脙颅 b脙陆t v prvn脙颅 linii tohoto boje a tady bez jak脙陆chkoliv kompromis脜炉.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Damian Boeselager, rapporteur for the opinion of the ECON Committee. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, Minister, colleagues, I'm very happy that the Chair of the Defence Committee actually reminded us of the fact that Trump is currently using public funds to extend the White House with a ballroom. I think it shows us that we have to maybe use this Defence Committee that we have to also defend ourselves against extravagance.

I've been asked by the ECON Committee to step in for a colleague to talk about ECON's priorities. It is very clear that we believe, also in our current situation, that fiscal stability is of primary importance, and therefore it is good that we have a large European budget and we should also consider this for the future, and that, of course, competitiveness is of a core importance.

If you actually look at how Canada is reacting to the US trade tariffs at the moment, you can see that they're really trying to strengthen the internal market, and I think that's something we can also get much better at 芒聙聯 deepening the internal market in general, and especially with the 28th regime.

As a last point, looking forward to the next MFF, I think it's important that we maintain the importance of the annual budgetary procedure and that we ensure the rights of this House as a budgetary authority.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Romana Tomc, pripravljavka mnenja za odbor EMPL. 芒聙聯 Gospa predsednica! Evropska unija se danes soo脛聧a z izzivi, ki niso zgolj ekonomski ali okoljski, ampak tudi socialni. V Odboru za zaposlovanje in socialne zadeve zato pozivamo k nadaljnjim jasnim in odlo脛聧nim prora脛聧unskim podporam socialnim politikam, takim, ki bodo kos realnim potrebam ljudi.

Rev脜隆脛聧ina otrok, brezposelnost mladih, stanovanjska kriza, du脜隆evno zdravje zaposlenih, starajo脛聧e se prebivalstvo, neenakost med regijami, rast 脜戮ivljenjskih stro脜隆kov 芒聙聯 vse to niso izjemne razmere, temve脛聧 realnost milijonov Evropejk in Evropejcev. In na vse te izzive je potrebno odgovoriti z zadostnimi finan脛聧nimi ukrepi.

Evropa ne more napredovati, 脛聧e zanemari svoje ljudi. Prora脛聧un mora biti zato ogledalo na脜隆ih vrednot. Evropa mora ostati ozemlje dr脜戮av, ki upravljajo z najbolj脜隆imi socialnimi modeli, in prora脛聧un 2026 mora biti orodje, ki pomaga to ohranjati.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Thomas Bajada, rapporteur for the opinion of the ENVI Committee. 芒聙聯 Madam President, let's stop beating around the bush: Europe is facing an environmental breaking point. Our rivers are dying, our soils depleted, our coasts eroding. It's not only me saying this; the European Environment Agency has clearly stated it in its recent state of the environment report, and yet we still draft budgets as if the health of our planet, and our livelihoods, were optional.

Weakening climate and nature funding now would not only be backtracking, but also a betrayal for future generations, a betrayal of Europe's competitiveness and growth. From ENVI's side, we are very clear: this budget must deliver for climate, biodiversity and water resilience. No excuses for missing the 10脗聽% biodiversity target, no backsliding on the 30脗聽% climate commitment and no weakening of the LIFE programme. The economy depends on a stable climate, our competitiveness depends on innovation, and both of them depend on a healthy planet.

Climate ambition is not a burden for Europe. It is our greatest advantage, and this budget must finally take that into account and prove it.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Eero Hein脙陇luoma, rapporteur for the opinion of the ITRE Committee. 芒聙聯 Madam President, a more competitive Europe means investing in the digital and climate transitions and fostering more industrial production in Europe. It means also investing for quality jobs and strategic autonomy.

A competitive EU industry also needs a strong focus on research and innovation to develop new technologies and to bring new solutions to the market. More resources for Horizon Europe and CEF are really needed.

It is more important than ever to increase investments into renewable and clean energy, ambitious electrification, grids and cross-border infrastructure.

The 2026 budget must respond also to the economic impact of the ongoing geopolitical instability. Especially the EU's Eastern border regions, which have been particularly affected by the suspension of cross-border trade as a consequence of Russia's brutal war of aggression against Ukraine.

脗聽脗聽Maria Grapini, raportoare pentru aviz a Comisiei IMCO. 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, domnule ministru, domnule comisar, stima脠聸i colegi, bugetul pentru pia脠聸a intern脛聝, eu reprezint punctul de vedere al Comisiei IMCO, este foarte important. Nu putem s脛聝 avem o pia脠聸脛聝 intern脛聝 solid脛聝, competitiv脛聝, f脛聝r脛聝 bugete, f脛聝r脛聝 investi脠聸ii. Liniile ro脠聶ii au fost deja anun脠聸ate aici.

Avem nevoie pentru fermieri, ast脛聝zi i-am v脛聝zut 脙庐n strad脛聝, nu putem s脛聝 nu sus脠聸inem agricultura, securitatea alimentar脛聝 p脙垄n脛聝 la urm脛聝.

脠聵i da, sigur, trebuie s脛聝 sus脠聸inem 脠聶i inova脠聸ia, 脠聶i tinerii, 脠聶i locuin脠聸ele, 脠聶i via脠聸a mai bun脛聝 a oamenilor. 脛聜sta este rolul nostru aici 脠聶i pentru asta am fost trimi脠聶i. Deci Comisia are acum un rol extraordinar 脠聶i ave脠聸i surse, putem s脛聝 g脛聝sim surse, domnule comisar, iat脛聝: a脠聸i amendat Google-ul.

Dac脛聝 supraveghem pia脠聸a mai bine, dac脛聝 c脛聝ut脛聝m criminalitatea economic脛聝, dac脛聝 vedem evaziunea pe TVA, de exemplu, 脠聶i avem Parchetul, avem OLAF, institu脠聸ii care trebuie s脛聝 aduc脛聝 ace脠聶ti bani la buget, pentru c脛聝 da, noi trebuie s脛聝 dezvolt脛聝m 脙庐n rela脠聸ia cu Statele Unite, cu celelalte pie脠聸e, pia脠聸a noastr脛聝 intern脛聝.

脗聽脗聽Gheorghe Falc脛聝, raportor pentru aviz al Comisiei TRAN. 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, dragi colegi, ca raportor din partea Comisiei pentru transport 脠聶i turism pentru bugetul 2026, sunt interesat de capacitatea de transport 脠聶i cre脠聶terea capacit脛聝脠聸ii logistice pentru func脠聸ionarea mai bun脛聝 a pie脠聸ei interne, dar 脠聶i pentru sprijinul regiunilor s脛聝race 脠聶i regiunilor periferice.

脙聨n acela脠聶i timp, sunt interesat de securitatea european脛聝 脠聶i apreciez faptul c脛聝 Comisia European脛聝 a crescut 脙庐n bugetul multianual 2028-2034 de 10 ori valoarea mobilit脛聝脠聸ii militare, de aceea trebuie s脛聝 creasc脛聝 脠聶i din 2026, pentru a fi o cre脠聶tere constant脛聝. 脙聨n acela脠聶i timp, doresc ca infrastructura TEN-T s脛聝 aib脛聝 un dublu rol, acela de utilitate civil脛聝 脠聶i utilitate militar脛聝 pentru toate segmentele din investi脠聸ii.

脗聽脗聽Dario Nardella, relatore per parere della commissione AGRI. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, signor Commissario, onorevoli colleghi, i programmi in ambito agricolo sono una voce centrale del bilancio europeo. Per il 2026, come socialisti e democratici, abbiamo puntato sul ricambio generazionale, che rappresenta una questione di sopravvivenza per l'agricoltura europea.

Oggi in troppe regioni l'et脙聽 media degli agricoltori supera 57 anni e ogni giovane che vorrebbe prendere il loro posto si scontra con ostacoli enormi. Abbiamo chiesto un aumento di 23 milioni di euro per il sostegno complementare al reddito dei giovani agricoltori rispetto alla proposta iniziale. Un primo passo, ma non basta: dobbiamo dare fiducia, reddito e prospettive a una nuova generazione di agricoltori, senza i quali non ci sar脙聽 sicurezza alimentare, transizione ecologica, n脙漏 futuro per le nostre aree rurali.

Inoltre, 脙篓 inaccettabile che i programmi di promozione agroalimentare gestiti dall'Unione siano stati azzerati. Questi fondi hanno sostenuto l'export europeo, la reputazione delle nostre produzioni e i consumatori. Bene, quindi, il loro ripristino: rafforzare la promozione europea dei nostri cibi significa difendere lavoro, territori e valore dell'Europa.

脗聽脗聽Giuseppe Lupo, relatore per parere della commissione PECH. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, signor Commissario, signor Ministro, onorevoli colleghi, la commissione per la pesca ritiene che i finanziamenti per la pesca e l'acquacoltura siano fondamentali per il benessere delle comunit脙聽 costiere e insulari, garanzia di una politica comune della pesca capace di conciliare competitivit脙聽, sostenibilit脙聽 ambientale e sociale.

Per questo la commissione per la pesca chiede di respingere le riduzioni di spesa chieste dal Consiglio per la pesca per il 2026 e di aumentare gli stanziamenti nel prossimo bilancio pluriennale 2028-2034. Perch脙漏 la sicurezza a bordo, il ricambio generazionale, l'equilibrio di genere e la protezione della biodiversit脙聽 marina non possono pi脙鹿 aspettare.

Difendiamo il lavoro dei pescatori e la sicurezza alimentare, affinch脙漏 il pesce sia disponibile in tutte le mense delle scuole d'Europa e non solo sulle tavole dei ricchi.

脗聽脗聽Ilhan Kyuchyuk, rapporteur for the opinion of the JURI Committee. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear Commissioner, dear Minister, thank you very much, Mr脗聽Halicki and Mr脗聽Nemec, for bringing together all the diverse positions of the European Parliament. Allow me to present my committee's priority.

The 2026 statement of estimates of the Court of Justice of the European Union requests the creation of 10 new permanent posts to meet Cybersecurity Regulation obligations. The Commission unfortunately rejected this demand, and ten posts have not been included in the 2026 draft budget, with a EUR脗聽570脗聽000 cut. This jeopardises the Court's ability to fulfil its cybersecurity obligations and to defend itself against cyberattacks.

In light of current institutional trends, we must safeguard the cybersecurity of our judicial branch. I shall therefore remain at the general rapporteurs' disposal during the forthcoming negotiations to contribute to the attainment of this essential objective.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Evin Incir, rapporteur for the opinion of the LIBE Committee. 芒聙聯 Madam President, we face serious challenges to our security and to our fundamental rights. Organised crime is a cancer that poisons our societies and robs our children of a safe and hopeful childhood. We must ensure that the European Union is fully equipped to both prevent and fight these threats. That is why the LIBE Committee has proposed to strengthen Europol, Eurojust and the European Public Prosecutor's Office (EPPO) with larger budget and more staff. Without the right resources, these institutions cannot deliver on the promises we make to our citizens.

The EPPO plays a vital role in protecting the EU's financial interests, investigating and prosecuting crimes, like VAT fraud and corruption, that steal from us all. But today it remains chronically underfunded. That means crimes go unpunished and stolen funds remain unrecovered 芒聙聯 this cannot continue. As we step up our efforts, we must never lose sight of our fundamental rights. Security and justice must always go hand in hand.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lina G脙隆lvez, rapporteur for the opinion of the FEMM Committee. 芒聙聯 Madam President, gender mainstreaming and gender budgeting are not a luxury, but an essential tool in the Union's arsenal to show our political priorities. A way for us to make our money go further. We have made progress in gender budgeting over the last few years, but we still need to effectively monitor the application to ensure the budget works for all.

I am pleased that the importance of items such as the European Social Fund Plus or the Daphne Programme are recognised and have their budget increased to some extent at least.

Let me remind you that gender-based violence has an estimated economic cost of billions per year. This is why the FEMM Committee has repeatedly called for Daphne to become an autonomous programme with its own separate budget line.

Finally, I would like to highlight the importance and valuable role the European Institute for Gender Equality plays in helping the Union achieve its objectives. Yes, EIGE has had its budget increase, but it still needs more resources and staff to deal with its increased tasks, especially now that there is orchestrated attacks against the gender-equality agenda.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bogdan Rzo脜聞ca, sprawozdawca komisji opiniodawczej 芒聙聯 komisji PETI. 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Ten bud脜录et zawiera wiele postulat脙鲁w ideologicznych. To si脛聶 nam nie podoba. Propozycja bud脜录etu na 2026 rok omija jednak脗聽wiele zagro脜录e脜聞. Ta propozycja zawiera tre脜聸ci, kt脙鲁re os脜聜abiaj脛聟 konkurencyjno脜聸脛聡 Unii Europejskiej, tkwi脛聟c w b脜聜脛聶dzie klimatycznych cel脙鲁w, kt脙鲁re niszcz脛聟 gospodark脛聶, os脜聜abiaj脛聟 konkurencyjno脜聸脛聡, utrudniaj脛聟 inwestycje.

Fa脜聜szywa promocja zdrowia seksualnego i reprodukcyjnego jest sprzeczna z natur脛聟 ludzk脛聟. To jest w tym bud脜录ecie, a po脜聸rednio taka propaganda aborcji stoi w sprzeczno脜聸ci z demograficzn脛聟 脜聸mierci脛聟 Europy. Mamy z tym problem. Musimy dok脜聜adnie ten problem opisa脛聡.

Unia przy takim bud脜录ecie staje si脛聶, moim zdaniem, takim wehiku脜聜em. Stoimy w miejscu, 脜聸wiat nam ucieka i to si脛聶 nam bardzo nie podoba, gdy脜录 chcieliby脜聸my by脛聡 i powinni脜聸my by脛聡 bardziej konkurencyjni, rywalizowa脛聡 naprawd脛聶 w technologiach, a nie sta脛聡 w miejscu i czasem si脛聶 cofa脛聡, co powoduje, 脜录e nie podobaj脛聟 nam si脛聶 芒聙聯 co do stosunku do tego 脜聸wiata, w kt脙鲁rym si脛聶 obracamy w tej chwili 芒聙聯 te za脜聜o脜录enia, kt脙鲁re s脛聟 w tym bud脜录ecie.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Siegfried Mure脜聼an, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, Minister, welcome to the Parliament. Firstly, I would like to congratulate the two rapporteurs, Andrzej Halicki and Matja脜戮 Nemec, for the report which they put forward. You are making the most with the limited resources that we have.

This is a budget in which young farmers, young students through the Erasmus programme, the Connecting Europe Facility, Frontex, Eurojust, Ukraine, our neighbourhood, military mobility receive solid support. This is a position that deserves to be supported. On behalf of the EPP Group, we are going to support the work done by our two rapporteurs and we are confident that they will receive a clear majority tomorrow, where we will see pro-European groups united.

They make the most with very limited resources and we are now going to, together, decide a budget for a year, which is close to the end of this Multiannual Financial Framework, and we can already see the consequences of some of the mistakes which we made when we designed this Multiannual Financial Framework.

We should not repeat these mistakes again for the next Multiannual Financial Framework. And the mistakes were: firstly, this budget was insufficient, there were not enough margins, not enough reserves, it could not react in situations of crisis. We see also the margins very limited for 2026, so we need to draw the lessons for the future there.脗聽Secondly, we did together NextGenerationEU. We see reimbursing NextGenerationEU costs money. The correct lesson to be drawn is we need to work together on the introduction of own resources.

And, Minister, if we all want Europe to do more, particularly on competitiveness, on security, on defence, that cannot be done at the expense of the traditional policies. We as a Parliament will defend, also for the next MFF, an ambitious but future-oriented common agricultural policy and cohesion policy. What I want to say is we cannot do more with less. This is something that we have to take into account also for the next MFF.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nils U脜隆akovs, on behalf of the S&D Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, Minister, colleagues, the 2026 EU budget marks key changes. It finally recognises the eastern flank for what it is: a strategic necessity. This is not just a win for eastern border Member States like my home country, Latvia; it is the only viable path for the stability and development of the entire Union. Let us be clear: a secure and prosperous eastern flank is a secure and prosperous Europe.

This budget starts translating political solidarity into concrete financial decisions. It acknowledges the disproportionate burden carried by the eastern border Member States and reinforces our collective resilience. And I would like to thank the rapporteur, Andrzej Halicki, and my colleagues in the Budgets Committee, in particular those who visited the Latgale border region on a special mission this April.

The resolution signals a paradigm shift. This House is finally accepting that EU funds can, and must, be spent on border and security, and we see enhanced support for the Asylum, Migration and Integration Fund. This, in its turn, strengthens the migration pact, providing essential tools for the return procedures of irregular migrants. This is vital for regions like Latgale which are confronting weaponised migration head on.

We see critical increase in investment in military mobility, which, among other things, may help my home country to complete the Rail Baltica project. This is a strong start, but as we look toward the 2027 budget and the new MFF, we must go further. Border infrastructure is vital, yet we must equally support the ordinary Europeans, municipalities and businesses in the border regions, who are absorbing the social and economic fallout of the war.

Finally, we cannot preach collective resilience while maintaining a hypocritical discrimination against farmers in central and eastern Europe. The next MFF must guarantee equal support for all farmers across the Union.

Dear colleagues, dear rapporteurs, Andrzej Halicki and Matja脜戮 Nemec, thank you for your work and thank you for the budget, and let us have it voted tomorrow at the plenary.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

PR脙聣SIDENCE: YOUNOUS OMARJEE
Vice-Pr脙漏sident

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Julien Sanchez, au nom du groupe PfE. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, s'il y a bien un domaine o脙鹿 l'Union europ脙漏enne est constante, je dois le reconna脙庐tre, c'est le domaine budg脙漏taire. En effet, comme chaque ann脙漏e, votre budget 2026 est 脙聽 la fois une honte et un scandale. Une honte d'abord, car l'Union europ脙漏enne poursuit une spirale d'endettement compl脙篓tement folle. Selon la Cour des comptes europ脙漏enne, l'encours des emprunt de l'Union a augment脙漏 de plus de 30脗聽% en 2024 et pourrait d脙漏passer 900 milliards脗聽d'euros d'ici 2027, environ dix fois le niveau de 2020 avant la cr脙漏ation de Next Generation, votre merveilleux plan d'urgence post-Covid, totalement irr脙漏fl脙漏chi, qu'on enseignera un jour dans les 脙漏coles pour expliquer aux 脙漏tudiants ce qu'il ne faut surtout jamais faire.

Cette spirale d'endettement p脙篓sera sur les contribuables jusqu'en 2058, r脙漏duisant durablement la marge de man脜聯uvre budg脙漏taire de l'Union. Un scandale ensuite, car 脙聽 cause des int脙漏r脙陋ts non ma脙庐tris脙漏s de votre dette irresponsable, vous 脙陋tes contraints de r脙漏duire les d脙漏penses. Et au lieu de couper les tr脙篓s nombreuses mauvaises d脙漏penses id脙漏ologiques comme nous le proposons, vous pr脙漏f脙漏rez faire le choix de sacrifier les vraies urgences, le soutien 脙聽 notre agriculture, la s脙漏curit脙漏 de nos fronti脙篓res, la comp脙漏titivit脙漏 de nos entreprises.

Pire, vous ajoutez des normes, vous ne suspendez pas le Green Deal, vous envisagez la cr脙漏ation de nouvelles taxes, y compris sur les entreprises, taxes appel脙漏es hypocritement ressources propres que mon groupe et moi m脙陋me refusons. Enfin, vous avez, selon la Cour des comptes, un taux d'erreur de 3,6脗聽% dans vos d脙漏penses, soit plus de 115 milliards. Incroyable! Alors, pour vous aider, le groupe des Patriotes pour l'Europe a travaill脙漏, d脙漏pos脙漏 plus d'un quart des amendements budg脙漏taires de ce budget 2026. Ils traduisent une vision claire, un budget europ脙漏en recentr脙漏 sur les priorit脙漏s r脙漏elles des citoyens et la suppression des d脙漏penses inutiles. Nous vous invitons 脙聽 lire ces amendements et 脙聽 les voter. En ajoutant les voix des groupes Patriotes, ECR, ESN et PPE, il y a une majorit脙漏 alternative dans ce Parlement. Si nos amendements de bon sens ne sont pas vot脙漏s, nous consid脙漏rerons que vous faites le choix de pr脙漏senter un budget socialiste et nous voterons donc contre.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Beata Szyd脜聜o, w imieniu grupy ECR. 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Panie Komisarzu! Szanowni Pa脜聞stwo! Jest czas wojny i dzisiaj dla Europejczyk脙鲁w, dla Europy, dla nas wszystkich najistotniejsze jest bezpiecze脜聞stwo. Ten bud脜录et nie oddaje tego priorytetu w odpowiednim stopniu. Konkurencyjno脜聸脛聡, gospodarka 芒聙聯 praktycznie na ka脜录dej debacie, o tym m脙鲁wimy. Trzeba r脙鲁wnie脜录 zaznaczy脛聡, 脜录e to potrzebuje wsparcia, ale ludzie chc脛聟 czu脛聡 si脛聶 bezpiecznie, mie脛聡 poczucie tego, 脜录e Europa jest miejscem, w kt脙鲁rym b脛聶d脛聟 mogli budowa脛聡 spokojnie i bezpiecznie swoj脛聟 przysz脜聜o脜聸脛聡. I trzeba o tym r脙鲁wnie脜录 pami脛聶ta脛聡, rozmawiaj脛聟c o bud脜录ecie, a mo脜录e przede wszystkim o bud脜录ecie. Mo脜录emy rozmawia脛聡 tutaj o r脙鲁脜录nego rodzaju pomys脜聜ach, projektach, g脜聜脙鲁wnie ideologicznych, ale to nie one musz脛聟 by脛聡 sednem rozmowy o tym, jaka b脛聶dzie przysz脜聜o脜聸脛聡 Europy. A przysz脜聜o脜聸脛聡 Europy b脛聶dzie zale脜录a脜聜a od tego, czy w tym momencie ocenimy zagro脜录enia i czy odpowiednio zabezpieczymy je r脙鲁wnie脜录 w脜聜a脜聸nie poprzez 脜聸rodki finansowe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Stine Bosse, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, Commissioner, Minister, the 2026 budget comes at a moment where Europe faces unprecedented pressure. We feel the competition from major global players such as the US and China, and we are confronted with migration and instability to the south. And we have war on our continent.

In this context, the European Union must show what we stand for: freedom, prosperity and shared responsibility. I want to take the opportunity to thank my colleagues and to thank you especially, Mr脗聽Halicki, for the perfect stewardship.

This budget is not just about numbers on a page. It is about Europe's ability to stay strong, to act swiftly and to protect the future of our citizens. We want to invest in research, innovation, green energy, health and defence because that is where Europe's strength and independence is built.

Again, with this budget, we take a step towards an even more resilient and capable Europe 芒聙聯 one that stands firm and defends its values and its model, strong businesses, access to education, healthcare and opportunities for all. These principles are under pressure, and that's precisely why this budget matters.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rasmus Andresen, im Namen der Verts/ALE-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Der Haushalt 2026 ist ein guter Zeitpunkt, um zu vergleichen脗聽芒聙聯 um zu vergleichen, wie ernsthaft wir es eigentlich mit Verbesserungen konkret f脙录r die Menschen bei uns in der Europ脙陇ischen Union meinen. Wir Gr脙录ne k脙陇mpfen f脙录r soziale Verbesserungen f脙录r alle Menschen. Wir k脙陇mpfen daf脙录r, dass Erasmus f脙录r mehr Jugendliche 芒聙聯 auch aus Elternh脙陇usern mit wenig Einkommen 芒聙聯 zug脙陇nglich wird. Wir k脙陇mpfen daf脙录r, dass mehr Geld f脙录r soziales Wohnen eingesetzt wird, dass die Mieten zuk脙录nftig sinken. Wir k脙陇mpfen daf脙录r, dass Grenzpendler besser unterst脙录tzt werden, wenn sie Probleme haben beim grenz脙录berschreitenden Arbeiten, und wir k脙陇mpfen auch daf脙录r, dass die Europ脙陇ische Union mehr Geld und nicht weniger f脙录r Gesundheit ausgibt. Wir brauchen soziale Fortschritte, und der Haushalt 2026, ja, der l脙露st 芒聙聯 das stimmt 芒聙聯 sicherlich nicht alles, denn zur Wahrheit geh脙露rt auch, dass die Spielr脙陇ume, die wir haben, inzwischen sehr eng sind.

Deshalb muss ich ganz ehrlich sagen, dass es mich ein St脙录ck weit entt脙陇uscht, wieder mit einer Position vom Rat konfrontiert zu sein, die einzig und allein auf eines setzt, n脙陇mlich wild durch die Gegend streicht und keine gemeinsamen Priorit脙陇ten vorlegt, anders als wir es hier im Parlament machen. Deshalb bin ich sehr gl脙录cklich dar脙录ber, dass wir es geschafft haben, im Parlament mit den Spielr脙陇umen, die wir haben, klare Priorit脙陇ten zu setzen und daf脙录r gemeinsam auch einzustehen. Das ist wichtig f脙录r den Haushalt jetzt, aber es wird noch wichtiger f脙录r den mehrj脙陇hrigen Finanzrahmen, der ja auch schon in den Korridoren besprochen wird.

Deshalb m脙录ssen wir hier gemeinsam stehen. Deswegen noch einmal meine Bitte f脙录r die Abstimmung morgen an alle Kollegen, dass wir da kein dummes Zeug machen, dass wir nicht auf die Fallen der Rechtsextremen reinfallen, die im 脙聹brigen auch zu diesen Debatten kaum noch anwesend sind und keine Antr脙陇ge von der Seite im Haus beschlie脙聼en. Sie machen hier nur Populismus. Wir probieren, an L脙露sungen zu arbeiten. Lassen Sie uns das gemeinsam tun!

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira, em nome do Grupo The Left. 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio Serafin, a revis脙拢o intercalar do atual quadro financeiro plurianual acentuou o desvio de recursos or脙搂amentais para o militarismo e a guerra e para a pol脙颅tica de expuls脙拢o de migrantes para fora das fronteiras, desprezando as necessidades dos povos e do desenvolvimento nacional.

Esta proposta de or脙搂amento da Uni脙拢o Europeia para 2026 reflete essas op脙搂脙碌es e aprofunda芒聙聭as, deixando ainda mais para tr脙隆s a resposta aos reais problemas de cada pa脙颅s e de cada povo.

Fora das prioridades e objetivos do or脙搂amento, ficam quest脙碌es como o acesso 脙聽 habita脙搂脙拢o, a coes脙拢o social, o combate 脙聽 pobreza, a melhoria dos sal脙隆rios e das condi脙搂脙碌es de vida, o investimento nos servi脙搂os p脙潞blicos para garantir o acesso 脙聽 sa脙潞de, 脙聽 educa脙搂脙拢o, 脙聽 prote脙搂脙拢o social, aos transportes ou 脙聽 cultura, o apoio a pol脙颅ticas nacionais de aproveitamento das capacidades e recursos produtivos, o equil脙颅brio ambiental e ecol脙鲁gico. Em sentido contr脙隆rio, o militarismo, a guerra, o neg脙鲁cio do armamento e o favorecimento das multinacionais absorvem a fatia de le脙拢o do or脙搂amento da UE.

Para contornar o desagrado dos povos, dissimula芒聙聭se a injusta e desigual reparti脙搂脙拢o dos recursos or脙搂amentais que deviam estar ao servi脙搂o dos povos. O exemplo disso s脙拢o as verbas da coes脙拢o que, inscritas na rubrica da coes脙拢o, ficam ainda mais curtas para esses objetivos, porque passam agora tamb脙漏m a servir para financiar o militarismo designado de defesa e seguran脙搂a. At脙漏 para a mobilidade militar se admite a possibilidade de utiliza脙搂脙拢o das verbas da coes脙拢o.

Este n脙拢o 脙漏 um or脙搂amento de progresso, de desenvolvimento nem de justi脙搂a social. 脙聣 um or脙搂amento de retrocesso e de agudiza脙搂脙拢o das desigualdades e das injusti脙搂as.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Milan Uhr脙颅k, za skupinu ESN. 芒聙聯 Kedysi sa na Slovensku povr脙隆valo, 脜戮e len v脛聫aka eurofondom m脙隆me peniaze na invest脙颅cie, rekon脜隆trukcie a opravy. 脜陆ia脛戮, pri poh脛戮ade na bud脙潞ci eur脙鲁psky rozpo脛聧et to vyzer脙隆 tak, 脜戮e budeme ma脜楼 peniaze akur脙隆t tak na nezmysly. Preto脜戮e op脙陇脜楼 脛聫al脜隆ie miliardy na zelen脙漏 experimenty, na vymyslen脙漏 projekty a na tis脙颅cky zbyto脛聧n脙陆ch 脙潞radn脙颅kov. A ja sa p脙陆tam, kde s脙潞 peniaze pre be脜戮n脙陆ch Eur脙鲁panov? Pre b脙陆vanie mlad脙陆ch, pre rodiny, pre 脛戮ud脙颅, ktor脙颅 nevl脙隆dzu plati脜楼 drah脙漏 n脙隆jmy, energie, potraviny. V tom novom rozpo脛聧te navrhujete zv脙陆脜隆i脜楼 vojensk脙漏 v脙陆davky na vyzbrojovanie Ukrajiny, zv脙陆脜隆i脜楼 podporu kreat脙颅vnych umelcov, mimovl脙隆dok, nez脙隆visl脙陆ch m脙漏di脙颅, fakt checkerov a popritom zavies脜楼 povinn脙漏 zelen脙漏 rodov脙漏 kv脙鲁ty a da脜楼 menej pe脜聢az脙颅 pre po脛戮nohospod脙隆rov, regi脙鲁ny, vidiek, menej pe脜聢az脙颅 pre eur脙鲁pskych 脜隆tudentov, ale zato zv脙陆脜隆i脜楼 rozpo脛聧et pre Erasmus tak, aby bolo mo脜戮n脙漏 doviez脜楼 do Eur脙鲁py ka脜戮doro脛聧ne 105 tis脙颅c 脜隆tudentov z Afriky. V Bruseli ste asi zabudli, kto s脙潞 eur脙鲁pski da脜聢ov脙颅 poplatn脙颅ci, a tento rozpo脛聧et je v脙陆smech t脙陆mto poplatn脙颅kom.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Karlo Ressler (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Mr Commissioner, dear colleagues, here we are in this perhaps late night debate on the annual budget for the next year, and the hour may be late, but the topic definitely deserves our full attention, since we are discussing the priorities that go much beyond this Chamber and all this in times of global uncertainty, but also growing competition in the world where the EU needs a budget that also can meet citizens expectations, and not only for the next year, but also for the MFF that we are discussing these days as well.

The main idea of the Parliament here is to restore the 1.3 billion for key programmes cut by the Council, compared with the Commission's draft budget. We cannot accept the cuts that would weaken the Union's strategic objectives and we need strong investment in research, in energy and transport connectivity as well, and in small- and medium-sized enterprises where the backbone of the European economy.

In the context of security challenges, we must also ensure additional resources for internal and external security.

The discussions have maybe been long and at times also difficult also in the Parliament, but also within our Groups. But they have in the end led us to a fair and balanced compromise. In that sense, I really congratulate both of the rapporteurs to Andrzej and Matija, and I really invite you to support the outcome support, because with tomorrow's vote, we enter the final stage of negotiations with the Council, a crucial step towards reaching a balanced agreement. And I hope that in the continued engagement with the Commission, we can count on the support of the colleagues.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jean-Marc Germain (S&D). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, je voudrais d'abord f脙漏liciter les rapporteurs de ce projet de budget 2026, Andrzej脗聽Halicki pour le PPE, Matja脜戮脗聽Nemec pour le groupe S&D, ainsi que tous les rapporteurs fictifs qui sont l脙聽 et qui ont 脜聯uvr脙漏 脙聽 ce texte.

Quel contraste avec le long cort脙篓ge des reculs sur le pacte vert qui 脙漏maillent encore la semaine! Qu'il s'agisse de la lutte contre la d脙漏forestation, de la responsabilit脙漏 sociale et environnementale des entreprises ou encore du devoir de vigilance, l'Europe se dissout dans la compromission et elle se renforce dans le compromis. C'est gr脙垄ce 脙聽 ce dialogue entre les Verts, Renew, S&D et le PPE, n'en d脙漏plaise 脙聽 l'extr脙陋me droite, que nous avons pu aboutir 脙聽 un projet de budget pour 2026 gagnant-gagnant. C'est un bon 脙漏quilibre qu'il faut pr脙漏server.

Mais soyons lucides, nous faisons ce que nous pouvons, mais nous ne faisons pas ce que nous devrions faire, car nous nous heurtons au plafond de verre du 1脗聽% du PNB qui s'impose 脙聽 nous comme un plafond de d脙漏penses annuelles. 脙聙 cause de ce plafond, pas plus tard que la semaine derni脙篓re, nous avons d脙禄 nous r脙漏soudre, en trilogue, 脙聽 doter le Fonds europ脙漏en pour les industries de d脙漏fense d'un montant d脙漏risoire de 1,5脗聽milliard d'euros. Ce n'est pas avec cela que l'on va convaincre Poutine de renoncer 脙聽 son entreprise meurtri脙篓re.

Les ann脙漏es qui viennent seront celles d'un besoin d'investissements immense pour la transition 脙漏nerg脙漏tique, pour la souverainet脙漏 脙漏conomique et pour la d脙漏fense europ脙漏enne. C'est la raison pour laquelle nous ne pouvons pas repartir, Monsieur le Commissaire, avec un nouveau CFP qui serait soumis au m脙陋me carcan que le pr脙漏c脙漏dent. Briser le plafond de verre, nous avons su le faire: ce fut NextGeneration脗聽EU, ce grand emprunt commun pour traverser sans effondrement la pand脙漏mie de COVID. Nous devons le refaire.

L'heure n'est plus 脙聽 g脙漏rer la p脙漏nurie avec cette proposition de CFP de la Commission, que nous rejetons, d'un budget limit脙漏 une nouvelle fois 脙聽 1脗聽% du PNB ou 脙聽 peine plus. Non, l'heure est 脙聽 nous donner les moyens脗聽芒聙聯 et je termine, Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident脗聽芒聙聯, par de nouvelles ressources propres et par l'emprunt commun, de nous faire les architectes d'un monde plus juste, plus s脙禄r et plus durable.

Mindezzel szemben az eur脙鲁paiak 脙漏s a magyarok els脙露pr脜聭 t脙露bbs脙漏ge b脙漏k脙漏t akar, sem katon脙隆t, sem p脙漏nzt, sem fegyvert nem akarnak adni ebbe a h脙隆bor脙潞ba. El脙漏g volt a br脙录sszeli h脙隆bor脙潞s tervekb脜聭l. Az eur脙鲁paiak 脙漏s a magyarok nem k脙漏rnek az illeg脙隆lis migr脙隆ci脙鲁b脙鲁l sem. B脙隆rhogyan is sz脙漏p脙颅tik, a migr脙隆ci脙鲁s paktum cs脙潞ny脙隆n megbukott. A Tisza 脙漏s a DK 脙隆ltal is t脙隆mogatott, katasztrof脙隆lis migr脙隆nssimogat脙鲁 politika egyenes k脙露vetkezm脙漏nye az Eur脙鲁p脙隆ban n脙露vekv脜聭 antiszemitizmus, a terrorizmus 脙漏s az er脜聭szakos b脜卤ncselekm脙漏nyek sz脙隆m脙隆nak emelked脙漏se.

R脙隆ad脙隆sul az Eur脙鲁pai Bizotts脙隆g eg脙漏szen arc脙隆tlanul 2026-ban tov脙隆bbi eur脙鲁milli脙隆rdokat akar k脙露lteni a br脙录sszeli korrupci脙鲁s h脙隆l脙鲁zat, a tag脙隆llamok szuverenit脙隆s脙隆t durv脙隆n t脙隆mad脙鲁 脙隆l NGO-k 脙漏s liber脙隆lis m脙漏dialerakatok politikai finansz脙颅roz脙隆s脙隆ra is. 脗聽脗聽Dick Erixon (ECR). 芒聙聯 Herr talman! Det 脙陇r l脙陇tt att vara gener脙露s med andras pengar. Det h脙陇r bet脙陇nkandet om budgeten 2026 脙陇r skaml脙露st i sitt sl脙露seri. Att f脙露respr脙楼ka nya inkomstk脙陇llor som pungsl脙楼r skattebetalarna, det 脙陇r inte acceptabelt.

I bet脙陇nkandet ogillas att r脙楼det sk脙陇r ner, sparar en del i kommissionens f脙露rslag. Men h脙陇r kan sk脙陇ras ner mer, i frugal anda.

I脗聽st脙陇llet f脙露r att bygga luftslott borde EU 脙楼terg脙楼 till det som 脙陇r k脙陇rnuppgiften f脙露r det h脙陇r samarbetet, f脙露r det europeiska samarbetet. Det 脙陇r s脙陇kerhet, frihandel och konkurrenskraft. Det h脙陇r f脙楼r inte komma bort.

Vi m脙楼ste 脙楼terg脙楼 till k脙陇rnuppgiften f脙露r att f脙楼 Europa p脙楼 f脙露tter och f脙露r att Europa inte ska halka efter.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lucia Yar (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, colleagues, the 2026 budget is not just about figures 芒聙聯 I hope we know it. It's about Europe's future. It's about the promises that we make to our citizens.

We, in Renew Europe, fought very hard to secure hundreds of millions more for research, for innovation, for health and for defence. These are investments that protect and empower Europeans.

But I turn to Member States, to the Council, because I cannot comprehend how Member States can keep on proposing cuts to Erasmus+, the EU's most successful and most beloved programme. Erasmus+ is not just a budget line, it is the promise of opportunity. It is the bridge that is connecting young people across borders. Instead of cutting it, we should be expanding it. We should be bold to make grants easier, more accessible.

Renew Europe and I will fight fiercely for this, because every euro in this budget must serve people and above all, our young generation.

脗聽脗聽Marcin Sypniewski (ESN). 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Bud脜录et Unii Europejskiej na rok 2026 to ju脜录 nie jest 脜录aden plan finansowy. To jest tak naprawd脛聶 manifest ideologiczny. Jest to wyraz filozofii i niestety jest to filozofia interwencjonizmu, redystrybucji i biurokracji. Znowu widzimy pr脙鲁b脛聶 rozlewania pieni脛聶dzy wsz脛聶dzie, gdzie si脛聶 tylko da, w przekonaniu, 脜录e wi脛聶cej pieni脛聶dzy daje wi脛聶cej efekt脙鲁w.

Tymczasem to nie brak 脜聸rodk脙鲁w jest problemem Europy, ale b脜聜脛聶dna diagnoza jej choroby, fakt, 脜录e Europa jest strukturalnie nieefektywna, przeregulowana i dusi inicjatyw脛聶 przez swoj脛聟 polityk脛聶 fiskaln脛聟, przez to, 脜录e p脜聜acimy wszyscy coraz wi脛聶cej podatk脙鲁w, coraz wi脛聶cej ukrytych koszt脙鲁w prowadzenia dzia脜聜alno脜聸ci i zwyk脜聜ego 脜录ycia dla obywateli. I w tym bud脜录ecie nie ma nic, co odwr脙鲁ci脜聜oby spadek konkurencyjno脜聸ci Europy wobec reszty 脜聸wiata.

Za to widzimy to wszystko, co ju脜录 by脜聜o, czyli podtrzymywanie sztucznego popytu, dotowanie zielonych projekt脙鲁w ideologicznych i kolejne miliardy na obs脜聜ug脛聶 aparatu centralnego. 脜禄aden nar脙鲁d, 脜录adna instytucja nie wygrywa dzi脛聶ki dotacjom, tylko dzi脛聶ki wolno脜聸ci. Wi脛聶cej wolno脜聸ci, mniej dotacji. Tak powinno by脛聡.

脗聽脗聽Thomas Geisel (NI). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, Herr Kommissar, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Warum wollen wir der Kommission eigentlich immer mehr Geld geben? Als Parlament ist es unsere Aufgabe, die Kommission zu beaufsichtigen und zu kontrollieren. Unsere Aufgabe ist es, daf脙录r zu sorgen, dass die Kommission ihre Kompetenzen nicht 脙录berschreitet, und unsere Aufgabe ist es auch, der Kommission jedenfalls dort keine Verwaltungskompetenz und keine Haushaltsmittel zu geben, wo die entsprechenden Aufgaben transparenter, effizienter und demokratischer auf der Ebene der Mitgliedstaaten, der Provinzen und der Kommunen erledigt werden k脙露nnen.

Der Kommissionsentwurf f脙录r den mehrj脙陇hrigen Finanzrahmen sollte uns eine Warnung sein. Was Frau von der Leyen anstrebt, ist eine Herrschaft der B脙录rokratie, die mit dem goldenen Z脙录gel des Geldes in Mitgliedstaaten hineinregiert, um ihre sogenannte Reformagenda durchzusetzen. Das Geld soll das Parlament bewilligen, zu sagen hat es ansonsten nicht viel. Liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, ich kann nur empfehlen, da nicht mitzumachen. Wir sollten uns nicht dem Verdacht aussetzen, die n脙录tzlichen Idioten der Kommission zu sein.

脗聽脗聽Isabel Benjumea Benjumea (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or comisario, se脙卤or ministro, el presupuesto es el origen y la raz脙鲁n de la existencia de un Parlamento: es el acto legislativo democr脙隆tico por excelencia; supone la plasmaci脙鲁n de las prioridades pol脙颅ticas, de las respuestas a los retos y las soluciones a los problemas.

Lamentablemente, el repago de la deuda de los fondos NextGenerationEU supone una limitaci脙鲁n. Aun as脙颅, el presupuesto que ma脙卤ana votamos responde a las prioridades de la Uni脙鲁n: es un buen presupuesto.

Recoge apuestas por la cohesi脙鲁n territorial, la ayuda a nuestro sector primario, la defensa de nuestras fronteras y el fortalecimiento de la seguridad, la competitividad de nuestras empresas, la creaci脙鲁n de empleo, la apuesta por la investigaci脙鲁n, la innovaci脙鲁n y la excelencia educativa; y, por ello, quiero dar la enhorabuena a su ponente y reiterar que es un buen presupuesto.

Sin embargo, en la Uni脙鲁n Europea tenemos una excepci脙鲁n triste y democr脙隆ticamente insostenible: el Gobierno de Pedro S脙隆nchez. Espa脙卤a lleva sin Presupuestos Generales del Estado desde el a脙卤o 2023. Tras dos a脙卤os sin presupuestos, el Gobierno incumple la Constituci脙鲁n y sus obligaciones con el Parlamento: Espa脙卤a es el 脙潞nico Estado miembro que no ha enviado su plan de presupuestos a las autoridades europeas. Esta es una triste excepci脙鲁n que me veo en la obligaci脙鲁n de denunciar en un d脙颅a como hoy.
