脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sandra G脙鲁mez L脙鲁pez (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or comisario, se脙卤or ministro, Europa y sus familias afrontan un momento muy complejo y complicado: crisis clim脙隆tica, la crisis de la vivienda, una desigualdad creciente, p脙漏rdida de competitividad y una guerra. Y de nosotros esperan una respuesta firme, con un presupuesto fuerte, justo y centrado en las personas.

Sin embargo, la propuesta que ha hecho el Consejo es de recortes en programas con un valor europeo incuestionable. Y 脗驴por qu脙漏? Pues porque no hay dinero. No tenemos recursos suficientes. Y esto tiene que ser una clara muestra de que, si en 2026 no llegamos, no podemos defender mantener el mismo tama脙卤o del marco financiero hasta 2034. Es que es absurdo. Por eso tenemos que ser m脙隆s ambiciosos y aprovechar esto para apoyar el aumento del tama脙卤o del marco financiero.

Y quiero pedirles que, por favor, tambi脙漏n voten la enmienda que hemos presentado como grupo pol脙颅tico para que las mujeres en permiso de maternidad puedan votar telem脙隆ticamente. Porque es vergonzoso que en este Pleno veamos a mujeres que tengan que traer a su beb脙漏 reci脙漏n nacido para poder ejercer su derecho al voto. Una imagen vergonzosa que tenemos que erradicar de este Pleno.

脗聽脗聽Bogdan Rzo脜聞ca (ECR). 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Nie ma problemu z tym, 脜录eby powiedzie脛聡, 脜录e Unia Europejska to dobry pomys脜聜, tylko 脜录e, niestety, jest 脜潞le zarz脛聟dzany. 脗聽脗聽Estrella Gal脙隆n (The Left). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or comisario, se脙卤or ministro, pocas cosas muestran mejor el rumbo equivocado de esta Uni脙鲁n Europea que este presupuesto.

La Uni脙鲁n Europea naci脙鲁 como un proyecto de paz entre los pueblos, sobre los escombros de la guerra y del fascismo. Naci脙鲁 para garantizar derechos, igualdad, dignidad para todas y todos.

Hoy ese proyecto est脙隆 siendo traicionado: en lugar de invertir en la paz, se financia la guerra; en lugar de proteger a la gente, se desv脙颅an los recursos de los fondos sociales a armas; en lugar de salvar vidas, se destinan millones de euros a levantar muros m脙隆s altos y a dejar morir personas en el mar.

Este presupuesto no es una casualidad, es una decisi脙鲁n pol脙颅tica vergonzosa. No estamos hablando solo de cifras, sino de una Europa que ha perdido el rumbo con l脙颅deres que han elegido la guerra por encima de la vida y de la justicia.

Los ciudadanos necesitan que se invierta en vivienda, en educaci脙鲁n, en sanidad, en justicia social... pero este presupuesto abandona a los europeos y a las europeas.

脗聽脗聽Carla Tavares (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, Senhor Ministro, em primeiro lugar, permitam芒聙聭me saudar esta proposta de or脙搂amento, bem como o trabalho efetuado pelos dois relatores, num contexto particularmente dif脙颅cil de incerteza econ脙鲁mica e geopol脙颅tica.

Sabemos que a margem de manobra or脙搂amental que temos 脙漏 limitada, mas sabemos que os desafios que enfrentamos s脙拢o quase ilimitados. Por isso, 脙漏 preciso fazer escolhas.

A primeira escolha 脙漏 saber onde queremos chegar em termos de or脙搂amento. Precisamos de um or脙搂amento para 2026 que seja cred脙颅vel, ambicioso, flex脙颅vel e que esteja centrado nas pessoas. Todos os desafios que enfrentamos atualmente, da Ucr脙垄nia ao clima, da competitividade 脙聽 democracia, t脙陋m um impacto direto na vida dos europeus e precisam de financiamento. Os europeus esperam de todos n脙鲁s respostas duradouras e n脙拢o simples rea脙搂脙碌es avulsas a humores externos.

Outra escolha 脙漏 o compromisso da Uni脙拢o Europeia com os seus valores. E permitam芒聙聭me saudar o facto de este or脙搂amento para 2026 ter um refor脙搂o de verbas para apoiar os refugiados palestinianos e, em particular, a UNRWA. 脙聣 preciso reconstruir Gaza do zero, e a Uni脙拢o tem de estar presente nesse apoio desde o primeiro minuto.

Este 脙漏 o nosso compromisso. Or脙搂amentos n脙拢o s脙拢o apenas n脙潞meros, s脙拢o tamb脙漏m compromissos com os nossos princ脙颅pios e com os nossos valores.

脗聽脗聽Tomasz Buczek (PfE). 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Panie Komisarzu! Bud脜录et na 2026 rok jest bud脜录etem sprzeczno脜聸ci. Mamy tu wci脛聟脜录 te same ideologiczne cele, lansowane w oderwaniu od rzeczywistych problem脙鲁w Europejczyk脙鲁w: dekarbonizacja, neutralno脜聸脛聡 klimatyczna, zielona energia, zielona stal, promocja aborcji i LGBT. A z drugiej strony, marzenia o poprawie gospodarki i konkurencyjno脜聸ci. Absurd. To nie dzia脜聜a脜聜o nigdy, nie dzia脜聜a i nie b脛聶dzie dzia脜聜a脛聡. Co musi si脛聶 wydarzy脛聡, aby脜聸cie otworzyli oczy?

Wasza polityka kontynuacji szkodliwych program脙鲁w jest dzisiaj najwi脛聶kszym zagro脜录eniem dla rozwoju, bezpiecze脜聞stwa i dobrobytu Europejczyk脙鲁w, w tym zw脜聜aszcza Polak脙鲁w, Panie Komisarzu. To Wasza polityka zamyka europejskie fabryki, wygasza hutnicze piece, likwiduje kopalnie, a w tym samym czasie te same bran脜录e rozkwitaj脛聟 w Azji, a ich towary zalewaj脛聟 nasze rynki. Pora zmierzy脛聡 si脛聶 z rzeczywisto脜聸ci脛聟 i porzuci脛聡 脜聸wiat bajek o ratowaniu planety. Nie chcemy nowych zasob脙鲁w w脜聜asnych Unii. Nie b脛聶dziemy p脜聜aci脛聡 za Wasze odrealnienie i brak odwagi przyznania si脛聶 do oczywistych b脜聜脛聶d脙鲁w.

脗聽脗聽Rudi Kennes (The Left). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, you said the EU budget is nothing more than politics expressed in numbers, a reflection of choices and priorities. I agree. Over EUR脗聽130脗聽billion for defence 芒聙聯 five times more. EUR脗聽18脗聽billion for military mobility 芒聙聯 ten times more. Border management tripled and EUR脗聽15脗聽million extra for the wine sector.

Your concerns are not those from the European people. You invent imaginary threats to divert attention from the real threats: not being able to pay your bills, healthcare and housing. Not one word on wages and pensions, and nothing for the 1.3 million homeless people.

You vilify and demonise migrants, but you know they are escaping our wars and climate disasters. They look after our elderly, they clean our hospitals, they cook in our canteens. Without them, our economies and societies would collapse.

You say you want an independent Europe, but then you take your orders from NATO. We can make Europe a place where everybody is treated with respect, or we can let it be a racist fortress. You make your choices 芒聙聯 we will continue to fight them.

脗聽脗聽Christophe Clergeau (S&D). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, je voudrais faire deux remarques sur le budget europ脙漏en de la sant脙漏. Ce programme EU4Health a subi 20脗聽% de baisse lors de la r脙漏vision 脙聽 mi-parcours du cadre financier pluriannuel. Un milliard s'est envol脙漏, dont 620 millions sur le volet sant脙漏 de la pr脙漏paration aux crises. Pour 2026, le Conseil propose une nouvelle coupe de 95 millions, soit 15脗聽%, ce qui est 脙漏norme et porte de nouveau atteinte 脙聽 la capacit脙漏 脙聽 se pr脙漏parer aux crises et 脙聽 prot脙漏ger la population. Il faut d脙漏sormais arr脙陋ter les doubles discours. Soit la sant脙漏 et la pr脙漏paration aux crises sont importants, soit ils ne le sont pas. J'esp脙篓re que l'engagement du Parlement pour r脙漏tablir ces cr脙漏dits permettra de convaincre le Conseil.

Ma seconde remarque porte sur le soutien aux ONG dans le domaine de la sant脙漏. En 2025, la Commission a chang脙漏 son approche en arr脙陋tant les subventions de fonctionnement pour les remplacer par des appels 脙聽 projets, sans aucune justification. Cela fragilise durablement les ONG qui n'ont plus de visibilit脙漏, dont certaines ont d脙禄 licencier 40脗聽% de leur personnel. Pourtant, nous avons besoin des ONG, de leurs voix libres, de leurs comp脙漏tences et de leur contribution pour construire les politiques europ脙漏ennes de la sant脙漏. Les ONG ont saisi le M脙漏diateur europ脙漏en. Merci 脙聽 la Commission de revoir sa copie.

脗聽脗聽Marc Botenga (The Left). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Ministre, Monsieur le Commissaire, 脗芦Je ne pars jamais en vacances脗禄, 脗芦Je saute des repas pour que mes enfants puissent manger脗禄, 脗芦L'hiver, je meurs de froid entre mes m脙漏dicaments et le chauffage脗禄: ce sont quelques-uns des messages que j'ai re脙搂us des gens, et c'est la r脙漏alit脙漏 d'une partie de l'Europe, d'environ 100脗聽millions d'Europ脙漏ens qui risquent aujourd'hui la pauvret脙漏.

Donc d'un c脙麓t脙漏 vous avez cette Europe-l脙聽 et de l'autre c脙麓t脙漏 vous avez une Europe diff脙漏rente, une Europe notamment des entreprises de l'armement, qui, aujourd'hui, affichent des profits records. Prenez l'allemande Rheinmetall, qui affiche une hausse de ses profits de 61脗聽% l'ann脙漏e pr脙漏c脙漏dente, et vous voyez qui a besoin d'argent.

Ah, c'est ma pension qui vole!

Cela ne marchera pas comme 脙搂a. Nous avons besoin d'autres priorit脙漏s. Sortons un peu de cette bulle europ脙漏enne. Disons-le, 脙聽 ces ministres, disons-le, 脙聽 ces commissaires, que ce sont les gens qui ont besoin de cela. Et pour cela, taxons les riches si vous avez besoin d'argent.

脗聽脗聽Nina Carberry (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President,脗聽I want to congratulate Mr脗聽Halicki and all the rapporteurs for their hard work on this.

Last year, 20脗聽000 Irish students took part in Erasmus+, gaining invaluable experience across Europe. Erasmus+ is more than a programme. It drives competitiveness, employability, innovation and resilience, while connecting young people to Europe. That is why I was deeply disappointed to see the Council propose cuts to the 2026 EU budget for Erasmus+. Young people must not pay the price for repaying NextGenerationEU.

We have come together in this Parliament to propose an increase of EUR脗聽5脗聽million for Erasmus+ in next year's budget to make Erasmus+ accessible for all. As we plan the next framework, we must focus more on skills, training and apprenticeships. We need to do more to increase to ensure Erasmus+ reaches beyond universities. We will better prepare the next generation by doing so.

脗聽脗聽Juan Fernando L脙鲁pez Aguilar (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, el Parlamento Europeo es autoridad presupuestaria de acuerdo con los Tratados. Pero no est脙隆 solo. Y, por eso, este presupuesto declina en relaci脙鲁n con los presupuestos expansivos que conocimos la pasada legislatura.

Y, aun as脙颅, el Grupo Socialdem脙鲁crata se ha esforzado por que haya financiaci脙鲁n para acometer los retos sociales y econ脙鲁micos, como la vivienda 芒聙聰urgencia absoluta芒聙聰; y la financiaci脙鲁n del programa Ciudadanos, Igualdad, Derechos y Valores, que sustenta la idea europea del Estado de Derecho; adem脙隆s del Fondo de Asilo, Migraci脙鲁n e Integraci脙鲁n, que permite la atenci脙鲁n al Pacto sobre Migraci脙鲁n y Asilo, y la Vecindad Meridional, que incluye tambi脙漏n una oferta para que sea mutuamente interesante con los pa脙颅ses de origen y tr脙隆nsito.

Pero la historia no termina con el presupuesto: est脙隆 encuadrada en un marco financiero plurianual. Y hay que decir que la Comisi脙鲁n amenaza con un marco financiero plurianual inaceptable para los a脙卤os 2028 a 2035, que hace irreconocible la pol脙颅tica regional y de cohesi脙鲁n. Y no lo podemos permitir.

Neabejotinai kalbu kaip buv脛聶s komisaras ir atstovav脛聶s maisto saugai ir visuomen脛聴s sveikatai. I脜隆ties visuomen脛聴s sveikatai ir 脜隆ie 2026-ieji metai nebus derlingi. Deja, taip yra. Ir EU4Health programa, ir Europe's Beating Cancer programa, ir Horizon programos, kurios reikalaut脜鲁 daug daugiau integruot脜鲁 ir didesni脜鲁 pastang脜鲁 芒聙聯 jos, deja, nepasiekia tokio finansinio lygio, kokio reik脛聴t脜鲁 pagal poreikius, nes poreikiai yra kur kas didesni. 脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, the EU budget has grown to become an uncontrollable monster. Billions of euros are wasted for projects promoting gender mainstreaming, climate ambition, Creative Europe and so on.

At the same time, European citizens struggle to make a living. Do Europeans really need more beautiful EU agencies and EU action plans? Instead of feeding this bloated bureaucracy, we should focus on serious topics, not ideological experiments. And most importantly: taking action now.

If you need more green or leftist workshops, please pay for them yourself. Let's bring discipline, responsibility and realism back to this Union. The taxpayer's duty is not to fund every Brussels fantasy.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Fin des interventions 脙聽 la demande)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Piotr Serafin, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, honourable Members, I would like to thank all of you for that constructive debate. The Commission has well noted the positions that were expressed by the honourable Members of this House, but also that of the Council.

The role of the Commission is to facilitate the upcoming negotiations, and we will play that role. In that spirit, we will present an assessment of the amendments proposed by the Parliament, and of the Council's position. We will have an opportunity to discuss it on 4 November when we meet in the format of the Conciliation Committee.

As I have said before, I am convinced that the key to successful negotiations lies in both institutions setting clear priorities. Bearing in mind that the financial resources in the next year's budget are very limited. Let me assure you that the Commission will act as an honest broker. My services, and me personally, we will do our utmost to prepare the ground for good and successful negotiations.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nicolai Wammen, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, honourable Members, dear rapporteurs, dear Commissioner, tonight I have listened carefully to the arguments raised during the debate, and I will inform my colleagues in the Council accordingly.

I would like to thank the Members of the European Parliament for their help in casting light on the European Parliament's priorities. We share the vision of a strong Europe. At the same time, we also have disagreements in terms of the size of the budget, but not when it comes to the importance of Europe.

I have noted that most of the suggestions tonight were on increasing expenditure. But, dear colleagues, at the end of the day there will be limits to the budget. Not everything can be equally important. We have to prioritise. That is not a job only for the Council, or the Commission, or the European Parliament 芒聙聯 it is a job for all of us. We will need to find common ground. As I said in my initial remarks, I am confident and optimistic that we will do so, because I know that the spirit of this House, the spirit of the Commission and the spirit of the Council is that that is our job, because we have a job to do for the citizens of Europe.

Therefore, the Council will do its utmost to engage in a constructive dialogue, allowing us to come to a balanced agreement on the budget for 2026. Therefore, I am looking forward to this process, and I am encouraged by both the interventions by the rapporteurs and by the Commission.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andrzej Halicki, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues, thank you for this debate. Yes, we are facing new challenges. Yes, we are living in very tough times. But we have to be also offensive and responsible. And I believe in dialogue. We are different, but what I would like to emphasise is that we had a good understanding of our priorities in building the strong, united proposal of Parliament, and we cannot destroy this work.脗聽From the Greens to the ECR by the Renew Group, the S&D and the EPP, together we have to build our strong united position to achieve success during the negotiations, which is achievable, of course.

So I would like to emphasise, once again, that we cannot accept the cuts, especially on flagship programmes like Erasmus+ or Horizon. We would like to have top-ups on very important issues, including EU4Health. We have a new committee and this is the first step for a better future, of course initially. It's the first time we have support for external border protection, including in the budget, support so widely in Parliament 芒聙聯 do not ignore that this is the first time.

So I think that we are close to also having satisfied conciliation and the negotiations, including understanding from both sides 芒聙聯 the European Commission and and the Council. But we have to be united and strong tomorrow. So please vote in favour of this resolution and the proposal.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Matja脜戮 Nemec, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear Minister, dear Commissioner, dear colleagues, thank you to Mr脗聽Halicki. I listened to all the speakers carefully. With many I can agree and some less. However, I believe that we share a vision with many of you of a better Europe 芒聙聯 a more social Europe that delivers for its citizens and that is effective. This Europe is not about individual partial interests, but about a common one, and Europe is strong when it is united. This is our next challenge at hand, finding a compromise that will be based on those principles.

Dear colleagues and shadow rapporteurs, I want to thank you for the very constructive work and cooperation so far. I believe tomorrow we will get strong support for the Parliament's position ahead of the conciliation talks.脗聽I look very much forward to these discussions and I am hopeful we will find an agreement that will be a good one.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Le Pr脙漏sident. 芒聙聯 Le d脙漏bat est clos.

Le vote aura lieu demain.

脗聽

19. Renewing the EU-Africa Partnership: building common priorities ahead of the Angola Summit (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Le Pr脙漏sident. 芒聙聯 L'ordre du jour appelle 脙聽 pr脙漏sent le d脙漏bat sur la d脙漏claration de la Commission intitul脙漏e 脗芦Renouveler le partenariat UE-Afrique: construire des priorit脙漏s communes dans la脗聽perspective du sommet en Angola脗禄 (2025/2933(RSP)).

Je donne la parole pour commencer, pour la Commission europ脙漏enne, au commissaire Magnus脗聽Brunner.

Monsieur le Commissaire, vous 脙陋tes attendu, d'ores et d脙漏j脙聽.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Magnus Brunner, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, sorry. Thank you for that. I just had to say hello to my former colleagues and present colleagues.

Honourable Members, 25 years ago, the first EU-Africa summit took place, in Cairo, actually, and now we are preparing the seventh EU-Africa summit on 24 and 25 November in Angola. Over this quarter of a century, we have been building strong relations between the European Union and Africa, also thanks to the active participation of the European Parliament and the Pan-African Parliament.

At a time of geopolitical competition, Africa and Europe have a shared interest in dialogue and also in mutual beneficial cooperation. Marking the partnership's 25th anniversary, 2025 is a crucial year, I would say, for the EU-Africa relations, with increased engagement that will culminate in the mentioned summit in November.

We are definitely Africa's prime partner. The European Union remains Africa's first trading partner, first investor by far, first partner on peace and also security, and first official development assistance provider and humanitarian donor. EU trade with Africa accounted for 33脗聽% of Africa's total trade. We have a positive agenda converging with many of the objectives of the African Union's Agenda 2063. So, the European Union is constantly adapting its relations with Africa at regional levels, but also at national levels, and the joint vision for 2030 adopted by the European Union and African Union Heads of State or Government at the last summit, in 2022, is actually guiding our partnership. The recently adopted pact for the Mediterranean will also contribute to the EU-Africa relations.

The EU partnership with Africa includes four key areas: its prosperity, its peace and security, its migration and mobility, and multilateralism. First of all, the prosperity part: the Global Gateway Africa-Europe Investment Package targets at least EUR脗聽150 billion of investments in Africa by 2027, and we are well on track to reach this target. Global Gateway is a very ambitious investment strategy and also evidence of Team Europe working together. It is more than an offer; it is also a choice: a choice of partners, a choice of values, of principles, of a world vision, of sustainable long term development.

The second point, peace and security: since 2021, the European Union has supported African partners with over EUR脗聽1 billion through the European Peace Facility (EPF). Around 70脗聽% of all EPF support 芒聙聯 taking Ukraine aside, of course 芒聙聯 is dedicated to African partners. The European Union has also 12 civilian and military missions in Africa, and this represents a European commitment of some 2脗聽000 soldiers, police and civil servants providing advice and support to African counterparts, and the European Union also strongly supports African peace support operations.

The third point, migration, key to me, of course, especially: and when managing migration, we work on preventing, of course, irregular migration, also combating migrant-smuggling and trafficking in human beings and improving effective, safe and also timely returns, but also readmission, of course, and also sustainable reintegration. And at the same time, we work on developing and also expanding pathways for legal migration, for regular migration, and also mobility of students and researchers.

And the fourth point, the last point, the multilateralism point: we have a very ambitious and also forward-looking partnership firmly rooted in a multilateral system, with the UN at its core, and we want to deliver a strong message on the respect of the rules-based international order and the commitment to work together in international fora. I am very much convinced that Europe and Africa working together on global challenges will have a definite effect on the 21st century.

So, the parliamentary dimension of the EU-AU partnership is a vital pillar that strengthens our political and also our democratic foundations, and parliaments altogether represent the voice of the people, of course, and through interparliamentary dialogue, we ensure that the partnerships also reflect not only the will of governments, but also the aspirations and concerns of our citizens. Regular engagement between the European Parliament and the Pan-African Parliament allows for deeper understanding, deeper mutual understanding, more transparency and earned accountability and, as demonstrated by the European Parliament, a strong parliament makes for a strong union.

We are very much confident that the existing partnership and the support provided by the European Union to the Pan-African Parliament will also further strengthen its role and its overall contribution to continental integration and to the EU-AU partnership.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Mandl, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, thank you very much for this deep dive into the important matter of the EU-Africa Partnership. And, in the long term, there is not much on the planet that matters more to Europe, to Europe's future, than the EU-Africa Partnership, since this is nothing other than an important, a close, a deep and meaningful neighbourhood 芒聙聯it's neighbourhood.

Some might think, when they think about Africa, in all its diversity, it would be about development, and development is important. Some might think it would be about migration, and migration is important. Some might also see it's about youth 芒聙聯 strong youth 芒聙聯 in Africa, striving for prospects. Some might think it is about business partnerships, and I want to clearly emphasise and underline that these things are connected. Actually, when it comes to development, time and again I quote Ronald Reagan, who said, 'The best social programme is a job'. And the same is true for development 芒聙聯 the best development aid is economic partnership.

This is why Global Gateway is of the utmost importance in our EU-Africa partnership, but also when it comes to other parts of the world, to try to influence the rest of the world under the umbrella of other values than the values we strive for. Then,脗聽time and again, I underline 芒聙聯 also to our African partners 芒聙聯 that it's the EU that is trustworthy, it's the EU that's rule-of-law based, and it's the EU that's reliable in the longer term. And this is why partners in the EU and partners in Africa belong together for the better of the population of both.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marit Maij, namens de S&D-Fractie. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, we spreken over de vernieuwing van het EU-Afrika-partnerschap en het is onmogelijk om in het menu te spreken over hoe belangrijk die relatie is tussen de EU en Afrika. Daarom wil ik er twee punten uitlichten.

Ten eerste is er het belang van uitwisseling tussen mensen, elkaar leren kennen en samenwerken, vooral jonge mensen die in Afrika gaan studeren of jonge mensen die in Europa komen studeren. Onderzoekers die samenwerken om de uitdagingen van deze tijd, zoals de klimaatverandering, het bestrijden van armoede en gendergelijkheid, aan te gaan. Programma's als Erasmus+ en Horizon moeten daarom worden versterkt.

Het tweede belang is het belang van eerlijke handel en eerlijke investeringen. We hebben de grondstoffen en mineralen uit de Afrikaanse bodem nodig voor onze energietransitie en voor onze digitale transitie. Maar we moeten veel meer actie ondernemen om te zorgen dat de mijnbouw die daarbij hoort geen schade toebrengt aan de mens en aan de natuur, en dat bloedmineralen de Europese Unie niet inkomen. Afrika is ons buurcontinent en in Nederland zeggen we: 芒聙聹Beter een goede buur dan een verre vriend.芒聙聺. Laat dat de spirit zijn van het vernieuwde EU-Afrika-partnerschap.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ma脜聜gorzata Gosiewska, w imieniu grupy ECR. 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Afryka nie potrzebuje ja脜聜mu脜录ny. Potrzebuje prawdziwego partnerstwa, szacunku i realnych dzia脜聜a脜聞. Zamiast pustych obietnic inwestujmy w edukacj脛聶. Zamiast kr脙鲁tkich akcji humanitarnych budujmy szpitale. Pomagajmy w budowie silnych i przejrzystych instytucji, kt脙鲁re dadz脛聟 ludziom zaufanie do w脜聜asnych pa脜聞stw, bo tylko wtedy zatrzymamy nielegaln脛聟 migracj脛聶 i handel lud脜潞mi.

I nie zapominajmy przy tym o tych, kt脙鲁rzy od lat s脛聟 na pierwszej linii pomocy: o wsp脙鲁lnotach religijnych i ko脜聸cio脜聜ach, zw脜聜aszcza chrze脜聸cija脜聞skich. To one nios脛聟 edukacj脛聶, opiek脛聶 i nadziej脛聶 tam, gdzie nie dociera pa脜聞stwo ani organizacje mi脛聶dzynarodowe. Buduj脛聟 szko脜聜y i szpitale, s脛聟 cz脛聶sto jedynym ratunkiem dla ubogich.

Europo! Masz wyb脙鲁r. Mo脜录esz dalej udawa脛聡, 脜录e pomagasz, albo zacz脛聟脛聡 budowa脛聡 prawdziwe partnerstwo i wsp脙鲁lne bezpiecze脜聞stwo. Bo je脜聸li Europa nie b脛聶dzie aktywnie uczestniczy脛聡 w tworzeniu zdrowych, sprawiedliwych spo脜聜ecze脜聞stw, zrobi脛聟 to Rosja i Chiny na sw脙鲁j autorytarny spos脙鲁b.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hilde Vautmans, namens de Renew-Fractie. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, Afrika is voor Europa een cruciaal continent. Wie vandaag naar Afrika kijkt, ziet een continent van groei, van jeugd, van innovatie. Dat is de eerste pijler waarop we die samenwerking moeten inzetten: een versterkte samenwerking rond Erasmus+. De tweede pijler is die van vrede en veiligheid en wij herhalen hier toch wel onze eerdere resoluties dat Europa een rol moet spelen in het be脙芦indigen van die oorlog tussen Rwanda en Congo. Afrika is op dat ogenblik ook een belangrijke partner in het bestrijden van irreguliere migratie. Samen kunnen we die grondoorzaken aanpakken voorbij de symptoombestrijding. En de derde pijler, zoals de commissaris heeft gezegd, is die van welvaart: de Global Gateway, samenwerken rond energie, rond onze havens, rond zeldzame aardmetalen.

We moeten beseffen dat Afrika vandaag voor Europa even belangrijk is als China of Amerika. Dat wij Afrika heel hard nodig gaan hebben om onze energieprijzen naar beneden te krijgen, om los te komen van onze afhankelijkheid van Russische en Chinese aardmetalen. Europa heeft verschillende fases gehad in haar samenwerking met Afrika. De eerste was die koloniale fase waarin Europa knechtte en de tweede was het tijdperk van ontwikkelingssamenwerking waarbij we altijd met dat vingertje zwaaiden. Vandaag moeten we werk maken van dat derde tijdperk waarin Europa en Afrika partners worden met wederzijds respect, wederzijdse belangen en wederzijdse sterktes. Tijd voor een vernieuwd partnerschap.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Erik Marquardt, im Namen der Verts/ALE-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, Herr Kommissar! Sch脙露n, dass Sie da sind. Sie haben ja in Ihrer Eingangsrede gesagt, dass wir der wichtigste Partner Afrikas in verschiedenen Bereichen sind. Ich fand es wichtig, dass Sie auch noch einmal herausgehoben haben, dass das eben nicht gottgegeben ist, sondern dass es da Konkurrenz gibt. Es geht ja l脙陇ngst nicht mehr um die Frage, ob es irgendwelche Partnerschaften mit Afrika gibt, sondern um die Frage, wer diese Partnerschaften hat und auf Grundlage welcher Werte diese Partnerschaften stattfinden.

Ich finde, in diesem Wettbewerb tun wir gut daran, das beste Angebot zu machen脗聽芒聙聯 ein Angebot, das auf Demokratie, auf Menschenrechten, auf nachhaltiger Entwicklung basiert. Und Demokratie, Menschenrechte, nachhaltige Entwicklung, das sind Dinge, die fallen auch in Afrika nicht einfach vom Himmel, sondern das ist harte Arbeit. Harte Arbeit, wo wir einen gro脙聼en Unterschied machen k脙露nnen zu unserer Konkurrenz, wo wir klarmachen k脙露nnen, dass wir diejenigen sind 芒聙聯 besonders im Verh脙陇ltnis zu Russland 芒聙聯, die wirklich Frieden wollen auf diesem Kontinent, dass wir diejenigen sind, die Demokratie nicht einfach 脙录berst脙录lpen wollen, sondern dass wir daran glauben, dass das die beste Form ist, das Zusammenleben zu organisieren.

Ich glaube, wenn man in einzelne Teilbereiche geht, dann werden wir auch schnell feststellen, dass, wenn unser Angebot ernsthaft ist, wir nat脙录rlich 脙录ber Migration reden m脙录ssen, dass wir aber 芒聙聯 und das eint uns ja 芒聙聯, wenn wir Schlepper bek脙陇mpfen wollen, eben auch Alternativen zu diesen Schleppern brauchen, wenn diese Partnerschaft von Erfolg gekr脙露nt sein will.

Ich m脙露chte zum Abschluss auch noch sagen: Afrika ist der Kontinent, der am wenigsten zur Klimakrise beigetragen hat, aber am h脙陇rtesten darunter leidet. Ich glaube, es ist wichtig, dass wir auch eine Verantwortung sp脙录ren, eine Verantwortung gegen脙录ber den Erwartungen, die Afrika gerechterweise an uns hat, dass wir dort investieren. Zum Abschluss: Es ist klar脗聽芒聙聯 Europa braucht Afrika, Afrika braucht Europa, und darauf sollte unsere Partnerschaft basieren. Daran sollten wir arbeiten. Ich finde gut, dass wir heute so konstruktive Gespr脙陇che hatten und auch eine breite Mehrheit im Parlament finden, um diese Entschlie脙聼ung dann am Donnerstag zu beschlie脙聼en.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marc Botenga, au nom du groupe The Left. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, pendant des d脙漏cennies, l'Europe a 脙漏t脙漏 le principal partenaire de l'Afrique. Dirigeants europ脙漏ens apr脙篓s dirigeants europ脙漏ens ont fait de grandes promesses: on a promis la paix aux Africains, ils ont vu la guerre, on a promis la prosp脙漏rit脙漏, ils ont vu la pauvret脙漏 et ils ont vu le sous-d脙漏veloppement.

Pendant des ann脙漏es, nous avons impos脙漏 aux Africains脗聽des accords 脙漏conomiques iniques et des r脙漏formes n脙漏olib脙漏rales avec de grandes promesses, encore une fois. Quel est le r脙漏sultat net aujourd'hui? Les 脙漏conomies africaines restent largement d脙漏pendantes de l'exportation des mati脙篓res premi脙篓res脗聽芒聙聯 75脗聽% de leurs exportations脗聽芒聙聯, chaque ann脙漏e l'Afrique perd 76脗聽milliards d'euros via la fuite des capitaux et l'脙漏vasion fiscale, et, quand ils arrivent en Europe, m脙陋me avec tous leurs papiers et passeport en ordre, les Africains risquent toujours de se faire humilier et renvoyer aux a脙漏roports.

Puis vous venez pleurnicher en disant: 脗芦Ah oui, mais les Africains ils vont voir les Chinois.脗禄 Ben oui, c'est normal. Les Africains nous disent: 脗芦Ils nous offrent un meilleur deal, eux, ils nous respectent.脗禄 Il ne faut pas pleurnicher, vous pleurnichez, parce que vous voulez encore leurs ressources et leurs richesses.

Changeons de cap. Arr脙陋tons de m脙漏priser les Africains, arr脙陋tons de voir uniquement leurs richesses comme une source de pillage et commen脙搂ons, lan脙搂ons un commerce 脙漏quitable et un partenariat d'脙漏gal 脙聽 脙漏gal.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ingeborg Ter Laak (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, the European Union and African Union partnership is more essential than ever. In an era of shifting political alliances, a strong partnership founded on equality, shared responsibilities and mutual trust is essential.

The EU is the largest trading and investment power in Africa. These policies should be aligned for more effective external action. Complex realities of fragile and conflict-affected settings, such as Sudan, need a separate strategy focused on governance, peace building and humanitarian aid. And yes, the EU should assist in financing gaps left by USAID in essential services such as health, food security and climate adaptation.

Mutual cooperation should be sought in the execution of the EU Pact on Migration and Asylum, exploration of natural resources and cooperation on critical raw materials for environmental transitions and transparent and traceable supply chains.

The seventh EU-African Union Summit should reaffirm the commitment of the partnership and create cooperation on common challenges.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Thank you, colleague, for accepting the blue card, and thank you, especially, for mentioning Sudan in this context.

You rightfully said that, in this conflict in Sudan, we need to send humanitarian aid and impose diplomatic pressure. At the same time, we see, especially by the RSF, the most heinous war crimes in a century. Would you agree with me that we should also consider military interventions in cases where human rights are violated on that scale?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ingeborg Ter Laak (PPE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 I used to work in the armed forces, and I would be careful in deploying any ammunition to Sudan. But yes, I think we should stress more political pressure, democratic pressure on Sudan and really stop the conflict. It's a tragedy and it's affecting a lot of people. People are starving there, they're being raped on a daily basis. It's millions of people. So, please put more pressure on Sudan.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Udo Bullmann (S&D). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, Herr Kommissar, werte Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Afrika ist der j脙录ngste Kontinent auf dieser Welt. 40脗聽Prozent der Bev脙露lkerung dort sind j脙录nger als 15脗聽Jahre. Das sind hunderte Millionen junger Menschen, die sehnlichst warten auf gute Bildung und Ausbildung, auf sinnvolle Arbeit, mit der sie einen Beitrag leisten k脙露nnen zur Zukunft ihrer L脙陇nder.

Genau hier liegt die Herausforderung des Gipfels in Angola, aber auch die Chance. Wir k脙露nnen beitragen, Gro脙聼es zu leisten. Wir k脙露nnen Partner sein脗聽芒聙聯 dann, wenn unsere Programme darauf ausgerichtet sind, unsere Zusammenarbeit darauf ausgerichtet ist, in der Tat die Probleme zu l脙露sen. Kampf gegen Ungleichheit, Wertsch脙露pfung in den Partnerl脙陇ndern fordern, gute Arbeitspl脙陇tze f脙录r die Zukunft: Das muss ins Aufgabenheft der Europ脙陇er f脙录r diesen Gipfel脗聽芒聙聯 nichts weniger, und dazu wollen wir beitragen.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tiago Moreira de S脙隆 (PfE). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Portugal e a Europa t脙陋m 脙聛frica na sua carne. N脙拢o apenas nos mapas, nas rotas do com脙漏rcio ou nos tratados da diplomacia, mas nas veias, no sangue, na mem脙鲁ria viva do nosso ser coletivo.

脙聛lvaro de Campos escreveu sobre as 脗芦naus antigas脗禄, os 脗芦portos de 脙聛frica脗禄 e os 脗芦ventos do Sul脗禄 que sentia na sua carne. Como o poeta, sentimos 脙聛frica na nossa carne, porque o nosso destino se cruzou com o dela, nas dores, nas esperan脙搂as, nas trocas e nas promessas.

Por isso, para n脙鲁s, 脙聛frica 脙漏 muito mais do que um continente rico em recursos ou um palco da disputa entre as grandes pot脙陋ncias. E 脙漏, por isso, que quando, na Nig脙漏ria, em Mo脙搂ambique ou no Congo, crist脙拢os s脙拢o perseguidos e mortos, uma ferida se abre no nosso corpo comum.

Agora, ao celebrarmos em Luanda os 25 anos da parceria Uni脙拢o Europeia芒聙聭脙聛frica, recordemos que esquecer esse corpo comum 脙漏 perder a pr脙鲁pria alma. Esquecer a carne de 脙聛frica 脙漏 perder a alma da Europa.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marion Mar脙漏chal (ECR). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, Monsieur脗聽Brunner, comme commissaire charg脙漏 des questions migratoires, il vous appartient d'en finir avec les logiques qui jusqu'ici ont pr脙漏valu dans le rapport de l'Europe avec le continent africain. La principale strat脙漏gie de la Commission a 脙漏t脙漏 d'encourager l'immigration et de se perdre dans un co脙禄teux saupoudrage, tout en cherchant 脙聽 imposer au passage aux Africains les id脙漏ologies vertes et les valeurs 脗芦woke脗禄 promues par Bruxelles.

Cette strat脙漏gie est une course 脙聽 l'ab脙庐me. Tandis que nous importons massivement la jeunesse africaine, nous laissons le continent 脙聽 nos rivaux commerciaux. Les Africains et les Europ脙漏ens ont pourtant un int脙漏r脙陋t mutuel 脙聽 tarir les sources d'immigration. Les pays d'accueil et de transit africains subissent les m脙陋mes probl脙篓mes que maints pays d'accueil europ脙漏ens: criminalit脙漏, prostitution, drogue, violence, ch脙麓mage, terrorisme islamiste, trafic d'脙陋tres humains. Et comme nous, les pays de d脙漏part voient leurs classes sup脙漏rieures s'exiler.

Monsieur le Commissaire, il est temps d'agir avec pragmatisme et de rompre avec la diplomatie st脙漏rile de l'aum脙麓ne et des droits de l'homme. Mme脗聽Meloni, elle, l'a bien compris avec son plan Mattei qui lie clairement coop脙漏ration 脙漏conomique et lutte contre l'immigration. C'est un mod脙篓le, me semble-t-il, qu'il faut suivre. Bruxelles doit s'inspirer de cette logique gagnant-gagnant.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Barry Andrews (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, colleagues, as we all know, there is a massive change in our relationship with Africa evolving over the last decade, and it is embodied in the Global Gateway initiative, which is all about leveraging private sector investment, using European capital to try and create investment in energy, digital, transport, and, of course, also in health and education. That's all good and it's all positive, and I think it's really scaled up in the last few years under Commissioner S脙颅kela.

But I have two worries 芒聙聯 first of all: debt. Debt is one of the most serious problems that Africa faces. Average bond yields in Africa are almost 10脗聽%, compared to just 3脗聽% in the European Union. And last year, developing nations paid USD脗聽487脗聽billion in external public debt 芒聙聯 that's USD脗聽25脗聽billion more than they received in new debt. So net debt flows are going from poor countries to rich countries, and this is really a crisis that we have to address. We have to recognise that when we move from overseas development assistance to loans, this is exactly what happens, so we have to be very careful about it.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael Gahler (PPE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, Herr Kommissar! Ich hoffe, dass wir diesen EU-AU芒聙聭Gipfel nutzen脗聽芒聙聯 dass wir ihn nutzen, Bilanz zu ziehen, Zwischenbilanz zu ziehen, und dass wir den afrikanischen Freunden und Partnern in unserer Nachbarschaft erneut unsere umf脙陇nglichen Angebote machen. Sie haben erw脙陇hnt, Herr Kommissar, wo wir f脙录hrend sind in den Kontakten mit Afrika im Vergleich zu anderen Kontinenten. Das m脙录ssen wir ausbauen und besser machen, wo es erforderlich ist.

Wir wissen nat脙录rlich, wo es die Probleme gibt. Sudan ist angesprochen worden. Wir haben L脙陇nder wie Tansania, da sitzt der Oppositionsf脙录hrer im Gef脙陇ngnis, oder in C脙麓te d'Ivoire wird er nicht zur Wahl zugelassen. Das sind Dinge, die uns auch nicht gefallen k脙露nnen, aber wir sollten als Europ脙陇er ein vern脙录nftiges, umfassendes Angebot machen, denn wir sind nicht alleine. Wir m脙录ssen unsere Art und Weise anbieten, wie wir helfen wollen, dass Afrika sich entwickelt, wie wir helfen wollen, dass Afrika auf der Weltb脙录hne eine st脙陇rkere wirtschaftliche und politische Rolle spielt.

Wir sind nicht alleine 芒聙聯 Russland ist da, China ist da. Aber ich bin zuversichtlich. Wenn wir es richtig anlegen, dann werden die Afrikaner in ihrer gro脙聼en Mehrheit auch erkennen, dass wir auf Dauer die besseren Partner sind. Machen wir auch das Beste draus.

脗聽脗聽Murielle Laurent (S&D). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, les 24 et 25 novembre prochain se tient 脙聽 Luanda, en Angola, le septi脙篓me sommet Union europ脙漏enne 芒聙聯 Union africaine. Il marque le 25谩碌聣 anniversaire du partenariat entre nos deux continents et intervient 脙聽 un moment charni脙篓re. Alors que les 脙聣tats-Unis ont suspendu une partie de leur aide aux populations des pays africains les plus vuln脙漏rables, l'Union europ脙漏enne doit plus que jamais 脙陋tre 脙聽 la hauteur. Elle doit 脙陋tre un partenaire fiable, solidaire et pr脙漏visible pour l'ensemble des pays d'Afrique.

Ce partenariat, qui est une priorit脙漏 pour les socialistes europ脙漏ens, doit 脙陋tre v脙漏ritablement 脙漏quilibr脙漏, fond脙漏 sur la r脙漏ciprocit脙漏 et des valeurs communes. Ensemble, nous devons lutter contre les atteintes 脙聽 l'脙漏tat de droit et la d脙漏mocratie, contre la violence, les in脙漏galit脙漏s et les disparit脙漏s en mati脙篓re d'脙漏ducation. Plus que jamais, le renforcement de notre coop脙漏ration dans des domaines essentiels, la paix, la sant脙漏, la transition 脙漏cologique et num脙漏rique ainsi que le d脙漏veloppement humain est indispensable. Je suis convaincue qu'ensemble, nous pouvons relever les d脙漏fis mondiaux et faire 脙漏merger des opportunit脙漏s partag脙漏es au service d'un avenir commun plus juste et plus durable, sans mis脙漏rabilisme, simplement d'脙漏gal 脙聽 脙漏gal dans un monde qui change.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Kruis (PfE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, in november reist Ursula von der Leyen af naar de EU-Afrika-top in Angola 芒聙聯 dat is niet het land van Ntula Kabongo, die kwam uit de Centraal-Afrikaanse Republiek 芒聙聯 als een soort Euromissionaris op safari met koffers vol Nederlands belastinggeld. Tientallen miljarden euro's verdwijnen de komende jaren in de eeuwige kringloop van hulp en afhankelijkheid. Ondertussen groeit de bevolking van Afrika sneller dan het begrotingstekort van Frankrijk. De bevolking van Afrika verviervoudigt de komende decennia. Aan het einde van deze eeuw zijn er meer dan 4脗聽miljard Afrikanen en de helft daarvan wil emigreren, velen naar Europa. Dat zien we nu al en dat terwijl de Europese bevolking krimpt.

Er is daarom maar 脙漏脙漏n prioriteit die telt, namelijk Afrika teruggeven wat Afrika ons brengt: migranten. Geen geld, geen partnerschappen, geen investeringen, geen handel, niets van dat alles zonder keiharde afspraken over het terugnemen van uitgeprocedeerde migranten. Want Nederland is geen Leger des Heils van Afrika. Het is tijd dat we stoppen met het redden van de wereld en eindelijk opkomen voor onze eigen mensen. Want wie alles weggeeft, houdt voor zichzelf niets over.

Andere mensen mochten langer doorpraten dan ik, dus u gaat niet interrumperen tot ik ben uitgepraat.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Paolo Inselvini (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, basta trattare l'Africa come un terreno di competizione tra potenze! Gi脙聽 altri lo stanno facendo e con grande rapidit脙聽.

Noi dobbiamo iniziare invece a considerarla come un alleato strategico e non solo un problema da gestire. L'Africa non 脙篓 solo un continente di bisogni: dobbiamo cooperare per condividere crescita, sicurezza e stabilit脙聽. Ha 1,4 miliardi di abitanti, che nel 2050 diventeranno quasi 2,5: et脙聽 media 19 anni e un potenziale di crescita del PIL stimato tra il 4 e il 5% annuo.

L'UE resta ancora il primo partner commerciale e il principale investitore ma la nostra quota si riduce ogni anno. Nel 2000 rappresentavamo il 36% del commercio africano; oggi siamo sotto il 25%. Abbiamo infatti consegnato la nostra quota di mercato ad altri, come la Cina. Quindi, o torniamo protagonisti o saremo costretti ad essere irrilevanti. Seguiamo quindi la strada del piano Mattei e non ripetiamo gli errori del passato.

Serve una cooperazione che generi valore per entrambi i continenti, vera, fondata su investimenti, infrastrutture, energia, formazione e non su aiuti calati dall'alto o, peggio, gestiti da multinazionali opache. Serve inoltre prevenire l'immigrazione clandestina alla radice e quindi non carit脙聽 ma strategia, non assistenza ma alleanza e, allo stesso tempo, non invasione ma cooperazione.

Secondo i dati della Commissione, infatti, ogni miliardo di euro investito in cooperazione produttiva 芒聙聯 come quella che l'Italia sta mettendo in campo con il piano Mattei 芒聙聯 pu脙虏 generare fino a 60.000 nuovi posti di lavoro e 1,8 punti di PIL. Questi sono numeri, non slogan, e devono diventare la base del nostro metodo. Serve per脙虏 coerenza: non possiamo parlare di collaborazione e poi imporre modelli o condizionalit脙聽 che non rispettano la sovranit脙聽 dei nostri interlocutori.

E lasciatemelo dire con chiarezza: l'Africa non aspetter脙聽 l'Europa! O ci saremo da protagonisti o ci ritroveremo spettatori del mondo che cambia. L'Italia si 脙篓 accorta di questa urgenza: ora 脙篓 il momento di seguirci.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽France Jamet (PfE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, 2019: Abidjan; 2022: Bruxelles; 2025: l'Angola. 脙聙 chaque sommet, l'Union europ脙漏enne nous sort un nouvel accord pour reconduire sa relation avec le continent africain. Et elle fait semblant de ne pas voir que les 脙聣tats d'Afrique n'ont pas besoin de notre aide, mais d'une coop脙漏ration 脙漏quilibr脙漏e entre peuples souverains, respectueux de leurs sp脙漏cificit脙漏s, de leur identit脙漏 et de leurs int脙漏r脙陋ts. Sur l'immigration, sur la souverainet脙漏 alimentaire, sur la protection des oc脙漏ans et de la biodiversit脙漏, nous avons beaucoup et tant 脙聽 faire ensemble.

Or, vous le savez, nous nous battons ici, 脙聽 Bruxelles, pour le respect de la souverainet脙漏 et de l'ind脙漏pendance de la France et de son peuple. C'est pourquoi nous d脙漏fendons aussi l'ind脙漏pendance et la souverainet脙漏 des peuples africains. Pour nous, souverainistes et patriotes, la d脙漏fense du droit des peuples 脙聽 disposer d'eux-m脙陋mes s'applique 脙聽 tous les peuples.

脗聽脗聽S脙漏rgio Humberto (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, caros colegas, o dia j脙隆 vai longo, a caminho das 23 horas, mas permitam芒聙聭me sublinhar uma dimens脙拢o que Portugal conhece bem: a das rela脙搂脙碌es hist脙鲁ricas, culturais e lingu脙颅sticas que unem a Europa e a 脙聛frica.

A Lusofonia 脙漏 um espa脙搂o natural de coopera脙搂脙拢o e di脙隆logo que j脙隆 serve de ponte entre continentes e de exemplo de como a Uni脙拢o Europeia se pode articular com a CPLP para uma presen脙搂a mais pr脙鲁xima, humana e eficaz.

Portugal tem demonstrado, ao longo das 脙潞ltimas d脙漏cadas, que 脙漏 poss脙颅vel refor脙搂ar os la脙搂os pol脙颅ticos, econ脙鲁micos e culturais entre os dois continentes, com respeito m脙潞tuo e ganhos partilhados.

Destaco tr脙陋s prioridades que n脙拢o podem faltar na renova脙搂脙拢o desta parceria. Primeiro, a paz, a seguran脙搂a e o Estado de direito; segundo, a digitaliza脙搂脙拢o e a conectividade; terceiro, a integra脙搂脙拢o econ脙鲁mica e o com脙漏rcio justo.

脙聛frica n脙拢o 脙漏 apenas um parceiro estrat脙漏gico. 脙聣 um parceiro de futuro, essencial para a prosperidade e a seguran脙搂a global.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Aleksandar Nikolic (PfE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, vous parlez de coop脙漏ration avec l'Afrique et de partenaires africains, mais vous 脙陋tes surtout paternalistes vis-脙聽-vis de ce grand continent. Une vraie relation commerciale passe par la recherche d'int脙漏r脙陋ts communs, de d脙漏veloppement mutuel, d'脙漏gal 脙聽 脙漏gal. Mais vous ne cherchez rien de tout cela, vous asservissez un continent en l'inondant d'aides et d'investissements dont nous ne verrons jamais la contrepartie. Vous n'脙陋tes m脙陋me pas capables de n脙漏gocier la reprise des immigr脙漏s clandestins que nous voulons expulser. Pire! Vous l'avez dit tout 脙聽 l'heure, vous facilitez l'immigration l脙漏gale.

Bruxelles prend l'argent des Fran脙搂ais et des Europ脙漏ens. Elle distribue 脙聽 l'aveugle, dans des projets purement locaux qui devraient 脙陋tre du ressort des 脙聣tats africains. En mettant ainsi sous tutelle ce continent, vous ne le d脙漏veloppez pas, vous le soumettez. Comme du temps de la colonisation, vous appauvrissez les Europ脙漏ens et infantilisez les Africains en freinant leur 脙漏mancipation. Vous les confortez dans l'id脙漏e que leur avenir c'est soit d'attendre l'argent des Europ脙漏ens, soit d'immigrer en Europe. Croire que les Africains doivent 脙陋tre syst脙漏matiquement d脙漏pendants des Europ脙漏ens comme de grands enfants s'apparente au pire des racismes. Le racisme de m脙漏pris.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Francisco Jos脙漏 Mill脙隆n Mon (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, desde la 脙潞ltima cumbre Uni脙鲁n Europea-脙聛frica 芒聙聰celebrada poco antes de la invasi脙鲁n rusa de Ucrania芒聙聰 hemos visto un aumento de las tensiones geopol脙颅ticas y de la incertidumbre global. En este contexto tan polarizado, debemos impulsar una asociaci脙鲁n m脙隆s estrecha con este continente vecino, en el que, adem脙隆s, crece la presencia e influencia de otros actores, como China y Rusia.

Son varias las 脙隆reas donde reforzar nuestra cooperaci脙鲁n: la sanidad, la educaci脙鲁n, el desarrollo de infraestructuras, la conectividad digital o la mejora institucional. Asimismo, nuestras inversiones y comercio deben contribuir al desarrollo econ脙鲁mico y al empleo en el continente africano: as脙颅 mejorar脙隆 la necesaria gesti脙鲁n de los flujos migratorios, un objetivo prioritario tambi脙漏n.

Pero todo ello ser脙颅a inalcanzable sin seguridad: se necesita estabilidad, superaci脙鲁n de conflictos y la lucha contra el terrorismo y el crimen organizado, muy presentes, por ejemplo, en el Sahel. Esta es una regi脙鲁n en una situaci脙鲁n muy preocupante que requiere la atenci脙鲁n de la cumbre. El Servicio Europeo de Acci脙鲁n Exterior debe presentar, cuanto antes, su estrategia revisada para el Sahel.

脙聛frica es un continente vecino, cada vez m脙隆s poblado. Su estabilidad y prosperidad repercuten directamente en Europa. Espero que la pr脙鲁xima cumbre sea fruct脙颅fera.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ang脙漏line Furet (PfE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, nous d脙漏battons aujourd'hui de la reconduction du partenariat entre l'Union europ脙漏enne et l'Afrique, 25脗聽ans apr脙篓s sa cr脙漏ation. Un partenariat qu'on nous pr脙漏sente comme renouvel脙漏, mais qui, dans les faits, n'a produit que des d脙漏clarations creuses et des frustrations r脙漏elles.

Sur le terrain, les peuples africains veulent de l'脙漏lectricit脙漏, des routes, des emplois; Bruxelles leur parle de neutralit脙漏 carbone et de transition verte. Pendant que la Commission r脙漏dige des rapports, la Chine construit des ports, la Turquie des h脙麓pitaux et la Russie des centrales.

La faute 脙聽 qui? 脙聙 une Commission europ脙漏enne d脙漏connect脙漏e du r脙漏el, men脙漏e par Ursula脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen qui, faute de programme, a fait du pacte vert son unique doctrine, qu'elle cherche d脙漏sormais 脙聽 imposer 脙聽 l'Afrique.

Nous d脙漏fendons un autre cap: autonomie 脙漏nerg脙漏tique, cod脙漏veloppement industriel et r脙漏ciprocit脙漏. Parce qu'un vrai partenariat repose sur le respect mutuel, pas sur la d脙漏pendance. L'Afrique m脙漏rite du respect, l'Europe un petit peu de lucidit脙漏.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hildegard Bentele (PPE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, sehr geehrter Herr Kommissar, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Keine Entwicklungshilfe mehr aus der Gie脙聼kanne, daf脙录r echte Zusammenarbeit durch langfristige und strategische Partnerschaften zwischen Europa und Afrika 芒聙聯 das ist das neue Angebot, das die EU als wichtigster Handelspartner an die afrikanischen L脙陇nder macht. Daf脙录r hat die Kommissionspr脙陇sidentin die Initiative Global Gateway gestartet. Als Europ脙陇isches Parlament sind wir aktuell dabei, diese Strategie im Hinblick auf die beteiligten Unternehmen, auf ihre strategische Ausrichtung und Bekanntheit, auf ihre Entscheidungsstrukturen und auf ihre Finanzierungsinstrumente hin auszubauen und zu verbessern.

Der脗聽Global Gateway Summit in Br脙录ssel letzte Woche, meine Gespr脙陇che vor Ort in Afrika mit Unternehmen auf beiden Seiten haben gezeigt, es besteht gro脙聼es Interesse an einer Zusammenarbeit zu echtem beiderseitigen Nutzen. Wir m脙录ssen aber jetzt liefern. Nach 25脗聽Jahren Partnerschaft zwischen Europ脙陇ischer und Afrikanischer Union wird es daf脙录r h脙露chste Zeit 芒聙聯 zwei Unionen, eine Vision. Der anstehende Gipfel in Angola ist eine gro脙聼e Chance, neue Dynamik in unsere Beziehungen zu bringen. Allen Beteiligten viel Erfolg dabei!

脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

Interventions 脙聽 la demande

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, building strong relations with Africa is an undisputed strategic need for Europe. But we cannot build them unconditionally; Europe needs to respect itself.

Our message to African leaders should be the following: you want to neglect democratic principles? OK, it has consequences. You want to support Russian aggression in Ukraine? OK, it has consequences. You want to refuse to accept the repatriation of your citizens? OK, it has consequences.

Europe is sitting on top of a huge economic tool that we are not using. That is insane. The Finnish Government has decided that it will not give development aid to countries that are either supporting Russian aggression, or are not accepting repatriation of their citizens.

Let's make this Finnish position a European position.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Fin des interventions 脙聽 la demande)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Magnus Brunner, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, ladies and gentlemen, it's quite late now, but still a couple of words to conclude maybe. I think the debate today demonstrated, quite importantly, both the importance and the depth of EU-Africa relations. And given Africa's role in the world, like we heard just before, the scale of the European Union's economic and also political relations with Africa, and also our cultural ties, our historical ties, we need to strengthen our engagement with Africa.

This is also in our interest in terms of managing migration flows, on the one hand, ending conflicts and also creating opportunities for European businesses. And the EU interests need to be combined with our traditional development support for Africa.

Our EU-Africa partnership is important at global level, at the United Nations and within our engagement with the Global South. The EU and Africa will remain key partners in terms of economic cooperation, but also on security, health, education, research and other issues. We need to keep fine-tuning our cooperation to reflect our geopolitical developments, where we count, of course, on the input of the European Parliament.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Le Pr脙漏sident. 芒聙聯 Merci beaucoup Monsieur le Commissaire. Je me permets un mot de conclusion pour ce d脙漏bat. Le d脙漏bat est 脙聽 pr脙漏sent clos.
